stem_zh:
Question 3 - Melplash Co
Metadata:
Syllabus areas (insert learning outcomes being tested in the
Workspace e.g. A1a)
A3(c), C1(b),
(e), C2(d),
C3(b)
Exhibits: Background page
The following exhibits, available on the left -hand side of the screen, provide
information relevant to the question.
1. Acquisition of transport operator
2. Financial information
3. Bid offer and acceptance
This information should be used to answer the question requirements within your
chosen response option(s).
Exhibits: Content
1 Acquisition of transport operator
Acquisition of Pymore Co
Melplash Co  is a leading bus operator in Wesnia, operating bus services in the
country’s south and central regions. Melplash Co’s board is keen to expand its bus
operations into other regions of the country and also would like to gain franchises to
operate trains on Wesnia’s rail network.
Melplash Co’s board has identified Pymore Co as an appealing takeover target.
Pymore Co operates bus services in Wesnia’s north and east regions and also a
franchise to run local train services in the east region. Pymore Co, in common with
other rail companies, has recently faced pressure from Wesnia’s government to
enhance investment in its trains and staffing in order to increase customer
satisfaction. Melplash Co’s board believes that Pymore Co will struggle to meet
these demands with the resources it has and therefore its directors will be open to a
takeover offer from Melplash Co.
Both companies are listed on Wesnia’s stock exchange. Melplash Co has a range of
institutional and smaller investors, with no shareholder holding more than 12% of
shares in the company. 45% of Pymore Co’s shares are owned by brothers Peter
and Patrick Tulham, who are respectively chief executive and operations director of
Pymore Co.
Reactions to the acquisition
Melplash Co’s board believes that its investors will be concerned about future
earnings per share if the acquisition goes ahead and how earnings per share might
differ between the two possible scenarios which the board envisages. They would be
sensitive about any increases in the gearing level and the implications for control of
the company of introducing new shareholders.
The Tulham brothers have discussed between themselves the possible terms of an
offer from Melplash Co. They have decided that they would consider accepting a
share offer provided they were promised executive directorships on the combined
company’s board with a package at least equivalent to what they were currently
receiving and service contracts for three years. Any financial impact of the Tulhams
becoming executive directors should be ignored in the calculations.
2 Financial information
Extracts from most recent accounts of Melplash Co and Pymore Co
Melplash  Pymore
$m $m
Profit before finance cost and tax 122 77
Finance cost -41 -35
Profit before tax 81 42
Tax -21 -9
Profit after tax 60 33
Issued $1 nominal shares 200 million 150 million
Earnings per share ($) $0.30 $0.22
Price-earnings ratio 13.2 11
Market price per share ($) $3.96 $2.42
Long-term liabilities (market value) ($m) 285 225
Gearing (Long-term liabilities/(Long-term liabilities +
Equity)) × 100% (market values) 26.50% 38.30%
Cash and cash equivalents ($m) 102 44
Industry average gearing is 34%.
3 Bid offer and acceptance
Melplash Co’s board is considering giving Pymore Co’s shareholders the choice
between two shares in Melplash Co for three shares in Pymore Co or a cash
alternative of $2.75 for each Pymore Co share. Melplash Co’s board is
contemplating two possible scenarios:
• All Pymore Co’s shareholders accept the cash offer.
• All Pymore Co’s shareholders accept the share offer.
Annual post-tax synergies from the merger are expected to be $8 million. The
combined company’s price-earnings ratio is expected to be the same as Melplash
Co’s was before the acquisition.
Cash payments for shares will be funded firstly by the balances of cash and cash
equivalents held by Melplash Co of $102m. Assume that the return on cash
surpluses which Melplash Co could make is 6% pre-tax.
The balance of the cash requirements for the cash payments for shares will be
funded by debt at a pre-tax cost of 9%.
Assume existing debt of Pymore Co will be taken on by Melplash Co and not paid off
using the balances of cash and cash equivalents, and that Pymore Co’s balances of
cash and cash equivalents will be maintained in the merged company.
Tax is payable at an annual rate of 25% on profits of both companies.
Exhibits: Requirements
Required:
(a) Calculate for the two scenarios:
• the funding required for the acquisition of Pymore Co;
• the impact on Melplash Co’s earnings per share; and
• the impact on Melplash Co’s gearing.
(9 marks)
(b) If the acquisition was to take place, discuss the concerns that Melplash
Co’s shareholders may have about:
• earnings per share;
• gearing level; and
• control of Melplash Co.
(7 marks)
(c) Discuss the regulatory and governance concerns relating to the promises
required by Peter and Patrick Tulham.
(4 marks)
Professional marks will be awarded for the demonstration of skill in analysis and
evaluation, scepticism and commercial acumen in your answer.
(5 marks)
(25 marks)
Response Types
Word processing and spreadsheet documents will be required.

stem_zh:
Q1
The following exhibits, available on the left-hand side of the screen,
provide information relevant to the question.
1. Walditch Co
2. Acquisition of Tincleton Co
3. Combined company valuation
4. Offer to Tincleton Co’s directors
This information should be used to answer the question
requirements within your chosen response option(s).
Requirements (50 marks)
(a) Prepare a report for the directors of Walditch Co which:
(i) Calculates the profit or loss on the sale of the infrastructure
support services division.
(2 marks)
(ii)
 Calculates the percentage gain or loss to the shareholders of
Tincleton Co under both the scenarios of all shareholders
accepting the cash offer and all shareholders accepting the
share exchange offer, and
 Concludes, solely on financial grounds, whether Tincleton Co’s
shareholders will accept the cash or share exchange offer.
(18 marks)
(iii) Discusses the problems with achieving the forecast operating
synergies from the acquisition, and the steps which Walditch Co’s
board can take to achieve the synergies.
(8 marks)
(iv) Discusses, for both the share exchange and cash offers:
 the impact on Walditch Co’s financial situation of the purchase
of Tincleton Co, and
 the likely reaction of Walditch Co’s shareholders.
​ (7 marks)
(b) Discuss the ethical considerations, from Walditch Co’s and
Tincleton Co’s viewpoints, relating to the possible private offer of
jobs and cash bonuses to Tincleton Co’s directors by Walditch Co.
(5 marks)
Professional skills marks will be awarded for the demonstration of
skill in analysis and evaluation, scepticism and commercial acumen
in your answer.
(10 marks)

Q2
The following exhibits, available on the left-hand side of
the screen, provide information relevant to the question.
1. Company information
2. New product investment
The information should be used to answer the
question requirements within your chosen response
option(s).
Requirements (25 marks)
(a) Calculate the adjusted present value (APV) for the
project and evaluate whether the investment should be
accepted or not. Show all relevant calculations.
(13 marks)
(b) Discuss the factors which will influence Fluftig Co’s
credit rating, evaluating the extent to which these will also
be important to its shareholders.
(7 marks)
Professional skills marks will be awarded for the
demonstration of skill in analysis and evaluation,
scepticism and commercial acumen in your answer.
(5 marks)

Q3
The following exhibits, available on the left-hand side of
the screen, provide information relevant to the question.
1. Darmsden Co hedging information
2. CEO comments
This information should be used to answer the
question requirements within your chosen response
option(s).
Requirements (25 marks)
(a) Recommend an appropriate hedging strategy for the
US$ cash payment which Darmsden Co is due to make,
discussing the factors which Darmsden Co’s board
should consider when deciding which hedging method to
use.
Show all relevant calculations, including the number of
contracts bought and sold in the exchange-traded currency
futures market.
(13 marks)
(b) Demonstrate to the CEO how the mark-to-market
process would work for the € futures hedge, including
discussing the cash flow implications and explaining what
will happen if Darmsden Co fails to maintain its margin
account.
Include calculations of the balance on the margin account
on both of the first two days of the hedge.
(7 marks)
Professional marks will be awarded for the demonstration
of skill in analysis and evaluation, scepticism and
commercial acumen in your answer.
(5 marks)

1 Washi Co is a large, unlisted company based in Japan and its local currency is the Japanese Yen
(JPY). It manufactures industrial equipment and parts. Initially Washi Co’s customers consisted of
other Japanese companies, but over the last 12 years it has expanded into overseas markets and
also sources its materials from around the world. The company ’s board of directors (BoD)
believes that the strategy of overseas investments, through subsidiary companies, branches and
joint ventures, has directly led to the company ’s substantial increase in value in the past few
years.
Washi 是一个未上市的日本公司，主要使用的是日元。他主要生产的是工业设备零件。起初
W 的客户主要是其他的日本公司， 但是在过去的12 个月中，W 扩大了海外市场且从全世界
采购原材料。公司董事会认为通过海外的战略投资，比如通过子公司，加盟，联营等方式，
在过去的几年中很大程度上增加了公司的价值。
Washi Co’s BoD is considering investing in a project based in Airone, whose currency is the Airone
Rand (ARD). It believes that the project will be an important addition to the company’s portfolio
of investments, because Washi Co does not currently have a significant presence in the part of
the world where Airone is located. It is intended that the project will commence in one year ’s
time. Details of the project are given below.
W 在考虑投资 A，A 的货币是 ARD，W 认为对 A 的投资很重要，因为 W 在 A 的所在地都有
很重要的经营。W 预计项目会在一年后开始。
Washi Co intends to finance the project through proceeds  from an agreed sale of a small
European subsidiary, with any remaining funding requirement being met by additional debt
finance issued in Japanese Yen. The company is due to receive the proceeds from the sale of a
European subsidiary company in six months ’ time and it will then invest these funds in
short-dated Japanese treasury bills for a further six months before they are needed for the
project. Washi Co has a centralised treasury department, which hedges expected future cash
flows against currency fluctuations.
W 想要通过卖掉欧洲的一个小的子公司，来筹集投资 A 的资金，其他的资金需求通过发行
日元债务来获得。W 卖掉欧洲子公司后，会在六个月后获得资金，他会短暂的把这些资金
投资到日元国债中去，直到需要用这笔钱。W 有统一的司库部门来进行对冲，防止出现货
币的价值波动。
Funding and financial information
The agreed proceeds from the sale of the European subsidiary company receivable in s ix months’
time are Euro (EUR) 80 million. The BoD is concerned about a negative fluctuation in EUR/JPY
rate between now and in six months when the EUR 80 million will be received. Therefore, it has
asked Washi Co ’s treasury department to hedge the expecte d receipt using one of currency
forwards, currency futures or exchange traded currency options. Washi Co’s  treasury
department has obtained the following information:
卖掉欧洲的子公司能够在六个月后收到欧元 80m,董事会担心六个月后欧元对日元发生贬值，
因此，找到司库部门对未来会收到的 EUR 80m 进行对冲。希望采用货币远期合同，期货或
者期权等方式进行对冲。
Airone’s annual inflation rate is 9% currently, but has fluctuated markedly in the last five years.
The Japanese annual inflation rate is 1.5% and has been stable for many years.
A 公司的通货膨胀率是 9%，但是在过去的 5 年中有着显著波动。日本的通货膨胀是 1.5%。
Pato Bank has offered Washi Co the possibility of using over-the-counter options to hedge the
EUR receipt instead of exchange traded currency options.
Pato 银行给 W 提供了 OTC 期权进行对冲。
Airone project information
A member of Washi Co’s finance team has produced the following estimates of the Airone
project which is expected to last for four years. The estimates are based on the notes given below
but not on the further information. The estimates have been checked and verified independently
for their numerical accuracy.
对 A 的投资预期持续 4 年，信息如下：
Notes (incorporated into the estimates above):
1. The estimates are based on using the end of the first year, when the project commences, as
the start of the project (year 0). The numbers are given in ARD million (m).
2. The total investment required for the project is ARD 10,150m and separated into buildings,
machinery and working capital in the table above. The machinery is eligible for tax allowable
depreciation on a straight-line basis and the working capital is redeemable at the end of the
project.
3. The impact of inflation has been incorporated into the sales revenue and cost figures, at
Airone’s current annual inflation figures.
数字已经考虑了通货膨胀
4. Corporation tax has been included based on Airone’s annual rate of 15%. The tax is payable in
the year that the tax liability arises.
税率 15%，且在产生所得税负债的当年应该交掉（税的现金流不递延）
Further information (not incorporated into the estimates above):
1. Undertaking the Airone project will result in lost sales for Washi Co. These sales would have
generated a pre-tax contribution of JPY 110m in the first year of the project, rising by the
Japanese rate of inflation in the following years 2 to 4 of the project.
收购 A 项目，会导致销售额的下降，这些销售额会产生第一年税前contribution JYP110m，
后面 2-4 年根据日本的通膨进行膨胀
2. The Airone project costs include components which are made in Japan by Washi Co and would
be imported to the Airone project. The pre-inflation revenues generated from the sale of the
components are estimated to be as follows:
These revenues are expected to increase by the Japanese inflation rate in years 2 to 4 of the
project. The contribution which Washi Co expects to earn on these components is 25% of
revenue.
2. The Japanese annual corporation tax rate is 30% and tax is payable in the year that the tax
liability arises. A bilateral tax treaty exists between Japan and Airone, which permits offset of
overseas tax against any Japanese tax liability on overseas earnings.
日本的所得税 30%，双边税收政策存在在日本和 Airon，这允许海外税和日本的税有一部分
可以抵消，不需要重复交税
4. Washi Co’s finance department has estimated a cost of capital of 12% to be used as a discount
rate for the project.
Required:
(a) Discuss how investing in overseas projects may enable Washi Co to gain competitive
advantage over its competitors, who only invest in domestic projects.             (5 marks)
答案
explanation_zh:
Washi Co may want to invest in overseas projects for a number of reasons which result in
competitive advantage for it, for example:
W 在以下情况下会进行海外投资：
Investing overseas may give Washi Co access to new markets and/or enable it to develop a
market for its products in locations where none existed before. Being involved in marketing and
selling products in overseas markets may also help it gain an understanding of the needs of
customers, which it may not have had if it merely exported its products.
投资可以进入新的市场或在以前没有进入过的地区发展产品。
Investing overseas may give Washi Co easier and cheaper access to raw materials it needs. It
would therefore make good strategic sense for it to undertake the overseas investment.
投资海外市场可以让 W 可以更容易和便宜的获得原材料
Investing in projects internationally may give Washi Co access to cheaper labour resources and/or
access to expertise which may not be readily available in Japan. This could therefore lead to
reduction in costs and give Washi Co an edge against its competitors.
海外投资可以获得更便宜的人工，或者可以获得其他地方专家的帮助，这会减少W 的成本
Closer proximity to markets, raw materials and labour resources may enable Washi Co to reduce
its costs. For example, transportation and other costs related to logistics may be reduced if
products are manufactured close to the markets where they are sold.
对市场有更强的有限性， 可以减少成本， 比如和地理位置相关的交通运输成本会减少。 可以
就近进行销售。
Risk, such as economic risk resulting from long-term currency fluctuations, may be reduced
where costs and revenues are matched and therefore naturally hedged.
经济因素造成的长期货币波动的风险可以降低，因为销售和支出本身就可以在币种上统一，
可以进行汇率波动的抵消
Washi Co may increase its reputation because it is based in the country within which it trades
leading to a competitive edge against its rivals.
增加公司的名声
International investments might reduce both the unsystematic and systematic risks for Washi Co
if its shareholders only hold well diversified portfolios in domestic markets, but not
internationally.
通过全球的投资组合多样化，可以减少系统风险和非系统风险
(Note: Credit will be given for alternative valid areas of discussion)
(b) Discuss the advantages and drawbacks of exchange traded option contracts compared with
over-the-counter options.                                                 (5 marks)
讨论一下场内期权交易和场外交易对比的优缺点
Advantages
Exchange traded options are readily available on the financial markets, their price and contract
details are transparent, and there is no need to negotiate these. Greater transparency and tight
regulations can make exchange traded options less risky. For these reasons, exchange traded
options’ transaction costs can be lower.
优点： 场内交易本身都是已经设置好的， 可以直接进行选择， 进行金融市场交易， 价格和合
同细节都是透明的， 不需要进行进一步的协商。 更高的透明度和严格的规则可以使得场内交
易的风险更小。因此场内交易的成本会更低一些。
The option buyer can sell (close) the options before expiry. American style options can be
exercised any time before expiry and most traded options are American style options, whereas
over-the-counter options tend to be European style options.
期权的购买这可以在到期钱就把期权卖掉。 美式期权可以再到期前的任何时候进行行权。 厂
内交易的大部分是美式期权。而 OTC 一般都是欧式期权
Disadvantages
The maturity date and contract sizes for exchange traded options are fixed, whereas
over-the-counter options can be tailored to the needs of parties buying and selling the options.
Exchange traded options tend to be of shorter terms, so if longer term options are needed, then
they would probably need to be over-the-counter.
A wider range of products (for example, a greater choice of currencies) is normally available in
over-the-counter options markets.
到期时间是固定的，而 OTC 是可以定制的。
一般场内交易的期权时间都是固定的且短暂的，如果想要有长期的期权，则需要选择 OTC。
因为场内交易的期权标的物种类较少，所以其他的标的物（比如币种）需要选择场外交易。
(c) Prepare a report for the board of directors of Washi Co which:
(i) Estimates the expected amount of JPY receivable under each hedge choice and the
additional debt finance needed to fund the Airone project for the preferred hedge choice;
(12 marks)
(ii) Estimates the net present value of the Airone project in Japanese Yen, based on the end of
year one being the start of the project (year 0);                                (9 marks)
(iii) Evaluates the preferred hedge choice made, the debt finance needed and whether the
Airone project should be undertaken, considering both financial and non-financial factors. (8
marks)
Professional marks will be awarded in part (c) for the format, structure and presentation of the
report. (4 marks)
问题解析：
给 W 董事会写一份报告，估计一下在对冲后可以收到的日元金额是多少，且有需要进行多
少的日元融资；
估计对 A 投资的项目的 NPV；
评估可选的对冲方法，融资需求和是否要对 A 进行投资
Report to the board of directors (BoD), Washi Co
Introduction
This report evaluates whether or not Washi Co should invest in the Airone project and the
amount of debt finance required of JPY 3,408·6 million (appendix 1) to fund the project. The
evaluation considers both the financial and the non-financial factors.
在写报告的时候， 一般建议在开头先简单阐述一下整份报告的结论， 说一下测算之后的结果
和决策。然后再具体展开。
这份报告评估了 W 是否应该投资 A，且仍需要的日元融资金额为 JPY3408.6m，评估从金融
和非金融两方面展开。
Evaluation of the preferred hedge choice and debt finance required
The income from the sale of the European subsidiary is maximised when futures contracts are
used. Therefore, these are chosen as Washi Co will borrow the least amount of debt finance as a
result. However, compared to the forward contract, futures are marked-to-market daily and
require a margin to be placed with the broker. This could affect Washi Co’s liquidity position. The
assumption has been made that basis reduces proportionally as the futures contracts approach
expiry, but there is no guarantee that this will be the case. Therefore, basis risk still exists with
futures contracts. Although forward contracts give a smaller return, there is no basis risk and
margin requirements. However, they do contain a higher risk of default as they are not market
traded. Options give the lowest return but would give Washi Co the flexibility of not exercising
the option should the Euro strengthen against the Yen.
这里可以对几种金融工具都进行说明，比较各自的特点，然后说出选择
当采用期货合同的时候， 卖欧洲子公司收到的钱是最多的， 因此这个对冲工具被选择， 可以
让 W 借最少的钱。但是和远期合同相比，期货是日盯市的，需要给券商缴纳保证金。这可
能会影响 W 的流动性。但是在这个题里没有特别强调，因此风险主要还是来源于期货合同
本身.虽然远期合同带来的回报小一些，但是他没有保证金的要求，因此风险也小。期权带
来的回报是最少的，但是会给 W 公司灵活性，当欧元升值的时候可以选择不行权。
Although the EUR 80 million receipt from the sale of the subsidiary has been agreed, there may
be a risk that the sale may fall through and/or the funds or some proportion of the funds are not
received. Washi Co may need to assess and factor in this risk, however small it may be.
虽然已经协定了将欧洲子公司卖 EUR 80m, 仍然存在一定的风险就是部分对价可能无法收
回。W 公司也要考虑这块儿的风险，虽然可能比较小。
The amount of interest on deposit is based on the current short-dated Japanese treasury bills and
the estimate of the borrowing requirement is computed from the predicted exchange rate
between ARD and JPY in a year’s time, based on the purchasing power parity. Both these
estimates could be inaccurate if changes occur over the coming months. Although insufficient
information is provided for a financial assessment, Washi Co should explore the possibility of
converting the EUR into ARD immediately on receipt and keeping it in an ARD bank account until
needed, instead of first converting EUR into JPY and then into ARD.
从货币市场对冲来说， 存款利息要根据购买力平价理论进行预测， 这可能也会导致一些预测
的不准确， 或者货币市场上发生一些超出预测的事项。 虽然这里我们没有充分的信息， 但是
W 公司还是需要去考虑一下把未来收到的 Eur 直接换成 ARD 然后存入 ARD 银行账户，知道
需要用钱的时候拿出来进行使用。而不是把欧元换成日元然后再在需要的时候换成ARD。
Using debt finance to make up any shortfall in the funding requirement may be appropriate for
Washi Co given that it is an unlisted company and therefore access to other sources of funding
may be limited. Nevertheless, Washi Co should assess how the extra borrowing would affect any
restrictive covenants placed on it and the impact on its cost of capital. Since the amount seems to
be small in the context of the project as a whole, this may not be a major problem.
收购需要额外的融资来弥补资金的不足，但是因为 W 是一个未上市公司，因此获得额外的
债券融资其实对 W 来说是有一些困难的。W 应该评估怎样去获取额外的借款， 且这些借款
会怎样影响到公司的资本成本， 财务风险。 但是可能这个金额不算是很大， 因此不太会给公
司带来太大的问题。
Washi Co should also explore whether or not investing in the Airone project restricts its ability to
fund other projects or affects its ability to continue normal business activity, especially if Washi
Co is facing the possibility of hard capital rationing.
W 公司还应该考虑一下投资 A 是否会对公司其他正在持续的商业活动带来影响，特别是要
从资本限额的角度进行考虑。
Evaluation of the Airone project
The net present value of the Airone project is estimated to be JPY (457) million (appendix 2).
Given the negative net present value, the initial recommendation would be to reject the project.
However, given that the result is marginal, Washi Co should consider the following factors before
rejecting the project.
NPV 是负数，通常负的 NPV 代表着不应该进行该项目的投资。但是因为这个结果从整体来
看其实是不是很大的赤字，W 公司应该再进一步考虑一下其他的因素，而不是直接决定拒
绝投资。
At present, Washi Co does not have a significant presence in the part of the world where Airone
is located. Taking on the project may make good strategic sense and provide a platform for Washi
Co to establish its presence in that part of the world.
现在 W 公司没有在 A 的所在地拥有很重要的地位和存在。收购 A 可以帮助 W 很大程度上
提升他在世界上的存在感和地位。
Furthermore, once Washi Co has established itself in Airone, it may be able to develop further
opportunities and new projects. The value of these follow-on options has not been incorporated
into the financial assessment. Washi Co should explore the possibility of such opportunities and
their possible value.
同时，W 可以通过 A 进一步发展他自己。拥有更多新项目和新机会。这些接下来的选项和
机会是不会体现在 NPV 的一开始的结果中的。因此 W 公司应该考虑未来机会的价值。
The financial assessment ends abruptly at the end of the four years. No indication is given on
what would happen to the project thereafter. It may be sold as a going concern or, if closed, its
land and assets may be sold. The cash flows from these possible courses of action need to be
incorporated into the assessment, and these could make the project worthwhile.
财务评估在四年后很突然的就结束了， 没有任何的信息和迹象能够说明在此之后项目的表现。
他也许会被卖掉， 或者关掉， 土地和资产会被卖掉。 那这些未来的现金流也应该纳入考量评
估，这样的现金流很可能会使得项目有投资价值。
A number of assumptions and estimates would have been made in the financial assessment. For
example, the rate of inflation used for future figures is the current rate and the tax rate used is
the current rate, these may well change in the coming years.
Therefore, it is best to undertake sensitivity analysis and produce a number of financial
assessments before making any firm commitment to proceed with the project or deciding to
reject it.
还有很多其他的假设和估计需要被考虑进财务评估中， 比如未来的通膨， 利率， 税率， 这些
都可能在未来发生变动。 因此最好是再进行一下敏感度分析，和一些列的财务分析， 再做出
相关的决策。
Conclusion and recommendation
The income from the sale of the European subsidiary is maximised when futures contracts used,
but Washi Co should weigh this against the benefits and drawbacks of all hedging instruments
before making a final decision.
采用期货合同的话，卖掉欧洲子公司的对价可以最大化，但是 W 公司要衡量相关的优缺点
再做出最终的结论。
Although the project is currently giving a negative net present value, rejecting it at the outset is
premature. A number of factors, discussed above, need to be considered and assessed before a
final decision is made. Sensitivity analysis would be very helpful in this respect.
虽然想哭目前是负的 NPV,但是拒绝该项目是为时过早的，其他的因素都应该考虑到，并进
行敏感性分析。
Finally, Washi Co should consider alternative uses for the funding which will be dedicated to the
project. These alternative uses for the finance need to be considered before any decision is made,
especially if Washi Co is facing the possibility of hard capital rationing.
最后 W 公司还应该考虑一下其他的融资方式来支持投资，这些融资方法都应该在最终决策
之前考虑到，特别是如果 W 公司还面临资本配合等问题。

(d) Washi Co’s chief operations officer (COO) has suggested that it would be more beneficial for
the company to let its major subsidiary companies have their own individual treasury
departments, instead of having one centralised treasury department for the whole company.
Required:
Discuss the validity of the COO’s suggestion. (7 marks)
问题解析：W 公司建议让子公司也有他们自己的司库系统，而不是拥有一个统一的中心化
的司库，请对此建议进行讨论。
It is difficult to conclude definitively whether a centralised treasury department is beneficial or
not in all circumstances and for all companies. It depends on each company itself and the
circumstances it faces. Washi Co should take this into account before making a final decision.
很难得出绝对的结论说中心化司库对所有的公司都好还是不好， 需要根据公司的情况进行判
断.
Benefits of a centralised treasury department
Having a centralised treasury management function avoids the need to have many bank accounts
and may therefore reduce transactions costs and high bank charges.
中心化的司库管理避免了大量的银行账户，也就减少了银行手续费和交易费用。
Large cash deposits may give Washi Co access to a larger, diverse range of investment
opportunities and it may be able to earn interest on a short-term basis, to which smaller cash
deposits do not have access. On the other hand, if bulk borrowings are required, it may be
possible for Washi Co to negotiate lower interest rates, which it would not be able to do on
smaller borrowings.
大额的现金存款可以是的 W 公司有大量的多样化的投资机会， 使W 公司能在短期内获得利
息收入，这是分散的小额投资没办法得到的。同时如果有大量的借款要求的话，W 公司可
以去协商一个更低的借款成本。
A centralised treasury function can offer the opportunity for Washi Co to match income and
expenditure and reduce the need for excessive risk management, and thereby reduce costs
related to this.
中心化给 W 公司了匹配收入和支出的机会， 这样可以减少额外的风险管理， 整体减少成本。
【资金的协调调拨】
A centralised treasury management department could hire experts, which smaller, diverse
treasury management departments may not have access to.
中心化可以给司库雇用专家来管理资金，这是小的司库管理部门不具备的
A centralised treasury function may be better able to access what is beneficial for Washi Co as a
whole, whereas local treasury functions may lead to dysfunctional behaviour.
中心化可以使得 W 公司从公司整体有利的角度来使用资金
Benefits of separate (decentralised) treasury departments
It could be argued that decentralised treasury departments are better able to match and judge
the funding required with the need for asset purchases for investment purposes on a local level.
Therefore, they may be able to respond quicker when opportunities arise and so could be more
effective and efficient.
去中心化对地方来说可以更快更有效率的使用资金
Individual departments within a subsidiary may have better relationships with the treasury
departments of that subsidiary and are therefore able to present their case without lengthy
bureaucratic delays.
子公司单独的司库部门， 和子公司各个环节关系更紧密， 因此在资金的使用审批上减少了层
级导致的延迟
Ultimately, the benefits may be implicit rather than explicit. Having decentralised treasury
departments may make the subsidiary companies’ senior management and directors more
empowered and have greater autonomy. This in turn may increase their levels of motivation, as
they are more in control of their own future, resulting in better decisions being made.
最后， 优点可能是隐含的， 而不是显露出来的， 去中心化的司库部门客户以是的子公司的高
管有更多的权利和自主性，这可以增加他们的工作积极性。
Section B – BOTH questions are compulsory and MUST be attempted
2  Tillinton Co
Tillinton Co is a listed company which has traditionally manufactured children’s clothing and toys
with long lives.
T 公司是一个上市公司，主要业务是传统的儿童副主管和玩具生产。
Five years ago, it began manufacturing electronic toys and has since made significant investment
in development and production facilities. The first electronic toys which Tillinton Co introduced
into the market were received very well, partly as it was seen to be ahead of its competitors in
making the most of the technology available.
The country where Tillinton Co is listed has seen a significant general increase in share prices over
the last three years, with companies in the electronic goods sector showing particularly rapid
increases.
五年前， 他开始进行电子玩具的生产， 这使得公司花费了大量的投资去提升生产设备。 第一
批电子玩具在市场上反响很好， 部分原因是人们认为它在充分利用技术方面领先于竞争对手。
T 的股价在过去三年有了快速的上涨，电子产品行业公司的股价也都有了快速的提升。
Statement by Tillinton Co’s chief executive
Assume it is now September 20X3. Tillinton Co’s annual report for the year ended 31 March 20X3
has just been published. Its chief executive commented when announcing the company’s results:
‘I am very pleased to report that revenue and gross profits have shown bigger increases than in
20X2, resulting in higher post-tax earnings and our company being able to maintain increases in
dividends. The sustained increase in our share price clearly demonstrates how happy investors
are with us. Our cutting-edge electronic toys continue to perform well and justify our sustained
investment in them. Our results have also benefited from improvements in operational
efficiencies for our older ranges and better working capital management. We are considering the
development of further ranges of electronic toys for children, or developing other electronic
products for adults. If necessary, we may consider scaling down or selling off our operations for
some of our older products.’Steph Slindon represents an institutional investor who holds shares
in Tillinton Co. Steph is doubtful whether its share price will continue to increase, because she
thinks that Tillinton Co’s situation may not be as good as its chief executive suggests and because
she believes that current share price levels generally may not be sustainable.
Ceo 发言：
去年公司的业绩表现有着快速的增长， 税后净利润使得公司有着增加的分红。 我们的领先的
电子玩具持续表现良好， 证明我们对他们的持续投资是有回报的。 营运资本的管理使我们的
效率更高， 我们正在考虑进一步提升和扩大儿童电子玩具产品， 并考虑发展成分电子玩具产
品，如果必要的话，我们会卖掉一些旧的产品。Steph 代表 T 公司的机构投资者，怀疑公司
的股价是否会一直上涨， 因为他认为T 公司的状况也许不香董事说的那样， 因为他认为现在
的股价不是可持续的。
Financial information
Extracts from Tillinton Co’s financial statements for the last three years and other information
about it are given below.
Required:
(a) Evaluate Tillinton Co’s performance and business prospects in the light of the chief
executive’s comments and Steph Slindon’s concerns. Provide relevant calculations for ratios
and trends to support your evaluation.
Note: 10 marks are available for the calculations. (20 marks)
Profitability
Tillinton Co’s chief executive is correct in saying that the absolute increase in revenue and gross
profits on all products was greater in 20X3 than 20X2, but the % increase in revenue was smaller
on all products, and the % increase in gross profit on toys was also lower. The % increase on the
electronic toys shows the biggest fall, possibly indicating greater competition.
盈利性：
T 公司的董事说他们的收入和毛利 X3 年比 X4 年有着很大的增加是正确的， 但是收入增加的
百分比相对小一些，玩具毛利增加的百分比也较低，电子玩具的增加比率有着很大的下跌，
可能意味着存在更大的竞争。
The improvements in operations mentioned by the chief executive seem to have maintained
gross and operating profit margins and resulted in the absolute overall increases in gross and
operating profits. However, this aspect of performance is almost all attributable to Tillinton Co’s
older products. The gross profit on electronic toys has hardly increased and the gross profit
margin has fallen over the last two years. Although the margin remains higher than on the other
products, even the 20X3 margin may not be sustainable. If competitors are starting to catch up
with Tillinton Co, then the profit margin on the current range of electronic toys may continue to
fall in future years, as prices fall to maintain market share.
董事提到的经营表现的提升包含了毛利和经营利润， 导致了整体的增加， 然而， 这些方面的
表现几乎都来自于 T 公司的就产品， 电子玩具的毛利润几乎没有增加， 毛利在过去两年还下
跌了。 虽然其他产品的利润率保持增加， 及时在X3 年， 这个利润率也看起来不一定会保持。
如果竞争者开始追赶 T 公司， 那他们现在电子产品的利润率可能会持续下跌， 因为为了保持
市场占有率，销售价格是下降的。
Despite the emphasis on developing the products, the revenue generated by electronic toys is
still below the revenue generated by non-electronic toys.
除了强调对产品的发展，电子产品的收入也是比废电子产品的收入要低的。
Asset turnover and return on capital employed have risen significantly over the last two years.
However, part of the reason for the 20X3 increases was the significant increase in current
liabilities. The further amount of investment which the chief executive appears to be
contemplating suggests that asset turnover and return on capital employed may fall in future
years, particularly if profit margins on electronic toys cannot be sustained.
资产周转率和 ROCE 在过去两年增长的很快，然而，一部分 X3 年增长的原因是流动负债的
增加。 董事考虑的更多的投资意味着公司的ROCE 和资产周转率可能还会在未来下降。 特别
是电子玩具的利润率很难保证。
Liquidity
The figures for other current assets seem to support the chief executive’s contention that
working capital is being managed better, as other current assets are falling as revenue and gross
profits are rising.
However, the fall of the current ratio from 1·52 to 0·64 is significant, and the biggest reason for
the fall in 20X3 was the large increase in current liabilities. Cash balances have remained at a low
level, despite higher revenues and profit. Possibly there is now a bank overdraft, which could
have contributed to the significant increase in finance costs between 20X2 and 20X3. It would
seem that cash reserves have been exhausted by the combination of investment in non-current
assets and the payments to finance providers (both interest and dividends), and Tillinton Co is
more dependent on short-term liability finance. Slowdown in any product area, particularly
electronic toys, may result in significant liquidity problems.
流动性： 其他资产的数据看起来证明了董事的观点， 公司的营运资本被有效的管理了。 因为
其他资产下降了但是收入和毛利增加了。
然而，流动比率从 1.52 下降到 0.64 是很重大的。最大的原因是流动负债的增加。除开增加
的收入和利润 （可能是应收款） 现金余额的保持水平较低， 可能现在较多的融资成本造成的
银行透支， 可以看出来现金盈余已经要被对长期资产的投资和对债权人的还款耗尽了 （包含
利息和分红），T 公司现在更依赖于短期借款融资。任何产品领域的节奏放缓，特别是电动
玩具，可能导致重大的流动性问题。
Solvency
Gearing has fallen over the last two years, but this is due to share price increases which may not
be sustainable. If book values rather than market values are used to calculate gearing, the fall in
gearing is much smaller. More information is needed about why finance costs have increased so
much, leading to the deterioration in interest rate cover. Tillinton Co has only taken out an
additional $30 million in long-term loans. Although costs on these may be higher than on its
current loans, this would not account for all the increase in finance costs. As discussed above,
Tillinton Co may be making use of overdraft finance. The fact that current liabilities have
increased much more than non-current liabilities could be an indication that Tillinton Co is having
problems raising all the longer term loan finance which it requires.
The figures suggest that Tillinton Co’s board needs to review future financing carefully if the
company wants to make further investment in electronic products. At some stage, the board will
have to consider raising further finance, either through an issue of shares or through selling off
parts of its operations.
杠杆下降， 这是因为股价的上升造成的， 可能是不持续的。 如果用账面价值， 而不是市场价
值来计算杠杆， 杠杆的下降就不是很明显。 更多的信息需要被用来解释为什么财务成本增长
的这么多。导致了利息保障倍数的下降。T 公司只有额外$30m 美元的长期借款。虽然成本
可能会比现在的负债要多一些，这不能解释财务成本的巨大上升。所以结合上面的讨论，T
公司可能有通过银行透支来融资。 流动负债比非流动负债增加的更多， 这一迹象可能说明了
T 公司在长期借款方面会有困难。
这些数据说明 T 公司的董事和高管需要仔细复核他们未来的融资， 如果公司还想要进一步增
加对电子玩具的投资的话。 在一些阶段， 董事需要考虑进一步的融资， 可能是股权融资，也
可能是卖掉部分业务。
Investor ratios
Both earnings and dividends per share have risen since 20X1, which could help explain the
significant increase in share price.
Dividend cover has remained around 2·0 despite an increase in earnings. Although dividends
have increased, dividend yield has fallen since 20X1. The increase in total shareholder return is
due solely to the increases in share price, which have also resulted in the price-earnings ratio
increasing in 20X3. The current rate of share price increase does not appear to be warranted
by the most recent results and may be partly due to generous dividend levels, which may not be
sustainable if more cash is required for investment.
每股收益和每股分红从 20X1 年开始都增加了，这可以解释股价的上升。股息支付比率大概
在 2.0，不考虑 earnings 的上升。增加的股东回报是因为增加的股价，这也导致 PEratio 在
20X3 年增加， 现在股价的增加是不能被保证的， 最近的现象可能都是源于慷慨的分红比例，
如果更多的现金被用于投资，这就不能保持了。
Conclusion
Despite the chief executive’s optimistic message in the annual report, the benefits from the
electronic toys development may be short-lived. There appears to be a mismatch between
investment, dividend and financing policies. As discussed, margins on current products may fall
further and there is no guarantee that margins on new electronic toys or other products will be
higher if competition generally is increasing.Further significant investment in electronic toys or
other goods may be difficult to finance. Increased reliance on short-term finance is clearly not
sustainable, but obtaining more debt maybe problematic, particularly if gearing levels rise as
share prices fall. Tillinton Co seems reluctant to take advantage of high share price levels to issue
equity capital. This, plus the increase in dividends, may indicate Tillinton Co’s board is unwilling to
risk upsetting shareholders, despite the large increases in share price. The chief executive may be
right in saying that funds may have to be obtained by selling off one of the other parts of the
business, but revenue and profits from the older products may be more sustainable. An increased
concentration on electronic products may be a high-risk strategy. Possibly, if investors become
less positive towards the electronic goods sector, they may realise this, resulting in an increase in
cost of capital and a fall in share price.
Note: Credit will be given for relevant, alternative approaches to the calculations and discussion.
Appendix：
结论： 高管所说的电子产品的优势是短期的， 投资，分红，融资政策之间是不配的。正如讨
论的， 现在的产品的利润率是可能下跌的如果没有办法保证电子产品和新产品能够有较强的
竞争性的话。
未来对电子玩具的投资或者其他产品的投资可能会因为融资困难而变的艰难。T 公司看起来
不愿意在高股价阶段通过股权融资， 再加上高分红的状况， 看起来公司不愿意去令股东利益
受伤害。 管理层说要通过卖掉部分业务来融资可能是对的， 但是因为旧产品的收入和利润是
可持续的， 更多的将经历集中在电子产品上可能会是一个高风险的战略。 另外， 如果投资者
对电子产品不是那么积极了，那么可能会导致借款成本的上升和股价的下降。
(b) Discuss how behavioural factors may have resulted in Tillinton Co’s share price being higher
than is warranted by a rational analysis of its position. (5 marks)
讨论一下行为因素会怎样影响 T 公司的股价，会使他比理性分析的结果更高
Tillinton Co’s shares may be overvalued because share prices generally are too high. The situation
may be a stock market bubble. Share prices have been rising consistently recently and this could
be encouraging investors to buy more shares, further increasing share prices.
T 的股价可能会被高估因为通常股价都会比较高， 这个情况可能是资本市场泡沫导致的。 股
价也许会持续上升来鼓励投资者去买更多的股票，进一步又导致股价的上升。
The bubble could be more localised. Tillinton Co seems to be positioning itself as much in terms
of producing technologically-advanced electronic products as manufacturing toys. The electronic
goods sector may be more likely than other sectors to attract investors on the basis of future
profit potential, with investors possibly following a herd instinct, investing because others have
been investing in the expectation of future gains.
泡沫可能和本地化有关。T 公司把自己定位成为一个生产技术相关的电子产品，而不仅仅是
做玩具，电子产品行业业务会更吸引投资者，投资者会倾注更多的期望。
Possibly, investors are more persuaded by the chairman’s confident language and future promises
than they are by the concerns the figures suggest. They may also be paying excessive attention to
the most recent set of results, rather than seeing them in the context of whether they can be
sustained in the future.
投资者可能被公司董事所表达的非常有信心的话语和承诺所干扰， 更多关注董事建议大家关
注的乐观的书，把更多的关注重心放在最近的结果上，而不是看是不是在未来可持续。
If investors are attempting to make a valuation, they could prefer using a model which confirms
what they believe the shares are worth (confirmation bias), rather than one which gives a more
reliable indication of value. As discussed above, shareholders may be basing their estimates of
value on the recent increases in dividend, even though it may be doubtful whether this is
sustainable.
如果投资者想要进行估值， 他们会选择用一些确认他们股票是有价值的模型 （认同偏向） 股
东更多会看现在的价值，即使他们对未来是否可持续抱有怀疑。
3  Selorne Co
Selorne Co is one of the biggest removal companies in Pauland, offering home and business
removals. It has a number of long-term contracts with large businesses, although it has not won
any new major contracts in the last two years.
Selorne Co is listed on Pauland’s stock market for smaller companies. Selorne Co is financed by a
mixture of equity and short and long-term debt, but its gearing level is below the average for its
sector.
S 公司是 P 国家最大的搬家公司之一， 提供家庭和公司搬家。 它和很多大的公司有长期合同。
虽然他在最近两年没有签订任何新的主要合同。
Selorne Co has four executive directors, who each own 20% of the company’s share capital, with
the other 20% owned by external shareholders. Selorne Co has paid a constant dividend since it
has been listed and its share price has risen slightly over the last three years.
Selorne Co is based in a number of the large cities and towns in Pauland and owns the majority of
the sites where it is located. Many of its employees have worked for the company for a long time.
Drivers of the lorries used by Selorne Co are required to have a special, heavy vehicles licence.
Salary levels at Selorne Co are relatively high compared with other companies in the sector.
S 公司有四个执行董事，每人拥有公司 20%的股票，另外 20%归属于外部股东。S 公司从上
市开始一直支付固定的分红，他的股价在这三年中有一些上升。
Chawon Co
Selorne Co is currently considering making a bid for Chawon Co, an unlisted company specialising
in distribution and delivery services. Chawon Co is owned 100% by its founder, Chris Chawon.
Chawon Co has built up a portfolio of small contracts over time. It has made unsuccessful bids for
two larger contracts over the last 12 months, the bids being rejected primarily because Chawon
Co was not felt to be big enough to be able to guarantee the level of service required.
S 公司现在考虑收购 C，一个非上市公司，主要业务是物流运输。C 公司由创立者百分之百
拥有，C 公司有一个小合同的组合， 它再过去12 个月中签了两个不成功的大合同。S 给 C 的
这个邀约被拒绝了因为 C 觉得他还不够大去承担这样的业务。
Chawon Co is based in many of the same cities and towns where Selorne Co is located, although
Chawon’s premises are all rented. The drivers of Chawon’s vehicles do not require a heavy
vehicles licence. Chawon Co has a few long-serving employees who are mostly centre managers.
Most of its drivers and staff, however, stay at Chawon Co for only a short time. Salary levels are
low, although Chawon Co pays high levels of overtime and high bonuses if target profit levels are
achieved. Chawon Co is highly geared, leading to recent media speculation about its financial
viability.
C 的业务有很多在 S 所在的城市，虽然 C 的厂房都是租来的。 C 的司机不要求有中型卡车
执照，C 公司有一些长期员工，主要是区域经理。大部分的司机，员工都是短期员工，工资
很低， 虽然C 会支付很高的加班工资和奖金如果达到目标利润的话。C 公司的杠杆很高， 导
致了近期每题对他财务稳定性的猜测。
Terms of bid for Chawon Co
In initial discussions about the acquisition, Chris Chawon indicated that he would prefer the
consideration to be a share-for-share exchange, the terms being one Chawon Co share for five
Selorne Co shares.
最开始的收购是以股换股，一股 C 的股票用 5 只 S 的股票收购
Chawon Co has 2 million $1 shares in issue, and Selorne Co has 50 million $0·50 shares in issue.
Each Selorne Co share is currently trading at $6·50, which is a multiple of 8 of its free cash flow to
equity. The multiple of 8 can be assumed to remain unchanged if the acquisition takes place.
Chawon Co’s free cash flow to equity is currently estimated at $7 million, with an expected
annual growth rate of 3%, and it is expected to generate a return on equity of 15%.
C 有 2m 支对外发行的股票，S 有 50m 支股本为$0.5 的股票。S 的股价是$6.5,是股东自由现
金流的 8 倍。如果收购发生，8 倍关系可以假设保持不变。C 的净资产自有现金流是$7m,预
期年增长率为 3%，预期 ROE 是 15%。
Chris Chawon expects that the total free cash flows to equity of the combined company will
increase by $5 million due to synergy benefits. He believes that Selorne Co will be able to win
more contracts because it is larger and because it will be diversifying the services which it offers.
He also believes that significant operational synergies can be achieved, pointing out the time
Selorne Co drivers spend idle during the winter months when removal activity is traditionally
lower. Chris Chawon believes that he can achieve the synergies if he is given management
responsibility for the operational reorganisation, including dealing with the staff employment and
retention issues. Chris Chawon thinks that synergies could also be achieved in central
administration and in premises costs.
C 预期全部净资产自由现金流会因为合力增加$5m,他相信 S 会获得更多的合同因为他更大，
且业务服务范围更大。 他也相信重要的经营合力会产生， 因为S 的司机在冬天的时候会因为
搬家人员的减少都是闲置的。C 公司相信他们能够达成合理， 如果他给管理层经营重组的责
任。包括解决员工保留的问题。C 公司认为合力也可以通过集中管理减少成本而实现。
The chief executive and the finance director of Selorne Co are in favour of bidding for Chawon Co.
However, one of the other executive directors is opposed to the bid. He is sceptical about the
level of synergies which can be achieved and does not want Chris Chawon to be brought into the
management of Selorne Co. He suggests that if the bid is to go ahead, it should be a cash offer
rather than a share exchange. Selorne Co’s chief executive has responded that Chris Chawon is
likely to ask for a higher equivalent price if the purchase is for cash.
S 的执行董事和财务总监智齿收购 C， 然而其中一个执行董事不同意， 他怀疑合力是否能达
到， 并且不想要C 参与到 S 的管理中。 她建议如果要收购， 应该用现金收购， 而不是股票，
S 的董事回复说如果要用现金对价，C 可能会要更高的收购对价。
Financing the bid for Chawon Co
Selorne Co’s finance director has pointed out that Selorne Co will need additional funding if
Chawon Co is purchased for cash. He has suggested that there may be a number of possible
sources of finance:
– A rights issue
– A fixed rate, long-term, bank loan
– A three-year, unsecured, mezzanine loan facility
– Convertible debt, with conversion rights being exercisable in five years’ time
Required:
(a) (i) Estimate the equity value of the combined company and the expected additional value
arising from the combination of Selorne Co and Chawon Co. (6 marks)
(ii) Estimate the share of the gain from the combination created for Chris Chawon and the
share of the gain created for Selorne Co’s shareholders and comment on your results. (6
marks)
问题解析：计算收购可能产生的价值，估计一下收购对 C 和 S 产生的收益。
这里 8 倍这个点可能大家会不知道如何去用， 因为题目说了是自由现金流的8 倍， 所以要先
用 S 的股的价值除以 8， 又因为在合并的时候保持不变， 所以在算合并公司价值的时候
再乘 8.
(b) Evaluate how reliable the estimates of the synergies for the combined company are likely to
be and discuss the factors which may prevent the forecast synergies from being achieved.
(7 marks)
讨论对合力估计的可靠性，讨论影响合力预测的原因
Reliability of synergy estimates
The reliability of the estimates may vary depending on the synergies involved. The synergies
relating to size and services offered will depend on the ability to gain large contracts and neither
company has had recent success in doing this. However, the contracts recently bid for by Chawon
Co might have been won if the larger combined company had bid. The synergies relating to
operations and working practices may be difficult to obtain if it is difficult to change the
employment conditions of Selorne Co drivers. Claims that improved driver utilisation may reduce
spare capacity may be true, but there is likely to be less spare capacity anyway if more contracts
are won.
合力的预测和获得大合同的能力有关，而两家公司最近都没签订大合同。然而，然而最近C
公司投标的合同可能会赢如果更大的集团投标的话。 如果不能改变S 公司员工条件的话和经
营相关的合力很难获得。 提升司机的利用效率， 减少空闲时间的生命可能有用， 但是如果有
更多的合同中标，可能司机会有更少的空余时间。
Other synergies may be easier to obtain. Duplication of premises in some locations should be
eliminated easily, providing Chawon Co does not have onerous rental contracts and there is space
on Selorne Co’s sites. Combining central administrative functions should reduce some staffing
costs, although these are likely to be smaller synergies than the potential operational synergies.
其他的合力也许会比较容易获得， 一些地区的重复的场所可以被简单的取消掉， 使C 没有亏
损的租金合同出现。 行政部门可以减少员工成本， 虽然这样做可能只能获得比潜在的经营合
力要小很多的合力。
Problems with achieving synergies
A significant problem may be lack of unity at the top of the company. Selorne Co’s directors are
not all keen on the acquisition and this may spill over into being unable to agree on a clear
post-acquisition plan. If lack of unity at board level becomes apparent to staff, it may be difficult
to achieve unity at employee level.
重要的问题是缺少公司高层的统一。S 公司的管理层不乐于收购，这可能会导致管理层不能
统一一个清晰的收购后计划。如果高层不统一让员工知道了，那么员工层面也很难统一。
Chris Chawon’s role in the combined company may also make synergies difficult to achieve. He
will have a significant shareholding and a place on the board, so it will be difficult for him not to
be involved. Possibly he has the abilities and desire to achieve changes in operational practices
which other board members lack. However, if Chris is given the leading role he requires, there
may be a change in management style which may upset long-serving Selorne Co staff. Some may
leave, jeopardising the continuity which seems to have been an important part of Selorne Co’s
success.
Chris Chawon 作为 C 的创始人的角色也可能很难造成合力， 因为他持有大量的股份， 所以在
董事会上他很难不参与 （基本上都会参与进来） 也许他有一些想法或者能力去改变经营但是
其他董事可能不具备。 然而如果C 如愿获得了领导地位， 他么管理层架构和方式可能会改变，
这可能会使得 S 公司的长期员工感到失望，一些人会离开，这可能会危机 S 的成功。
Another reason for possible problems with staff is the differing remuneration arrangements.
Selorne Co’s staff may have stayed with the company because both their job prospects and their
remuneration have been safe. Attempts to change their employment conditions may lead to
resistance and employee departures. Ex-Chawon Co employees who have been with the
company for a while may expect salaries to be increased to be more in line with Selorne Co’s
employees, particularly if bonus arrangements become less generous.
另外一个可能的问题是员工的不同的薪酬待遇。S 的员工留在公司因为他们的工作未来和工
资待遇是安全的， 改变他们的雇佣条件可能会导致部分员工的离开。 前C 的员工可能会希望
工资上涨的笔 S 的员工多，特别是他们的奖金变的少的时候。
The success of the acquisition may also depend on how well the staff of the two businesses
integrate. Integration may be difficult to achieve. Many of Chawon Co’s staff will not have the
necessary licence to drive the Selorne Co lorries and may not wish to go through the process of
obtaining this licence. Selorne Co drivers may be reluctant to drive the smaller vehicles. Staff
sticking to what they have been used to driving is likely to prolong a ‘them and us’ culture.
收购成功可能也一来与两公司的员工如何融合合作。员工的融合很难大成，很多C 的员工
没有 S 的必须的驾驶证来开 S 的货车，也不想要获得这个执照。S 的司机可能会不愿意开
小车，员工如果想要坚持做以前一直在做的事情，可能会延长这种人员之间不融合的情况。
(c) Discuss the factors which Selorne Co’s board will consider when determining which source
or sources of finance are chosen to finance a possible cash bid for the share capital of
Chawon Co. (6 marks)
讨论 S 公司的董事会会考虑哪些因素，当他们考虑选择哪种融资方式去进行现金收购。
Availability
Although the finance director has identified possible sources of finance, there is no guarantee
that they will necessarily be available. The success of a rights issue may well depend on the
willingness and ability of the director-shareholders to subscribe.
It may be difficult to find others willing to take up the directors’ rights if they do not subscribe, as
the directors’ unwillingness may be seen as indicating a lack of confidence in the business. A
rights issue may also take longer to arrange than other methods, which may be significant if
Selorne Co needs the finance quickly to complete the acquisition.
可行性： 虽然财务总监已经讨论了可能的融资资源， 但是并没有办法保证就一定是可以获得
的。成功发行配股可能取决于管理层和股东之间的关系，看股东是否愿意订购配股。
Obtaining a bank loan or mezzanine finance may be difficult if Selorne Co takes on Chawon Co’s
debt and is viewed as too highly geared as a result. The success of a convertible debt issue may
depend on the terms, also how possible subscribers view the future prospects of Selorne Co and
the marketability of the shares.
如果 Selorne Co 承担 Chawon Co 的债务， 可能很难获得银行贷款或夹层融资，结果被认为负
债过高。 可转换债券发行的成功与否可能取决于条款， 以及可能的认购人如何看待Selorne Co
的未来前景和股票的可流通性。
Cost
Cost will be another significant factor. The cost of equity will normally be viewed as higher
anyway than the cost of debt. Issue costs of equity are likely to be higher than those of debt. As
Selorne Co’s share price is stable, its current external shareholders appear content with the
dividends paid, so there does not appear to be pressure to increase them. In any case, the board
is not required to pay dividends every year.
Fixed interest cost on the bank loan may become a burden if interest rates fall, but the cost can
be forecast with certainty.
Because the mezzanine finance is unsecured, it is likely to have a higher interest cost than the
bank loan. The rights of conversion to shares attaching to the convertible debt will mean a lower
rate can be set for this, but the cost will depend on how appealing the possibility of conversion is.
Again, the finance cost of debt will depend on the finance providers’ attitude towards the
increased debt burden resulting from the acquisition of Chawon Co.
成本将是另一个重要因素。 股本成本通常被视为高于债务成本。 股票的发行成本可能高于债
券的发行成本。 由于Selorne Co 的股价稳定， 其当前的外部股东似乎对支付的股息感到满意，
因此似乎没有增加股息的压力。在任何情况下，董事会都不需要每年支付股息。
如果利率下降，银行贷款的固定利息成本可能会成为负担，但成本是可以预测的。
由于夹层融资是无担保的， 它的利息成本很可能高于银行贷款。 可转换债券的股票转换权意
味着可以为此设定较低的利率， 但成本将取决于转换可能性的吸引力有多大。 此外， 债务的
融资成本将取决于融资公司对收购 Chawon 公司所增加的债务负担的态度。
Director preferences
The choice will also be determined by Selorne Co’s board’s attitude to gearing as well as how the
possible finance providers view the company’s gearing level. The board may feel that Selorne Co
has reached, or exceeded, the gearing level which it would regard as desirable by taking on
Chawon Co’s debt. If this is the case, the board would have to use equity finance. The board may
also be influenced by how gearing is likely to change over time. Over the next few years gearing
may fall as Selorne Co makes profits and (hopefully) its share price increases. Chawon Co’s debt
may be repaid and not replaced. The convertible debt and mezzanine finance will also not be
long-term sources of debt finance.
这一选择还将取决于 Selorne 公司董事会对杠杆的态度，以及可能的融资提供者如何看待该
公司的杠杆水平。 董事会可能会认为，Selorne Co 已经达到或超过了它认为通过承担 Chawon
Co 的债务所应达到的杠杆水平。如果是这样的话，董事会将不得不使用股权融资。随着时
间的推移，杠杆率的变化可能也会影响董事会。在接下来的几年里，随着Selorne 公司盈利
和(希望)股价上涨，杠杆率可能会下降。Chawon Co 的债务可以偿还，而不是替换。可转换
债务和夹层融资也不会是债务融资的长期来源。
Control of Selorne Co
Selorne Co’s board decision may also be determined by the implications of the different sources
of finance for control of the company. The directors’ control of the company will not be
diminished if a rights issue is used and they take up their rights.
An issue of shares arising from the convertible debt would change the balance of shareholdings,
so the directors would have to decide how significant this would be. Mezzanine finance may also
offer conversion rights, but possibly these could only be exercised if Selorne Co defaulted, which
the board may view as unlikely.
Using a bank loan will have no impact on share capital, but the bank may impose restrictions
which the directors are unwilling to bear, particularly if high gearing is an issue. These conditions
could include restrictions on the sale of assets, limitations of dividends, or requiring accounting
figures, for example, liquidity or solvency ratios, not to go beyond certain levels.
Selorne 公司董事会的决定也可能取决于对公司控制权的不同资金来源的影响。如果使用配
股发行，董事行使其权利，他们对公司的控制不会减弱。
由可转换债务引起的股票发行将改变股权平衡， 因此董事们必须决定这将有多大的影响。夹
层融资也可能提供转换权， 但这些权利可能只有在Selorne Co 违约的情况下才能行使， 而董
事会可能认为这种情况不太可能发生。
使用银行贷款不会对股本产生影响， 但银行可能会施加董事们不愿承受的限制， 尤其是在高
杠杆率是个问题的情况下。这些条件可能包括限制出售资产，限制股息，或要求会计数字，
例如流动性或偿付能力比率，不得超过一定水平。
Mix of finance
Ultimately the board may also consider the possibility of a mix of finance. The offer could be
backed by a core of equity finance from a rights issue, but if Selorne Co has to pay a higher price
than expected, the difference could be made up by mezzanine finance.
最终， 董事会可能还会考虑混合融资的可能性。 该要约可能得到配股融资的核心支持， 但如
果 Selorne Co 不得不支付高于预期的价格，其中的差额可能由夹层融资弥补融资。
Note: Credit will be given for alternative, relevant answers
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 2018 D 真题解析.pdf

stem_zh:
要求解析 (50 分)
为 McKeever 公司的董事会准备一份报告，该报告:
(i) 以英镑为单位，估算项目基于 Erat 的现金流； (8 分)
(ii) 以美元为单位，估算项目的净现值； (9 分)
(iii) 评估营销总监的替代汇率假设对项目 NPV 的影响，建议是否应接受该项目； (6 分)
(iv) 讨论 NPV 计算中所做的假设，并回应营销总监对(ii)中用于估计预期汇率的方法的
担忧； (8 分)
(v) 讨论董事会在做出最终决定前应考虑的政治风险； (5 分)
(vi) 讨论首席执行官关于调整项目折现率以考虑国家风险影响的建议是否合理。 (4 分)
在你的回答中表现出的沟通交流能力、分析和评估技巧、职业怀疑精神和商业洞察力
将会得到专业分数。 (10 分)
Sample answer for Question 1
Report to the board of directors on the viability of the investment in Erat
Introduction
This report considers the suitability of a proposal to manufacture and sell scientific
instruments in Erat. It takes into account the project’s net present value (NPV) calculation,
which is included in an appendix to the main report and discusses the assumptions on
which the project’s cash flows are based, including the impact of an adjustment to the
exchange rate assumption, which was suggested by the marketing director. The report
continues with a discussion on political risk, explaining whether or not the discount rate
should be adjusted for country risk.
这份报告考虑了在 Erat 国制造和销售科学仪器的这个提案的可行性。它考虑了项目净
现值的计算，该计算包含在主报告的附录中，并讨论了项目现金流 计算所基于的假设，
包括市场营销总监建议的汇率假设调整的影响。报告 还讨论了政治风险，解释了 折现
率是否应根据国家风险进行调整。
Results
On a purely financial basis, the NPV of $1·1m suggests the project should be approved.
However, sensitivity analysis would be useful in testing the sensitivity of the project’s NPV
to each of the inputs in order to identify critical variables.
从纯粹的财务角度来看，110 万美元的净现值表明该项目应该获得批准。然而，敏感性
分析将有助于测试项目净现值对每一项投入的敏感性，以确定关键变量。
Evaluation
There is conflicting evidence about the future direction of the exchange rate since the
inflation differentials provided in the scenario were used to predict a strengthening of the
pound relative to the dollar whereas the historical trend analysis seems to be predicting
a weakening pound.
If the pound does in fact weaken, the project’s cash flows will be worth less in terms of
dollars. A weakening currency will affect the lat er cash flows more than the earlier cash
flows. It is therefore not unexpected that the project’s NPV is significantly lower under
these circumstances since the resale value of the land and buildings has a significant
impact on the project’s value. In fact , the NPV switches from being positive to negative
(i.e. $0·5m) when the exchange rate assumption is altered.
The analysts’ predictions suggest such a scenario is extremely unlikely, but the marketing
director’s approach would result in a project being rejected even when the overwhelming
likelihood is that it would be wealth increasing, simply because there is a remote
possibility that it could result in a marginally negative NPV.
Ultimately, the final decision will depend on the board’s attitude to risk bu t based on
expected values, the project will increase shareholder wealth and should, therefore, be
accepted unless the board is very risk averse since the downside is only marginally
negative and is considered to be an unlikely outcome.
关于汇率的未来走向，存在相互矛盾的证据 ，因为该情景中提供的通胀差异被用于预
测英镑相对于美元的走强，而历史趋势分析似乎预测英镑将走弱。
如果英镑真的走弱，该项目的现金流以美元计算将会贬值。疲软的货币对后期现金流
的影响要大于前期现金流。因此，在这种情况下，项目的 NPV 明显较低并不意外，因
为土地和建筑物的转售价值对项目价值有重大影响。事实上，当汇率假设改变时，净
现值从正变为负(即 50 万美元)。
分析师的预测表明，这种情况是极不可能的，但营销总监的方法将导致一个项目被拒
绝，即使压倒性的可能性是财富增加，只是因为有一个微小的可能性，它可能会导致
边际负净现值。
最终，最终决策将取决于董事会对风险的态度，但基于预期价值，该项目将增加股东
财富，因此应被接受，除非董事会非常厌恶风险，因为负面影响只是轻微的负面影响，
被认为是不太可能的结果。
Assumptions
It is assumed that the inputs used in the NPV calculation, such as the selling prices, market
demand, variable and fixed costs, tax, working capital, inv estment cost, residual value,
transfer price, component contribution, cost of capital and inflation differentials are
estimated accurately. The project value also assumes certain inputs remain constant or
adjusted for inflation for the duration of the proj ect such as the tax rates, tax allowable
depreciation, transfer price, component contribution and the working capital requirement.
The exchange rates are based on the purchasing power parity model, using analysts’
predictions about future inflation differentials. However, research suggests that this
model is more effective at predicting long -term fluctuations, rather than short -term
movements in the exchange rate.
The calculations assume the investment will take place immediately, assuming the project
is approved, although in reality there are likely to be delays. There is also an assumption
that the project will only last for four years, so any change to this assumption will impact
the NPV.
假设 NPV 计算中使用的输入数据（如销售价格、市场需求、可变成本和固定成本、税
收、营运资本、投资成本、 残值、转移价格、组件贡献、资本成本和通货膨胀差异）
均得到准确估计。项目价值还假设某些输入在项目持续期间保持不变或根据通货膨胀
进行调整，例如税率、税收允许折旧、转让价格、组件贡献和营运资本要求。
这些汇率基于购买力平价模型，利用了分析师对未来通胀差异的预测。然而，研究表
明，这种模型在预测汇率的长期波动方面比短期波动更有效。
计算假设投资将立即进行，假设项目获得批准，尽管实际上可能会有延迟。还有一个
假设是该项目仅持续四年，因此对该假设的任何更改都将影响 NPV。
Marketing director’s concerns
Lack of consistency between analysts’ predictions
There is nothing unusual about the lack of consistency between the analysts’ predictions.
Predictions about the future are inherently subjective and there are a number of variables
which could affect future inflation rates (e.g. government spending, monetary policy and
economic growth). Each analyst will rely on different methodologies and forecasting
biases with different assumptions about each of the input variables.
分析师的预测缺乏一致性
分析师的预测缺乏一致性并不奇怪。对未来的 预测本质上是主观的，有许多变量可能
影响未来的通货膨胀率（例如政府支出、货币政策和经济增长） 。每个分析师将依赖不
同的方法和预测偏差，对每个输入变量进行不同的假设。
Finance director’s methodology
The theoretically correct approach to managing this type of risk is to attach probabilities
to each of the possible outcomes and calculate expe cted inflation rates which can be
incorporated into the purchasing power parity model. Therefore, the finance director’s
methodology is fundamentally sound. The key issue is whether the probability estimates
are credible and what information was used to generate these estimates.
The marketing director’s historical trend analysis predicts a weakening of the pound
relative to the dollar, in contrast to the output of the purchasing power parity model.
Although it is tempting to rely on historical trends, this would ignore the government’s
incentives for foreign investment and their likely impact on the future direction of the
exchange rate. It is therefore not unexpected that the pound would strengthen when the
government is actively promoting foreign investment.
However, there is some logic to the marketing director’s suggestion if it is used to provide
a measure of the project’s risk and allows the board to attach a probability to a plausible
downside scenario.
财务总监的方法
管理这类风险的理论上正确的方法是将概率与每一种可能的结果联系起来，并计算预
期通货膨胀率，将其纳入购买力平价模型。因此，财务总监的方法基本上是合理的。
关键问题是概率估计值是否可信，以及使用了什么信息来生成这些估计值。
营销总监的历史趋势分析预测了英镑相对于美元的疲软，与购买力平价模型的输出形
成对比。尽管依赖历史趋势很有诱惑力，但这将忽略政府对外国投资的激励及其对汇
率未来走向的可能影响。因此，当政府积极促进外国投资时，英镑走强并不出人意料。
然而，如果营销总监的建议被用来提供项目风险的度量，并允许董事会将可能性附加
到似乎合理的下行场景，那么它就有一些逻辑。
Political risk
The project in Erat may give rise to significant political risks. A recent opinion poll suggests
that the governing party is already losing popular support only a few months after the
last general election. Even though the general election took place fairly recently, there is
also a potential for political instability if Erat’s history of more frequent changes in
government repeats itself. A new government could have a significant impact on the
project’s cash flows, particularly if the agreement to act as a b uyer of last resort for the
land and buildings is withdrawn. This agreement allows McKeever Co to recoup almost
all of its investment cost and forms a significant portion of the project’s present value. In
a stagnating property market, there is a risk that the project is not feasible without such
an agreement. On the other hand, it is possible that the political reforms begin to take
effect by year four and the drive to increase foreign investment has a positive impact on
Erat’s property market.
政治风险
Erat 项目可能会引发重大政治风险。最近的一项民意调查显示，距离上次大选仅几个
月时间，执政党就已经失去了民众的支持。尽管大选最近才举行，但如果埃拉特频繁
更换政府的历史重演，也有可能出现政治不稳定。新政府可能会对项目的现金流产生
重大影响，尤其是在作为土地和建筑最终买家的协议被撤销的情况下。该协议使
McKeever 公司收回了几乎全部投资成本，并构成了该项目的现值的很大一部分。在停
滞不前的房地产市场中，如果没有这样的协议，该项目就有不可行的风险。另一方面，
政治改革有可能在第四年开始生效，增加外国投资的努力对埃拉特的房地产 市场产生
积极影响。
There is also a risk that the favourable tax regime is withdrawn. Minor increases in the tax
rate would not have an impact on the project’s NPV because McKeever Co profits are
taxed at a higher rate in its home country anyway. However, any incre ase above the US
corporation tax rate of 25% would have an impact on the project’s cash flows. In the
context of a less favourable tax regime, any reduction in the transfer price would also
have an adverse impact on the expected outcome. A new regime may seek to investigate
the legitimacy of the transfer price and there is a risk that this could be reduced, shifting
profits from a lower to a higher tax rate. There is also a risk that Erat could impose tariffs
or quotas affecting the supply of the component which is to be imported from the US
and this could have an impact on the project’s manufacturing capacity.
优惠税收制度也有被撤销的风险。税率的小幅提高不会对项目的 NPV 产生影响，因为
无论如何，McKeever Co 的利润在其母国的税率较高。然而，任何高于 25%的美国公司
税率的增长都会对项目的现金流产生影响。在税收制度不太有利的情况下，转让价格
的任何降低也会对预期结果产生不利影响。一个新的制度可能会寻求 调查转让价格的
合法性，这种合法性可能会降低，从而将利润从较低的税率转移到较高的税率。此外，
Erat 还可能征收关税或配额，影响从美国进口的零部件的供应，这可能会对项目的制
造能力产生影响。
There is also a possibility that a new government could re -introduce restrictions on
annual remittances. Any change in this assumption would impact the project’s NPV since
delayed remittances would need to be discounted at a less favourable discount rate.
McKeever Co could potentially bypass such a restriction, for example, by charging a
management or royalty fee, but this assumes the exchange controls are not appli ed to
repatriations in general. Although extreme, there remains a risk that a new government
might attempt to appropriate McKeever Co’s assets.
新政府也有可能重新限制年度汇款。这一假设的任何变化都会影响项目的净现值，因
为延迟汇款需要以较低的贴现率贴现。McKeever 公司有可能绕过这种限制，例如通过
收取管理费或版税，但这是假设外汇管制不适用于一般遣返。尽管有些极端 ，但新政
府仍有可能试图侵吞 McKeever 公司的资产。
McKeever Co would need to assess the likelihood of a change of government over the
next four years. If the next election is more than four years away, these issues may not be
a material concern as long as the government remains stable.
McKeever 公司需要评估未来四年政府更迭的可能性。如果距离下一次选举还有四年多
的时间，只要政府保持稳定，这些问题可能不是实质性的问题。
Discount rate
All things being equal, the cost of capital would normally be expected to provide
reasonable compensation for the risk of a new project when the re is no change in
McKeever Co’s business or financial risk. Intuitively, however, it is not difficult to
understand the CEO’s concern since this project carries more risk than an equivalent
project in McKeever Co’s home country due to the exposure to both  foreign exchange
and political risk. However, the key question is whether this risk is diversifiable or not.
折现率
在所有条件相同的情况下，当 McKeever 公司的业务或财务风险没有变化时，资本成本
通常会为新项目的风险提供合理的补偿。然而，凭直觉，不难理解首席执行官的担忧，
因为该项目比 McKeever Co 所在国家的同等项目风险更大，因为该项目同时面临外汇
和政治风险。然而，关键问题是这种风险是否可以分散。
Foreign exchange risk will affect different companies in different ways. Some companies,
such as McKeever Co, will be adversely affected by a weakening of the pound whereas
other companies will benefit. In addition, losses in the Eratian pound may be  offset by
gains in another currency assuming there is a weak correlation across different currencies.
It is therefore possible to reduce most, if not all, exposure to foreign exchange risk by
investing in countries whose currencies do not all move in the same direction. This is
McKeever Co’s first foreign investment project, which means it is not a globally diversified
company. However, McKeever Co’s shareholders are mainly institutional investors whose
portfolios are already likely to be well diversified across global markets. It is therefore
unlikely they would be willing to pay a premium in the form of a higher discount rate for
a risk which is diversifiable within their own portfolios.
外汇风险会以不同的方式影响不同的公司。一些公司，如McKeever Co，将受到英镑贬
值的不利影响，而其他公司将受益。此外，假设不同货币 之间的相关性较弱，欧元的
损失可能会被另一种货币的收益抵消。因此，通过投资于货币走势不一致的国家，可
以减少大部分(如果不是全部)外汇风险。这是 McKeever Co 的第一个海外投资项目，这
意味着它不是一家全球多元化的公司。然而，McKeever Co的股东主要是机构投资者，
他们的投资组合很可能已经在全球市场上实现了多元化。因此，他们不太可能愿意为
自己投资组合中可分散的风险支付更高贴现率的溢价。
The evidence for political risk, on the other hand, indic ates there is a significant positive
correlation across countries and this relationship is becoming stronger over time as global
markets become more integrated. This suggests there is an element of political risk, which
cannot be diversified away, for whic h investors would expect to be compensated in the
form of an increase to the discount rate.
另一方面，政治风险的证据表明，各国之间存在显著的正相关关系，随着全球市场变
得更加一体化，这种关系正变得越来越强。这表明存在一种无法分散的政治风险因素，
投资者希望以提高贴现率的形式获得补偿。
Recommendation
The project generates a positive NPV of $1·1m and the refore appears acceptable.
However, the project’s assumptions need to be investigated, including the potential need
for an adjustment to the project’s discount rate to account for country risk. Scenario and
sensitivity analysis would also be useful in identifying critical variables.
该项目产生 110 万美元的正净现值，因此似乎可以接受。但是，需要对项目的假设进
行调查，包括调整项目贴现率以考虑国家风险的潜在需要。情景和敏感性分析也有助
于确定关键变量。

附表一解析
这道题的(i)问和(ii)问合在一起其实就是常规的海外投资评估 Foreign NPV investment
appraisal。
第一个相关现金流是 contribution = sales revenue – variable cost。由于这两个组成成分
都和销量有关，且都是按照 Erat 国的通胀率逐年调增，所以可以直接两者相减以
contribution 的形式列示。需要注意题目直接给的是 year 1 的数据，所以从 year 2 开
始，每一年在上一年的基础上按照当年的通胀率水平进行通胀调增。
第二个相关现金流是固定成本，同样题目直接给的是 year 1 的数据，所以从 year 2 开
始按照 5%的年通胀率水平逐年调增。
算 foreign taxable profit 之前将可以抵税的折旧从现金流中扣除，这道题只有 plant and
machinery 是按照直线法计提折旧的，残值为零，预计可使用年限（1/25%=4 years）与
项目期限相等，所以每年的折旧额都是£18m×25%=£4.5m。
虽然海外 Erat 国的实际税率是 30%，但是由于对于外商有税收优惠，所以 外商
McKeever 公司在海外是按照优惠税率 20%交税的，而本国 US 的税率是 25%，按照双边
税收协定 bi-lateral tax treaty 的规定，需要按照两国中最高的税率 25%交税，所以需要
McKeever 公司在海外以 foreign taxable profit 为基础按照 20%的汇率交税，现金流汇回
本国后，再以 foreign taxable profit 为基础按照 5%(=25%-20%)补税差。
本题还有对于营运资本的投资 。题目条件说营运资本在项目每年年初进行投资，投资
水平是 contribution 的 15%，期末可以转回。所以营运资本每年的投资水平如下 （all
amounts in £’000）：Year 1 9625×15%=1444，Year 2 12311×15%=1847，Year 3
15670×15%=2351，Year 4 17877×15%=2683。由于营运资本是流动性很好的资金资源，
所以每年年初只需要追加投入 incremental 的那部分就行，所以 Year 0 = 1444, Year 1 =
1847-1444 = 403, Year 2 = 2351 – 1847 = 504, Year 3 = 2683 - 2351 = 332。到了最后
一年年末，可以按照题目条件将所有的营运资本2683 在 Year 4年末全部 release back。
另外项目还有初始投资包括 3200 万英镑的土地和建筑+ 1800 万英镑的机器设备，发生
在 Year 0。这两个组成部分在项目最后一年 Year 4 可以收回的残值只有土地和建筑的
3200 万英镑。
将税后化外币标价的现金流按照预测出来的汇率转换成本币后，需要与 本国的项目相
关现金流进行合并。由于本题中有 inter-company flows ，题目条件明确集团母公司
McKeever 可以从中获得 pre-tax contribution of £10 per unit，所以这对集团母公司是一
笔应税收入，需要按照母公司所在国税率 25%完税后，再与从海外子公司汇回的税后化
的现金流进行合并，从而得出 net cash flows in home currency ，最后再按照母公司的
折现率 14%进行折现加总，得出项目的净现值。
附表二解析
这道题的(iii)其实是看如果项目期间的汇率预测发生变化调整，对于项目净现值的影响。
所以需要将涉及到汇率的相关现金流，都按照基于历史变化趋势预测出来的汇率进行
转换。包括海外子公司汇回母公司的现金流，以及 inter-company flows 需要按照母公
司所在国税率交的税。
对于集团间内部交易对于 contribution 的影响，题目条件已经直接给与项目现金流的现
值之和了，所以将 PV of post-tax component contribution $485,000 直接考虑进去就行。
最后还是按照母公司的折现率 14%进行折现加总，得出项目的净现值。

1. Southmed restaurants
Southmed Restaurants Co (Southmed) is a chain of restaurants operating in the country
of Pangland, specialising in Southern Mediterranean food. Southmed aims to provide
customers with a better dining experience than other chains offering similar food, with
more comfortable seating and stylish surroundings. Sourcing of food and drink from
suppliers is organised by a central procurement function, acting on orders from
restaurants. Restaurants, however, have some discretion in the prices they charge in
response to local business conditions and also in their staffing policies.
Southmed Restaurants Co (Southmed)是一家在 Pangland 经营的连锁餐厅，专营南地
中海美食。Southmed 旨在为顾客提供比其他提供类似食物的连锁店更好的用餐体验，
更舒适的座位和时尚的环境。从供应商处采购食品和饮料是由一个中央采购部门组织
的，根据餐馆的订单行事。然而，餐馆可以根据当地的商业条件和员工政策自行决定
价格。
For the purposes of segmental reporting, Southmed divides its restaurants into three
segments, located in:
– The six largest cities in Pangland
– Tourist centres, mostly coastal locations
– Smaller cities and towns
Southmed’s board is currently reviewing its strategic positioning and financing. The
directors wish to ensure, as far as possible, that Southmed will have sufficient finance to
sustain investment and keep shareholders happy by maintaining dividend levels. They
view as a significant competitive threat the growth of more expensive restaurants
offering higher quality food and better surroundings than Southmed does, particularly
in the six largest cities in Pangland.
出于分部报告的目的，Southmed 将其餐厅分为三个分部，分别位于:
–Pangland 最大的六个城市
–旅游中心，主要是沿海地区
–较小的城镇
Southmed 的董事会目前正在审查其战略定位和融资。董事们希望尽可能确保
Southmed 有足够的资金维持投资，并通过维持股息水平让股东满意。他们认为，与
Southmed 相比，价格更高、提供更高质量食物和更好环境的餐厅的增长是一个重大的
竞争威胁，尤其是在 Pangland 的六个最大城市。
About five years ago, Southmed expanded the number of restaurants it had in tourist
centres in response to a campaign by Pangland’s government aiming to increase the
number of holidays taken in Pangland. The board has been pleased with the results of
these restaurants, but there is considerable competition now in the tourist centres,
particularly from restaurants which serve high customer numbers at cheaper prices than
Southmed.
大约五年前，Southmed 扩大了其在旅游中心的餐厅数量，以回应 Pangland 政府旨在
增加在 Pangland 度假人数的活动。委员会对这些餐馆的结果感到满意，但现在旅游中
心的竞争相当激烈，特别是那些以比 Southmed 更便宜的价格接待大量顾客的餐馆。
Southmed’s board wishes to assess its current business situation, based on the figures
in its most recent financial statements, before deciding on how it should respond to the
competitive threats the company faces and how the response should be financed.
Southmed 的董事会希望根据其最近财务报表中的数字评估其当前的业务状况，然后决
定如何应对公司面临的竞争威胁以及如何为应对措施提供资金。
