Capital rationing 资本配额【背】
Capital rationing is a restriction on an organization’s ability to invest capital funds,
caused by an internal budget ceiling being imposed on such expenditure by
management( soft capital rationing), or by external limitations being applied to the
company, as when additional borrowed funds cannot be obtained( hard capital
rationing).资本配给是对一个组织的投资资本的能力的限制。由管理层对这类开
支造成的内部预算上限，成为软性资本配合；由外部限制被应用于公司，这时额
外借来的资金不能获得（硬性资本配给）。
1. Soft capital rationing- Internal factors: 公司可以融资，但不愿意融资
1） Reluctance to surrender control 发股票融资，可能降低对公司控制
2） Wish only to use retain earnings 只愿意用自有资金
3） Reluctance to dilutes EPS 发股票融资，会稀释 EPS
4） Reluctance to pay more interest 不愿意增加利息支付
5） Capital expenditure budgets 资本费用预算节制
2. hard capital rationing- external factors 公司想要融资，但融不到资
1） Depressed stock market 股票市场表现不好，投资者不愿意投资
2） Restrictions on bank lending 银行借不到款，可能信用不好，可能政府的挤
出效应“crowding-out”
3） Conservative lending policies 借款政策 保守，企业不满足条件
4） Issue costs 发行成本太高，无法发行
（题目：2012-12-Q4）
Centralized treasure management 资金集中管理优点【背】
优点：
1. 集中管理可以评估公司整体的财务需求，批量融资更容易磋商取得好的利率。
The centralized treasury department should be able to evaluate the financing
requirements of the group as a whole and it may be able to negotiate better rates
when borrowing in bulk,
2. 调节集团内部的盈余资金，用到其他部门的项目上，避免不必要的对外融资。
The department could operates as an internal bank and undertake matching of funds.
Therefore it could transfer funds from subsidiaries which have spare cash resources
to ones which need them , and thus avoid going into the costly external market to
raise funds.
3. 进行多边抵消，作为一种外币对冲的方式来降低外汇损失。The department
may be able to undertake multilateral internal netting and thereby reduce costs
relates to hedging activity.
4. 同一功能的专家和资源集中在一起，避免多余的费用。Experts and resources
within one location could reduce duplication costs.
5. 更利于做有效率的决定，更高质量的风险监督和控制。The concentration of
experts and resources within one central department may result in a more effective
decision-making environment and higher quality risk monitoring and control.
6. 更利于多样化的投资，为公司创造财富。Further, having access to the Keshi Co
group’s entire cash funds may give the company access to larger and more diverse
investment markets. These factors could result in increasing the company’s cash
inflows, as long as the benefits from such activity outweigh the costs
(题目：2014-12-Q2）
Decentralized treasury management 资金分权管理优点【背】
优点：
1. 地 方 部 门 可 能 更 容 易 发 现 多 样 化 的 资 金 来 源 和 匹 配 当 地 资 产 。 Local
departments may find it easier to diversify sources of finance and match local assets.
2. 不利于鼓舞地方部门的管理层，他们没有自主权来做现金管理，也很难评估
其表现，尤其重大决定都是由中央来做的时候。 Centralized management means
that managers in subsidiaries and divisions are not motivated by being given the
autonomy to deal with cash management and it may be difficult to assess their
performance if the major decisions are being made centrally.放权给地方部门，可以
激励他们更努力为公司赚钱。Giving subsidiary companies more autonomy on how
they undertake their own fund management may result in increased motivation and
effort from the subsidiary’s senior management and thereby increase future income.
3. 但某个运营单位有需求时，分权财政功能可以快速反应。A decentralized
treasury function may be more responsive to the needs of individual operating units.
4. 当地方部门有盈余资金，分权的财政功能可以更快更方便的将其投入到短期
项 目 上 来 赚 钱 。 A decentralized operation may find it easier to invest its own
balances quickly on a short-term basis than a centralized function would.
5. 如果分部很偏远，中央集权很难监督，无法获得相关的信息。A central function
may find it difficult to monitor remote sites, it may be difficult to obtain information
from those sites.
6. 子公司更清楚当地的法规，习俗和实践经验。Each subsidiary company may be
better p;aced to take local regulations,custom and practice into consideration.
7. Subsidiary companies which have access to their own funds may be able to
respond to opportunities quicker and establish competitive advantage more
effectively.子公司有自己来钱的渠道，便于更快抓住机遇，更有效率的建立竞争
优势。
（题目：2014-12-Q2）
汇率 3 种风险&PPP 与经济风险的关系
1. Transaction risk: Is the risk of adverse exchange rate movements between the
date the price is agreed and the date cash is received /paid ,arising during normal
international trade. 交易风险：由于约定价格日 VS 收付款日之间的汇率的不利波
动的风险。
2. Translation risk: Is the risk that the organization will make exchange losses when
the accounting results of its foreigh branches or subsidiaries are translated into the
home currency. 折算风险：将国外分部的资产、负债折算成本币时，账面上的损
失。但实际不产生现金流，但会导致财务报表不好看，影响股价，或者股东对公
司的看法。
3. Economic risk: Is the risk that the present value of a company’s future cash flow
might be reduced by adverse exchange rate movements. 经济风险：是公司的未来
现金流的 PV 值会因为汇率的不利不动而减少。
补充：PPP 购买力平价理论认为，两国汇率波动受两国的通胀率的影响。在市场
有效的情况下，因为通胀导致的汇率损失会被售价上涨所弥补。但长此以往，这
个国家的货币会失去竞争力 competitive position。 自此，该货币的定价权‘law of
one price’ 将不在持续。比如，历史上的 USA 崛起&UK 的衰弱。所以，如果一国
货币长期处于相对较弱的经济形势中，很可能会面临经济风险，因为从长期来看，
未来现金流的价值会 decline.
(题目：2016-06-Q1,2012-12-Q1）

Basis risk 【背】
Basis risk= 利 息 承 担 的 资 产 和 负 责 可 能 不 是 互 相 完 美 匹 配 。 Interest bearing
liabilities and interest bearing assets may not move perfectly in line with each other
基差=future-spot,基差风险是因为 basis 不是按照恒定的比率波动。（如果是汇率
基差风险，则定义为 Basis risk is the irsk that futures price movement may differ
from underlying currency movement.）如果期货和农到期后赎回，则不会有基差
风险衍生品价格=潜在实物资产价格。但如果合同是在到期前平仓，则期货合同
的价格未必能够与（基于基差计算出的）预测值一致。比如说，计算所得基差
0.54，期货合同是 6 个月后的，已经过了 4 个月，那么预测的 unexpired basis=0.18，
但实际的期货合同价格可能大于或小于这个计算值。basis risk occurs when the
basis does not diminish at a constant rate. In this case, if a futures contract is held till
it matures when there is no basis risk because at maturity the derivative price will
equal the underlying asset’s price.
However, if a contract is closed out before maturity(here the June futures contracts
will be closed two months prior to expiry)there is no guarantee that the price of the
futures contract will equal the predicted price based on basis at that date.
For example, in part (b) above the predicted futures price in four months assumes
that the basis remaining is 0.18,but it could be more or less. Therefore the actual
price of the futures contract could be more or less.

Interest future
1. 计算合同张数=（借款额/合同标准额）>（借款期/合同期）
2. 计 算 基 差 Basis=Spot price-future price, 则 unexpired basis=( 剩 余 月 / 总 月
数）>basis
3. Cost of borrowing funds(波动后利率+银行手续费率）
4. Expected future price=100-波动后利率-unexpired basis
5. Gain on future market=(行权价-Expected future price) *tick value* 合同张数，
如果 loss 则 lapse option 不行权
6. Net cost=(2-5),effective rate=(net cost/借款总额）*（12 月/借款期）

Interest option on future
1. 行权价格 exercise price & 期货价格 future price（interest future 已算出）,决
定是否行权。
2. Cost of borrowing funds(波动后利率+银行手续费率）
3. Gain on option=(行权价-Expected future price)*tick value* 合同张数，如果 loss
则 lapse option 不行权
4. Premium=费率（*100）*tick value* 合同张数
5. Net cost=(2-4),effective rate=(net cost/借款总额）*（12 月/借款期）
（题目：Pilot-Q2,22015-12-Q2,2015-06-Q4,2013-12-Q2）
Interest collar
1. 确认买卖方向
借钱 buy 高利率的 put；卖出另一个；投资 buy 低利率的 call，卖出另一个。
目的：对冲确保有借款封顶利率&投资保底利率。
2. Cost of borrowing funds(波动后利率+银行手续费率）
3. 由行权价格 exercise price&期货价格 future price(interest future 已算出），决
定我方是否行权，以及对方是否要求行权。
4. Gain on (buy) option=(行权价- Expected future price)* tick value *合同张数
5. Loss on (sell) option=(行权价- Expected future price)* tick value *合同张数
6. Premium =(buy premium-sell premium)*tick value* 合同张数
7. Net cost=(3-6),effective rate=(net cost/借款总额）*（12 月/借款期）
（题目：Pilot-Q2,2015-12-Q2,2015-06-Q4）
Interest SWAP
1.选择优势 fixed rate，和相对优势 floating rate
2.算出绝对优势+相对优势=总优势
3.按照提议分配 SWAP benefit
4.Net result=另一个 Rate +/-SWAP benefit+/-bank fee

2.计算 Annual interest payable on Y1-Y4(固定）
3.计算每年实际收付：收>付，表示净收；收<付，表示净付

Interest 对冲方法比较
1.从风险来看，Forward & Future 相当于锁定汇率，所以风险最小。Forward >
Future > Collar> Option
2.从成本和收益来看,Option 是非常具有投机性的 speculative,如果管理层企图享
有利率很大波动造成的收益，则可以选择 option
3.Forward 是 OTC，可能面临银行的违约风险 default risk
4.Future 则会有 basic risk，所以计算得到的 effective rate 会有偏差
5.Option 期货有个很大的特点是保证金制度 margin，和日盯视 mark to market.
6.Option 最大的优点是便宜。Collar 是同时买和卖掉一个相反的 option，因此获
得了卖掉 option 的 premium。这份收入可以降低支付的 option premium。Collar
的缺点：Option 可以 get full benefit of any upside movement。然而 collar hedge
掉的那部分 upside movement is capped 有一个封顶值。所以 Collar 相当于将汇率
波动锁定在一个区间内。
7.SWAP 也是一个固定汇率，且优于不对冲的固定汇率。
(题目：Pilot-Q2)

4. SWAP
Currency swaps effectively involve the exchange of debt from one currency to
another.
Currency swaps can provide a hedge against exchange rate movements for longer
periods than the forward market and can be a means of obtaining finance new
countries.
Advantage Disadvantage
1)Swaps are easy to arrange and are
flexible since they can be arranged in any
size and are reversible.
2)Transactions costs are low,only
amounting to legal fees,since there is no
commission or premium to be paid.
3)The parties can obtain the currency
they require without subjecting
themselves to the uncertains of the
foreign exchange markets.

finance in another country and currency
where it is little known, and
consequently has a poorer credit
rating,than in its home country.It can
therefore take advantage of lower
interest rates than it could obtain if it
arranged the currency loan itself.

restructure the currency base of the
company's liabilities.This may be
important where the company's is
trading overseas and receiving revenues
in foreign currencies, but its borrowings
are denominated in the currency of its
home contry.Currency swaps therefore
provide a means of reducing exchange
rate exposure.
1)There is the risk of one of the parties
defaulting.
2)There is the risk that interest rates and
exchange rates could move in such a way
that the net payments arising as a result

have been had swap not been undertake.
3)If the bank takes on a temporary role in
the financing during the arrangement of
the swap,it runs the risk that rates could
move during the delay involved in
completing the tranctions.

收益率曲线的形状反映了：
Expectations theory:利率预计将在未来上升，因此向上倾斜（预期下降，反之）
Liquidity preference theory：投资者牺牲了流动性，需要回报做补偿，向上倾斜
Market segmentation theory:银行偏爱短期债券；养老基金更喜欢长期债权；有不
同的市场和投资者，即便预知利率波动，也不会改变，因此曲线扭曲或不连续

M & M- with corporate taxation
Traditional theory: There is an optional captial mix at which the weighted average
cost of capital is minimised.Shareholders demand increased returns to compensate
for greater risk as gearing rises(due to increased financial risk).At high gearing
debtholders also require higher returns.存在一个最优的资本结构，加权平均资本
成本最小化。股东需求增加的回报，以弥补更大的风险，因为资产负债率上升（由
于增加的财务风险）。当负债增加时，债权人也要求更高的回报。
M & M:The WACC is not influenced by changes in capital structure.The benefits of
issues debt are counterbalanced by the increased cost of equity.资本结构的变化不
影响资本的加权平均成本。发行债券的收益抵消了增加的权益的成本。
Pecking order theory:
1.Use internal funds if available 先是自有资金
2.Use debt 其次是债
3.Issue new equity 最后才考虑发股

Limitation of behavioural finance 行为金融学的理论 不足
Critics of the behavioural finance approach have argued that even if individuals make
decisions when left by themselves, participating in finance markets helps discipline
them to act rationally by giving them opportunities to learn from their experiences.
The consequences of irrational decisions are short-term anomalies. In the
longer-term general theories, such as the effective market hypothesis, will apply. 即
使个人做出不合理的决定，但参与金融市场有助于训练他们的行为，让他们有机
会从经验中学习。所以不理性知识短期异常。较长期的一般理论，入有效市场假
说，仍将适用。

Basis risk
基差/基准风险
In theory the futures market provides a fixed and stable outcome when hedging currency or
interest rate risk, but in practice futures contracts are exposed to basis risk.
理论上， 期货市场在对冲货币或利率风险时提供了固定和稳定的结果， 但在实践中期货合约
面临基差风险。
Basis is the difference between the futures and spot prices and, for the purposes of recommending
a hedging strategy, it is often assumed to diminish at a constant rate. Basis risk arises when the
price of a futures cont ract does not have a predictable relationship with the spot price of the
instrument being hedged. When basis risk is introduced to a scenario, it may mean an alternative
hedging method would provide a better result.
基差是现货和期货价格之间的差额， 为了推荐对冲策略， 通常假定它以恒定的速度递减。 当
期货合约的价格与被套期保值工具的现货价格没有可预测的关系时， 就会产生基差风险。 当
基差风险被引入情景时，这可能意味着另一种对冲方法将提供更好的结果。
In order to illustrate this point, we will use the information provided in a sample question from the
September/December 2017 exam sessions, Wardegul Co, and investigate the impact of basis risk
on the hedging recommendation. The company’s treasury department would like to hedge the
following transaction.
为了更好的说明该知识点，我们使用 2017 9/12 月 Wardegul Co 的一道真题作为讲解，去说
明考试中基差对于对冲方案的影响及公司的司库部门为何更愿意使用该对冲方式去进行风
险管理。
Transaction to be hedged
Today’s date is 1 October 2017. The treasury department plans to hedge a receipt, in Eurian Dinar
(D), of D27m. The receipt is expected on 31 January 2018 and will need to be invested until 30 June
2018.
The central bank base rate in Euria is currently 4 .2% and the treasury team believes that it can
invest funds in Euria at the central bank base rate less 30 basis points. However, treasury staff have
seen predictions that the central bank base rate could increase by up to 1.1% or fall by up to 0·6%
between now and 31 January 2018.
For the purposes of this example we will consider the following possibilities to hedge the receipt:
Interest rate futures
Exchange-traded options on interest rate futures

Comments
As expected, the futures market provides a fixed return of 4.58% whether the central bank base
rate increases to 5.3% or reduces to 3.6%. The 95.25 option provides a better outcome as long as
interest rates rise but is significantly lower if interest rates fall. If the board is at all risk averse the
futures outcome would be preferable. The 94.25 option is marginally lower than the futures
outcome under both scenarios but may be preferable if the base rate rises higher than 5.41%, the
point at which the option would not be exercised.
结论：
正如预期的那样，无论央行基准利率上调至 5.3%还是降至 3.6%，期货市场均提供 4.58%的
固定回报。只要利率上升，95.25 期权就会提供更好的结果，但如果利率下降，则价格会大
大降低。如果董事会完全厌恶风险，则期货结果将更可取。在这两种情况下，94.25 期权略
低于期货结果，但如果基准利率高于 5.41%，则可能更可取，此时期权不会被行使。
(2) Impact of basis risk
（2） 基差风险的影响
Today’s date is 31 January. The prediction that the central bank base rate might increase by 1.1%
to 5.3% turns out to be exactly correct. Based on our previous basis calculation we would have
expected today’s price for the March futures contracts to fall to 94.36.
今天的日期是 1 月 31 日。央行基准利率可能上升 1.1%至 5.3%的预测被证明是完全正确的。
根据我们之前的基差计算，我们预计 3 月期货合约的今天价格将跌至 94.36。
However, futures contracts are exposed to basis risk, which means the closing March futures price
on 31 January could be more or less than predicted. For the purposes of this exampl e, we will
assume the closing futures price on 31 January is 94.16, only marginally less than our prediction
from before.
然而，期货合约面临基差风险，这意味着 1 月 31 日 3 月收盘的期货价格可能高于或低于预
期。出于本例的目的，我们假设 1 月 31 日的收盘期货价格为 94.16，仅略低于我们之前
的预测。

Implications for hedging strategy
对套期保值策略的影响
The futures market provides a lower return than both option contracts although we have already
determined that a risk averse treasury manager would have ignored the 95.25 option. However,
the 94.25 option now looks more attractive than the futures outcome even though the central
bank base rate increased exactly as predicted.
期货市场的回报率低于两种期权合约，尽管我们已经确定规避风险的财资经理会忽略95.25
期权。然而，94.25 期权现在看起来比期货结果更具吸引力，尽管央行基准利率的上升与预
期完全一样。
If the relationship between the futures and spot price is not predictable, there is no guarantee that
the closing futures price on 31 January will match the price predicted by our basis calculation. On
1 October the futures price prediction for 31 January is 94.36, assuming the base rate increases to
5.3%, but it could be either more or less even when the base rate increases exactly as predicted.
对套期保值策略的影响 期货市场的回报率低于两种期权合约，尽管我们已经确定规避风险
的财资经理会忽略 95.25 期权。然而，94.25 期权现在看起来比期货结果更具吸引力，尽管
央行基准利率的上升与预期完全一样。
This exposure to basis risk means the actual futures price fell to 94.16 on 31 January instead of
94.36. An unexpected change in basis, even marginally, reduces the return on the futures hedge
from 4.58% to 4.38% as opposed to a return of 4.45% using the 94.25 option. With the benefit of
hindsight the 94.25 option would have provided a better outcome. However, the treasury
department have limited visibility of the future when choosing an optimal strategy at the time the
hedge is set up on 1 October . The treasury department’s best estimate of the closing futures price
is based on a simplifying assumption that basis diminishes at a constant rate, which may not hold
true in practice. Basis risk, therefore, introduces an element of unpredictability which the treasury
staff need to be aware of at the outset. Scenario analysis would be useful in determining the final
strategy in accordance with the company’s risk preferences.
这种基差风险敞口意味着实际期货价格在 1 月 31 日从 94.36 跌至 94.16。基差的意外变化，
即使是轻微的变化，也会使期货对冲的回报率从 4.58%降至 4.38%，而使用 94.25 期权的回
报率为 4.45%。 事后看来，94.25 选项将提供更好的结果。 然而，司库部门 10 月 1 日建立对
冲时选择最佳策略时对未来的可见性有限。 司库部对收盘期货价格的最佳估计是基于一个简
化的假设， 即基差以恒定的速度减少， 这在实践中可能并不成立。 因此， 基准风险引入了财
务工作人员在一开始就需要意识到的不可预测性因素。 情景分析将有助于根据公司的风险偏
好确定最终战略。
Written by a member of the AFM examining team

stem_zh:
BOND VALUATION AND BOND YIELDS
债券估值和债券收益率
Bonds and their variants such as loan notes, debentures and loan stock, are IOUs issued by governments and corporations
as a means of raising finance. They are often referred to as fixed income or fixed interest securities, to distinguish them
from equities, in that they often (but not always) make known returns for the investors (the bond holders) at regular
intervals. These interest payments, paid as bond coupons, are fixed, unlike divid ends paid on equities, which can be
variable. Most corporate bonds are redeemable after a specified period of time. Thus, a ‘plain vanilla’ bond will make
regular interest payments to the investors and pay the capital to buy back the bond on the redemption  date when it
reaches maturity.
债券及其变形，比如贷款票据、债券和贷款股票，作为筹集资金的一种手段，是政府和公司发行的借据。它们通
常被称为固定收益或固定利率的有价证券，以区别于股票，因为它们经常(但不总是)定期为投资者(债券持有人)
带来确定的回报。 这些作为债券票面利息支付的利息是固定的， 不像股票支付的股息是可变的。 大多数公司债券
在一段特定的时间后可以赎回。 因此,“普通” 债券将定期向投资者支付利息， 并在债券到期时支付资本以在赎回
日回购债券。
This article, the first of two related articles, will consider how bonds are valued and the relationship between the bond
value or price, the yield to maturity and the spot yield curve. It addresses, in part, the learning required in Sections B3a
and B3e of the the Advanced Financial Management Syllabus and Study Guide.
这篇文章是两篇相关文章中的第一篇， 将涉及如何对债券进行估值， 以及债券价值或价格、 到期收益率和即期收
益率曲线之间的关系。它部分解决了高级财务管理教学大纲和学习指南B3a 和 B3e 部分中要求的学习。
Bond value or price
债券的价值/价格
Example 1
How much would an investor pay to purchase a bond today, which is red eemable in four years for its nominal value or
face value of $100 and pays an annual coupon of 5% on the nominal value? The required rate of return (or yield) for a
bond in this risk class is 4%.
一个投资者今天会花多少钱去购买一张债券，这张债券可以在四年后按照面值 100 美元赎回，票面利率是 5%。
这个风险等级的债券对应的回报率(或收益率)是 4%。
As with any asset valuation, the investor would be willing to pay, at the most, the present value of the future income
stream discounted at the required rate of return (or yield). Thus, the value of the bond can be determined as follows:
与任何资产的估值一样， 投资者最多愿意支付未来现金流入按要求的回报率(或收益率)折现后的现值之和。 因此，
债券的价值可以确定如下:
对于债券投资人来说，为期 4 年的债券，year 1-4 的每年都可以收到票面利息$5（= coupon rate × par value = 5% ×
$100） 。债券到期那年除了票面利息，还可以收到债券的赎回价值$100，所以 year 4 那年的现金流入是 $105
（=$5+$100） 。按照债权人要求的回报率4%对 year 1-4 的年金流入进行折现加总，所以这批现金流的现值之和
$103.62 就是这张面值$100 的债券的价值/价格。
If the required rate of return (or yield) was 6%, then using the same calculation method, the price of the bond would be
$96.54. And where the required rate of return (or yield) is equal to the coupon – 5% in this case – the current price of the
bond will be equal to the nominal value of $100.
如果要求的回报率(或收益率)是 6%，那么使用相同的计算方法，债券的价格将是 96.54 美元。
如果要求的回报率(或收益率)等于票面利率——在这种情况下为 5%——债券的当前价格将等于票面价值 100 美
元。
Thus, there is an inverse relationship between the yield of a bond and its price or value. The higher rate of return (or yield)
required, the lower the price of the bond, and vice versa. However, it should be noted that this relationship is not linear,
but convex to the origin.
因此，债券的收益与其价格/价值成反比。要求的回报率(或收益率)越高，债券的价格就越低，反之亦然。但需要
注意的是，这种关系不是线性的，而是凸向原点的非线性的关系（斜率的绝对值递减）。
The plain vanilla bond with annual coupon payments in the above example is the simpler type of bond. In addition to the
plain vanilla bond, candidates – as part of their Advanced Financial Management studies and exam – are required to have
knowledge of, and be able to deal with, more complicated bonds such as: bonds with coupon payments occurring more
frequently than once a year; convertible bonds and bonds with warrants which contain option fea tures; and more
complicated payment features such as repayment mortgage or annuity type payment structures.
在上面的例子中， 每年支付票面利率的普通债券是一种比较简单的债券。 除了普通债券之外， 作为高级财务管理
学习和考试的一部分，考生还需要了解并能够处理更复杂的债券，例如:每年付息次数超过一次的债券；可转换
债券和带有期权特征的认股权证债券；以及更复杂的支付特征，例如偿还抵押贷款或年金类型的支付结构。
Yield to maturity (YTM) (also known as the [Gross] Redemption Yield (GRY))
到期收益率（也称为[总]赎回收益率）
If the current price of a bond is given, together with details of coupons and redemption date, then this information can
be used to compute the required rate of return or yield to maturity of the bond.
如果给出了债券的当前价格， 以及票面利率和赎回日期这些详细信息， 那么这些信息可以用来计算债券持有至到
期所需的回报率或收益率。
Example 2
A bond paying a coupon of 7% is redeemable in five years at nominal value ($100) and is currently trading at $106.62.
Estimate its yield (required rate of return).
票面利率为 7%的债券可以在 5 年后按照票面价值(100 美元)赎回，目前交易价格为 106.62 美元。估算其收益率
(要求收益率)。
假设这个债券的收益率 （要求收益率） 是r， 债券现在的价值106.62 等于债券投资者在未来可以收到的所有现金
流入（包括每年$7 的票面利息收入和到期的赎回价值$100） ，按照债权人要求的回报率r 折现的现值之和。
The internal rate of return approach can be used to obtain r.
我们可以使用计算内涵报酬率的方法推导 r。
Since the current price is higher than $100, r must be lower than 7%.
由于债券现在的价格超过$100，所以 r 一定小于票面利率 7%。 （原因参考Example 1）
Initially, try 5% as r:
$7 x 4.3295 [5%, five - year annuity] + $100 x 0.7835 [PV 5%, five - year] = $30.31 + $78.35 = $108.66
所以一开始，我们可以尝试 5%作为收益率，债券的价值等于$108.66。
Try 6% as r:
$7 x 4.2124 [6%, five - year annuity] + $100 x 0.7473 [PV 6%, five - year] = $29.49 + $74.73 = $104.22
然后尝试将 6%作为收益率，债券的价值等于$104.22
利用线性插值法，我们可以推导得出这个债券的收益率是 5.46%。
Yield = a% + ((
NPVa
NPVa−NPVb) (b% − a%)) = 5% + ((
108.66−106.62
108.66 – 106.62−(104.22−106.62)) (6% − 5%)) = 5.46%
其中 a%=5%，NPVa = 108.66-106.62，b%=6%， NPVb = 104.22-106.62。
The 5.46% is the yield to maturity (YTM) (or redemption yield) of the bond. The YTM  is the rate of return at which the
sum of the present values of all future income streams of the bond (interest coupons and redemption amount) is equal
to the current bond price. It is the average annual rate of return the bond investors expect to receive from the bond till
its redemption. YTMs for bonds are normally quoted in the financial press, based on the closing price of the bond. For
example, a yield often quoted in the financial press is the bid yield. The bid yield is the YTM for the current bid price (the
price at which bonds can be purchased) of a bond.
5.46%就是债券的持有至到期收益率(YTM)(赎回收益率)。到期收益率是令债券所有未来的现金流入(票面利率和赎
回价值)的现值之和正好等于债券当前交易价格的折现率。它是债券投资者直到债券被赎回，期望从债券中获得
的平均年回报率。 债券的到期收益率通常在金融媒体上基于债券的收盘价报价。 例如， 金融媒体上经常引用的收
益率就是出价收益率。出价收益率是债券当前买价(现在购买债券的价格)的持有至到期收益率。
Term structure of interest rates and the yield curve
利率期限结构和收益率曲线
The yield to maturity is calculated implicitly based on the current market price, the term to maturity of the bond and
amount (and frequency) of coupon payments. However, if a cor porate bond is being issued for the first time, its price
and/or coupon payments need to be determined based on the required yield. The required yield is based on the term
structure of interest rates and this needs to be discussed before considering how the price of a bond may be determined.
到期收益率是根据债券当前的市场价格、债券的到期时间和票面利息支付的金额(和频率)计算的。然而，如果公
司债券是首次发行，其价格和/或票面利率的需要根据投资者要求的收益率来确定。要求的收益率是基于利率的
期限结构得出的，这需要在考虑确定债券价格之前进行讨论。
It is incorrect to assume that bonds of the same risk class, which are redeemed on different dates, would have the same
required rate of return or yield. In fact, it is evident that the markets demand different annual returns or yields on bonds
with differing lengths of time before their redemption (or maturity), even where the bonds are of the same risk class.
This is known as the term structure of interest rates and is represented by the spot yield curve or simply the yield curve.
假设风险等级相同， 在不同到期日赎回的债券具有相同的要求回报率或收益率是不正确的。 事实上， 很明显， 即
使债券的风险等级相同， 市场对不同到期时间的债券会要求不同的年回报率或收益率。 这就是所谓的利率期限结
构，用即期收益率曲线或简单的收益率曲线来表示。
For example, a company may find that if it wants to issue a one - year bond, it may need to pay interest at 3% for the year,
if it wants to issue a two - year bond, the markets may demand an annual interest rate of 3. 5%, and for a three-year bond
the annual yield required may be 4.2%. Hence, the company would need to pay interest at 3% for one year; 3.5% each
year, for two years, if it wants to borrow funds for two years; and 4.2% each year, for three years, if it wants to borrow
funds for three years. In this case, the term structure of interest rates is represented by an upward sloping yield curve.
例如，一家公司可能会发现，如果它想发行一年期的债券，它可能需要支付 3%的年利率，如果它想发行两年期
的债券，市场可能会要求 3.5%的年利率，而三年期的债券的年利率可能需要达到 4.2%。因此，对于一年就到期
的债券，该公司需要支付 3%的利息；如果公司需要借款两年，则每年需要支付 3.5%的利息；如果公司想借款三
年，那么每年需要支付的利息是 4.2%。在这种情况下，利率期限结构是通过一条向上倾斜的收益率曲线来表示
的（如下图所示） 。
The normal expectation would be of an upward sloping yield curve on the basis that bonds with a longer period of
maturity would require a higher interest rate as compensation for risk. Note here that the bonds considered may be of
the same risk class but the longer time period to maturity still adds to higher uncertainty.
正常的预期是收益率曲线向上倾斜， 因为期限较长的债券需要更高的利率来补偿投资者面临的风险。 请注意， 所
考虑的债券可能具有相同的风险等级，但更长的到期时间仍然增加了更多的不确定性。
However, it is entirely normal for yield curves to be of many different shapes dependent on the perceptions of the markets
on how interest rates may change in the future. Three main theories have been advanced to explain the term structure
of interest rates or the yield curve: expectations hypothesis, liquidity -preference hypothesis and market-segmentation
hypothesis. Although it is beyond the remit of this article to explain these theories, many textbooks on investments and
financial management cover these in detail.
然而，通常来说收益率曲线有许多不同的形状， 取决于市场对未来利率变化的看法。 解释利率期限结构或收益率
曲线的理论主要有以下三种：预期假说、 流动性偏好假说和市场分割假说。 虽然解释这些理论超出了本文的范围，
但是许多关于投资和财务管理的教科书都会详细介绍这些理论（FM Syllabus G2c）。
Valuing bonds based on the yield curve
基于收益率曲线的债券估值
Annual spot yield curves are often published by the financial press or by central banks (for example, the Bank of England
regularly publishes UK government bond yield curves on its website). The spot yield curve can be used to estimate the
price or value of a bond.
年化的即期收益率曲线通常由金融媒体或央行发布(例如，英格兰银行会定期在其网站上发布英国政府债券收益
率曲线)。零息债券收益率曲线可以用来估计债券的价格或价值。
Example 3
A company wants to issue a bond that is redeemable in four years for its nominal value or face value of $100, and wants
to pay an annual coupon of 5% on the nominal value. Estimate the price at which the bond should be issued.
一家公司想发行一支面值为 100 美元、四年到期并按照面值赎回的债券，希望支付的票面利率是 5%。要求计算
债券的发行价格。
The annual spot yield curve for a bond of this risk class is as follows:
该风险等级下的零息债券的年化收益率曲线如下：
One-year             3.5%          一年期债券的年化收益率是 3.5%
Two-year             4.0%          两年期债券的年化收益率是 4.0%
Three-year            4.7%         三年期债券的年化收益率是 4.7%
Four-year             5.5%         四年期债券的年化收益率是 5.5%
The four-year bond pays the following stream of income:
Year                 1            2            3             4
Payments            $5           $5           $5           $105
每支四年期债券会向投资者支付以下现金流：
第一年到第四年，每年 5 美元的利息，第四年除了利息，还有 100 美元的赎回价值。
This can be simplified into four separate bonds with the following payment structure:
Year                     1             2             3             4
Bond 1                  $5
Bond 2                                $5
Bond 3                                               $5
Bond 4                                                           $105
这支债券可以被简化成四支相互独立的债券：
债券 1：在第一年支付 5 美元
债券 2：在第二年支付 5 美元
债券 3：在第三年支付 5 美元
债券 4：在第四年支付 105 美元
Each annual payment is a single payment in that particular year, much like a zero-coupon bond, and its present value can
be determined by discounting each cash flow by the relevant yield curve rate, as follows:
每笔年度付款是那个特定年份的唯一的一次付款， 和零息债券很像， 所以它的现值可以通过相关收益率曲线对应
年份的利率折现每笔现金流来确定，如下所示：
The sum of these flows is the price at which the bond can be issued, $98.57.
这些现金流的现值之和$98.57 就是这支债券的发行价格。
The yield to maturity of the bond is estimated at 5.41% using the same methodology as example 2.
使用与范例 2 一样的方法，可以计算出这支债券的到期收益率是 5.41%。计算过程如下：
Initially, try 5% as r:
$5 x 3.5460 [5%, four - year annuity] + $100 x 0.8227 [PV 5%, four - year] = $17.73 + $82.27 = $100
所以一开始，我们可以尝试 5%作为收益率，债券的价值等于$100。
Try 6% as r:
$5 x 3.4651 [6%, four - year annuity] + $100 x 0.7921 [PV 6%, four - year] = $17.33 + $79.21 = $96.54
然后尝试将 6%作为收益率，债券的价值等于$96.54。
利用线性插值法，我们可以推导得出这个债券的收益率是 5.46%。
Yield = a% + ((
NPVa
NPVa−NPVb) (b% − a%)) = 5% + ((
100−98.57
100 – 98.57 –(96.54 – 98.57)) (6% − 5%)) = 5.41%
其中 a%=5%，NPVa = 100-98.57，b%=6%， NPVb = 96.54-98.57。
Some important points can be noted from the above calculation; firstly, the 5.41% is lower than 5.5% because some of
the ret urns from the bond come in earlier years, when the interest rates on the yield curve are lower, but the largest
proportion comes in Year 4. Secondly, the yield to maturity is a weighted average of the term structure of interest rates.
Thirdly, the yield to maturity is calculated after the price of the bond has been calculated or observed in the markets, but
theoretically it is term structure of interest rates that determines the price or value of the bond.
有些要点从上面的计算中可以注意到：
首先，5.41%的到期收益率比四年期债券的年化收益率 5.5%要低一些，因为该债券的一些回报是出现在早些年，
而当时的收益率曲线上的利率较低，只不过大部分的回报发生在第四年。
第二，到期收益率是利率期限结构的加权平均值。
第三， 到期收益率是在市场上计算或者观察债券价格后计算出来的， 但从理论上， 是利率的期限结构决定了债券
的价格/价值。
Mathematically:
数学上：
债券的价值等于债权人在持有债券的未来可以收到的所有的现金流入，按照零息债券的收益率曲线在对应年份
的年化收益率折现的现值之和。
In this article it is assumed that coupons are paid annually, but it is common practice to pay coupons more frequently
than once a year. In these circumstances, the coupon payments need to be reduced and the time period frequency needs
to be increased.
在这篇文章中，我们假设票面利率每年支付一次 ，不过在实际操作中，票面利率的支付频率超过一年一次是很
常见的。在这种情况下，每次支付的票面利息会减少，而支付的周期和频率会增加。
Estimating the yield curve
估算收益率曲线
There are different methods used to estimate a spot yield curve, and the iterative process based on bootstrapping coupon
paying bonds is perhaps the simplest to understand. The following example demonstrates how the process works.
可以用不同的方法来估算零息债券的收益率曲线，基于反复 将市场上所有带票面利息的债券 简化成由许多个票
面利息和赎回价值共同组成构成的零息债券的这个过程（如范例 3）可能是最容易理解的。下面的范例就演示了
该过程是如何进行的。
Example 4
A government has three bonds in issue that all have a face or nominal value of $100 and are redeemable in one year, two
years and three years respectively. Since the bonds are all government bonds, let’s assume that they are of the same risk
class. Let’s also assume that coupons are payable on an annual basis. Bond A, which is redeemable in a year’s time, has
a coupon rate of 7% and is trading at $103. Bond B, which is redeemable in  two years, has a coupon rate of 6% and is
trading at $102. Bond C, which is redeemable in three years, has a coupon rate of 5% and is trading at $98.
一个政府发行了三种债券， 每种债券的面值都是100 美元， 分别是一年期， 两年期和三年期按照面值赎回。 由于
债券都是政府债券， 所以我们可以假设它们的风险等级相同。同时我们假设债券的票面利息是一年支付一次。 那
么一年期可赎回债券 A 的票面利率是 7%，交易价格是 103 美元；两年期可赎回债券 B 的票面利率是 6%，交易
价格是 102 美元；三年期可赎回债券 C 的票面利率是 5%，交易价格是 98 美元。
To determine the yield curve, each bond’s cash flows are discounted in turn to determine the annual spot rates for the
three years, as follows:
为了确定收益率曲线， 我们对每种债券的现金流依次贴现， 以确定三个年度的零息债券各自的收益率。 如下所示：
假设第一个年度的年化收益率是 r1， 那么债券A 的交易价格$103 就等于对一年到期后可以收到的票面利息$7 和
赎回价$100 按照 r1 折现的现值之和。所以 r1 计算出来是 3.88%。
假设第二个年度的年化收益率是 r2， 那么债券B 的交易价格$103 就等于对第一年可以收到的债券 B 的票面利息
$6 按照刚算出来的第一年的折现率 3.88%进行折现，加上第二年债券 B 到期后可以收到的票面利息$6 和赎回价
$100 按照 r2 折现的现值之和。所以 r2 计算出来是 4.96%。
假设第三个年度的年化收益率是 r3， 那么债券C 的交易价格$98 就等于对第一年可以收到的债券 C 的票面利息$5
按照刚算出来的第一年的折现率 3.88%进行折现，加上第二年可以收到的债券 C 的票面利息$5 按照刚算出来的
第二年的折现率 4.96%进行折现，加上第三年到期后可以收到的票面利息$5 和赎回价$100 按照 r3 折现的现值之
和。所以 r3 计算出来是 5.80%。
The annual spot yield curve is therefore:
Year
1    3.88%
2    4.96 %
3    5.80%
因此，我们推算出的零息债券的年化收益率曲线如下：
第 1 年的年化收益率是 3.88%
第 2 年的年化收益率是 4.96%
第 3 年的年化收益率是 5.80%
Discussion of other methods of estimating the spot yield curve, such as using multiple regression techniques and
observation of spot rates of zero coupon bonds, is beyond the scope of the Advanced Financial Management syllabus.
当然我们还有其他的方法计算零息债券的收益率曲线， 例如使用多元回归计算观察零息债券的年化利率， 只是这
个超出了我们高级财务管理这门课的考纲范围。
As stated in the previous section, often the financial press and central banks will publish estimated spot yield curves
based on government issued bonds. Yield curves for individual corporate bonds can be estimated from these by adding
the relevant spread to the bonds. For example, the following table of spreads (in basis points) is given for the retail sector.
如前一节所述，金融媒体和央行通过会根据政府发行的债券预测零息债券的收益率曲线。个体公司发行的债券，
其收益率曲线可以在此基础上， 加上对应的信贷息差体现违约风险大小。 例如， 下表给出了零售部门的信贷基差
（以基点 bp 为单位，1 bp = 0.01%）
Rating            1 year         2 year         3 year
AAA              14             25            38
AA               29             41            55
A                46             60            76
Example 5
Mason Retail Co has a credit rating of AA, then its individual yield curve – based on the government bond yield curve and
the spread table above – may be estimated as:
Year 1            Year 2             Year 3
4.17%            5.37%             6.35%
Mason 零售公司的信用评级是 AA，公司的收益率曲线是在政府债券收益率曲线的基础上根据信贷息差表进行调
整的，按照如下模式进行推算：
Mason 零售公司一年期债券的年化收益率， 需要在一年期政府债券年化利率4.17%的基础上加上对应期限和对应
信用评级的信贷息差 29 个基点，也就是 4.46% = 4.17% + 0.29%。
以此类推。
Mason 零售公司两年期债券的年化收益率， 需要在同等期限也就是两年期政府债券年化利率5.37%的基础上加上
对应期限和对应信用评级的信贷息差 41 个基点，也就是 5.78% = 5.37% + 0.41%。
Mason 零售公司三年期债券的年化收益率， 需要在同等期限也就是三年期政府债券年化利率6.35%的基础上加上
对应期限和对应信用评级的信贷息差 55 个基点，也就是 6.90% = 6.35% + 0.55%。
These would be the rates of return an investor buying bonds issued by Mason Retail Co would expect, and therefore
Mason Retail Co would use these rates as discount rates to estimate the price or value of coupons when it issues new
bonds. And Mason Retail Co’s existing bonds’ market price would reflect its individual yield curve.
这些是购买 Mason 零售公司所发行债券的这些投资者所期望的回报率，因此 Mason 零售公司在发行新债券时，
会使用这些回报率作为折现率估算债券的价格/价值。而且 Mason 零售公司现有债券的市场价格也会反映单个公
司的收益率曲线。
Conclusion
This article considered the relationship between bond prices, the yield curve and the yield to maturity. It demonstrated
how bonds can be valued and how a yield curve may be derived using bonds of the same risk class but of different
maturities. Finally it showed how individual company yield curves may be estimated.
本文考虑的债券价格、 收益率曲线和到期收益率之间的关系。 它展示了如何对债券进行估值， 以及如何利用风险
等级相同但到期日不同的债券得出收益率曲线。最后，它展示了如何估计单个公司的收益率曲线。
A following article will discuss how forward interest rates are determined from the spot yield curve and how they may be
useful in determining the value of an interest rate swap. It will address the learning required in Sections E1 and E3 of the
Advanced Financial Management Syllabus and Study Guide.
下一篇文章将讨论如何从即期收益率曲线中确定远期利率， 以及它们如何有助于确定利率互换的价值。 它将有助
于解决高级财务管理教学大纲学习指南 E1 和 E3 部分的问题。
Written by the Advanced Financial Management examining team

stem_zh:
PATTERNS OF BEHAVIOUR
行为模式
Are all financial decisions rational? The assumption that they are underpins theories of economic behaviour and stock
market models, such as the efficient market hypothesis.
所有的财务决策都是理性的吗？ 他们是基于支撑经济行为理论和股票市场模型的假设的，比如有效市场假说
Why then do stock market booms and busts occur if investors are acting rationally? Rational behaviour surely implies
no shocks, with stock markets showing steady movements in share prices, but not sudden spurts. However,
unexpected and significant news could still result in sudden shocks.
那么， 如果投资者的行为是理性的， 那么为什么股市会出现繁荣和萧条呢？理性行为肯定意味着没有冲击， 股票
市场显示股价稳定波动，但不会突然飙升。然而，意想不到的重大消息仍可能导致突然的冲击。
Also, why are some mergers and acquisitions considered to be poor deals? If a listed company is  being acquired, surely
the acquisition price should be based on the market value of its shares, if the markets are valuing it fairly. Why then is
there uncertainty about the true value of many acquired companies? Why also do many acquisitions run into
difficulties?
另外， 为什么一些并购被认为是糟糕的交易？如果上市公司被收购， 如果市场公平地评估它， 收购价格肯定应该
基于其股票的市场价值。 那么， 为什么许多被收购公司的真实价值存在不确定性呢？为什么很多收购也会遇到困
难？
If proper due diligence has been done and decisions are made rationally, surely the directors of the acquiring company
will only go ahead if the combination stands a very good chance of success.
如果已经进行了适当的尽职调查并做出了合理的决策， 那么收购公司的高管肯定只有在合并有很大的成功机会的
情况下才会继续。
Behavioural finance attempts to explain how decision makers take financial decisions in real life, and why their
decisions might not appear to be rational every time and, hence, have unpredictable consequences. Behavioural
finance has been described as ‘the influence of psychology on the behaviour of financial practitioners’ (Sewell, 2005).
Behavioural finance seeks to examine the following assum ptions of rational decision making by investors and financial
managers:
行为金融学试图解释决策者在现实生活中如何做出财务决策，以及为什么他们的决定可能每次看起来都不理性，
因此会产生不可预测的后果。行为金融学被描述为“心理学对金融从业者行为的影响”（Sewell，2005）。行为金
融学旨在研究投资者和财务经理理性决策的以下假设
1. Financial decision makers seek to maximise their utility  and do so by trying to maximise portfolio or company
value.
2. They take financial decisions based on analysis of relevant information.
3. The analysis of financial information that they undertake is rational, objective and risk-neutral.
1. 财务决策者寻求最大化其效用，并通过尝试最大化投资组合或公司价值来做到这一点。
2.根据对相关信息的分析做出财务决策。
3.对财务信息的分析是理性、客观和风险中立的。
Let’s look at how behavioural factors may influence decision making and, therefore, stock markets’ and companies’
financial strategies.
让我们看看行为因素如何影响决策，从而影响股票市场和公司的财务战略。
Investors
投资者
Maximisation of utility
用途最大化
Rational decision making by investors implies that their decisions about their investment portfolios will aim to
maximise their long-term wealth and, hence, their utility. However, behavioural factors may influence investors to take
decisions that are not the best ones for achieving maximum value from their portfolios. Investors may have
preferences for particular stocks on non-financial grounds – for example, companies that they consider are acting with
social responsibility. They may also avoid ’sin stocks’ – companies operating in sectors that they regard as unethical.
投资者的理性决策意味着他们对投资组合的决策将旨在最大化他们的长期财富，从而最大化他们的效用。然而，
行为因素可能会影响投资者做出并非从其投资组合中获得最大价值的最佳决策。 投资者可能出于非财务原因对特
定股票有偏好——例如， 他们认为具有社会责任感的公司。 他们也可能避免 “罪恶股票”——在他们认为不道德
的行业运营的公司。
Investor utility may also be linked to the process of decision making. Some investors hold on to shares with prices that
have fallen over time and are unlikely to recover. They may do this becaus e it will cause them psychological hurt to
admit, even only to themselves, that their decision to invest was wrong. This is known as cognitive dissonance.
投资者效用也可能与决策过程有关。 一些投资者持有价格随着时间的推移而下跌且不太可能恢复的股票。 他们这
样做可能是因为承认， 即使只对自己， 承认他们的投资决定是错误的， 也会给他们带来心理伤害。 这被称为认知
失调。
Analysis of relevant information
分析相关信息
Behavioural finance next looks at the basis that investors use to take decisions. It suggests that  decisions may not be
based on an assessment of relevant financial information, but on other grounds. Investors may use information that is
not relevant but is readily available, possibly to simplify the decision making process (known as  anchoring). For
example, investors may buy shares that in the past have had high values, on the grounds that these represent their
true potential values, even though rational analysis suggests that the prices of these shares will remain low in the
future
行为金融学接下来着眼于投资者用来做出决定的基础。 它表明， 决定可能不是基于对有关财务信息的评估， 而是
基于其他理由。投资者可能会使用不相关但随时可用的信息，可能是为了简化决策过程（称为锚定）。例如，投
资者可能会购买过去具有高价值的股票， 理由是这些股票代表了它们的真实潜在价值， 即使理性分析表明这些股
票的价格在未来将保持低位。
Investors may also believe that the probability of a future outcome will be influenced by how often the same out come
has occurred in the past. A non-financial example of this idea would be the situation when a coin is flipped eight times,
comes up as tails every time and it is said that heads is more likely the ninth time as, by the ‘law of averages’, heads
must come up soon.
投资者也可能认为， 未来结果的可能性将受到过去相同结果发生频率的影响。 这个想法的一个非金融例子是， 一
枚硬币被掷出八次，每次都以反面的形式出现，据说正面更有可能是第九次，因为根据“平均法则”，正面必须
很快出现。
If the value of a company’s shares has risen for some time, investors will be using similar logic to the coin example if
they sell those shar es on the grounds that the shares have gained in value for ‘long enough’ and their price must
therefore soon start to fall, even if rational analysis suggest that the rise in price will continue. This is known as
the gambler’s fallacy.
如果一家公司股票的价值已经上涨了一段时间， 投资者将使用与硬币示例类似的逻辑， 如果他们出售这些股票的
理由是股票的价值上涨的已经 “足够长” ， 因此它们的价格必须很快开始下跌， 即使理性分析表明价格上涨将继
续。这被称为赌徒谬误。
Another deviation from rational analysis is the  herd instinct, where investors buy or sell shares in a company or sector
because many other investors have already don e so. Explanations for investors following a herd instinct include social
conformity, the desire not to act differently from others. Following a herd instinct may also be due to individual
investors lacking the confidence to make their own judgements, beli eving that a large group of other investors cannot
be wrong. If many investors follow a herd instinct to buy shares in a certain sector, for example the IT sector, this can
result in significant price rises for shares in that sector and lead to a stock market bubble.
与理性分析的另一个偏差是羊群本能， 即投资者购买或出售公司或行业的股票， 因为许多其他投资者已经这样做
了。 对遵循从众本能的投资者的解释包括社会一致性， 即不采取与他人不同的行为的愿望。 遵循从众本能也可能
是由于个人投资者缺乏做出自己判断的信心， 认为一大群其他投资者不会错。 如果许多投资者遵循从众本能购买
某个行业（例如 IT 行业）的股票，这可能会导致该行业的股票价格大幅上涨并导致股市泡沫。
Investors may not therefore base their decisions on ra tional analysis, but there is also evidence to suggest that stock
market ‘professionals’ often don’t do so either. Studies have shown that there are traders in stock markets who do not
base their decisions on fundamental analysis of company performance and prospects. They are known as noise traders.
因此，投资者可能不会基于理性分析做出决定，但也有证据表明，股市“专业人士”通常也不会这样做。研究表
明，股票市场中有些交易者的决策并非基于对公司业绩和前景的基本面分析。他们被称为噪音交易商。
Characteristics associated with noise traders include making poorly timed decisions and following trends. Chartism,
using analysis of past share prices as a basis for predicting the future, is an example of noise trading.
与噪音交易者相关的特征包括做出不合时宜的决策和跟随趋势。 图表主义使用对过去股价的分析作为预测未来的
基础，是噪音交易的一个例子。
Fund managers may also be subject to behavioural influences. Fund managers who wish to give the impression that
they are actively managing their investment portfolios, may periodically reposition their portfolios into new sectors,
even though the old sectors continue to have good prospects. Some fund managers also ignore companies wi th low
market capitalisation, with the result that their shares are not purchased and their value remains low (known as  small
capitalisation discount).
基金经理也可能受到行为影响。 希望给人一种他们正在积极管理其投资组合的印象的基金经理， 可以定期将其投
资组合重新定位到新行业， 即使旧行业继续具有良好的前景。 一些基金经理也忽略了市值低的公司， 结果他们的
股票没有被购买，价值仍然很低（称为小盘值折扣）
Rational, objective and risk-neutral analysis
理性，客观和风险中立分析
Investors may base their decision on an analysis of available information, but behavioural finance has highlighted that
this analysis can be subjective. One aspect is confirmation bias, taking an approach or paying attention to evidence that
confirms investors’ current beliefs about their investments and ignoring evi dence that casts doubt on their beliefs. In
the dotcom boom, some investors used a variety of methods to value high -tech companies at a large premium, but
ignored models such as cash flow valuation models that indicated the worth of those companies was much lower.
投资者可能基于对可用信息的分析做出决定， 但行为金融学强调， 这种分析可能是主观的。 一个方面是确认偏见，
采取一种方法或关注证实投资者当前对其投资信念的证据， 而忽略对其信念产生怀疑的证据。 在互联网繁荣时期，
一些投资者使用各种方法以高溢价对高科技公司进行估值， 但忽略了现金流估值模型等模型， 这些模型表明这些
公司的价值要低得多。
Another aspect of investor bias is attitudes towards risks. Rational theory suggests that risk-neutral investors will adopt
a long -term approach based on expected values. However, behavioural finance has highlighted various attitudes
towards the risks of making profits or losses. Some investors may be attracted by a company that offers the p ossibility
of making very high returns, even if the possibility is not very great (again, the dotcom boom provides evidence of this).
投资者偏见的另一个方面是对风险的态度。 理性理论表明， 风险中性投资者将采用基于预期价值的长期方法。 然
而， 行为金融学强调了对盈利或亏损风险的各种态度。 一些投资者可能会被一家有可能获得非常高回报的公司所
吸引，即使这种可能性不是很大（互联网繁荣再次证明了这一点）。
Other investors may have  regret aversion , avoiding investments that have the risk of making losses, even though
expected value analysis suggests that, in the long -term, they will make significant capital gains. Investors with regret
aversion may also prefer to invest in companies that look likely to make stable, but low, profits, rather than companies
that may make higher profits in some years but possibly losses in others.
其他投资者可能会后悔厌恶， 避免有亏损风险的投资， 尽管预期价值分析表明， 从长远来看， 他们将获得可观的
资本收益。 厌恶后悔的投资者也可能更愿意投资那些看起来可能获得稳定但利润较低的公司， 而不是在某些年份
可能获得较高利润但在其他年份可能亏损的公司。
There is also evidence that many investors pay most attention to the last set of financial results and other recent
information about a company, and take less notice of data that has been available for a while. E xplanations for this
have included recent information being more readily accessible and more immediate in investors’ minds than older
information. A consequence of this may be over-reaction when companies release information, with share prices rising
or falling quickly after information is released and then going back in the opposite direction to an equilibrium value
over time.
还有证据表明， 许多投资者最关注的是公司的最新财务业绩和其他最新信息， 而较少关注已经有一段时间的数据。
对此的解释包括， 与旧信息相比， 投资者更容易获得最新信息， 也更直接。 其后果可能是公司发布信息时反应过
度，股价在信息发布后迅速上涨或下跌，然后随着时间的推移向相反的方向回到均衡值。
Behavioural finance also suggests that there may be a  momentum effect in stock markets. A period of rising share
prices may result in a general feeling of optimism that price rises will continue and an increased willingness to invest in
companies that show prospects for growth. If a momentum effect exists, then it is likely to lengthen periods of stock
market boom or bust.
行为金融学还表明，股市可能存在动量效应。股价上涨的时期可能会导致人们普遍对价格上涨将继续感到乐观，
并且投资具有增长前景的公司的意愿增加。如果存在动量效应，那么它可能会延长股市繁荣或萧条的时期。
Finance managers
财务经理
Behavioural finance studies have also looked at decision making by managers of companies. They have identified
factors that affect investment decisions of all types, but particularly focused on mergers and acquisitions, since many
do not appear to fulfil the expectations of the acquiring company.
行为金融学研究还着眼于公司经理的决策。 他们确定了影响各类投资决策的因素， 但特别侧重于合并和收购， 因
为许多因素似乎没有达到收购公司的期望。
Maximisation of utility
效用最大
Companies can be regarded as maximising their utility by long -term maximisation of their shareholders’ wealth.
However, it is not just behavioural finance that casts doubt on whether company managers are seeking this objective
for their shareholders. Agency theory also highlights that managers may have different objectives from shareholders,
such as maximising their own short -term rewards and expanding the company by acquisition or other means in order
to enhance their own reputation.
公司可以被视为通过长期最大化股东财富来最大化其效用。 然而， 不仅仅是行为金融学让人怀疑公司经理是否在
为股东寻求这一目标。 代理理论还强调， 管理者可能与股东有不同的目标， 例如最大化自己的短期回报， 通过收
购或其他方式扩大公司，以提高自己的声誉。
However, behavioural finance has highlighted that managers’ objectives may not be explainable rationally. Studies
have looked at contested takeovers, where different companies bidding against each other has forced the acquisition
price up to a lev el that was significantly greater than many outside the companies involved thought was reasonable.
One theory for this is that once managers enter into competition, it makes acquiring a company that others have
sought to buy as well, a source of satisfacti on in itself. The acquirer’s managers are unwilling to let someone else have
what they have been trying to acquire (known as loss aversion bias).
然而， 行为金融学强调， 管理者的目标可能无法合理解释。 研究着眼于有争议的收购， 其中不同的公司相互竞标
迫使收购价格上升到一个明显高于许多外部公司认为合理的水平。 对此的一种理论是， 一旦管理者进入竞争， 就
会使收购一家其他人也试图收购的公司成为满足感的来源。 收购方的经理不愿意让别人拥有他们一直试图收购的
东西（称为损失厌恶偏见）。
Analysis of relevant information
分析相关信息
There is also evidence that when managers choose to bid for another company, the factor is sometimes not a rational
assessment of the target’s potential, but their belief in their own abilities. Some managers of acquiring companies
seem to believe that, however poor the outlook for the target seems, their own considerable management skills will
improve its prospects after the merger takes place. A symptom of this belief could be managers arguing that the target
should be valued not using its own price-earnings ratio, but using the (higher) price-earnings ratio of the acquirer.
还有证据表明， 当管理者选择竞标另一家公司时， 因素有时不是对目标公司潜力的理性评估， 而是他们对自己能
力的信念。 一些收购公司的经理似乎认为， 无论目标公司的前景看起来多么糟糕， 他们自己相当可观的管理技能
将在合并后改善其前景。 这种信念的一个症状可能是经理们认为， 目标公司的估值不应使用其自身的市盈率， 而
应使用收购方的（较高）市盈率。
Once an acquisition or any other strategy has been implemented, what influences managers may be the need to show
that they have made the right decisions. Managers may feel that a failing strategy would damage their reputation, and
possibly their future prospects. Therefore, they may decide to commit more funds trying to ensure that the strategy is
successful, rather than admitting defeat and taking steps to mitigate losses (known as entrapment).
一旦收购或任何其他策略得以实施，影响管理者的可能是需要表明他们做出了正确的决策。经理人可能会觉得，
失敗的策略會損害他們的声誉， 甚至可能損害他們的未來前景。 因此， 他们可能会决定投入更多资金， 试图确保
策略成功，而不是承认失败并采取措施减轻损失（称为陷阱）。
Rational, objective and risk-neutral analysis
理性，客观和风险中立分析
Managers may also be subjective when they analyse information. Also, they may have confirmation bias, paying
attention to information that suggests that an acquisition will enhance value and ignoring evidence that indicates that
the target will not be a good buy. They may also seek information that provides a simple yardstick for their own
decision making, however flawed that information may be. The value put on the target company by its own directors
may be subject to considerable bias, but the acquirer’s directors may regard it as a good indication of what the target’s
fair value is.
管理者在分析信息时也可能是主观的。 此外， 他们可能有确认偏见， 关注表明收购将提高价值的信息， 而忽略表
明目标公司不会是一个好买卖的证据。 他们也可能寻求为自己的决策提供简单标准的信息， 无论这些信息可能存
在缺陷。 目标公司自己的董事对目标公司的价值可能存在相当大的偏见， 但收购方的董事可能会将其视为目标公
司公允价值的良好指标。
Limitations of behavioural finance
行为金融学的局限
Critics of the behavioural finance approach have argued that even if individuals make irrational decisions when left by
themselves, participating in finance markets helps discipline them to act rationally by giving them opportunities to
learn from their experiences. The consequences of irrational decisions are short -term anomalies. In the longer -term
general theories, such as the efficient market hypothesis, will apply.
行为金融学方法的批评者认为， 即使个人在自己离开时做出了非理性的决定， 参与金融市场也有助于通过给他们
机会从经验中学习来约束他们理性行事。 非理性决策的后果是短期异常。 在长期的一般理论中， 例如有效市场假
说，将适用。
Conclusion
结论
Behavioural finance has identified a number of factors that may take individuals away from a process of taking
decisions to maximise economic utility on the basis of rational analysis of all the information supplied. If these factors
apply in practice, they can lead to movements from what would be considered a fair price for an individual company’s
shares, and the market as a whole to a period where share prices are collectively very high or low. For an acquisition, it
can lead to a purchase price that differs significantly from what appears to be a rational valuation.
行为金融学已经确定了一些因素， 这些因素可能使个人偏离在对所提供的所有信息进行理性分析的基础上做出最
大化经济效用的决策过程。 如果这些因素在实践中适用， 它们可能导致个别公司股票和整个市场的公平价格从被
认为是公平价格的变动到股价集体非常高或非常低的时期。 对于收购， 它可能导致购买价格与看似合理的估值大
不相同。
If you’re asked in the  Advanced Financial Management  exam to consider behavioural factors that may influence the
decisions of investors or managers, you’ll need to read the question scenario very carefully. Look out for information
about how investors or managers may be taking decisions, or factors in the situation that may trigger biases that the
decision makers have.
如果您在高级财务管理考试中被要求考虑可能影响投资者或经理决策的行为因素， 您需要非常仔细地阅读问题场
景。留意有关投资者或经理如何做出决策的信息，或者可能引发决策者偏见的情况中的因素。
You may not be able to come to a firm conclusion about what decision makers will do and why, but you should be
looking to discuss various possibilities. Bringing real life into your answer has to mean questioning the assumption that
all financia l decisions are taken rationally, and at least admitting that behavioural factors may influence decision
makers.
你可能无法就决策者会做什么以及为什么做得出一个明确的结论， 但你应该讨论各种可能性。 将现实生活带入你
的答案意味着质疑所有财务决策都是理性做出的假设，并且至少承认行为因素可能会影响决策者。
Written by a member of the examining team for Advanced Financial Management

stem_zh:
Reverse takeovers – an explanation
反向收购
Reverse takeovers – an explanation
An RTO involves a smaller quoted company taking over a larger unquoted company by a
share-for-share exchange. In order to acquire the larger unquoted company, a large number
of shares in the quoted company will have to be issued to the shareholders of the larger
unquoted company. Hence, after the takeover the current shareholders in the larger
unquoted company will hold the majority of the shares in the  quoted company and will
therefore have control of the quoted company.
反向收购(RTO)是指一家小型上市公司通过股份交换收购一家较大的未上市公司。为了收购
较大的未上市公司， 必须向较大的未上市公司的股东发行大量上市公司的股票。 因此， 在收
购之后， 较大的未上市公司的现有股东将持有上市公司的大部分股份， 并因此控制上市公司。
On completion of an RTO, it is usual for the quoted company to be managed by the senior
management team from the previously unquoted company and to take the name of the
previously unquoted company.  Through the RTO, the previously unquoted company has
effectively achieved a listing on the stock exchange. Eddie Stobart, a road haulage company
based in the UK, achieved a listing in this way in 2007 by combining with Westbury, a property
and logistics company.
完成反向收购后， 通常情况下， 由以前未上市公司的高级管理团队管理上市公司， 并采用以
前未上市公司的名称。
通过 RTO， 以前未上市公司实际上在股票交易所上市了。 英国的道路运输公司Eddie Stobart
在 2007 年与房地产和物流公司 Westbury 合并，借以此方式上市。
It is worth noting that in the USA, the term 'reverse merger' is often used as opposed to the
term reverse takeover.
As ever, there are many variations on the basic idea. For instance, an RTO may involve a
quoted company, which is actively trading, or a shell company, which is not actively trading.
值得注意的是，在美国，常用“反向合并”这一术语来代替“反向收购”这一术语。
在基本思想上有许多变化。例如，RTO 可能涉及正在积极交易的上市公司或未在积极交易
的空壳公司。
RTOs have often been deemed to be the poor man’s initial public offering (IPO) perhaps due
to US studies showing that companies achieving a listing through a reverse merger generally
have lower survival rates and underperform compared to companies who have achieved their
listing through a traditional IPO.
However, studies in the UK have shown that th is is not necessarily the case. Indeed, during
the period 1995 to 2012, RTOs seem to have survival rates similar to those for IPOs. The best
results seem to arise with RTOs, which involve a quoted company that is actively trading, as
the takeover is then able to benefit from synergy gains. Equally, small RTOs seem to perform
better than larger ones.
RTO 通常被认为是穷人的首次公开发行(IPO)的方法，可能是由于美国的研究表明，与通过
传统 IPO 实现上市的公司相比通过反向合并实现上市的公司通常具有较低的存活率并且表
现不佳。
但是，在英国进行的研究表明，情况并非总是如此。事实上，在 1995 年至 2012 年期间，
RTO 似乎具有与 IPO 相似的生存率。最好的结果似乎是由积极交易的上市公司参与的 RTO
带来的，因为这样的收购能够从协同效益中获益。同样，小型RTO 似乎比大型 RTO 表现更
好。
Reverse takeovers – the potential benefits
反向收购-潜在的好处
As previously stated, an RTO is effectively a way that a currently unquoted company can
achieve a listing. Hence, just as with an IPO, the compa ny obtains the benefits of the public
trading of its securities. These benefits include:
如前所述，RTO 实际上是一种目前未上市公司可以实现上市的方式。因此，与 IPO 一样，
公司获得了上市的好处。这些好处包括：
Easier access to capital markets
As a listed company, more finance is likely to be available and the cost of that finance is likely
to be lower than if the company was still unquoted.
更容易进入资本市场
作为上市公司，可能有更多的融资可用，并且这种融资的成本可能比公司仍未上市时要低。
Higher company valuation
As the shares in the company will be listed, potential investors will deem the shares to be less
risky as the company will have to abide by the relevant rules and regulations. Additionally,
they will know that the shares are liquid and that whenever they wish to sell there will be a
willing buyer. As a result of this, investors are likely to attribute a higher value to the shares.
更高的公司估值
一旦公司股票上市， 潜在投资者会认为该公司的股票风险较小， 因为该公司必须遵守相关规
则和法规。 此外， 他们将知道这些股票是流动的， 无论何时想要卖出都会有愿意购买的买家。
因此，投资者可能会给这些股票赋予更高的价值。
Enhanced ability to carry out further takeovers
Once the shares in a company are listed, the company is able t o acquire other companies
through further share-for-share exchanges.
增强收购能力
一旦公司股票上市，该公司可以通过进一步的股份交换收购其他公司。
Enhanced ability to use share based incentive plans
Once the shares of a company are listed, share based incentive plans can be used as a key
tool to attract and retain good quality employees.
增强使用股票激励计划的能力
一旦公司股票上市，股票激励计划可以成为吸引和留住高质量员工的关键工具。
In addition to the above, an RTO has a number of other potential benefits when compared
to a normal IPO. These include the following:
除以上优势外，相比于普通 IPO，RTO 还有其他一些潜在的好处，包括：
Speed
An IPO can often take between one and two years to complete whereas an RTO can be
completed in as little as 30 days. Furthermore, the work required to complete an IPO can
mean that the managers of a company have less time to run the company, which may prove
detrimental to the growth prospects of the company. The variability of market conditions can
also make the speed of an RTO attractive, as in the time taken to prepare for an IPO, the
market may deteriorate such that the IPO is not f inally worth completing. Furthermore,
particular circumstances in a market may make RTOs attractive. For instance, in China the IPO
process is notoriously slow and there is usually a significant queue of companies waiting to
carry out an IPO. An RTO allows a company to jump this queue.
速度
IPO 通常需要一到两年才能完成，而RTO 可以在 30 天内完成。此外，完成IPO 所需的工作
可能会导致公司管理人员时间不足， 这可能对公司的增长前景不利。 市场状况的变化性也可
能使 RTO 的速度具有吸引力，因为在准备 IPO 的时间里，市场可能会恶化，导致最终无法
完成 IPO。此外，市场中的特定情况可能使RTO 具有吸引力。例如，在中国，IPO 流程非常
缓慢，通常有大量公司排队等待进行 IPO。RTO 使公司可以跳过这个队列。
Cost
Just as an IPO is a time-consuming process, it is also an expensive one due to the volume of
work required by investment banks, sponsors, accountants and other advisers. An RTO will
usually, but not always, cost less.
成本
IPO 不仅是一个耗时的过程，也是一个昂贵的过程，因为投资银行、保荐人、会计师和其他
顾问所需的工作量很大。通常情况下，RTO 的成本要低于 IPO，但并非总是如此。
Availability
In a market downturn it is not easy to convince investors to support an IPO, whereas this does
not seem to be the case with RTOs. Studies have shown that the volume of RTO transactions
is far more resilient to market downturns. During the market correction that followed the
bursting of the dotcom bubble, the number of RTOs actually increased while the number of
IPOs fell very significantly.
Similarly, the fall in the number of RTOs was less than the fall in the number of IPOs following
the more recent financial crisis. This is probably because, with an RTO, the deal is
fundamentally between the shareholders of the quoted and unquoted companies involved
and, hence, market sentiment has much less import.
Furthermore, while an RTO is often accompanied by a concurrent secondary offering to raise
new finance, the amount of new finance being raised in both $ and % terms is usually less than
that which is raised during an IPO. Hence, even in a downturn, investors are often more willing
to support an RTO rather than an IPO.
可用性
在市场下滑期间， 很难说服投资者支持IPO， 而这似乎不适用于RTO。 研究表明，RTO 交易
的数量在市场下跌时更具弹性。在随后的互联网泡沫破裂引发的市场调整期间，RTO 的数
量实际上增加，而 IPO 的数量显著下降。同样，随着最近的金融危机，RTO 的数量下降的
程度比 IPO 要小。这可能是因为 RTO 的交易基本上是在涉及上市和未上市公司的股东之间
进行的，因此市场情绪的影响要小得多。 此外，尽管RTO 通常伴随着同时进行的二次发行
以筹集新资金， 但所筹集的新资金在美元和百分比方面通常少于IPO 筹集的资金。 因此， 即
使在经济下行期，投资者通常也更愿意支持 RTO 而不是 IPO。
Existing analyst coverage
A listed company subject to an RTO is likely to have existing analyst coverage and, after the
RTO, this analyst coverage usually continu es. However, companies that use an IPO may
struggle to get significant analyst coverage especially if they are smaller. Without reasonable
analyst coverage, potential investors may not have much awareness of the company and,
hence, are unlikely to want to invest in the company.
现有的分析师覆盖范围
受 RTO 约束的上市公司很可能已经被分析师覆盖， 并且在RTO 之后， 这种分析师覆盖通常
会继续。 但是， 使用IPO 的公司可能很难获得重要的分析师覆盖， 尤其是如果它们较小。 如
果没有合理的分析师覆盖， 潜在投资者可能不会对公司有太多的了解， 因此不太可能投资该
公司。
Reverse takeovers – the potential drawbacks
反向收购 - 潜在的缺点
RTOs do, however, have a number of potential drawbacks when compared to an IPO and any
company considering an RTO should be aware of these.
然而，与 IPO 相比，RTO 也存在一些潜在的缺点，任何考虑进行RTO 的公司都应该意识到
这些缺点。
Lack of expertise
A company achieving a listing through an RTO may  find that it does not have the expertise
to understand and deal with all the regulations and procedures that listed companies must
comply with. The long process of listing through an IPO can be viewed as a valuable training
period and any company that has  been through the process is in a better position to deal
with the requirements of the exchange than a company catapulted onto the market through
an RTO. Hence, any company considering an RTO must consider the need to hire and/or
retain staff from the exis ting listed company who are able to keep the company compliant
with all the relevant regulations.
缺乏专业知识
通过 RTO 获得上市地位的公司可能会发现，他们没有足够的专业知识来理解和应对上市公
司必须遵守的所有法规和程序。通过 IPO 的长期上市过程可以被视为有价值的培训期，任
何经历过这个过程的公司都比通过 RTO 进入市场的公司更能够应对交易所的要求。因此，
任何考虑进行 RTO 的公司都必须考虑雇用和/或留住来自现有上市公司的员工， 这些员工能
够让公司遵守所有相关法规。
Reputation
As previously discussed, an RTO has often been viewed as a poor man’s IPO. Hence,
companies that achieve their listing in this way may be viewed less favourably by investors
than companies that have completed an IPO. To  some extent, the reasons for this lie in the
recent past.
In 2011 and 2012, there were a number of accounting scandals involving Chinese firms that
gained access to the US markets through RTOs. Indeed, over 100 companies were suspended
or delisted as a result. A public bulletin issued by the US Securities and Exchange Commission
in June 2011 warned investors by stating that ‘many companies either fail or struggle to
remain viable following a reverse merger’ and that there have been ‘instances of fraud and
other abuses involving reverse merger companies’.
Since that time, the regulations and standards that apply to reverse mergers / RTOs on the
US stock exchanges and other exchanges around the word have been tightened up in order
to prevent similar problems arising in the future.
声誉
如前所述，RTO 通常被视为较差的 IPO 替代方案。因此，通过RTO 实现上市的公司可能会
比完成 IPO 的公司更不受投资者青睐。在某种程度上，这种情况的原因在于最近的一些事
件。 在 2011 年和 2012 年，有一些通过RTO 进入美国市场的中国公司涉及会计丑闻。事实
上，超过 100 家公司因此被暂停或摘牌。美国证券交易委员会在 2011 年 6 月发布的一份公
开通知中警告投资者，称“许多公司通过反向合并要么失败，要么难以保持生存”，并且“存
在涉及反向合并公司的欺诈和其他滥用行为”。 自那时起， 适用于在美国证券交易所和其他
全球交易所上市的反向合并/ RTO 的规定和标准已得到加强，以防止类似的问题再次出现。
Risk
As a result of the lower level of scrutiny that is applied to an RTO compared to an IPO,
investors must be aware of the higher level of risk that is attached to companies achieving a
listing in this way. In particular, the unquoted company carrying out an RTO must ensure that
there is a thorough investigation of the listed company which they are taking over so that all
potential problems and liabilities are revealed.
风险
由于相较于 IPO，RTO 的审核程度较低， 投资者必须意识到以这种方式上市的公司所面临的
更高风险水平。特别是，进行 RTO 的非上市公司必须确保对其收购的上市公司进行彻底的
调查，以揭示所有可能存在的问题和责任。
Regulation
Although RTOs can generally be completed more quickly than an IPO as there is less
regulation and scrutiny involved, it must be recognised that there are still a significant amount
of regulatory hurdles to overcome. It should be understood that RTOs are, to some extent,
combinations of acquisitions and IPOs and, as such, are potentially complex and difficult deals
to manage. By way of example, two regulatory issues that may arise are now discussed:
虽然相比 IPO，RTO 的监管和审查要少一些，但必须认识到，仍然需要克服许多监管障碍。
需要了解的是，RTO 在某种程度上是并购和 IPO 的组合，因此是可能复杂和难以管理的交
易。以下是可能出现的两个监管问题：
• Suspension
The Financial Conduct Authority’s (FCA) standard view is that when an RTO is announced or
leaked, there w ill generally be insufficient information publicly available on the proposed
transaction. In particular, information on the unquoted company contemplating the takeover
could well be limited compared to the information that is available on listed companies. As a
result of this, the listed company will not be able to accurately assess its financial position and
inform the market. Hence, the FCA will often consider that a suspension of trading in the
shares is appropriate. This standard view can be rebutted, b ut there is significant work
required to achieve this. However, this work is essential as the listed company will not want
to contemplate a scenario where its listing is suspended and is quite likely to walk away from
the proposed transaction were this to occur.
• 暂停交易
英国金融市场行为监管局（FCA）的标准观点是，当公布或泄露 RTO 时，公众可获得的关
于拟议交易的信息通常不足。 特别是， 与上市公司可获得的信息相比， 关于考虑收购的未上
市公司的信息可能会受到限制。 因此， 上市公司将无法准确评估自己的财务状况并告知市场。
因此，FCA 通常认为暂停股票交易是适当的。 虽然可以反驳这种标准观点， 但需要大量工作
才能实现。然而，这项工作是必要的，因为上市公司不会愿意考虑其上市被暂停的情况，如
果发生这种情况，它很可能会放弃拟议的交易。
• Mandatory offer
If, individually or with their closely connected persons or friends, any shareholder in the
unquoted company carrying out an RTO will on completion of the transaction hold shares
that carry 30% or more of the voting rights of the listed com pany, then that shareholder will
be required to make a general cash offer for the remaining shares in the listed company under
the mandatory bid rule. This would obviously undermine the reason for doing the RTO in the
first place. While the takeover panel will usually consent to a waiver of this requirement as
long as certain conditions are satisfied, it is another regulatory obstacle which must be
navigated around carefully.
• 强制要约
如果未上市公司进行 RTO 后，其股东（包括其密切相关人或朋友）在完成交易时持有的表
决权股份占上市公司的 30%或更多，则该股东将根据强制要约规则被要求对上市公司的剩
余股份进行普通现金要约。这显然会破坏进行 RTO 的初衷。虽然收购委员会通常会同意豁
免此要求，只要满足某些条件，但这是必须小心地绕过的另一个监管障碍。
Share price decrease
Many listed companies which could make potential RTO targets are in that position because
of past problems. Hence, they may have shareholders who are keen to exit from the company
as soon as a suitable opportunity arises and, hence, they may ‘dump’ their shares shortly after
the RTO has completed. To safeguard against the risk of a ‘dump’ occurring, the shareholders
may need to guarantee that they will not sell their shares until a certain period of time has
elapsed since the deal is completed. This is called a lock-up and/or a lock-up period.
股价下跌
许多可能成为反向收购目标的上市公司之所以处于这种位置，是因为过去存在问题。因此，
它们可能有一些股东渴望在合适的机会出现后退出公司，因此他们可能会在 RTO 完成后不
久就“抛售”其股份。为了防范“抛售”的风险，股东可能需要保证在交易完成一定时间之前不
会出售其股份。这被称为锁定和/或锁定期。
Cost
While a reverse takeover is usually cheaper than an IPO, there are still significant direct and
indirect costs involved and, hence, the total cost can easily be far more than was originally
anticipated. A number of these costs are now considered:
尽管逆向收购通常比 IPO 更便宜，但仍涉及重大的直接和间接成本，因此总成本很容易超
出最初预期。现在考虑其中的一些成本：
• Regulatory costs
As mentioned previously, an RTO is a complex transaction and to ensure that the regulatory
hurdles are successfully overcome will incur significant cost.
• 监管成本 如前所述，RTO 是一项复杂的交易，为确保成功克服监管障碍，将产生重大成
本。
• Acquisition cost
As a result of an RTO being seen as an easier and quicker option than an IPO, especially in
the Chinese market, the value of potential listed company targets are often at a significa nt
premium to their true value. Furthermore, the pressure to find a target has resulted in some
unusual combinations such as a mobile computer game developer getting listed through the
acquisition of a shoe company! It is hard to imagine there were any syn ergy gains available
here and, indeed, resolving cultural and other issues that may well have arisen would have
further added to the indirect cost of achieving the listing.
• 收购成本 由于 RTO 被视为比 IPO 更容易、更快捷的选择，尤其是在中国市场，潜在的上
市公司目标价值往往比其真实价值高得多。 此外， 找到一个目标的压力导致了一些不寻常的
组合， 例如通过收购一个鞋业公司， 一个移动电脑游戏开发商获得了上市。 很难想象在这里
有任何协同效益，事实上，解决可能出现的文化和其他问题还会进一步增加间接成本。
• Investor relations
Although an RTO may benefit from existing analyst coverage, RTO transactions only really
introduce liquidity to a previously private company if there is r eal investor interest in the
company. In many cases, in order to generate this interest, a comprehensive investor relations
and investor marketing programme will be required. This is another potential indirect cost of
an RTO.
• 投资者关系 虽然 RTO 可能受益于现有的分析师覆盖，但仅当投资者对公司真正感兴趣
时，RTO 交易才会真正为此前的私人公司引入流动性。 在许多情况下， 为了产生这种兴趣，
将需要一个全面的投资者关系和投资者营销计划。这是 RTO 的另一个潜在间接成本。
Conclusion
As with anything that seems too good to be true, it must be recognised that an RTO is not
without significant complication and cost. Just as there is no such thing as a free lunch, there
is also no easy way to achieve a listing.
就像任何看起来过于美好的事情一样，必须认识到 RTO 不是没有重大的复杂性和成本的。
就像没有免费午餐一样，也没有轻松的方式来实现上市。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:AFM 考官文章解析 Reverse takeovers.pdf

stem_zh:
RISK MANAGEMENT
风险管理
The management of risk is a key area within a number of ACCA exams, and exam questions related to this area are
common. It is vital that students are able to apply risk management techniques, such as using derivative instruments to
hedge against risk, and offer advice and recommendations as required by the scenario in the question. It is also equally
important that students understand why corporations manage risk in theory and in practice, because risk management
costs money but does it actually add more value to a corporation? This article explores the circumstances where the
management of risk may lead to an increase in the value of a corporation.
风险管理是 ACCA 多门考试中的关键领域，与此领域相关的试题是很常见的。学生必须能够应用风险管理技术，
如使用衍生工具来对冲风险， 并根据案例中的问题按要求提供建议。此外，学员们要理解为什么企业会在理论和
实践中去管理风险。风险管理需要花钱， 但它真的为公司增加了更多的价值吗？本文探讨了风险管理可能如何为
公司增加价值。
Risk, in this context, refers to the volatility of returns (both positive and negative) that can be quantified through statistical
measures such as probabilities, standard deviations and correlations between different returns. Its management is about
decisions made to change the volatility of returns a corporation is exposed to, for example changing a company’s exposure
to floating interest rates by swapping them to fixed rates for a fee. Since business is about generating higher returns by
undertaking risky projects, important management decisions revolve around which projects to undertake, how they
should be financed and whether the volatility of a project’s returns (its risk) should be managed.
本文中，风险指的是回报的波动性（包括正的和负的），可以通过诸如概率、标准差和不同回报之间的相关性等
统计指标来量化。 它的管理是与改变公司回报的波动性相关的， 例如， 通过将浮动利率转换为固定利率来改变公
司对浮动利率的敞口。企业是通过承担有风险的项目来获得更高的回报得，因此选择哪些项目，项目应该如何融
资，以及是否应该管理项目回报的波动性（其风险）都是重要的管理决策。
The volatility of returns of a project should be managed if it results in increasing the value to a corporation. Given that
the market value of a corporation is the net present value (NPV) of its future cash flows discounted by the return required
by its investors, then higher market value can either be generated by increasing the future cash flows or by reducing
investors’ required rate of return (or both). A risk management strategy that increases the NPV at a lower comparative
cost would benefit the corporation.
如果项目会使得公司的价值增加， 就应该管理项目回报的波动。考虑到公司的市场价值是其未来现金流按照投资
者所需的回报进行折现的现值（NPV），那么更高的市场价值可以通过增加未来现金流或减少投资者所需的回报
率（或二者同时满足）。一种以较低的比较成本增加净现值的风险管理策略将使公司受益。
The return required by investors is the sum of the risk free rate and a premium for the risk they undertake. If investors
hold well-diversified portfolios of investments then they are only exposed to systematic risk as their exposure to firm -
specific risk has been diversified away. Therefore, the risk premium of their required return is based on the capital asset
pricing model (CAPM). Research suggests companies with diverse equity holdings do not increase value by diversifying
company specific risk, as their equity holders have already achieved this level of risk diversification. Moreover, risk
management activity designed to transfer systematic risk would not provide additional benefits to a corporation because,
in perfect markets, the benefits achieved from risk management activity would at most equal the costs of undertaking
such activity. Therefore, in a situation of perfect markets, it may be argued that risk management activity is at best neutral
or at worst detrimental because costs would either equal or be more than the benefits accrued.
投资者所要求的回报是无风险利率和他们所承担的风险溢价的总和。 如果投资者持有非常多样化的投资组合， 那
么他们只会面临系统风险， 因为跟特定公司相关的风险的风险已经被分散。 因此， 其所需回报的风险溢价是基于
资本资产定价模型（CAPM）的。研究表明，持有不同股权的公司不会通过分散公司特定风险来增加价值，因为
他们的股东已经实现了风险多元化。 此外， 旨在转移系统风险的风险管理活动不会给公司带来额外的好处， 因为
在完美的市场中， 风险管理活动所获得的好处最多等于承担这种活动的成本。 因此， 在完美市场的情况下人们可
能会认为风险管理活动是中性或有害的，因为其成本会等于或大于其收益。
Such an argument would not apply to smaller companies which have concentrated, non -diversified equity holdings. In
this case the equity holders, because they are exposed to both specific and systematic risk, would benefit from risk
diversification by the company. Therefore, whereas larger companies may not create value from risk management activity,
smaller companies can and should undertake risk management. However, empirical research studies have found that risk
management is undertaken mostly by larger companies with diverse equity holdings and not by the smaller companies.
The accepted reason for this is that the costs related to risk management are large and mostly fixed. Small companies
simply cannot afford these costs nor can they benefit from the economies of scale that large companies can.
这种论点不适用于那些集中的、非多元化持股的小公司。在这种情况下，由于股东面临特定的（非系统）和系统
的风险，他们将受益于公司的风险多样化。 因此， 虽然较大的公司可能不能从风险管理活动中创造价值， 但较小
的公司可以而且应该进行风险管理。 然而， 实证研究发现， 风险管理主要由持有不同股权的大公司进行， 而不是
由较小的公司进行。 公认的原因是， 与风险管理相关的成本很大， 而且大多成本是固定的。 小公司根本负担不起
这些成本，也不能像大公司那样从规模经济中获益。
In addition to the ability of larger companies to undertake risk management , market imperfections may provide the
motivation for them to do so. Market imperfections that exist in the real world, as opposed to the perfect world conditions
assumed by finance or economic theory, may provide opportunities to reduce volatility in cash flows and thereby reduce
the costs imposed on a corporation. The following discussion considers the circumstances which may result in providing
such opportunities.
除了大公司进行风险管理的能力外， 市场不完美可能提供他们这样做的动机。 与金融或经济理论所假定的完美世
界条件相反， 现实世界中存在的市场不完美， 可能会提供机会来减少现金流的波动性， 从而降低公司的成本。 下
面的讨论考虑了可能产生这种机会的情况。
Taxation
税
Risk management may help in reducing the amount of tax that a corporation pays by reducing the volatility of the
corporation’s earnings. Where a corporation faces taxation schedules that are progressive (that is the corporation pays
proportionally higher amounts of tax as its profits increase), by reducing the variability of that corporation’s earnings and
thereby staying in the same low tax bracket will reduce the tax payable.
风险管理可以通过减少公司收益的波动性来帮助减少公司所缴纳的税款。 当公司面临累进的纳税计划 （即公司随
着利润的增加而按比例支付更高的税款） 时， 通过减少公司收入的可变性， 从而保持在相同的低纳税等级， 将减
少应缴税款。
According to academics, corporations could often find themselves in situations where they face progressive tax functions,
for example, when they have previous losses which are not written off or, in the case of multinational corporations, due
to the taxation treaties which exist between different countries. The amount of taxation that can be saved depends upon
the corporation’s individual circumstances.
根据学者们的说法，公司经常用到累进税的一些功能（可以节约一些税款），例如，由于他们以前的损失没有被
结转或跨国交易时由于不同国家之间存在税收条约。可以节省的税额取决于公司的具体情况。
Insolvency and financial distress
破产和财务困境
A corporation may find itself in a situation of being insolvent when it cannot meet its financial obligations as they fall due.
Financial distress is a situation that is less severe than insolvency in that a corporation can operate on a day-to-day basis,
but it finds that these operations are difficult to conduct because the parties dealing with it are concerned that it may
become insolvent in the future. When facing financial distress a corporation will incur additional costs, both direct and
indirect, due to the situation it is facing.
当一个公司无法按期偿还其财务债务时， 它可能会发现自己处于将破产的状态。 财务困境是一种没有破产那么严
重的情况，它意味着一个公司可以日常经营， 但由于各方担心它在未来可能会破产使得其日常经营很难进行。 当
企业面临财务困境时，它将由于其所面临的情况而产生直接和间接的额外成本。
The main indirect costs of financial distress relate to the higher costs of contracting with the corporation’s stakeholders,
such as customers, employees and suppliers. For example, customers may demand better warranty schemes or may be
reluctant to buy a product due to concerns about the corporation’s ability to fulfil its warranty; employees may demand
higher salaries; senior management may ask for golden hellos before agreeing to work for the corporation; and suppliers
may be unwilling to offer favourable credit terms.
财务困境的主要间接成本与与公司的利益相关者（如客户、员工和供应商）以更高的成本签订合同相关。例如，
客户可能要求更好的保修计划， 或者可能因为担心公司履行保修的能力而不愿购买产品； 员工可能要求更高的工
资；高级管理层在同意为公司工作之前可能会要求补偿金；供应商可能不愿意提供有利于公司的信用条款。
Academics exploring this area postulate that because stakeholders are subject to the corporation’s full risk, as opposed
to only systematic risk, which is faced by the corporation’s equity holders, the stakeholders would demand greater
compensation for their participation. Where an organisation actively manages its risk and prevents  (or reduces the
possibility of) situations of financial distress, it will find it easier to contract with its stakeholders and at a lower cos t.
Hence, the more volatile the cash flows of a corporation, the more likely the need to manage its risk in order to reduce
the costs related to financial distress.
研究这一领域的学者提出， 由于利益相关者受到公司的全部风险的影响， 而不是公司的股东所面临的系统性风险，
利益相关者将为他们的参与要求更多的补偿。当一个组织积极管理其风险并防止（或减少）财务困境的情况时，
它会发现更容易与利益相关者签订合同且相关成本也更低。 因此， 一个公司的现金流越不稳定， 就越有可能需要
管理其风险，以减少与财务困境相关的成本。
External funding and agency costs
外部资金和代理成本
Another consequence of financial distress is the impact this may have on the corporation’s ability to undertake profitable
future investment. Financial distress may make the cost of external debt and equity funding so expensive that a
corporation and its management may be forced reject profitable projects. Academics refer to this as the under investment
problem.
财务困境的另一个后果是， 这可能会对公司未来进行盈利性投资的能力产生影响。 财务困境可能会使债权和股权
融资的成本变得很昂贵，以至于公司及其管理层可能被迫拒绝盈利项目。学者们称这是投资不足问题。
Equity holders in effect hold a call option on a corporation’s assets and debt holders can be considered to have written
the option. In cases of low financial distress the company may be considered to be similar to an at-the-money option for
its equity holders, and, therefore, they would be more willing to undertake risky projects as they would benefit from any
increase in profitability, but the impact of any loss is limited. In the case of substantial financial distress, the option could
be considered to be well out-of-money. In this situation there is little (or no) benefit to equity holders of undertaking new
projects, as the benefits of these will pass to the debt holders initially. However, debt holders would be reluctant to lend
to a severely distressed company in any case.
股东实际上持有公司资产的看涨期权， 债权人可以被视为已写下了期权。 在低财务困境的情况下， 公司可能被认
为是类似于其股东的实值期权， 因此， 他们会更愿意承担有风险的项目， 因为他们会盈利能力的增加中获益，但
损失的影响是有限的。 在出现重大财务困境的情况下， 可以认为期权是虚值期权。 在这种情况下， 从事新项目对
股权持有人的好处很少（或没有），因为这些项目的好处最初会转移给债权人。然而，无论如何，债权人都不愿
向严重陷入困境的公司放贷。
Therefore, when raising debt capital, a corpo ration that is subject to low levels of financial distress would face higher
agency costs, with lenders imposing higher borrowing costs and more restrictive covenants. Whereas debt holders get a
fixed return on their investment, any additional benefit due to higher profits would go to the equity holders. This would
make the debt holders reluctant to allow the corporation to undertake risky projects or to lend more finance to the
corporation because they would not gain any benefit from the risky projects.
因此， 在筹集债务资本时， 处于低水平财务困境的公司将面临更高的代理成本， 贷款机构会施加更高的借贷成本
和更严格的契约。债权人的投资获得固定回报， 任何更高利润带来的额外收益都将归股东。 这将使债务持有人不
愿公司做高风险的项目或不愿向公司提供更多的资金，因为他们不会从高风险项目中获得任何好处。
A corporation that faces high levels of financial distress would find it difficult to raise equity capital in order to undertake
new investments. If corporations try to raise equity finance for relatively less risky projects then the profits earned from
