such projects would initially go to the debt holders and the equity holders will gain only residual profits. Therefore equity
holders would put pressure on the corporation and its management to reject good, low risk projects, which may have
been acceptable to the bondholders.
一个面临严重财务困境的公司将会发现， 很难筹集到股权资本来进行新的投资。 如果公司试图为风险相对较低的
项目进行股权融资， 那么从这些项目中获得的利润最初将流向债权人， 而股权持有者将只能获得剩余利润。 因此，
股权人将给公司及其管理层施加压力，要求其拒绝债权人可能愿意接受的良好的、低风险的项目。
Therefore, risk management in reducing financial distress by reducing the volatility of the corporation’s cash inflows may
help the management to obtain an optimal mix of debt and equity, and to undertake profitable projects.
因此，风险管理通过减少公司现金流入的波动性来减少财务困境，可以帮助管理层获得最佳的债务和股权组合，
并投资盈利的项目。
Capital structure and information asymmetry
资本结构与信息的不对称性
Risk management can help a corporation obtain an optimal capital structure of debt and equity to maximise i ts value.
Since risk management stabilises the variability of cash inflows, this would enable a corporation to take more debt finance
in its capital structure. Stable cash flows indicate less risk and therefore debt holders would become more willing to lend
to the corporation. Since debt is cheaper to finance than equity because of lower required rates of return and the tax
shield, taking on more debt should increase the value of the corporation. Risk management can help achieve this.
风险管理可以帮助公司获得最优的债权和股权资本结构， 从而使其价值最大化。 由于风险管理稳定了现金流入的
变化性， 这将使一个公司能够在其资本结构中获得更多的债务融资。 稳定的现金流表明风险更小， 因此债权人将
更愿意借钱给公司。 由于债务融资比股权融资更便宜 （因为债权人要求回报率更低且利息有抵税的作用）， 债务
融资占比增加会增加公司的价值。风险管理有助于实现这一目标。
Academics have observed that managers would prefer to use internally generated funds rather than going to the external
markets for funds because it is cheaper and less intrusive on the corporation. They suggest that borrowing money from
the external markets, whether equity or debt, would involve parties who do not have the complete information about
the corporation. This information asymmetry would make the external sources of funds more expensive. If risk
management stabilises the cash flows that the corporation receives from year to year, then this would enable managers
to plan when the necessary internal funds will become available for future investments with greater accuracy. They will
then be able to align their investment policies with the availability of funding.
学者们观察到， 管理者更愿意使用内部来源的资金而不是去外部市场上融资， 因为它更便宜且对公司的干扰也更
小。 他们认为从外部市场融资， 无论是通过股票还是债务， 都将涉及到那些不掌握公司完整信息的当事人。 这种
信息不对称将使外部来源的资金更加昂贵。 如果风险管理稳定了公司每年获得的现金流， 那么这将使管理人员能
够更准确地规划未来投资提供必要的内部资金。然后，他们将能使得其投资政策与可用资金相匹配。
Manager behaviour towards risk management
经理对风险管理的行为
In his seminal paper, Rene Stulz suggests that managers, whose performance reward structure includes large equity stakes
in a corporation, are more likely to reduce the corporation’s risk, as opposed to managers whose performance reward
structure is based primarily on equity options. Managers who hold concentrated equity stakes in a corporation face
increased levels of risk when compared to other equity holders. As discussed previously, investors hold well -diversified
portfolios and face exposure to systematic risk only. But managers with concentrated equity stakes would face both
systematic and unsystematic risk. Therefore, they have a greater propensity to reduce the unsystematic risk.
Rene Stulz 在他的开创性论文中指出，与绩效回报结构主要基于股票期权的经理相比，绩效回报结构中包括持有
一家公司大量股票的经理更有可能降低公司的风险。 与其他股东相比， 持有公司集中股权的经理面临更高的风险。
如前所述， 持有良好的多元化的投资组合的投资者只面临系统性风险。 但持有集中股权的经理人将同时面临系统
性和非系统性风险。因此，他们更倾向于降低非系统风险。
However, if investors do not reward corporations that are reducing unsystematic risk, because they have diversified this
risk away themselves. And if a corporation’s managers use the corporation’s resources to reduce unsystematic risk,
thereby reducing the corporation’s value. Then it is worth exploring under what circumstances would equity investors
allow managers to act to reduce unsystematic risk and whether such actions could actually result in the value of the
corporation increasing.
然而， 如果投资者因为他们自己已经将这种风险多样化了就不奖励那些降低非系统风险的公司。 如果一个公司的
经理利用公司的资源来减少非系统的风险，因而降低了公司的价值。那么，值得探索的是，在什么情况下，股权
人会允许管理者采取行动来降低非系统的风险，以及这些行动是否真的会导致公司的价值增加。
Stulz argues that encouraging managers to hold concentrated equity positions but allowing them to reduce unsystematic
risk at the same time, may enable them to act in the best interests of the corporation and the result may be an increase
in the corporate value. He explains that managers, who do not have to worry about risks that are not under their control
(because they have hedged them away), would be able to focus their time, expertise and experience on the strategies
and operations that they can control. This focus may result in the increase in the value of the corporation, although the
impact of this increase in value is not easily measurable or directly attributable to risk management activity.
Stulz 认为， 鼓励经理持有集中的股票头寸，且同时允许他们减少非系统的风险， 可能使他们能够按照公司的最佳
利益行事，最终使得公司价值增加。 他解释说， 管理者不必担心不受他们控制的风险 （因为他们已经对冲了这些
风险） ， 他们将能够将他们的时间、 专业知识和经验集中在他们可以控制的战略和操作上。 这可能使得公司价值
的增加，尽管这种价值增加的影响可能不易衡量或不易直接归因于风险管理活动。
As an aside, one could pose the question, why don’t managers, who are rewarded by equity, diversify the risk of
concentrated equity investments themselves? They could sell equity in their own corporation and replac e it by buying
equity in other corporations. In this way they do not have to hold concentrated equity positions and then would be like
the normal equity holders facing only systematic risk. A research study on wealth management, which looked at
concentrated equity positions and risk management, found that senior managers are reluctant to reduce their
concentrated equity positions because any attempt to sell the equity would send negative signals to the markets, and
cause their corporation’s value to decrease unnecessarily.
顺便说一句， 有人可能会提出这样一个问题： 为什么获得股票回报的经理们不把集中股票投资的风险分散化？他
们可以出售自己公司的股权， 并通过购买其他公司的股权来替代它。 这样一来， 他们就不必持有集中的股票头寸，
然后就会像那些只面临系统风险的普通股东一样。 一项关于财富管理的研究着眼于集中股票头寸和风险管理， 发
现高级经理不愿减少集中股票头寸， 因为任何出售股票的尝试都会向市场发出负面信号， 导致公司价值不必要地
下降。
Contrary to the behaviour of managers who hold concentrated equity stakes, managers who own equity options, which
will be converted into equity at a future date, will actively seek to increase the risk of a corporation rather than reduce it.
Managers who hold equity options are interested in maximising the future price of the equity. Therefore in order to
maximise future profits and the price of the equity, they will be more inclined to undertake risky projects (and less inclined
to manage risk). Equity options, as a form of reward, have been often criticised because they do not necessarily make
managers behave in the best interests of the corporation or its equity investors, but encourage them to act in an overly
risky manner.
与持有集中股权的经理的行为相反， 持有股票期权的经理将积极寻求增加而不是降低公司的风险， 而股票期权将
在未来一天转换为股权。 持有股票期权的经理对最大化股票的未来价格感兴趣。 因此， 为了使未来的利润和股权
的价格最大化，他们将更倾向于承担有风险的项目（而更不倾向于管理风险）。股票期权作为一种奖励形式，经
常受到批评， 因为它们不一定使经理人的行为符合公司或其股票投资者的最佳利益， 而是鼓励他们以过度冒险的
方式行事。
A number of empirical studies looking at manager behaviour support the above discussion (see for example Tufano’s
study published in 1996 in the Journal of Finance).
一些关于管理者行为的实证研究支持上述讨论（例如 Tufano1996 年发表在《金融杂志》上的研究）。
Testing the impact of risk management
测试风险管理的影响
In addition to the above, empirical research studies have looked at the risk management policies and actions pursued by
corporations and their impact on corporate value. Although the studies have provided varying results when studying each
area of market imperfections and their impact, the overarching conclusion from these studies is that: corporations
manage their risks in the belief that this would create or increase corporate value, although a direct link between risk
management and a corresponding increase in corporate value has not been established.
除上述内容之外， 实证研究还考察了企业所采取的风险管理政策和行动及其对企业价值的影响。 虽然这些研究在
研究市场缺陷的每个领域及其影响时提供了不同的结果， 但这些研究的总体结论是： 尽管风险管理和相应的企业
价值增长之间的直接联系尚未建立，但企业在管理风险时相信这将创造或增加企业价值。
Hence the belief held among managers is that the management of risk does create value, and certainly corporations and
their senior managers seem to believe and act in a manner th at it does. However, the jury is still out on whether risk
management actually does lead to increased corporate value. There seem to be strong theoretical reasons for managing
risk, but empirical research has not proven the impact of risk management activity on corporate value.
管理者认为风险管理确实创造了价值，且公司和他们的高级管理者似乎相信并以这样的方式行事。 然而，针对风
险管理是否真的会使得企业价值提高这一问题， 目前尚没有定论。 风险管理似乎有强有力的理论理由， 但实证研
究尚未证明风险管理活动对企业价值的影响。
Written by a member of the examining team for Advanced Financial Management
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:AFM 考官文章解析 Risk management.pdf

stem_zh:
Securitisation and tranching
资产证券化和分级
Securitisation became a topical issue during the credit crunch, and still remains a relevant issue for
financial management and Advanced Financial Management students.
证券化在信贷紧缩期间成为一个热门问题， 对财务管理和高级财务管理学生来说仍然是一个
相关问题。
Securitisation through Collateralised Debt Obligations (CDOs)
通过抵押贷款进行证券化
One common use of securitisation occurs when banks lend through mortgages, credit cards, car
loans or other forms of credit, they invariably move to ‘lay off’ thei r risk by a process of
securitisation. Such loans are an asset on the statement of financial position, representing cash
flow to the bank in future years through interest payments and eventual repayment of the principal
sum involved. By securitising the lo ans, the bank removes the risk attached to its future cash
receipts and converts the loan back into cash, which it can lend again, and so on, in an expanding
cycle of credit formation.
证券化的一个常见用途是， 当银行通过抵押贷款、 信用卡、 汽车贷款或其他形式进行贷款时，
它们总是通过证券化的过程来“降低”风险。这些贷款是（银行） 资产负债表上的一项资产，
代表了银行的现金流入， 通过未来几年的利息支付和本金偿还。 通过将贷款证券化， 银行消
除了其未来的现金收款所带来的风险， 并将贷款转换回现金， 在不断扩大的信贷周期中可以
再次进行贷款。
Securitisation is achieved by transferring the lending to specifically created companies called
‘special purpose vehicles’ (SPVs). In the case of conve ntional mortgages, the SPV effectively
purchases a bank’s mortgage book for cash, which is raised through the issue of bonds backed by
the income stream flowing from the mortgage holder. In the case of sub-prime mortgages, the high
levels of risk called for a different type of securitisation, achieved by the creation of derivative-style
instruments known as ‘collateralised debt obligations’ or CDOs.
证券化是通过将贷款转移给专门创建的公司，即“特殊目的载体”（（SPV） ，来实现的。在传统
抵押贷款的情况下， 特殊目的公司有效地用现金购买了银行的抵押贷款账目， 通过发行由贷
款持有人的收入为支持的债券来筹集现金。 在次级抵押贷款的情况下， 高水平的风险需要不
同类型的证券化，通过创建“债务抵押债券”（ CDO）的衍生工具来实现。
Securitisation may be also appropriate for an organisation which wants to enhance its credit rating
by using low -risk cash flows, such as rental income from commercial property, which will  be
diverted into a "ring-fenced" SPV.
证券化也可能适用于希望通过使用低风险现金流 （如商业地产的租金收入） 来提高其信用评
级的组织，这些现金流将被转移到“围栏式”特殊目的公司。
CDOs are a way of repackaging the risk of a large number of risky assets such as sub -prime
mortgages. Unlike a bond issue, where the risk is spread thinly between all the bond holders, CDOs
concentrate the risk into investment layers or ‘tranches’, so that some investors take
proportionately more of the risk for a bigger return and others take little or no risk for a much
lower return.
CDO 是一种将大量风险资产 （如次级抵押贷款）的风险进行重新包装的方式。 与债券发行不
同的是， 在债券发行中风险在所有债券持有人之间分散得很小，CDO 将风险集中到投资层或
“层级”中， 因此一些投资者为了获得更大的回报而承担更多的风险， 而另一些投资者则为了
获得更低的回报而几乎不承担风险。
Each tranche of CDOs is securitised and ‘priced’ on issue to give the appropriate yield to the
investors. The investment grade tranche of CDOs will be the most highly priced, giving a low yield
but with low risk attached. At the other end, the ‘equity’ tranche carries the bulk of the risk – it will
be very lowly priced but with a high potential, but very risky, yield. There is more detail on this in
the next section.
每一层级的 CDO 都是证券化的， 并在发行时“定价”， 为投资者提供适当的收益。 投资级CDO
将是定价最高的，收益率较低，但风险较低。另一方面，“权益”层级承担了大部分风险——
它的价格很低，但收益率很高，但风险很大。下一节将对此进行详细介绍。
CDOs are, therefore, a mechanism whereby losses are transferred to investors with the highest
appetite for risk (such as hedge funds), leaving the bulk of CDOs’ investors (mainly other banks)
with a low risk source of cash flow.
因此，CDO 是一种将损失转移给风险偏好最高的投资者（如对冲基金）的机制，使 CDO 的
大部分投资者（主要是其他银行）的现金流风险较低。
The structure of CDOs
CDO 的结构
An example of a possible structure for a CDO is as follows. For a pool of mortgages taken over by
the SPV, three tranches of CDOs are created:
一种可能的 CDO 结构如下所示。对于 SPV 收购的抵押贷款池，创建了三类级别 CDO：
Tranche 1 (highest risk) known as the ‘equity’ tranche and normally comprising about 10% of the
value of the mortgages in the pool. Throughout the CDOs’ life, the equity tranche will absorb any
losses brought about by default on the part of mortgage holders, up to the point that the principal
underpinning the tranche is exhausted. At this point the investment is worthless.
级别 1（ （风险最高）被称为“股权”级，通常占池中抵押贷款价值的10%左右。在 CDO 的整个
生命周期中， 股权部分将吸收抵押贷款持有人违约带来的任何损失， 直到支撑该部分的本金
耗尽，此时投资毫无价值。
Tranche 2 (intermediate risk or ‘mezzanine’ tranche) consists of around 10% of the principal and
will absorb any losses not absorbed by the equity tranche until the point at which its principal is
also exhausted.
级别 1（（中等风险或“夹层”部分）约占本金的 10%，将吸收股权部分未吸收的任何损失，直
到其本金耗尽。
Tranche 3 (AAA or ‘senior’ tranche) consists of the balance of the pool value and will absorb any
residual losses.
级别 3（ AAA 或“高级”部分）由池中剩余价值组成，将吸收剩余损失。
The proportion of the principal held in each tranche is known as the CDO ‘structure’, and if there
is perceived to be little risk of default then the percentage of value in the mortgage pool forming
the equity and mezzanine tranches will be quite small. However, if the risk is high then CDOs will
be created with a greater proportion of the principal in the equity and m ezzanine tranches and a
relatively smaller proportion in the senior tranche.
每一部分中持有的本金比例被称为 CDO“结构”， 如果认为违约风险很小， 那么抵押贷款池中
股权和夹层部分的价值占比将很小。然而，如果风险很高，那么 CDO 中，股权和夹层部分
将所占本金的比例更大，而在高级部分所占的比例较小。
When cash flows are received from borrowers in the form of interest payments and loan
repayments, these payments are paid to tranche 3 first until their obligation is fulfilled, then
tranche 2, and anything left over is paid to the equity tranche. Any defaults hit tranche 1 first, then
tranche 2 and so on. The repayments represent a ‘waterfall’ of cash with the investors holding the
tranches like buckets. The senior tranches get filled first, the mezzanine holders get filled next and
anything left falls into the equity pools at the bottom.
当以利息支付和贷款偿还的形式从借款人那里收到现金流时，这些付款首先支付给第 3 级
别， 直到义务履行完成；然后支付给第 2 级别， 剩下的任何款项都支付给股权部分。 任何违
约都会先发生在第 1 级别，然后发生在第 2 级别，以此类推。还款代表着现金的“瀑布”，投
资者像水桶一样持有这些部分。 优先股先被填满， 夹层持有人接着被填满， 剩下的都会落入
底部的权益池。
Example
示例
A bank has made a number of mortgage loans to customers with a current total value of $350
million. The mortgages have an average term to maturity of ten years. The net income from the
loans is 7% per year. The bank will use 85% of the mortgage pool as collateral for a securitisation
with the following structure:
一家银行向客户提供了一系列抵押贷款， 目前总价值为3.5 亿美元。 抵押贷款的平均到期期
限为十年。 贷款的净收入为每年7%。 银行将使用抵押贷款池中的 85%作为证券化的抵押品，
其结构如下：
 75% of the collateral value to support a tranche of A -rated loan notes offering investors 6%
per year. 75%的抵押物价值用于支持 A 级债券，每年向投资者提供 6%的收益。
 15% of the collateral value to support a tranche of B-rated loan notes offering investors 11%
per year. 15%的抵押物价值用于支持 B 级债券，每年向投资者提供 11%的收益。
 10% of the collateral value to support a tranche of subordinated certificates which are unrated.
10%的抵押物价值，用于支持未评级的次级层。
The estimated cash flows for this arrangement would be as follows:
该合约的预计现金流如下所示：
Cash inflows
现金流入
Inflows from mortgages $350m x 7% = $24.5m
从抵押贷款中每年取得$24.5m 的现金利息
Cash outflows
现金流入
A-rated loan notes
$350m x 85% x 75% x 6% = $13.4m
向 A 级债券支付$13.4m 的现金利息
B-rated loan notes
$350m x 85% x 15% x 11% = $4.9m
向 B 级债券支付$4.9m 的现金利息
Total outflows = $13.4m + $4.9m = $18.3m
总的现金流出为$18.3m。
The difference between the inflows and the outflows is returned to the high -risk unrated
certificates.
现金流入和流出之间的差额用于给付未评级的高风险层级
Difference in cash flows = $24.5m – $18.3m = $6.2m
现金流差额= $24.5m – $18.3m = $6.2m
The subordinated certificates have a value of $350m x 85% x 10% = $29.75m.
次级层的价值为$350m x 85% x 10% = $29.75m
The return on this high-risk investment is $6.2m/$29.75m = 20.8%
高风险投资回报率为$6.2m/$29.75m = 20.8%
However this return is at risk should there be a reduction in the income from the mortgages
resulting from customers defaulting on their mortgages. Because of this level of risk, the equity
tranche may be unattractive to investors for some securitisation arrangements.
然而，如果客户违约将导致抵押贷款的收入减少，此时该（高风险投资） 回报将面临风险。
由于这种风险水平，权益层级对一些证券化的投资者可能没有吸引力。
Note that all of the income from the mortgages is used to pay the tranche holders, not just 85%
representing the securitised amount. The reason why the securitisation is performed is to get
money in quickly. In order to sell the various tranches there needs to be an incentive; for this to be
present for all tranches not all of the available pool of mortgages is securitised, but all of the income
from the pool is distributed. The bank, in theory, loses out from this approach by distributing 100%
of the income instead of keeping 15%, but has achieved the objective of getting the initial funds
from the tranche holders as quickly as possible.
请注意，抵押贷款的所有收入都用于支付各层级的持有人，而不仅仅是 85%的证券化金额。
进行证券化的原因是为了快速获得资金。 为了出售不同的层级， 需要有激励措施； 要使这一
点适用于所有层级， 并不是所有可用的抵押贷款池都被要证券化，但是来自该池子的所有收
入都被分配。理论上，该银行会分配 100%的收入而不是保留 15%的收入，这会导致银行产
生一些损失，但它已经实现了尽快从部分持有人那里获得初始资金的目标。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:AFM 考官文章解析 Securitisation and tranching.pdf

stem_zh:
ALTERNATIVE METHODS OF LISTING
替代性的上市方法
The Advanced Financial Management (AFM) syllabus now includes alternative methods which
can be used to obtain a stock market listing and introduces the following new topics, which are
examinable from September 2023 onwards:
Dutch auctions
special purpose acquisition companies
direct listings
高级财务管理 （AFM） 课程现在包括了可以用来在股票市场上市的替代方法， 并引入了以下
新知识点，这些知识点从 2023 年 9 月起将纳入考试范围：
荷兰式拍卖
特殊目的收购公司
直接上市
This article begins with a brief summary of the method which companies have traditionally used
to achieve a stock market listing, ie an initial public offering (IPO), and then discusses each of the
alternative approaches highlighted above, including their advantages and disadvantages.
本文首先简要总结了公司传统上用来实现股票市场上市的方法，即首次公开募股（IPO），
然后讨论了上述每一种替代方法，包括它们的优势和劣势。
IPO
首次公开募股
This topic is included in the Financial Management syllabus and is assumed knowledge for the
AFM exam.
这个知识点包含在财务管理课程大纲中，并且是 AFM 考试中默认考生已掌握的知识。
An IPO is the most common route for a private company to obtain a listing. These are often highly
publicised events and the publicity can help attract new investors. An IPO will allow a company to
access a much wider pool of capital and raise the funds required to take advantage of future
growth opportunities.
首次公开募股（IPO）是私营公司获得上市的最常见途径。这通常是高度公开化的事件，相
关宣传可以帮助吸引新投资者。IPO 将使得公司能接触到更广泛的资本池，并筹集到有助于
抓住未来增长机会的资金。
An IPO is typically underwritten by one or more investment banks. The underwriter evaluates the
risks associated with the IPO and effectively guarantees the share issue. This concept of
underwriting a share issue is a critical component of the initial public offering process but it can
be expensive, typically 2% to 7% of the issue proceeds.
IPO 通常由一家或多家投资银行承销。 承销商评估与IPO 相关的风险， 并保证了股票的发行。
承销股票发行是首次公开募股过程中的一个关键组成部分， 但这可能很昂贵， 通常占发行收
益的 2%到 7%。
In periods of economic uncertainty, an IPO may not be successful in attracting potential investors
and may even have to be abandoned mid -way through the process. Market sentiment can
change quite quickly and if an IPO is cancelled, it means a lot of time and money will have been
wasted without anything to show for it.
在经济不确定的时期，IPO 可能无法吸引潜在投资者，甚至可能不得不在过程中半途而废。
市场情绪可以迅速改变， 如果IPO 被取消，则意味着浪费了很多时间和金钱且没有任何成果。
An IPO can also take a long time, typically between one and two years, mainly due to the legal
requirements associated with a listing and the need to build interest amongst potential investors.
An underwriter which provides bookbuilding services will determine the price at which shares
must be sold after estimating investor appetite but this can be time consuming because it will
involve a series of sales pitches to various institutional investors in the form of a roadshow. This
price discovery process can also sometimes be unreliable. For example, LinkedIn shares were
priced at $45 for its IPO but closed at $94.25 at the end of the first day of trading. Such an
increase implies the IPO was significantly underpriced and when this happens, it means the
issuing company loses out because it ends up raising less capital.
IPO 也可能需要很长时间，通常在一年到两年之间，主要是由于上市相关的法律要求以及需
要在潜在投资者中建立兴趣。提供簿记建档服务的承销商， 将在估计投资者需求后， 确定股
票必须以何种价格出售。但这可能很耗时， 因为它将涉及一系列以路演形式向各种机构投资
者进行的销售推介活动。这个价格发现过程有时也可能不可靠。例如，LinkedIn 的股票在其
IPO 中定价为 45 美元，但在第一天交易结束时收盘价为94.25 美元。这样的增长意味着 IPO
被显著低估了， 而当这种情况发生时， 意味着发行公司会遭受损失， 因为它最终筹集的资金
较少。
Dutch auction
荷兰式拍卖
A Dutch auction is a price discovery process which is used to minimise the potential for
mispricing in an IPO. Once a company has decided on the number of shares it wishes to sell,
interested investors will submit a bid stating the quantity and price at which they are willing to
purchase those shares. The final share price for the IPO is based on the highest price at which all
the shares can be sold. Since all bidders will pay the same share price it means some bidders may
end up paying less than the amount they were initially willing to pay. However, at least in theory,
the price is set at the maximum level which ensures demand equals supply and the entire share
offering is sold.
荷兰式拍卖是一种价格发现过程，用于最小化首次公开募股（IPO）中的定价失误的风险。
一旦公司决定了它希望出售的股票数量， 感兴趣的投资者将提交一个出价， 声明他们愿意以
何种数量和价格购买这些股票。IPO 的最终股票价格基于所有股票能够卖出的最高价格。由
于所有投标者将支付相同的股票价格， 这意味着一些投标者最终可能支付的金额会低于他们
最初愿意支付的金额。 然而， 至少在理论上， 价格设定在能够确保需求等于供应时， 也能保
证整个股票发行都被售出的最高水平。
Advantages of a Dutch auction
There is evidence to suggest that Dutch auctions may result in a premium being paid to the
issuing company compared to the proceeds from a more traditional IPO. This reduces the spread
between the final offer price and the actual market price on the first day of trading.
有证据表明，与传统首次公开募股（IPO）相比，荷兰式拍卖可能使得发行公司获得更多的
溢价。这缩小了最终发行价格与首个交易日实际市场价格之间的差额。
For bidders, there is a trade -off between price and certainty in a way that favours the issuing
company. If a bid is too low, the bidder may lose out but the nature of a Dutch auction means it is
protected if it bids too high since all bidders ultimately pay the same market clearing share price.
A Dutch auction therefore incentivises more aggressive bids, which benefits the company being
listed.
对于投标者来说，存在着一种有利于发行公司的价格和确定性之间的权衡。如果出价太低，
投标者可能会错失机会。荷兰式拍卖的性质意味着如果出价过高， 它会受到保护， 因为所有
投标者最终支付相同的市场清算股价。 因此， 荷兰式拍卖激励更激进的出价， 这对上市的公
司有利。
A Dutch auction reduces the involvement of the underwriters which results in lower transaction
fees for the issuing company.
荷兰式拍卖减少了承销商的参与，这导致发行公司的交易费用更低。
Often in a traditional IPO, the underwriter will market the offer to institutional investors first and
retail investors are only allowed to participate after the company is admitted to trading. However,
Dutch auctions allow retail investors to compete against the institutional investors because
shares are sold to the highest bidder, effectively democratising public offerings.
在传统的 IPO 中， 承销商通常首先向机构投资者推销发行， 零售投资者只有在公司获准交易
后才能参与。 然而， 荷兰式拍卖允许零售投资者与机构投资者竞争， 因为股份是卖给最高出
价者，这使得公开募股更加民主化。
Disadvantages of a Dutch auction
As previously discussed, the share offer price in a traditional IPO is determined by the issuing
company and its underwriter, typically following a roadshow where the shares are marketed to
institutional investors. In a Dutch auction, the underwriter loses this ability to direct the share
price because ultimately it is the potential investors who determine the final price since this is
fixed by the market at a level which maintains the balance between supply and demand.
如前所述， 在传统的首次公开募股(IPO)中， 发行价是由发行公司及其承销商确定的，且通常
是在向机构投资者进行路演营销股票之后确定的。 在荷兰式拍卖中， 承销商失去了指导股价
的能力， 因为最终是由潜在的投资者来决定价格， 市场会将价格固定在一个维持供需平衡的
水平。
In exactly the same way that a traditional IPO can be undersubscribed, the possibility remains
that the listing based on a Dutch auction will also be unsuccessful if demand is weaker than
anticipated.
就像传统的 IPO 可能会认购不足一样， 如果需求低于预期的话，基于荷兰式拍卖的上市也有
可能不成功。
Retail investors are less sophisticated than institutional investors and may not conduct
sufficient due diligence  on the issuing company. There is therefore a risk they might pay an
excessively high price. As a result, they may attempt to liquidate their holdings as soon as trading
commences and cause the share price to plummet.
零售投资者不像机构投资者那样老练， 可能不会对发行公司进行足够的尽职调查。 因此， 存
在他们可能支付过高价格的风险。因此， 他们可能会在交易开始后立即尝试清算他们的持股，
导致股价暴跌。
Special purpose acquisition company (SPAC)
特殊目的收购公司（SPAC）
Another way for a private company to become publicly traded is by merging with an already
listed SPAC. A SPAC starts off as a private shell company which undergoes an IPO to become listed.
The SPAC is usually formed by people with relevant industry experience who provide the initial
capital prior to the SPAC being listed. These people are known as the SPAC sponsors. The purpose
of the listing is to raise sufficient funds from other investors to acquire a controlling stake or
purchase outright an existing private company in a target industry, although at the time of the
IPO, the target company is often unknown to investors. Even though there is usually a target
industry in mind when the SPAC is formed, there is typically enough flexibility built into the
agreement to allow opportunities in other sectors to be pursued too. After the SPAC has obtained
a listing, it normally has up to two years to identify a suitable target or it is closed down and the
funds are returned to the investors.
私营公司成为上市公司的一种方式是与已经上市的 SPAC 合并。SPAC 最初是一个私营空壳公
司，通过首次公开募股（IPO）成为上市公司。SPAC 通常由具有相关行业经验的人士组成，
他们在 SPAC 上市前提供初始资本。 这些人被称为SPAC 发起人。 上市的目的是从其他投资者
那里筹集足够的资金，以获得目标行业现有私营公司的控制权或完全收购，虽然在 IPO 时，
目标公司通常对投资者来说是未知的。在成立 SPAC 时通常有一个目标行业在考虑之中，但
协议中通常有足够的灵活性，允许投资者追求其他行业的机会。SPAC 上市后，通常有最多
两年的时间来确定一个合适的目标，否则它将被关闭，资金将退还给投资者。
Advantages of a SPAC listing
One of the main advantages of a SPAC transaction is that it can take significantly less time to
complete compared to an IPO, typically completing within three to six months. There is also a
significant cost advantage. Once acquired, the target company becomes a publicly traded
company without paying the fees and underwriting costs associated with an IPO since these are
covered before the target company is ever involved. The target company also sidesteps the legal
and regulatory obstacles associated with an IPO and the need to generate interest amongst
potential investors since the SPAC is already listed when the deal is executed, which saves further
time and expenditure.
SPAC 的一个主要优势是，与 IPO 相比，它可以显著减少完成时间，通常在三到六个月内完
成。还有显著的成本优势。一旦收购完成，目标公司就成为一家上市公司，无需支付与IPO
相关的费用和承销成本， 因为这些费用在目标公司参与之前就已经被覆盖了。 目标公司还可
以绕过与 IPO 相关的法律和监管障碍，以及在潜在投资者中产生兴趣的需要，因为 SPAC 在
交易执行时已经上市，这进一步节省了时间和开支。
With an IPO, the share price depends on the market conditions at the time of the listing whereas
with a SPAC transaction the target company is in a position to negotiate before the deal is
executed. This provides greater certainty about the pricing of the shares. In theory, the target
company will therefore not have to worry about the funds raised being insufficient. In a volatile
market, this can be a key advantage compared to an IPO and might explain the significant growth
in SPAC transactions during the early stages of the pandemic.
在 IPO 中，股价取决于上市时的市场条件，而在 SPAC 交易中，目标公司可以在交易执行前
进行谈判。 这为股票定价提供了更大的确定性。 因此， 理论上， 目标公司不必担心筹集的资
金不足。 在波动的市场中， 这可以是与IPO 相比的一个关键优势， 并且可能解释了在疫情早
期阶段 SPAC 交易的显著增长。
The SPAC sponsors typically include individuals with relevant financial and industrial experience,
who can draw on their expertise and even take on a role themselves on the board. The SPAC
sponsors may also have experience of listing requirements and therefore in theory may be in a
position to guide the target company through the process, including any post -admission
compliance issues.
SPAC 的发起人通常包括具有相关金融和行业经验的个人，他们可以利用自己的专业知识，
甚至在董事会中亲自担任角色。SPAC 的发起人可能还有上市要求的经验，因此理论上可能
处于指导目标公司通过整个过程的位置，包括任何上市后的合规问题。
Disadvantages of a SPAC listing
The terms of a SPAC transaction are negotiated between the SPAC sponsors and the target
company. Depending on the outcome of those negotiations, there may be changes made to the
target company’s management team. This contrasts with a traditional IPO, where an offering is
typically built on the reputation and success of the historical management team. Any change in
the composition of the management team may result in cultural or strategic dissonance which
could lead to a failure in execution.
SPAC 交易的条款是 SPAC 发起人和目标公司之间协商确定的。 根据谈判结果， 可能会对目标
公司的管理团队进行调整。 这与通常基于历史管理团队的声誉和成功来构建发行的传统 IPO
形成对比。管理团队的任何变化都可能产生导致执行失败的文化或战略上的不和谐。
Although there have been SPAC transactions that seem to have added value to shareholders,
there is evidence to suggest that many SPAC transactions underperform relative to initial
expectations. One of the possible reasons suggested for the mixed track record is that the
knowledge and expertise attributed to the SPAC sponsors is sometimes overstated and of limited
benefit to the target company post-execution.
尽管有些 SPAC 交易似乎为股东增加了价值， 但有证据表明许多SPAC 交易的表现不如最初的
预期。这可能是因为 SPAC 发起人的知识和专长有时被夸大，且对目标公司执行后的实际帮
助有限。
Although one of the main advantages of a SPAC is to be able to achieve a listing within a
compressed timeline, a company going public must meet the same regulatory requirements as
any other public company, regardless of the route to market. For a SPAC this task must be
accomplished in a matter of months rather than the year or two that a traditional IPO can take. In
theory, the SPAC sponsor may help with the regulatory and compliance issues but in practice the
target company often takes on most of the burden and within a much shorter timeline.
尽管 SPAC 的一个主要优势是能够在更短的时间内实现上市，但无论一家公司的市场进入方
式如何，其公开上市都必须满足与其他任何上市公司相同的监管要求。对于 SPAC 来说，这
项任务必须在几个月内完成，而不是传统 IPO 可能需要的一年或两年。理论上，SPAC 发起
人可能会帮助处理监管和合规问题， 但实际上目标公司通常需要在更短的时间内承担大部分
负担。
The target company will need to do thorough due diligence on the SPAC sponsors and advisors.
This will involve investigating the sponsors’ track record and the source of the funds raised and
ensuring the merger agreement does not contain any terms and conditions which are
detrimental to the target company. It will also be important to ensure the shell company was set
up properly and is compliant with regulatory requirements.
目标公司需要对 SPAC 发起人和顾问进行彻底的尽职调查。这将涉及调查发起人的业绩记录
和资金来源， 并确保合并协议不包含任何对目标公司不利的条款和条件。 确保壳公司正确设
立并符合监管要求也非常重要。
The SPAC is obligated to put the potential merger to a shareholder vote. The SPAC shareholders
normally have a right to redeem their shares and have their funds returned if they do not
approve of the final business combination. The SPAC may then have to turn to the debt markets
or raise additional equity to make up the shortfall, which will cost further time and money.
SPAC 有义务将潜在的合并事宜提交给股东投票。 如果SPAC 股东不批准最终的商业合并， 他
们通常有权赎回他们的股份并收回资金。然后 SPAC 可能不得不转向债务市场或筹集额外的
股权来弥补差额，这将需要更多的时间和金钱。
Direct listing
直接上市
In a direct listing a private company’s shares are admitted to trading on a public market, allowing
its existing shareholders to sell their shares directly to the public. The end result is essentially the
same as the outcome of an IPO in the sense that a formerly private company becomes traded on
a public exchange. However, the route a direct listing takes to get there is different from a
traditional IPO since a direct listing involves listing only the company’s existing shares rather than
issuing new ones. And since no new shares are issued, there is no need for an underwriter. Once
listed, however, it is worth noting that all companies enjoy the same liquidity benefits of being
publicly traded, regardless of their route to market.
在直接上市中， 一家私营公司的股票被允许在公开市场上交易， 允许其现有股东直接向公众
出售他们的股份。结果本质上与首次公开募股（IPO）的结果相同，因为一个曾经的私营公
司变成了在公开交易所交易的公司。 然而， 直接上市到达那里的路线与传统的IPO 不同， 因
为直接上市只涉及公司现有的股份， 而不是发行新的股份。 由于没有发行新的股份， 因此不
需要承销商。 然而值得注意的是， 无论上市路线如何，一旦上市，所有公司都享有公开交易
带来的流动性好处。
A direct listing therefore provides an exit route for the private company’s early investors,
potentially including its directors and employees, but unlike an IPO, no new capital is raised since
no new shares are issued.
因此， 直接上市为私营公司的早期投资者提供了一条退出途径， 可能包括其董事和员工， 但
与 IPO 不同的是，由于没有发行新的股份，因此没有筹集新的资本。
Advantages of a direct listing
A direct listing provides a much more cost -effective route to market since it bypasses the need
for an underwriter and the transaction fees associated with a traditional IPO. Companies that do
not need to raise capital may prefer this method since it also avoids the time delays associated
with an IPO. This also minimises the risk of the listing being abandoned since it sidesteps the
need for a bookbuilding process and the potential for the sort of unforeseen economic events
which can trigger a sudden shift in investor appetite.
直接上市提供了一条更具成本效益的上市途径， 因为它绕过了传统IPO 所需的承销商和相关
交易费用。 不需要筹集资金的公司可能会更倾向于这种方法， 因为它也避免了与IPO 相关的
时间延迟。 这也最小化了由于绕过簿记建档过程导致上市被放弃的风险和改变投资者需求的
不可预见经济事件发生的可能性。
A direct listing allows companies to access a broader shareholder base, including institutional and
retail investors, without the restrictions on trading which are associated with IPOs. This feature
may be particularly attractive to early investors who may want to sell their shares on the first day
of trading which is typically not possible with an IPO due to the requirement for a lockup period.
Since no new shares are issued, this method can also help avoid a dilution of the existing
shareholders.
直接上市允许公司接触到更广泛的股东基础， 包括机构和零售投资者， 而无需像IPO 那样受
到交易限制。 这一特点可能对早期投资者特别有吸引力， 他们可能希望在上市首日就卖出他
们的股份， 这在IPO 中通常由于锁定期要求而不可能实现。 由于没有发行新股份， 这种方法
还可以帮助避免现有股东的股份被稀释。
There is a lower risk of mispricing with a direct listing since the process of price discovery is
determined by the opening auction on the first day of trading.
直接上市的价格发现过程由上市首日的开盘拍卖决定，因此价格错配的风险较低。
Disadvantages of a direct listing
A direct listing is not the most suitable option for a company that needs capital to finance its
future growth plans. Without an underwriter, there is also no guarantee the shares will sell. If the
listing is to be successful, any company using a direct listing will therefore need to be confident
that there is sufficient investor appetite to allow a market to develop for its shares. This explains
why companies that use this route to market typically have strong brand recognition e.g. Spotify.
The availability of shares also depends on early investors and employees wanting to sell their
shares. Without an underwriter, it can also be difficult to protect against volatility and
uncertainty in the early days of trading after a direct listing.
直接上市对于需要资本来资助其未来增长计划的公司来说， 并不是最合适的选择。 没有承销
商， 也无法保证股票能够售出。想要直接上市成功， 公司需要确信有足够的投资者感兴趣以
使其股票有市场。 这就解释了为什么使用这种方式上市的公司通常有很强的品牌认知度， 例
如 Spotify。 股票的供应也取决于早期投资者和员工是否愿意出售他们的股票。 没有承销商，
在直接上市后的交易早期也很难防止波动性和不确定性。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Alternative methods of listings.pdf

stem_zh:
Aspects of Islamic finance
伊斯兰金融的相关拓展
This article aims to continue to develop the understanding of Islamic finance and follows the first
article on this subject, 'Islamic finance – theory and practical use of sukuk bonds'. The first article
focused on Sukuk finance and case studies where Sukuk finance was utilised. This article is more
general and considers different aspects, issues and developments of Islamic finance. It also
extends the explanations provided in the appendix to the first article.
Questions are likely to focus on the application of Islamic finance as an additional source of
finance, and assessing its benefits and drawbacks, in different business scenarios and situations.
这篇文章旨在继续发展对伊斯兰金融的理解，并继之前关于该主题的文章《伊斯兰金融
——Sukuk 债券的理论与实际应用》之后发表。第一篇文章主要关注 Sukuk 金融以及 Sukuk
金融被应用的案例研究。 本文则更为广泛， 考虑了伊斯兰金融的不同方面、 问题和发展。 它
还扩展了第一篇文章附录中提供的解释。
考试问题可能会集中在伊斯兰金融作为额外融资来源的应用，以及在不同商业场景和情况
下评估其优势和劣势
Overview 综述
Islamic virtues and tenets specify the need  for ethical behaviour and fair treatment. Within a
business context, this means that organisations should maintain high ethical standards in all
business dealings. Specifically, business and enterprise should be conducted with honesty and
integrity, maintaining truthfulness and morality in all dealings. In particular, such business and
enterprise should not capitalise on the misfortune of others or take unfair advantage. For
example, higher prices should not be charged to an individual because they lack knowledge and
information about the fair price of a product that they are purchasing. Profit creation should be
the result of business activity that benefits society at large.
伊斯兰的道德和教义规定了对道德行为和公平对待的需求。 在商业环境中， 这意味着组织应
在所有商业交易中保持高标准的道德行为。 具体来说， 商业和企业应以诚实和正直的方式进
行， 保持所有交易的真诚和道德。 特别是， 此类商业和企业不应利用他人的不幸或不公平地
获利。 例如， 不应因为个人缺乏对所购买产品的公平价格的了解而向其收取更高的价格。 利
润的创造应该是对社会整体有益的商业活动的结果。
Within this context, the Islamic finance framework is based on certain prohibitions. In particular,
money (and money substitute products such as gold and silver) should not be viewed as
commodities, but rather as means of exchange. Therefore, interest (or riba) cannot be paid or
received on loans. Furthermore, although it is fully acceptable to engage in profitable business
activities, such business should be ethical. In particular dealing in alcohol, pork -related products,
armaments, gambling and other socially detrimental activities is not acceptable. Engaging in
activities involving speculation is also not allowed, limiting the use of derivative instruments and
money markets, which are based on interest.
在这一背景下， 伊斯兰金融框架基于某些禁忌。 特别是， 货币 （以及黄金和白银等货币替代
品）不应被视为商品，而是交换的手段。因此，贷款不能收取或支付利息（或称为 riba）。
此外， 虽然从事盈利的商业活动是完全可以接受的， 但此类商业活动应该是道德的。 特别是，
涉及酒精、 猪肉产品、 武器、 赌博和其他对社会有害的活动是不被接受的。 从事涉及投机的
活动也不允许，这限制了基于利息的衍生工具和货币市场的使用。
Operationalising Islamic finance 伊斯兰金融的运作
Organisations need access to short -term and long -term sources of finance. The basic,
fundamental function of banks is to provide a channel that enables the flow of financial
resources from investors to borrowers, and thereby provides a source of finance for organisations.
Investors invest their excess funds to earn interest, and borrowers use the funds in business
activity to generate profits, some of which are then used to pay interest on the borrowings.
Among other sources of finance that involve the payment and receipt of interest are corporate
and government bonds.
组织需要短期和长期的融资来源。 银行的基本功能是为投资者和借款人之间提供资金流动的
渠道， 从而为组织提供融资来源。 投资者将多余的资金融资以获得利息， 借款人则使用这些
资金进行商业活动以产生利润， 其中一部分用于支付借款的利息。 其他涉及利息支付和收取
的融资来源包括公司债券和政府债券。
The question that could be asked is how could finance flow between investors and borrowers
without involving interest. The answer provided by Islamic finance, in its basic form, is through
profit-sharing arrangements or partnerships. The article on Islamic finance published in February
2013 explained it as follows:
In an Islamic bank, the money provided in the form of deposits is not loaned, but is instead
channelled into an underlying investment activity, which will earn profit. The depositor is
rewarded by a share in that profit, after a management fee is deducted by the bank.
可以提出的问题是， 资金如何在不涉及利息的情况下在投资者和借款人之间流动。 伊斯兰金
融的基本答案是通过利润分享安排或合伙关系。2013 年 2 月发表的伊斯兰金融文章解释如
下：
在伊斯兰银行中， 以存款形式提供的资金不是被贷出， 而是被用于基础投资活动， 以赚取利
润。存款者在银行扣除管理费后，通过分享利润获得回报
Islamic finance institutions (IFIs), including banks, could raise finance via Mudaraba and
Musharaka equity -type contracts through multi -partnership contracts (see below). Here,
investors (known as rub -ul-mal) would invest funds with the IFI (known as the mudareb or
investment manager). The funds are then pooled and used in profit -making projects while also
keeping within Sharia rules. Therefore, the IFI would effectively become the rub -ul-mal and the
corporation that uses the funds for investment purposes becomes the mudareb. In each case, the
emphasis is on partnerships, and the profits earned are shared between the corporation, the
bank and the investors. It is possible that all three parties share the losses as well, if the business
venture is not successful.
伊斯兰金融机构（下文简称 IFIs），包括银行，可以通过 Mudaraba 和 Musharaka 股权型合
同通过多合伙合同筹集资金（见下文）。在这里，投资者（称为 Rub-ul-Mal）将资金投资于
IFI（称为 Mudareb 投资主体企业或投资经理）。资金随后被汇集并用于符合伊斯兰教法规
定的盈利项目。因此，IFI（投资经理）实际上成为 Rub-ul-Mal（投资者），而使用资金进行
投资的企业成为 Mudareb（投资主体企业）。在每种情况下，重点是合伙关系，赚取的利
润在企业、银行和投资者之间共享。如果商业 风险投资不成功，所有三方参与者也可能共
享损失。
However, with corporations requiring different modes of finance and IFIs keen on providing these,
different types of Islamic financial products have been developed. The challenge for IFIs is to
ensure that the products comply with Sharia rulings, as well as normal financial regulations and
law.
然而， 由于企业需要不同模式的融资， 而伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 渴望提供这些融资， 因此开发
了不同类型的伊斯兰金融产品。 伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 面临的挑战是确保这些产品符合伊斯兰
教法规定，以及正常的金融法规和法律
Common Islamic financial products 常见的伊斯兰金融产品
IFIs offer two broad categories of financial products: equity -based and fixed income -based. The
appendix to the February 2013 article on Islamic finance explained many of these financial
products and it is recommended that this article is read in conjunction with the first article for
further detail.
伊斯兰金融机构 IFIs 提供两类金融产品：股权型和固定收益型。2013 年 2 月关于伊斯兰金
融的文章的附录解释了许多这些金融产品， 建议将本文与第一篇文章一起阅读以获取更多细
节。
Equity-based financial products 股权型伊斯兰金融产品
Equity-based financial products consist of Mudaraba and Musharaka contracts. With these
contracts, the investor or IFI (rub-ul-mal) and the investment manager or corporation (mudareb)
share the profits from the business venture, in which the funds are invested, in a pre -arranged
agreement. The key differences between the two contracts are two-fold.
股权型金融产品包括 Mudaraba 和 Musharaka 合同。 在这些合同中， 投资者或IFI（Rub-ul-Mal）
投资经理或投资主体企业（Mudareb）根据预先安排的协议分享投资项目的利润。这两种合
同之间的关键区别有两个方面：
With a Mudaraba contract:
all losses are borne solely by the investor (IFI), although provisions can be set up to carry forward
these losses against future profits, and the mudareb, as the expert in the business venture takes
the sole responsibility for running the business.
在 Mudaraba 合同中：
所有损失仅由投资经理（IFI）承担，尽管可以设立条款将这些损失结转到未来利润中，
Mudareb 作为商业风险投资的专家，承担经营业务的全部责任。
With a Musharaka contract:
losses are shared between the two parties in proportion to their monetary investment or
investment-in-kind, and  both parties would participate in managing and running the venture
jointly.
在 Musharaka 合同中：
损失按双方的货币投资或实物投资的比例分担，
双方将共同参与管理和经营投资。
Diminishing Musharaka contracts are a recent innovation where not only are the profits shared
between rub -ul-mal and the mudareb, but the mudareb would pay greater amounts to the
rub-ul-mal. In this way the mudareb owns greater and greater proportion of the asset, until
eventually the ownership of the asset is passed to the mudareb entirely.
递减 的 Musharaka 合同是一项最新创新，其中不仅 投资者 Rub-ul-Mal 和投资主体企业
Mudareb 之间分享利润，而且投资主体企业 Mudareb 会向投资者 Rub-ul-Mal 支付更多的金
额。 通过这种方式，Mudareb 拥有资产的份额越来越大， 直到最终资产的所有权完全转移到
投资主体企业 Mudareb 手中。
Fixed-income based financial products 固定收益型金融产品
With Murabaha contracts, the IFI purchases the asset and then sells it to the business or
individual at cost plus a fair profit. The business or individual pays for the asset in pre -agreed
instalments and over a pre-agreed time period.
Ijara contracts are similar to short -term leases where the IFI purchases an asset for the business
or individual to use. The lease payments, the lease period and payment terms are agreed at the
start of the contract. The lessor is responsible for the maintenance and insurance of the asset.
Provisions can be made to allow the lessee to purchase the asset for a nominal fee at the end of
the contract.
在 Murabaha 合同中，投资经理 IFI 购买资产，然后以成本加合理利润的价格将其卖给企业
或个人。企业或个人按预先约定的分期付款和时间支付。
Ijara 合同类似于短期租赁，其中投资经理 IFI 购买资产供企业或个人使用。租赁付款、租赁
期限和付款条款在合同开始时约定。 出租人负责资产的维护和保险。 可以制定条款允许承租
人在合同结束时以象征性费用购买资产
Sukuk bonds were covered in some detail in the February 2013 article on Islamic finance and it is
recommended that you study that article in detail. Sukuk bonds have been based on underlying
securitised Islamic contracts such as Ijara and Mudaraba, as well on individual or groups of
physical assets. Some Sukuk bonds have been based on securitised Murabaha contracts, but
there is some debate on whether these comply with Sharia rulings, as they may be viewed as
debt on debt and therefore attracting riba. Some Sharia rulings have allowed minor proportions
of Murabaha and Istisna contracts within the securitised asset portfolio, used as the underlying
asset portfolio.
Sukuk 债券在 2013 年 2 月关于伊斯兰金融的文章中详细讨论过， 建议详细研究该文章。Sukuk
债券基于基础证券化的伊斯兰合同，如 Ijara 和 Mudaraba，以及单个或一组实物资产。一些
Sukuk 债券基于证券化的 Murabaha 合同，但对其是否符合伊斯兰教法规定存在一些争论，
因为它们可能被视为债务上的债务， 因此吸引利息。 一些伊斯兰教法规定允许在证券化资产
组合中使用少量的 Murabaha 和 Istisna 合同，该资产组合作为基础资产组合。
Salam contracts are similar to forward contracts, where a commodity (or service) is sold today for
future delivery. Cash is received immediately from the IFI and the quantity, quality, and the future
date and time of delivery are determined immediately. The sale will probably be at a discount so
that the IFI can make a profit. In turn, the IFI would probably sell the contract to another buyer
for immediate cash and profit, in a parallel Salam arrangement. Salam contracts are prohibited
for commodities such as gold, silver and other money-type assets.
Salam 合同类似于远期合同，其中商品（或服务）今天出售以供未来交付。投资经理 IFI 立
即收到现金，数量、质量以及未来交付的日期和时间立即确定。销售可能会以折扣价进行，
以便投资经理 IFI 能够获利。 反过来， 投资经理IFI 可能会将合同卖给其他买家以换取即时现
金和利润，在并行的 Salam 安排中。Salam 合同禁止用于黄金、白银和其他货币型资产
Istisna contracts are often used for long -term, large construction projects of property and
machinery. Here, the IFI funds the construction project for a client that is delivered on
completion to the IFI’s client. The client pays an initial deposit, followed by instalments, to the IFI,
the amount and frequency of which are determined at the start of the contract.
Istisna 合同通常用于长期、大型的房地产和机械建设项目。在这里，投资经理 IFI 为客户资
助建设项目， 完成后交付给IFI 投资经理的客户。 客户支付首付款， 然后向投资经理IFI 支付
分期付款，金额和频率在合同开始时确定。
Sharia boards 伊斯兰教法委员会
Sharia Boards (SBs) ensure that all products and services offered by IFIs are compliant with the
principles of Sharia rules. They review and oversee all new product offerings made by the IFI and
make judgments on an individual case -by-case basis, regarding their acceptability with Sharia
rulings. Additionally, SBs often oversee Sharia compliant training programmes for an IFI’s
employees and participate in the preparation and approval of the IFI’s annual reports.
伊斯兰教法委员会 （Sharia Boards， 简称SBs） 确保所有由伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 提供的产品和
服务符合伊斯兰教法规定的原则。它们审查和监督投资经理 IFI 提供的所有新产品，并逐个
案例判断其是否符合伊斯兰教法规定。此外，SBs 通常监督投资经理 IFI 员工的伊斯兰教法
合规培训计划，并参与投资经理 IFI 年度报告的准备和批准。
SBs are normally made up of a mixture of Islamic scholars and finance experts to ensure that fair
and reasonable judgments are made. Where necessary, the finance experts can explain the
products to the Islamic scholars. The Islamic scholars often sit on several SBs of a number of
different IFIs. SBs are in-turn supervised by the International Association of Islamic Bankers (IAIB).
SBs face several challenges when making judgments. Sharia law can be open to different
interpretations, leading to different outcomes on the acceptability of the same products by
different SBs and Islamic scholars. Furthermore, precedents set by SBs are not binding, and
changes in SB’s personnel over time may shift the balance of the SB’s collective opinions and
judgments on the acceptability of existing and new products.
SBs 通常由伊斯兰学者和金融专家组成，以确保做出公平合理的判断。必要时，金融专家可
以向伊斯兰学者解释产品。 伊斯兰学者通常在多个不同投资机构 IFI 的伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs
中任职。伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs 反过来由国际伊斯兰银行家协会（IAIB）监督。
伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs 在做出判断时面临几个挑战。 伊斯兰教法可以有不同的解释， 导致不
同的伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs 和伊斯兰学者对同一产品的可接受性有不同的结果。 此外， 伊斯
兰教法委员会 SBs 设定的先例不具有约束力， 伊斯兰教法委员会SBs 人员随时间的变化可能
会改变 SBs 对现有和新产品可接受性的集体意见和判断。
SBs need considerable resources to operate effectively, especially where Sukuk finance is
concerned. IFIs need to ensure that their SB members are well informed about the developments
and trends in global financial markets.
伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs 需要相当的资源才能有效运作，特别是在涉及 sukuk 金融的情况下。
IFIs 需要确保其 SB 成员对全球金融市场的最新发展和趋势有充分的了解。
Benefits, drawbacks and challenges 优势、劣势和挑战
Benefits 优势
Corporations, individuals and IFIs engaged in raising and issuing funding based on Islamic finance
virtues may be viewed as belonging in stakeholder-type partnerships that are engaged in deriving
benefits from ethical, fair business activity. The result of these partnerships is one of mutual
interest, trust and co -operation. The ethical stance and fair dealing of Islamic finance virtues
means that partnerships, business activity and profit creation comes from benefiting the
community as a whole.
从事基于伊斯兰金融美德的融资筹集和发行的企业、 个人和伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 可以被视为
属于利益相关者型的合伙关系， 这些合伙关系从事道德、 公平的商业活动以获得利益。 这些
合伙关系的结果是相互利益、 信任和合作。 伊斯兰金融美德的道德立场和公平交易意味着合
伙关系、商业活动和利润创造来自于对整个社区的益处。
Since the virtues of Islamic finance and enterprise prohibit speculation and short -term
opportunism, it encourages all parties to take a longer term view of success from the partnership.
It focuses all the parties’ attention on creating a successful outcome to the venture. This should
result in a more stable financial environment. Indeed, literature in this area suggests that had
banks and other financial institutions conducted their business activity based on Islamic finance
principles, the negative impact of the banking and sovereign debt financial crises would have
been much reduced.
由于伊斯兰金融和企业的美德禁止投机和短期机会主义， 它鼓励各方从合伙关系中采取长期
的成功观。 它将各方的注意力集中在创造冒险的成功结果上。 这应该会导致更稳定的金融环
境。 事实上， 该领域的文献表明， 如果银行和其他金融机构根据伊斯兰金融原则进行商业活
动，银行和主权债务金融危机的负面影响将会大大减少。
IFIs or conventional financial institutions with products based on Islamic finance principles gain
access to Muslim funds across the world and provide finance for organisations and individuals
who need them. As the February 2013 article stated, it is estimated that Islamic financial assets
have exceeded $1,600bn worldwide. As the world emerges from the global financial crisis and
business activity increases, this should increase. Furthermore, access to Islamic finance is not
restricted to Muslim communities only. The wider business community could have access to new
sources of finance. This may be particularly attractive to corporations focused on ethical
investments that Islamic finance virtues stipulate.
基于伊斯兰金融原则的伊斯兰金融机构 IFIs 或传统金融机构能够获得全球穆斯林资金， 并为
需要资金的组织和个人提供融资。正如 2013 年 2 月的文章所述，全球伊斯兰金融资产估计
已超过 1.6 万亿美元。随着世界走出全球金融危机，商业活动增加，这一数字应该会增加。
此外，伊斯兰金融的获取不仅限于穆斯林社区。更广泛的商业社区可以获得新的融资来源。
这可能特别吸引那些专注于伊斯兰金融美德规定的道德投资的企业。
Drawbacks and challenges
Because of the prohibitions of riba and on speculation, IFIs may be slower to react to market
demand and changes. They may lack sufficient flexibility in their product offering when compared
to conventional financial institutions and may be less able to take advantage of short -term
opportunities.
由于禁止利息和投机， 伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 可能在对市场需求和变化的反应上较慢。 与传统
金融机构相比，它们在产品供应上可能缺乏足够的灵活性，可能无法充分利用短期机会。
Moral hazard and principal -agent issues may be more pronounced between IFIs and
organisations and individuals to whom they lend funds. This is because Islamic finance virtues
stipulate close relationships from partnership -like arrangements. However, information
asymmetry between the IFI and the borrower of funds will always exist. Therefore, costs related
to increased level of due diligence and negotiating are probably higher for IFIs.
道德风险和委托-代理问题在伊斯兰金融机构 IFIs 与它们贷款的企业和个人之间可能更为突
出。这是因为伊斯兰金融美德规定了类似合伙关系的密切关系。然而，投资者 IFI 和资金借
款人之间的信息不对称总是存在的。 因此， 伊斯兰金融机构IFIs 在尽职调查和谈判方面的成
本可能更高。
Costs related to developing new financial products may also be higher for IFIs because not only
will the products have to comply with normal financial laws and regulations but also with Sharia
rules. As stated above, the resources required by SBs can be considerable.
开发新金融产品的成本也可能更高， 因为产品不仅要符合正常的金融法律和法规， 还要符合
伊斯兰教法。如上所述，伊斯兰教法委员会 SBs 所需的资源可以相当可观。
Added to this, because these financial products need to go through stages of compliance and
layers of complications before they are approved, the approval process can take time. The pace
of innovation of new Islamic financial products may be considerably slower than that of
conventional products. This may make the IFI less able to compete with conventional financial
institutions and may make it restrict its activities to smaller, niche markets.
此外， 由于这些金融产品在获得批准之前需要经过合规阶段和多层复杂性， 批准过程可能需
要时间。新伊斯兰金融产品的创新步伐可能远远慢于传统产品。这可能使投资经理 IFI 在与
传统金融机构的竞争中处于劣势，并可能使其将活动限制在较小的利基市场。
Some Islamic financial products may not be compatible with international financial regulation –
for example, a diminishing Musharaka contract may not be an acceptable mortgage instrument in
law, although it could be constructed as such. The need to ensure that such products comply with
regulations may increase legal and insurance costs.
一些伊斯兰金融产品可能与国际金融法规不兼容——例如， 递减的 Musharaka 合同可能在法
律上不被视为可接受的抵押贷款工具， 尽管它可以被构建为如此。 确保这些产品符合法规的
需要可能会增加法律和保险成本。
The interpretation of Sharia rulings may allow certain Islamic finance products to be acceptable in
some markets, but not in others. This has led to some Islamic scholars, who are experts in Sharia
and finance, to criticise a number of product offerings. For example, some Murabaha contracts
have been criticised because their repayments have been based on prevailing interest rates
rather than on economic or profit conditions within which the asset will be used. Some Sukuk
bonds have faced similar criticisms in that their repayments have been based on prevailing
interest rates, they have been credit -rated and their redemption value is based on a nominal
value rather than on a market value, and thereby, perhaps, making them too close to
conventional bonds and their repayments too similar to riba. On the other hand, the opposite
argument could be that in order to make Islamic financial products competitive in all markets,
their valuations need to be comparable. Therefore, benchmarking them using conventional
means is necessary.
伊斯兰教法规定的解释可能允许某些伊斯兰金融产品在某些市场被接受， 但在其他市场则不
被接受。 这导致一些精通伊斯兰教法和金融的伊斯兰学者批评了一些产品供应。 例如， 一些
Murabaha 合同因其还款基于现行利率而非资产将被使用的经济或利润条件而受到批评。一
些 Sukuk 债券也面临类似的批评， 因为其还款基于现行利率， 它们被信用评级， 其赎回价值
基于名义价值而非市场价值，从而可能使它们过于接近传统债券，其还款过于类似于利息。
另一方面， 相反的观点可能是， 为了使伊斯兰金融产品在所有市场中具有竞争力， 其估值需
要具有可比性。因此，使用传统手段对它们进行基准化是必要的。
So far the discussion on drawbacks and challenges has focused on  IFIs, as providers of Islamic
finance. It is also important to consider the drawbacks and challenges that corporations may face
when using Islamic finance.
到目前为止， 对劣势和挑战的讨论集中在作为伊斯兰金融提供者的金融机构上 IFIs 上。 考虑
企业使用伊斯兰金融时可能面临的劣势和挑战也很重要。
From the above discussion, the costs related to developing and gaining approval for Islamic
financial products is likely to be passed down to customers and possibly make these products
more expensive. In addition to this, access to new products and flexibility within existing
products may be limited, due to the more complicated approval process that is necessary. These
more expensive and less flexible sources of finance may make the corporation using them less
competitive when compared to rivals who have access to cheaper, more flexible sources of
finance.
从上述讨论中， 开发和获得伊斯兰金融产品批准的成本可能会转嫁给客户， 可能使这些产品
更昂贵。 此外， 由于必要的更复杂的批准过程， 对新产品的获取和现有产品内的灵活性可能
受到限制。 这些更昂贵且灵活性较低的融资来源可能会使使用它们的企业在与能够获得更便
宜、更灵活融资来源的竞争对手相比时处于不利地位。
The partnership nature of Islamic finance contracts may also cause agency type issues within
corporations. These may be more prevalent in joint venture type situations or where the diverse
range of stakeholders may make it more difficult for corporations to determine and act upon the
importance of various stakeholder groups. For example, in the case of a Musharaka contract,
where the IFI and the organisation are both involved in the management of a project, dealing
with other stakeholder groups may be more challenging.
伊斯兰金融合同的合伙性质也可能在企业内部引起代理类型的问题。 这些问题在合资企业类
型的情况中可能更为普遍， 或者在利益相关者范围广泛的情况下， 可能使企业更难确定和采
取行动以应对各种利益相关者群体的重要性。例如，在 Musharaka 合同的情况下，IFI 和企
业都参与项目的管理，与其他利益相关者群体打交道可能更具挑战性。
Before the financial crisis, trading in asset backed and securitised Sukuk products, issued by
corporations, has been limited (a notable exception was Sukuk products denominated in
Malaysian ringgits). Furthermore, since the financial crisis, issuance in new Sukuk products has
reduced somewhat.
在金融危机之前， 企业发行的资产支持和证券化Sukuk 产品的交易是有限的 （一个显著的例
外是马来西亚林吉特计价的 Sukuk 产品）。此外，自金融危机以来，新Sukuk 产品的发行有
所减少。
Using Islamic finance may also increase the cost of capital for a corporation. For example, it may
be more difficult to demonstrate that repayments for Mudaraba, Musharaka and Sukuk contracts
are like debt, and therefore they may not attract a tax -shield. However, an equivalent
organisation which raises the same finance using conventional debt finance may be able to lower
its cost of capital due to tax-shields and therefore increase the value of its investment.
使用伊斯兰金融可能会增加企业的资本成本。例如，可能更难证明 Mudaraba、Musharaka
和 Sukuk 合同的还款类似于债务， 因此它们可能不会获得税收抵免。 然而， 使用传统债务融
资筹集相同资金的同等组织可能能够通过税收抵免降低其资本成本， 从而增加其投资的价值。
Conclusion 结论
The increasing global interest in and use of Islamic finance means that this is an important source
of finance which organisations need to consider. Its many attributes that are common to ethical
investment and finance would make it an attractive source of finance particularly to organisations
that place importance on ethical issues. The innovations in Islamic financial products by IFIs, such
as Sukuk and diminishing Musharaka, have meant new and innovative Islamic finance products
are being developed and are coming into the market. This is likely to continue as the impact of
the financial crisis recedes.
However, IFIs face a number of challenges such as agency -related issues, increased costs, lack of
flexibility, difficulties with complying with Sharia rulings, and also normal regulations and law. The
IFIs (and the wider Islamic finance regulatory bodies) need to put into place mechanisms and
strategies that will help to overcome these challenges, and thereby ensure that Islamic
finance-based products compete and compare with conventional riba-based financial products.
全球对伊斯兰金融的兴趣和使用不断增加， 这意味着这是组织需要考虑的一个重要融资来源。
其许多与道德投资和金融共同的属性使其成为特别注重道德问题的组织的有吸引力的融资
来源。IFIs 对 Sukuk 和递减 Musharaka 等伊斯兰金融产品的创新，意味着新的和创新的伊斯
兰金融产品正在开发并进入市场。随着金融危机的影响逐渐消退，这种情况可能会继续。
然而，伊斯兰金融机构 IFIs 面临许多挑战，如代理相关问题、成本增加、缺乏灵活性、遵守
伊斯兰教法规定的困难， 以及正常的法规和法律。IFIs（以及更广泛的伊斯兰金融监管机构）
需要制定机制和策略， 以帮助克服这些挑战， 从而确保基于伊斯兰金融的产品能够与传统的
基于利息的金融产品竞争和比较。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Aspects of Islamic finance.pdf

stem_zh:
Business valuations
Business valuation is ‘an art not a science’. These are the words used by many ACCA financial management
tutors (including myself) when introducing this topic to students preparing for  Advanced Financial
Management. The words imply that when trying to value the equity capital of a business, there is range of
possible correct answers, all of which can be justified as being the most appropriate. To a certain extent this
is true but, as I like to put it, ‘there are different degrees of correctness’.
商业估值是“一门艺术，而不是科学”。这是许多 ACCA 财务管理导师(包括我自己)在向备考高级财务管
理课程的学生介绍这个话题时使用的词语。这句话暗示，当试图评估一家企业的权益资本时，有一系
列可能的正确答案，所有这些答案都可以被证明是最合适的。在某种程度上，这是正确的，但正如我
喜欢说的，“有不同程度的正确性”。
Questions on Business Valuations are included in every  Financial Management exam. The questions have
typically tested the ‘basic’ equity valuation methods of:
关于企业估值的问题包含在每个财务管理考试中。这些问题通常测试了以下几种“基本”股权估值方法:
• net assets 净资产
• dividend valuation model (or dividend growth model) 股息估值模型(或股息增长模型)
• earnings model using P/E ratio or earnings yield 收益模型，使用市盈率或收益收益率
The Advanced Financial Management syllabus builds on those methods tested at the lower level paper. The
concept is the same – to find the value of equity. However, the techniques and methods are more
sophisticated. As I stated above, ‘there are different degrees of correctness’.
高级财务管理教学大纲建立在那些低阶段考试的基础之上。概念是一样的——找到权益的价值。然而，
技术和方法更为复杂。正如我上面所说的，“有不同程度的正确性”。
The primary purpose of this article is to demonstrate how to tackle an  Advanced Financial
Management business valuation question. The detailed understanding of this topic will be gained from your
studies, whichever mode you choose to use. My aim is to show you how to successfully apply this knowledge
under exam conditions.
本文的主要目的是演示如何解决高级财务管理的业务估值问题。无论您选择使用哪种模式，您都可以
从学习中获得对本主题的详细理解。我的目的是向你展示如何在考试中成功地应用这些知识。
Equity valuation – categorising the methods 股权估值-方法分类
As stated above, there are more methods and models that can be used to find an equity share price
in Advanced Financial Management. The official textbooks explain these in detail and choose different ways
of categorising them within their material. I prefer to take a simple view of equity valuation by allocating the
methods into two main categories:
如前所述，在《高级财务管理》中有更多的方法和模型可以用来寻找股票的价格。官方教科书详细解
释了这些，并在教材中选择了不同的分类方式。我倾向于将待股票估值的方法分为两大类:
• Pre-acquisition 收购前
• Post-acquisition 收购后
Under the first category, the question will be asking the students to ascertain an equity value for a company.
The entity may be a private company and, hence, no stock market price exists or that even if the company is
listed, the market price may not be appropriate for the relevant situation. The valuation methods
appropriate here are:
在第一类中，问题将要求学生确定一家公司的股权价值。该实体可能是一家私营公司，因此不存在股
票市场价格，或者即使公司上市，其市场价格也可能不适合相关情况。适用的估值方法有
• net assets 净资产
• dividend valuation model (or dividend growth model)股息估值模型(或股息增长模型)
• earnings model using P/E ratio or earnings yield收益模型，使用市盈率或收益率
• net assets + calculated intangible value (CIV)净资产+无形资产
• free cash flows (FCF)自由现金流
Past questions have, in my view, clearly indicated which method should be used to arrive at the share price.
However, it is fair to say that the free cash flow model has been tested more than any other method.
在我看来，过去的问题已经清楚地表明，应该使用哪种方法来计算股价。然而，公平地说，自由现金
流模型比任何其他方法都经过了更多的测试。
Post-acquisition valuation requires a different mindset and series of methods. Here, students will need to
ascertain the value of the combined companies after acquisition. More importantly, past exam requirements
have requested students to ascertain the percentage gain or loss to both groups of shareholders – those of
both the buying and selling companies.
收购后估值需要不同的思维方式和一系列方法。在这里，学生需要确定收购后合并公司的价值。更重
要的是，过去的考试要求要求学生确定两组股东(买卖公司的股东)的收益或损失百分比。
The post-acquisition valuation methods are:
收购后估值方法有：
• bootstrapping – applying the price earnings ratio of the buyer to the combined expected earnings of
the two entities
• 自举（由自己的市盈率推倒代替并购后的市盈率）——将买方的市盈率应用于两个主体的合并
预期收益
• combining the pre-acquisition values of the two companies and appending these with the fair value
of the synergies
• 合并两家公司收购前的价值，并将其与协同效应的公允价值相结合
• free cash flows (FCF) – present value of the combined companies FCF using the relevant discount
rate.
• 自由现金流量(FCF) -合并后公司的自由现金流量在相关折现率下的现值。
As I have stated above, your preparation should include ample time to study and understand the equity
valuation methods above, allowing you to apply your knowledge successfully in the exam room.
正如我上面所说的，你的准备工作应该包括充足的时间来学习和理解上述股权评估方法，使你能够在
考场上成功地应用你的知识。
Below is a worked sample question illustrating how I would tackle a 25 -mark exam style question, based
broadly on previous exam content.
下面是一个工作样题，说明了我如何解决一个 25 分的考试类型的问题，主要基于以前的考试内容。
Borgonni and Venitra
Borgonni Co is a very successful entity. The company has consistently followed a business strategy of
aggressive acquisitions, looking to buy companies that it believes were poorly managed and hence
undervalued. Borgonni can be described as a modern day conglomerate and its business interests stretch far
and wide.
Borgonni 公司是一个非常成功的实体。该公司一直遵循积极收购的商业战略，寻求收购它认为管理不
善而被低估的公司。Borgonni 可以说是一个现代的企业集团，它的商业利益延伸得很远。
Its board of directors has chosen the takeover targets with care. Always looking for companies with potential,
but which were poorly managed and having a below par market value, Borgonni has maintained its price
earnings (P/E) ratio on the stock market at 12.2.
它的董事会谨慎地选择了收购目标。Borgonni 一直在寻找有潜力的公司，但这些公司管理不善，市值
低于面值，因此 Borgonni 在股市上的市盈率(P/E)保持在 12.2。
Borgonni’s 20X3 figures show a profit after tax of $886m and it has 375m shares in issue.
Borgonni 2013 年的数据显示税后利润为 8.86 亿美元，发行了 3.75 亿股股票。
Venitra Pvt is a well -established owner -managed business. In financial terms it has a rather chequered
history with its up and downs corresponding directly with the state of the global economy. Over the past five
years, its profits have fallen each year with the 20X3 values standing at:
Venitra Pvt 是一家成熟的业主管理企业。 在财务方面， 它有着相当曲折的历史， 它的起起落落直接与全
球经济状况相对应。在过去的五年里，它的利润逐年下降，20X3 的价值为:
$m
Revenue 1500
Operating profit 480
Interest (137)
Profit before tax 343
Taxation@25% (86)
Profit after tax 257
Number of shares in issue 150m
EPS $1.72
However, with economists predicting an upturn in the Western economies, Venitra’s management team feel
that revenue will increase by 6% per annum up to and including 20X7. The company’s operating profit
margin is not expected to change for the foreseeable future.
然而，随着经济学家对西方经济好转的预测，Venitra 的管理团队认为，到 20X7 年，收入将以每年 6%
的速度增长。在可预见的未来，该公司的营业利润率预计不会发生变化。
Operating profits are shown after deducting non -cash expenses (including tax allowable depreciation) of
$125m. This is expected to increase in line with sales. However, the company has recently spent $210m on
purchase of non -current assets. Venitra’s management believes this value will have to increase by 10% per
annum until 20X7 to enable the company to remain competitive. Venitra has estimated its overall cost of
capital to be approximately 12%, but this assumes it will maintain its debt to equity ratio at 40:60.
营业利润是在扣除 1.25 亿美元的非现金支出(包括允许抵税的折旧)后得出的。 预计这将随着销售额的增
长而增加。然而，该公司最近斥资2.1 亿美元收购非流动资产。Venitra 管理层认为，到 20X7 年，这一
价值必须以每年 10%的速度增长，才能使公司保持竞争力。Venitra 估计其总资本成本约为 12%，但这
是假设它将保持其债务与权益比率为 40:60。
Some of Venitra’s major shareholders are not so confident about the future and would like to sell the
business as a going concern. The minimum price they would consider would be the fair value of the shares,
plus a 10% premium. Venitra’s CFO believes the best way to find the fair value of the shares is to discount
the forecasted free cash flows of the firm, assuming that beyond 20X7 these will grow at a rate of 3% per
annum indefinitely.
Venitra 的一些大股东对未来不太有信心，他们希望将公司作为持续经营的企业出售。他们会考虑的最
低价格是股票的公允价值加上 10%的溢价。Venitra 的首席财务官认为，找到股票公允价值的最佳方法
是贴现公司预测的自由现金流，假设这些现金流在 20X7 之后将无限期地以每年 3%的速度增长。
Requirement
(a) As at 1 January 20X4, prepare a schedule of Venitra’s forecast free cash flows for the firm. Ascertain the
fair value of the Venitra’s equity on a per share basis.
(10 marks)
在 2004 年 1 月 1 日，准备一份 Venitra 预测公司自由现金流的时间表。确定Venitra 每股股权的公允价
值。
(b) Borgonni intends to make an offer to Venitra based upon a share for share swap. Borgonni will exchange
one of its shares for every two Venitra shares. Assuming that Borgonni can maintain its earnings rating at
12.2, calculate the percentage gain in equity value that will earned by both groups of shareholders?
(8 marks)
Borgonni 打算向 Venitra 提出基于股份交换的报价。Borgonni 将以 1 股换 2 股 Venitra 股票。 假设Borgonni
能够维持其市盈率在 12.2，计算两组股东将获得的股权价值增长百分比?
(c) What factors should the Venitra shareholders consider before deciding whether to accept or reject the
offer made by Borgonni?
(7 marks)
Venitra 股东在决定接受或拒绝 Borgonni 的收购要约之前，应该考虑哪些因素?
(25 marks)
Solution
At this stage, you can choose one of two ways to follow my approach to answering this question. My
solution to each part along with the relevant explanation is shown below.
在这个阶段，您可以选择以下两种方法中的一种来遵循我的方法来回答这个问题。我对每个部分的解
决方案以及相关的解释如下所示。
(a) Venitra – Forecast Free Cash Flows and Value of Equity
Notes and explanations:
1. As we are preparing a valuation as at 1 January 20X4, I have set up columns for each future period. I
need to prepare a detailed forecast for the first four years only. After 20X7, the FCF of the firm will increase at
the rate of 3% per annum. I have started with a 20X3 column just as a reference point.
1.由于我们正在准备 2024 年 1 月 1 日的估值，我已经为未来的每个时期设置了对应列。我只需要准备
一份前四年的详细预测。在 20X7 年之后，公司的 FCF 将以每年 3%的速度增长。我已经开始与 20X3 列
只是作为一个参考点。
2. Revenue has been increased by 6% per annum using the 20X3 sales as a base point.
2.以 20X3 的收入为基准，收入每年增长 6%。
3. The operating margin in 20X3 was 32% (480/1,500). This will be maintained for the foreseeable
future.
3.20X3 年营业利润率为 32%(480/ 1500)。在可预见的将来，这种情况将保持下去。
4. One key factor is to ignore the interest payment. FCF for the firm must EXCLUDE interest. This is
because the cost of capital used to discount these flows is the company WACC. The WACC takes into account
the interest element and its tax benefit.
4.一个关键因素是忽略利息支付。 公司的FCF 必须不包括利息。 这是因为用于贴现这些流动的资本成本
是公司的 WACC。WACC 考虑了利息因素及其税收优惠。
5. Income tax on company profits is charged at 25%. In this case, it is to be paid in the same year as the
profits are earned.
5.公司利润的所得税按 25%征收。本例中，它将在赚取利润的同一年支付。
6. The operating profit is after deducting non -cash expenses, which are allowable for taxation. These
include tax allowable depreciation. In this question, these expenses will increase in line with sales and they
have to be added back after the tax charge has been computed.
6.营业利润是扣除非现金费用后的利润， 非现金费用是允许纳税的。 这些包括可课税折旧。 在这个问题
中，这些费用会随着销售额的增加而增加，在计算完税费后，这些费用必须重新加回去。
7. Venitra needs to set aside cash each year to maintain its non -current asset (NCA) base. The amount
of capital expenditure will increase by 10% per annum for the next four years.
Please note that in some past questions, it has been assumed that the non-cash expenses equal the required
investment in NCAs. Hence, the add back and deduction will cancel out.
7.Venitra 每年都需要留出现金来维持其非流动资产(NCA)基础。在未来四年，资本开支将以每年 10%的
速度增长。请注意，在过去的一些问题中，已经假设非现金费用等于NCAs 所需的投资。因此，添加和
扣除将被抵消。
8. The forecast FCF for the firm is a simple totalling up process for the first four years. After 20X7, the
FCF are expected to grow at a rate of 3% per annum indefinitely. Therefore, the 20X8 value is calculated as
$305m x 1.03.
8.预测公司的现金流动资金是一个简单的前四年累计过程。在 20X7 之后，预计 FCF 将无限期地以每年
3%的速度增长。因此，20X8 的价值计算为 3.05 亿美元 x 1.03。
9. As stated in point 4, the relevant discount rate to apply to the FCF of the firm is Venitra’s WACC. This
has been estimated as 12%. The first four discount factors have been copied from the discount tables
provided at the end of the exam paper. The discount factor for 20X8 and beyond must take into account both
a 3% per annum growth rate as well as a cost of capital of 12%. The financial mathematics for a delayed
perpetuity with an annual growth rate is (1/(0.12 – 0.03) x 0.636).
9.如第 4 点所述， 适用于公司FCF 的相关贴现率是 Venitra 的 WACC。 据估计这一比例为12%。 前四个折
现系数是从试卷末尾提供的系数表中抄来的。20X8 年及以后的贴现系数必须考虑到每年 3%的增长率和
12%的资本成本。固定增长率的永续年金系数为(1/(0.12 - 0.03)x 0.636)。
10. The value of the entity is the total of the present value of the forecast FCF. However, this amount
represents a combination of the debt and equity together. Venitra’s equity is equal to 60% of the value of the
firm.
10.该实体的价值是预测 FCF 现值的总和。但是，这个数额是债务和权益的总和。Venitra 的股权相当于
公司价值的 60%。
11. The question requirement is to ascertain the equity value per share. Therefore, $1,866m /150m =
$12.44. This is the fair value of one share of Venitra.
11.问题的要求是确定每股的权益价值。因此，18.66 亿美元/ 1.5 亿美元= 12.44 美元。这是 Venitra 一股
的公允价值。
12. Finally, I have computed the P/E ratio for Venitra. Although this was not specifically asked for, this
value will be needed for part (b).
12.最后，我计算了 Venitra 的市盈率。虽然没有特别要求，但(b)部分将需要此值。
(b) Percentage gain in equity value – both groups of shareholders
The first stage is to compute the current market price per share for Borgonni:
$m
2023-earnings 886
x
P/E ratio 12.2
Value of equity $10,809m
No of issued shares 375m
Value per share (Po) $28.82
Borgonni expects to maintain its P/E ratio after acquiring Venitra. Therefore, the post-acquisition value of the
two entities combined together can be ascertained by applying Borgonni’s P/E ratio to the sum of the latest
earnings of each company. As the P/E ratio of Borgonni (12.2) exceeds that of Venitra (7.23) this is known as
‘bootstrapping’.
Borgonni希望在收购 Venitra后保持其市盈率。 因此， 两个实体合并后的收购价值可以通过将Borgonni P/E
比率应用于每个公司最新收益的总和来确定。 由于Borgonni(12.2)的市盈率超过 Venitra(7.23)， 这被称为
“自举”。
$m
Borgonni – 20X3 PAT 886
Venitra – 20X3 PAT 257
P/E ratio – Borgonni 12.2
Post-acquisition value $13,945m
The purchase is to be funded via a share for share exchange. Borgonni will issue one new share in its
company in return for every two shares in Venitra.
此次收购将通过股份交换的方式进行融资。Borgonni 将发行其公司的一股新股，以换取 Venitra 的每两
股。
Borgonni issued share capital 375m
Additional shares issued  (150m/2) 75m
New total issued share capital 450m
The new equity value for a Borgonni share is now $13,945m/450m = $30.99.
现在，Borgonni 股票的新股本价值为 139.45 亿美元/ 4.5 亿美元= 30.99 美元。
However, although many candidates may stop at this point (believing they have reached Utopia!) the
requirement has not been addressed. The question asks candidates to ascertain the gain that will be made
on the equity value to each group of shareholders. Looking at each in turn:
然而，尽管许多候选人可能会在这一点上停下来(相信他们已经达到了乌托邦!)但这个要求还没有得到
解决。这个问题要求候选人确定每组股东的股权价值将带来的收益。依次来看:
Borgonni shareholders’ gain (($30.99 – $28.82)/$28.82) x 100 = 7.53%
Borgonni 股东收益(30.99 - 28.82 美元)/ 28.82 美元)× 100 = 7.53%
To compute the gain for the Venitra shareholders, the candidate must first compute the post -acquisition
value of a Venitra share. Venitra shareholders gave up two shares in their company to receive one new
Borgonni share. Therefore, the equivalent post -acquisition value of a Venitra share will be $30.99/2 =
$15.50.
为了计算 Venitra 股东的收益，候选人必须首先计算 Venitra 股票的收购后价值。Venitra 的股东放弃了
他们公司的两股股票，以获得一股新的 Borgonni 股票。因此，Venitra 股票的收购后等价价值为 30.99
美元/2 = 15.50 美元。
The fair value of a Venitra share, per part (a), was $12.44. Therefore, the Venitra shareholders gain 24.60%.
Venitra 每股(a)部分的公允价值为 12.44 美元。因此，Venitra 股东收益为 24.60%。
(c) Factors to consider – for the Venitra shareholders
There is no one correct answer to this part. As long as the candidate produces a reasonable number of valid
points, they will earn decent marks.
这部分没有唯一的正确答案。只要考生写出有效的得分点，他们就能获得不错的分数。
My answer would read as follows:
• Venitra shareholders wanted a gain of at least 10% on the fair value of the shares. Based upon the
figures, they are gaining nearly 25%, which is likely to encourage them to accept the offer.
Venitra 股东希望股票的公允价值至少增加 10%。根据这些数据，他们获得了近 25%的收益，这可能会
鼓励他们接受这份工作。
• The share for share exchange may be beneficial for tax planning. Any capital gain earned on the sale
of the shares will be rolled over until the gain is realised in cash.
股份交换的股份可能有利于税务筹划。出售股票所得的任何资本利得都将转到现金兑现为止。
• Venitra may decide to reject this bid believing that Borgonni will make a more lucrative offer in the
future.
Venitra 可能会决定拒绝这个出价，因为他相信 Borgonni 在未来会提出一个更有利可图的报价。
• The fair value of the Venitra shares has been based upon forecasts and estimates. Some sensitivity
analysis needs to be carried out to ensure the value is robust.
Venitra 股票的公允价值基于预测和估计。需要进行一些敏感性分析，以确保该值是可靠的。
• There is no guarantee that Borgonni can maintain its P/E ratio at 12.2. There may well be an element
of dilution given the much lower P/E of Venitra. Hence, the post-acquisition value is then uncertain.
谁也不能保证 Borgonni 能保持 12.2 的市盈率。考虑到 Venitra 的低得多的市盈率，很可能存在稀释因
素。因此，收购后的价值是不确定的。
• Not all Venitra shareholders want to sell the company. The constitution of the company may allow
the takeover to be blocked unless a certain percentage majority of the shareholders agree.
并非所有 Venitra 股东都想出售公司。 公司章程可能允许收购被阻止， 除非有一定比例的多数股东同意。
• Venitra shareholders may also feel that as the economic conditions are improving, their business
prospects and value will get better. They may reject Borgonni’s approach and stay as an independent
company.
Venitra 股东也可能认为，随着经济状况的改善，他们的业务前景和价值将会变得更好。他们可能会拒
绝 Borgonni 的做法，继续作为一家独立公司存在。
As you can see, business valuation questions require you to have a disciplined approach and to demonstrate
that you have studied and understand this key area of the syllabus. Although equity valuations are an ‘art
not a science’, you have to produce an answer that is pleasing to the eyes of the examining and marking
team.
正如你所看到的，商业评估问题要求你有一个严格的方法，并证明你已经学习和理解了教学大纲的这
个关键领域。尽管股票估值是一门“艺术而非科学”，但你必须给出一个让阅卷小组满意的答案。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Business valuations.pdf

stem_zh:
Conditional probability
条件概率
Management decision making in both financial and performance management involves making choices based upon what
may happen in the future. Such future events can be predicted but decision makers can rarely be 100% certain that these
future events will actually occur or will occur in the way in which they are forecast.
财务和绩效管理中的管理决策涉及到根据未来可能发生的事情做出选择。 这样的未来事件是可以预测的， 但决策
者很少能 100%确定这些未来事件是否会实际发生或将以预测的方式发生。
There are a variety of ways of dealing with such uncertainty in forecast outcomes. In this article, we shall look at how to
consider all of the possible outcomes that may arise, together with their associated chances of occurring. This is referred
to as a situation where we are evaluating risk, since there is past information or experience which can provide statistical
evidence in order to assist in determining the possibility of each event occurring.
在预测结果中， 处理这种不确定性的方法有多种。 在本文中， 我们将研究如何考虑可能出现的所有可能结果， 以
及它们发生的相关机会。 这被我们称为评估风险， 因为过去的信息或经验可以提供统计证据， 以帮助确定每个事
件发生的可能性。
Expected values, decision trees and combined probabilities
期望值、决策树和组合概率
A commonly used way of evaluating decisions is via the use of expected values.
评估决策的常用方法是使用期望值。
An expected value summarises all the different possible outcomes by weighting the possible outcomes by their
probabilities and then summing the result.
期望值通过对可能的结果进行概率加权，然后对结果进行求和，得出所有不同可能的结果。
Problems where one or more decisions have to be taken can become more complex and may require the use of a decision
tree, with expected values being used to evaluate each of the decisions.
必须做出一个或多个决策的问题可能会变得更加复杂，并且可能需要使用决策树，使用期望值来评估每个决策。
A decision tree is a diagrammatic representation of a problem, where the decision maker needs to consider the logical
sequence of events.
决策树是一个问题的图解表示，帮助决策者考虑事件的逻辑顺序。
Since one event may depend upon another, we may get situations where event one has a certain probability of occurring
and event two, which depends on event one occurring, has another probability of occurring. In such circumstances, we
have a situation of combined probabilities
由于一个事件可能依赖于另一个事件， 我们可能会得到这样的情况： 事件一具有一定的发生概率， 而事件二依赖
于事件一的发生，具有另一种发生概率。在这种情况下，我们有一个组合概率的情况。
For example, if event one has a 0.6 chance of occurring and subsequent event two a 0.75 chance of occurring, then overall
the probability of both events occurring is:0.6 x 0.75 = 0.45 ie a 45% chance of occurring.
例如， 如果事件一有0.6 的发生概率， 而随后的事件二有0.75 的发生概率， 那么两个事件发生的总体概率为：0.6
x 0.75=0.45 即发生的可能性为 45%。
We shall look at such concepts in the following example, which demonstrates how techniques acquired from the Applied
Skills exams can be used in the Strategic Professional exams.
我们将在下面的例子中研究这些概念，该例子展示了如何在战略专业考试中使用该技术。
Scenario
Brisport Master Motor Co (Brisport) designs, manufactures and sells a range of components for the motor car industry.
The design team has recently designed a new component for inclusion into hybrid cars. The component greatly enhances
the battery ‘road time’ and therefore reduces the frequency with which the battery has to be recharged.
Brisport Master Motor Co(Brisport) 为汽车行业设计、制造和销售一系列零部件。设计团队最近设计了一种用于混
合动力汽车的新零件。该组件大大增强了电池的“上路时间”，从而减少了电池充电的频率。
The company can either sell the design now, for its initial market value of $400,000, or attempt to develop the design into
a marketable product, which can be supplied to the motor industry. This development would have an initial outlay of
$300,000 now and the component would take one year to be developed. In such a fast moving market, the component
is likely to have a market life as a saleable product of just five years after development.
该公司可以现在以 40 万美元的初始市场价值出售该设计，也可以尝试将该设计开发为可销售的产品，供汽车行
业使用。该项目的初期支出目前为 30 万美元，开发该项目需要一年时间。在这样一个快速变化的市场中，该零
件可能在开发后仅有五年的市场寿命。
If the company decides to develop the component, the chances of succeeding in developing the design into the
marketable product are 80%. If the attempt to develop fails, the design can only be sold, in one year’s time, for half of its
earlier market value.
如果公司决定开发零件， 成功将设计开发为可销售产品的概率为 80%。 如果开发尝试失败， 设计只能在一年内以
其早期市场价值的一半出售。
If the attempt to develop the design succeeds the company has a choice of either selling both the design and the rights
to sell the developed component, or marketing the component themselves.
