如果开发设计的尝试成功，公司可以选择出售设计和出售开发零件的销售权，或者自己销售零件。
Selling the design would yield $300,000 in one year’s time and $160,000 in royalty payments for each of the five years
thereafter (years 2 to 6).
出售该设计将在一年内获得 30 万美元，并在此后的五年中每年获得 16 万美元的特许权使用费（第 2 年至第 6
年） 。
If the component is marketed by Brisport then there is a 75% probability that the product will be popular and will generate
cash inflows of $440,000 per annum but there is a 25% probability that it will be unpopular and it will generate cash
outflows of $55,000 per annum. Both cash flow figures are also for each of years 2 to 6.
如果该零件由 Brisport 销售，则有 75%的可能性该产品将受到欢迎，并将每年产生 440,000 美元的现金流入，但
有 25%的可能性它将不受欢迎，并将产生每年 55000 美元的现金流出。这两个现金流也是产生于第 2 年至第 6
年。
Brisport uses a weighted average cost of capital of 7% to discount its future cash flows.
Brisport 使用 7%的加权平均资本成本来贴现其未来现金流。
The management of Brisport Master Motor Co seeks your advice as to their best course of action.
Brisport Master Motor Co 管理层向您寻求最佳行动方案的建议。
Solution
There are two decisions which need to be taken by the company:
1. sell the design or develop it
2. if it is developed, then whether to sell the design and the rights to sell the developed component, or market it
themselves
公司需要做出两项决定：
1. 出售或开发设计
2. 如果它已经开发，那么是出售设计和出售开发零件的销售权，还是自己销售它
Initial workings relating to present value of  future cash flows using discount  factors at 7%  (all values rounded to the
nearest $000s):
Discount factor for T1=0.935
Annuity factor(T2-T6)3.832(Annuity factor(T1-T6)-0.935=(4.767-0.935))
'Development fails'
Selling the design in one year's time for half of its initial market value.
200x0.935=187
'Development succeeds'
Selling the design in one year's time plus royalties (T2-T6)
(300x0.935)+(160x3.832)=281+613=894
Successful marketing of the component (T2-T6)
440x3.832=1686
Unsuccessful marketing of the component (T2-T6)
-55x3.832=-211
In order to evaluate decision 1, decision 2 needs to be evaluated first. In other words, the values we use in decision 1
need to be determined by the decision we take in decision 2.
为了评估决定 1，需要首先评估决定 2。换句话说，我们在决策 1 中使用的值需要由我们在决策 2 中做出的决策
来确定。
Decision 2
The net present value ($000s) on the 75% path is
75%可能性上的净现值为
1,686 – 300 = 1,386
Taken together with the net present value ($000s) on the 25% path of
25%可能性上的净现值为
(211) – 300 = (511)
there is an expected net present value of choosing to market the component of ($000s):
自己销售净现值的期望值
[0.75 x 1,386]+[0.25 x(511)] =1,040 – 128=912
This is a higher value than the option of selling the design and the rights to sell the developed component for a net
present value of $594,000 ($894,000 – $300,000). Therefore, if the development goes ahead, it will be more beneficial
to market the product.
这比出售设计和出售开发零件的销售权的净现值 594,000 美元（894,000 -300,000）价值更高。因此，如果开发进
行下去，自己出售产品更有利。
Of course, we still need to evaluate decision 1, whether to develop at all. The ‘success’ of the expected present value of
$912,000 in decision 2 has an 80% chance of arising, but there is a 20% chance of the development not succeeding and
recouping just half of the initial market value, that being $187,000 in present value terms, resulting in the company being
worse off by $113,000 in present value terms after taking the development costs into account.
当然，我们仍然需要评估决定 1，是否要研发。决定 2 中预期现值 912,000 美元的“成功”有 80%的机会出现，但
开发不成功的可能性为 20%，仅收回最初市场价值的一半，即按现值计算的187,000 美元，在考虑到开发成本后
最后结果是该公司的现值损失了 113,000 美元。
Hence, the expected net present value of the development option of decision 1 can be calculated ($000s):
因此，可以计算出决定 1 的发展备选方案的预期净现值（000 美元）
= [0.80 x 912] + [0.20 x (113)]
=730 – 23
= 707
Since this is higher than the option to sell the design at time 0, $400,000, on an expected value basis, the component
should be developed and marketed.
由于在期望值的基础上计算结果比在初始时出售设计的选项 400,000 美元高，因此应开发和销售该零件。
Attitude to risk
The expected value approach assumes risk neutrality, but not all management decision makers are risk neutral. A risk
averse management would, in this scenario, be concerned with the 20% probability of being $113,000 worse off in
present value terms should the development decision go on to fail.
期望值方法假设风险中性， 但并非所有管理决策者都是风险中性的。 规避风险的管理将关注于如果开发决策继续
失败，按现值计算，损失 113,000 美元的可能性为 20%。
Furthermore, having taken the decision (at node 2) that marketing the component is preferred to selling both the design
and developed component there is a further risk of losses, since there is a 25% chance of the component being unpopular
leaving the company worse off by $511,000 in present value terms.
此外， （在节点2 处）决定销售组件而不是同时销售设计和开发组件，存在进一步的损失风险，因为组件不受欢
迎的可能性为 25%，按现值计算，公司损失为 511,000 美元。
Combined with the 80% probability of the development being successful, there is an overall 20% chance of this $511,000
loss. This 20% is known as a conditional probability since it depends upon the 80% (0.80) success rate firstly and then
depends on the 25% (0.25) unpopularity chance. Hence,0.80 x 0.25 = 0.20 ie 20%
再加上开发成功的概率为 80%，这 511,000 美元的损失总体概率为 20%。这 20%被称为条件概率，因为它首先取
决于 80%(0.80)的成功率，然后取决于 25%(0.25)的不受欢迎机会。
For completeness, there is of course a 75% chance of the component being popular if marketed, and hence the overall
combined probability of a successful development together with a marketing campaign which results in popularity is:
0.80 x 0.75 = 0.60 ie 60%
为了完整性， 如果销售该组件，则有75%的机会受到欢迎， 因此， 成功开发后营销活动也受欢迎的总体组合概率
为 60%
Summary
Outcome Probability Net present value ($000s)
Development succeeds and component is popular 60% 1,386
Development succeeds but component is unpopular 20% (511)
Development fails 20% (113)
Therefore, be aware that expected values can lead to a false sense of security. The expected NPV of $707,000 is an average.
In other words, it is the average NPV if the decision is repeated over and over again. But is that useful in this situation?
This is a one-off development of a product and therefore only one of the outcomes listed in the table above will actually
occur. (This is analogous to tossing a coin once. We know that the outcome will either be a head or a tail, not the expected
value of ‘half a head’ or ‘half a tail’). As can be seen above, there is a 40% chance that the NPV will be negative, and that
is maybe a risk that the company is not prepared to take.
因此，请注意，期望值可能导致虚假的安全感。净现值的期望值为 707,000 美元是加权平均值。换句话说，它是
是该决策被反复重复后得出的平均结果。 但这在这种情况下有用吗？投资是对产品的一次性开发， 因此上表中列
出的结果中只有一个实际发生。(这类似于抛硬币一次。我们知道结果要么是正面，要么是反面，而不是“半个正
面”或“半个反面”的期望值)。 正如上面所看到的，NPV 有 40%的可能性是负的， 这可能是公司不准备承担的风险。
Furthermore, be aware that the analysis largely depends upon the values of the probabilities prescribed. Often these are
subjective estimates made by the decision makers and it would only take relatively small changes in these to alter one of
the decisions.
此外， 请注意， 分析在很大程度上取决于给定的概率值。 这些通常是决策者所做的主观估计， 只需对其中一个数
值进行相对较小的更改。
For example, in decision 2, if the probability of successful marketing falls to 55%, then the expected NPV of ‘marketing’
falls to:[0.55 x 1,386] + [0.45 x (511)]=762 - 230 = 532
例如，在决策 2 中，如果成功销售的概率降至 55%，则“销售”的预期 NPV 降至 532,000 美元。
This is now a lower value than the option of selling the design and the rights to sell the developed component for a net
present value of $594,000.
这将会低于出售设计和出售开发零件的销售权净现值为 594,000 美元。
Such sensitivity analysis can be performed on other variables within the model.
也可以对模型中的其他变量进行这种敏感性分析。
Of course, decision models such as this are only as good as the information used. In reality there would probably be a
much wider range of possible outcomes than the discrete outcomes described above. In other words, the problems in
examination questions are a simpler version of what is usually found in reality, but nonetheless are very useful techniques
of which you should be aware.
当然， 像这样的决策模型与所使用的信息的质量是正相关的。 实际上， 可能会有比上述结果更广泛的可能性。换
句话说，试题中的问通常题是现实中问题的简单版本，但期望值仍然是非常有用的技术。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Conditional probability.pdf

stem_zh:
Currency swap
货币互换
A currency swap is an agreement in which two parties exchange the principal amount of a loan and the interest in
one currency for the principal and interest in another currency.
货币互换是指双方达成的一项协议，在该协议中，双方将一种货币的贷款本金和利息与另一种货币的本金
和利息进行交换。
At the inception of the swap, the equivalent principal amounts are exchanged at the spot rate.
在互换开始时，双方按即期汇率交换等额的本金。
During the length of the swap each party pays the interest on the swapped principal loan amount.
在互换存续期间，各方支付所交换本金贷款金额的利息。
At the end of the swap the principal amounts are swapped back at either the prevailing spot rate, or at a pre-agreed
rate such as the rate of the original exchange of principals. Using the original rate would remove transaction risk on
the swap.
在互换结束时， 本金按当时的即期汇率或预先商定的汇率 （如最初本金交换时的汇率） 换回。 采用最初的汇
率可以消除互换中的交易风险。
Currency swaps are used to obtain foreign currency loans at a better interest rate than a company could obtain by
borrowing directly in a foreign market or as a method of hedging transaction risk on foreign currency loans which it
has already taken out.
货币互换用于帮助企业以比直接在国外市场借款更优惠的利率获取外币贷款，或者作为一种对冲企业已取
得的外币贷款交易风险的方法。
We will consider how a fixed for fixed currency swap works by looking at an example.
我们将通过一个例子来探讨固定利率对固定利率的货币互换是如何运作的。
An American company may be able to borrow in the United States at a rate of 6%, but requires a loan in rand for an
investment in South Africa, where the relevant borrowing rate is 9%. At the same time, a South African company
wishes to finance a project in the United States, where its direct borrowing rate is 11%, compared to a borrowing
rate of 8% in South Africa.
一家美国公司在美国借款利率可能为  6%，但该公司在南非投资需要兰特贷款，而南非当地的借款利率为
9%。与此同时，一家南非公司希望为其在美国的一个项目融资，该公司在美国直接借款的利率为 11%，而
在南非的借款利率为 8%。
Each party can benefit from the other's interest rate through a fixed -for-fixed currency swap. In this case, the
American company can borrow U.S. dollars for 6%, and then it can lend the funds to the South African company at
6%. The South African company can borrow South African rand at 8%, then lend the funds to the U.S. company for
the same amount.
通过固定利率对固定利率的货币互换， 双方都能从对方的利率优势中获益。 在这种情况下， 美国公司可以以
6% 的利率借入美元， 然后以 6% 的利率将资金贷给南非公司。 南非公司可以以 8% 的利率借入南非兰特，
然后以相同金额将资金贷给美国公司。
Currency swaps can also involve exchanging two variable rate loans, or fixed rate borrowing for variable rate
borrowing. Let’s consider a case where a company exchanges fixed rate borrowing for variable rate borrowing.
货币互换还可以涉及交换两笔浮动利率贷款，或者将固定利率借款与浮动利率借款进行交换。让我们来看
一个公司将固定利率借款转换为浮动利率借款的案例。
Barrow Co, a company based in the USA, wants to borrow €500m over five years to finance an investment in the
Eurozone.
美国的巴罗公司（Barrow Co）希望借入 5 亿欧元，期限为五年，以资助其在欧元区的一项投资。
Today’s spot exchange rate between the Euro and US $ is €1·1200 = $1.
当前欧元与美元的即期汇率为 1.12 欧元 = 1 美元。
Barrow Co’s bank can arrange a currency swap with Greening Co. The swap would be for the principal amount of
€500m, with a swap of principal immediately and in five years’ time, with both these exchanges being at today’s
spot rate.
巴罗公司的银行可以安排其与格林宁公司 （Greening Co） 进行货币互换。 此次互换的本金金额为 5 亿欧元，
在互换开始时和五年后进行本金交换，且这两次交换均按当前的即期汇率进行。
Barrow Co’s bank would charge an annual fee of 0.4% in € for arranging the swap.
巴罗公司的银行将按互换本金的 0.4% 每年收取欧元手续费。
The benefit of the swap will be split equally between the two parties.
互换带来的收益将由双方平分。
The relevant borrowing rates for each party are as follows:
双方的相关借款利率如下：
Barrow Co
巴罗公司
Greening Co
格林宁公司
USA
美国
3.6% 4.5%
Eurozone
欧元区
ESTR + 1.5%
欧元短期利率（ESTR）+ 1.5%
ESTR + 0.8%
欧元短期利率（ESTR）+ 0.8%
We will see what the gain on the swap for each party will be.
我们来看看双方在此次互换中的收益情况。
Barrow Co 巴罗公司 Greening Co 格林宁公司 Benefit 收益

Gain on swap
互换收益
0.8% 0.8% 1.6%
Bank fee
银行手续费
(0.2%) (0.2%) (0.4%)
Final gain
最终收益
0.6% 0.6% 1.2%
Using this gain to work out the overall result for each company, we can provide an illustration of how the swap could
work as follows:
利用这些收益来计算每家公司的总体结果，我们可以通过以下方式展示此次互换的运作情况：
Barrow Co 巴罗公司 Greening Co 格林宁公司

The overall result show each party paying 0.6% less than they would have paid in they had borrowed directly in the
foreign markets.
总体结果表明，双方的支付利率均比直接在国外市场借款低 0.6%。
Barrow Co’s original principal amount of €500m would be exchanged at the inception of the swap for $446,428,517.
The principal would be swapped back five years later, at the end of the agreement, at the original spot rate.
巴罗公司最初的 5 亿欧元本金在互换开始时将兑换为 446,428,517 美元。五年后，在协议结束时，本金将
按最初的即期汇率换回。

stem_zh:
Determining interest rate forwards and their application to swap valuation
确定利率远期合同及其在互换估值中的应用
The previous article on bonds (see 'Related links') considered the relationship between bond prices, the yield curve and
the yield to maturity. It demonstrated how bonds can be valued and how a yield curve may be derived using bonds of
the same risk class but of different maturities. It also showed how individual company yield curves maybe estimated.
上一篇关于债券的文章（见 “相关链接”）探讨了债券价格、收益率曲线与到期收益率之间的关系。文章展示
了如何对债券进行估值， 以及如何利用相同风险等级但不同期限的债券推导出收益率曲线。 此外， 还说明了如何
估算单个公司的收益率曲线。
This article follows on from that, and will show how interest rate forwards may be determined from the spot yield curve.
It will then briefly discuss what they mean, before proceeding to show how they may be used in determining the value
of an interest rate swap. The second article addresses the learning required in the E1 and E3 areas of the Advanced
Financial Management Syllabus and Study Guide.
本文承接上文， 将阐述如何从即期收益率曲线确定远期利率。 接着， 在说明远期利率如何用于确定利率互换价值
之前， 本文将简要探讨其含义。 第二篇文章涉及 《高级财务管理教学大纲及学习指南》 中 E1 和 E3 部分所需学
习的内容。
Determination of interest rate forwards
确定远期利率
Supposing that a bank assesses and quotes the following rates to a company, based on the annual spot yield curve for
that company’s risk class:
假设一家银行根据某公司风险等级对应的年度即期收益率曲线，对该公司评估并报出以下利率
One-year: 3.50%
Two-year: 4.60%
Three-year: 5.40%
Four-year: 6.10%
Five-year: 6.30%
This indicates that the company would have to: pay interest at 3.50% if it wants to borrow a sum of money for one year;
pay interest at 4.60% per year if it wants to borrow a sum of money for two years; pay interest at 5.40% per year if it
wants borrow a sum of money for three years; and so on.
这意味着该公司若有以下借款需求，需支付相应利率：
若借款期限为一年，年利率为 3.50%；
若借款期限为两年，每年利率为 4.60%；
若借款期限为三年，每年利率为 5.40%；依此类推
Alternatively, for a two-year loan, the company could opt to borrow a sum of money for only one year, at an interest
rate of 3.50%, and then again for another year, commencing in one year’s time, instead of borrowing the money for a
total of two years.
或者，对于一笔为期两年的贷款，该公司可以选择仅以 3.50% 的利率借入一笔资金一年，然后从一年后开始，
再借入一年，而不是一次性借入资金两年
Although the company would be uncertain about the interest rate in one year’s time, it could request a forward rate
from the bank that is fixed today – for example, through a 12v24 forward rate agreement (FRA). The question then
arises: how may the value of the 12v24 FRA be determined?
尽管该公司无法确定一年后的利率，但它可以向银行申请一个如今就确定的远期利率 —— 例如，通过一份
12v24 远期利率协议（FRA）。于是问题就来了：12v24 远期利率协议的价值要如何确定呢？
A forward rate commencing in one year for a borrowed sum lasting a year can be calculated as follows:
一年后起息、借款期限为一年的远期利率，可按如下方式计算
两年借款，复利计息的本息和为 1.046^2
一年复利计息的本息和为 1.035，则 12-24 一年期远期合同利率为（1.046^2）/1.035=1.057
剩下的以此类推
In summary:
Supposing the company wants to borrow a sum of money for three years on the basis of the above rates:
i. it could pay annual interest at a rate of 5.40% in each of the three years, or
ii. it could pay interest at a rate 3.50% in the first year, 5.71% in the second year and 7.02% in the third year, or
iii. it could pay annual interest at a rate of 4.60% in each of the first two years and 7.02% in the third year.
假设该公司想根据上述利率借入一笔为期三年的款项，有以下几种情况：
i. 公司可以在三年中的每一年都按 5.40% 的年利率支付利息；
ii. 公司可以在第一年按 3.50% 的利率支付利息，在第二年按 5.71% 的利率支付利息，在第三年按 7.02% 的
利率支付利息；
iii. 公司可以在前两年的每一年都按 4.60% 的年利率支付利息，在第三年按 7.02% 的利率支付利息。
Using interest rate forwards to value a simple interest rate swap contract
运用远期利率对简单利率互换合约进行估值
Supposing the above company has $100m borrowings in the form of variable interest rate loans repayable in five years
and pays interest based on the above yield curve. It expects interest rates to increase in the future and is therefore
keen to fix its interest rate payments.
假设上述公司有 1 亿美元的可变利率贷款借款，期限为五年，且根据上述收益率曲线支付利息。该公司预计未
来利率会上升，因此渴望锁定其利息支付。
The bank offers to swap the variable interest rate payments for a fixed rate, such that the company pays a fixed rate of
interest to the bank in exchange for receiving a variable rate of return from the bank based on the above yield rates
less 50 basis points. The variable rate receipts from the bank will then be used to pay the interest on the loan.
银行提出以固定利率互换该公司的可变利率支付， 即公司向银行支付固定利率， 作为交换， 公司从银行获得基于
上述收益率减去 50 个基点的可变利率回报。公司从银行收到的可变利率款项将用于支付贷款利息。
The fixed equivalent rate of interest the company will pay the bank for the swap can be calculated as follows:
公司为此次互换向银行支付的固定等效利率可按以下方式计算
The current expected amounts of interest the company expects to receive from the bank, based on year 1 spot rate
and years 2, 3, 4 and 5 forward rates are:
基于第 1 年的即期利率以及第 2、3、4 和 5 年的远期利率，公司预期从银行收取的当前预期利息金额如下：
Year 1     0.0300 x $100m = $3.00m
Year 2     0.0521 x $100m = $5.21m
Year 3     0.0652 x $100m = $6.52m
Year 4     0.0773 x $100m = $7.73m
Year 5     0.0660 x $100m = $6.60m
Note: The rates used to calculate the annual amounts are reduced by 50 basis points or 0.5%.
用于计算年度金额的利率下调了 50 个基点，即 0.5%，因此每年的预期利率为
Yr 1 3.5%-0.5%=0.0300
Yr 2: 5.71%-0.5%=0.0521
Yr3: 7.02%-0.5%=6.52% 以此类推
At the start of the swap, the net present value of the swap receipts based on the variable rates from the bank will be
the same as the costs based on the fixed amount paid to the bank.
在互换开始时， 基于从银行获得的可变利率计算的互换收益净现值， 将与基于支付给银行的固定金额计算的成本
相同
Let’s say R is the fixed amount of interest the company will pay the bank, then
假设 R 是公司将支付给银行的固定利息金额
Removing the brackets, the above expands to:
去掉括号后，上述式子展开为
2.90m – 0.966R + 4.76m – 0.914R + 5.57m – 0.854R + 6.10m – 0.789R + 4.86m – 0.737R= 0
Simplifying this, adding all the $ flows together and R-flows together, gives:
对此进行简化，将所有资金流（$flows）相加，同时将所有与 R 相关的资金流相加
24.19m – 4.26R = 0
$5.68m = R
In percentage = $5.68m/$100m = 5.68%
化成百分数
In practice the receipts and payments of the swap would be netted off such that the company will expect to pay $2.68m
($5.68m – $3.00m) to the bank in year one, and expect to receive $0.84m ($6.52m – $5.68m) from the bank in year
three, and so on for the other years. The present values of these n et annual flows, discounted at the yield curve rates,
will be zero. The fixed rate of 5.68% is lower than the five -year spot rate of 6.30% because some of the receipts and
payments related to the swap contract occur in earlier years when the spot yield curve rate is lower.
在实际操作中，互换的收支会相互抵减，这样公司预计在第一年向银行支付 268 万美元（568 万美元 - 300 万
美元），在第三年预计从银行收到 84 万美元（652 万美元 - 568 万美元），其他年份以此类推。这些年度净
现金流按照收益率曲线利率折现后的现值将为零。固定利率 5.68% 低于五年期即期利率 6.30%，这是因为与互
换合约相关的部分收支发生在较早年份，而那时的即期收益率曲线利率较低。
Although at the commencement of the contract, the present value of the swap is zero, as interest rates fluctuate, the
value of the swap will change. For example, if interest rates increase and the company pays interest at a fixed rate, then
the swap’s value to the company will increase. The value of the swap contract will also change as the swap approaches
maturity, and the number of receipts and payments reduce.
虽然在合约起始时，互换合约的现值为零，但随着利率波动，互换的价值会发生变动。例如，若利率上升，且公
司按固定利率支付利息， 那么该互换对公司而言价值将会增加。 随着互换合约临近到期， 收付次数减少， 互换合
约的价值同样会发生变化。
Conclusion
The previous article and this article considered the relationship between bonds, interest rates, spot and forward yield
curves, culminating with how the forwards rates can be used to determine the equivalent fixed rate of a simple swap
contract. The examples used were simplified into annual cash flows and rates, and students undertaking Advanced
Financial Management should be able to demonstrate their knowledge and understanding of these areas to this extent.
上一篇文章和本文探讨了债券、 利率、 即期与远期收益率曲线之间的关系， 最终阐述了如何运用远期利率来确定
简单互换合约的等效固定利率。 文中所举例子已简化为按年计算的现金流和利率， 学习 《高级财务管理》 的学生
应能在这一层面展示他们对这些领域的知识与理解。
In practice, valuation of bonds and related products is more complicated because of factors such as: cash flows
occurring more frequently than once a year, early redemption of products, change in product values, and so on.
However, these aspects are beyond the scope of the Advanced Financial Management syllabus.
在实际操作中， 由于诸如现金流发生频率高于一年一次、 产品提前赎回、 产品价值变动等因素， 债券及相关产品
的估值更为复杂。然而，这些内容超出了《高级财务管理》教学大纲的范畴。
Written by a member of the Advanced Financial Management examining team
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Determining interest rate forwards and their application to swap valuation.pdf

stem_zh:
Exchange traded foreign exchange derivatives
交易所内交易的外汇衍生金融工具
The aim of this article is to consider both foreign exchange futures and options using real market
data. The basics, which have been well examined in the recent past, will be quickly revisited. The
article will then consider areas which, in reality, are of  significant importance but which, to date,
have not been examined to any great extent.
本文的目的是使用真实市场数据来考虑外汇期货和期权。基础知识已经得到了充分的研究，
因此将会进行简要回顾。 接下来，本文将考虑一些实际上具有重要意义但迄今尚未深入研究
的领域
Foreign exchange futures – the basics
Scenario
外汇期货 – 基础知识
例子：
Imagine it is 10 July. A UK company has a US$6.65m invoice to pay on 26 August. They are
concerned that exchange rate fluctuations could increase the £ cost and, hence, seek to effectively
fix the £ cost using exchange traded futures. The current spot rate is $1.71110/£1.
Research shows that £/$ futures, where the contract size is denominated in £, are available on the
CME Europe exchange at the following prices:
September expiry – 1.71035
December expiry – 1.70865
The contract size is £100,000 and the futures are quoted in US$ per £1.
Note:
CME Europe is a London based derivatives exchange. It is a wholly owned subsidiary of CME Group,
which is one of the world’s leading and most diverse derivatives marketplace, handling (on average)
three billion contracts worth about $1 quadrillion annually!
假设现在是 7 月 10 日。一家英国公司有一笔665 万美元应付款要在 8 月 26 日支付。他们
担心汇率波动可能会增加最终换汇的英镑成本（支付美元要先用英镑换成美元支付），因此
希望使用交易所的场内交易期货以有效固定英镑成本。当前的即期汇率为 1.71110 美元/1
英镑。
研究表明，以英镑为合约币种计价的英镑/美元期货在CME欧洲交易所以下列价格提供：
九月到期：– 1.71035
十二月到期：– 1.70865
合约规模为100,000英镑，期货以每1 英镑的美元数量报价。
注意：
CME Europe 是一家总部位于伦敦的衍生品交易所。它是芝商所（芝加哥商品期货交易所）
的全资子公司，芝商所是世界领先和最多样化的衍生品市场之一，平均每年处理价值约 1
万亿美元的 30 亿份合约！
Setting up the hedge
Date? – September:
The first futures to mature after the expected payment date (transaction date) are chosen. As the
expected transaction date is 26 August, the September futures which mature at the end of
September will be chosen.
建立对冲
日期？–选择九月底到期的期货：
选择在预计付款日（交易日）之后第一个到期的期货。由于预计交易日为8 月 26 日，因此
将选择9月底到期的9月期货。
Buy/Sell? – Sell:
As the contract size is denominated in £ and the UK company will be selling £ to buy $ they should
sell the futures.
买/卖？–卖出英镑期货合约：
由于合约规模以英镑计价，英国公司将卖出英镑以买入美元，他们应该卖出英镑期货。
How many contracts? – 39
As the amount to be hedged is in $ it needs to be converted into £ as the contact size is
denominated in £. This conversion will be done using the chosen futures price. Hence, the number
of contracts required is: ($6.65m ÷ 1.71035)/£100,000 ≈ 39.
多少份合同？– 39手
由于要对冲的金额以美元为单位，因此需要转换为英镑，因为联系人大小以英镑计价。此转
换将使用所选的期货价格（即9月期货的价格）完成。因此，所需的合同数量为：
($6.65m ÷ 1.71035)/£100,000 ≈ 39 约为39手合约
Summary
The company will sell 39 September futures at $1.71035/£1.
Outcome on 26 August:
On 26 August the following was true:
Spot rate – $1.65770/£1
September futures price – $1.65750/£1
Actual cost:
$6.65m/1.65770 = £4,011,582
Gain/loss on futures:
As the exchange rate has moved adversely for the UK company a gain should be expected on the
futures hedge.
总结：
该公司将在 7 月 10 号以 1.71035 美元/ 1 英镑的价格出售 39 手 9 月到期的期货。
在 8 月 26 日的结果：
当天的即期汇率：$1.65770/£1
当天的 9 月到期的期货价格：$1.65750/£1（使用基差随着时间消耗均匀到 0 的原理计算）
当天在即期市场用即期汇率将美元换成英镑的数量：
$6.65m/1.65770 = £4,011,582
在期货市场上交易的损益：
由于汇率对英国公司的不利波动（即英国公司付出 1 单位英镑能够获得更少的美元） ，英国
公司应该在期货市场上获得收益以对冲现货市场上的损失。
操作 $/1 英镑
7 月 10 号先卖出 1.71035
8 月 26 日再平仓买入 (1.65750)
收益 0.05285
This gain is in terms of $ per £ hedged. Hence, the total gain is:
0.05285 x 39 contracts x £100,000 = $206,115
这是每单位英镑的美元收益，因此合计美元收益为：
0.05285 x 39 contracts x £100,000 = $206,115
Alternatively, the contract specification for the futures states that the tick size is 0.00001$ and that
the tick value is $1. Hence, the total gain could be calculated in the following way:
0.05285/0.00001 = 5,285 ticks
5,285 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $206,115
当然也可以按照每tick的方式去进行计算，这道题是tick size 是十万分一（因为报价汇
率是小数点后5位），因此tick的总数是0.05285/0.00001 = 5,285
合计美元收益是5,285 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $206,115
This gain is converted at the spot rate to give a £ gain of:
$206,115/1.65770 = £124,338
将期货交易中的美元收益按照8月 26日的即期汇率换成英镑数，即为
$206,115/1.65770 = £124,338
Total cost:
£4,011,582 – £124,338 = £3,887,244
因此，在8月26日日支付的总英镑数为：
即期市场换汇的英镑数减去期货市场上的英镑收益=£4,011,582 – £124,338 = £3,887,244
This total cost is the actual cost less the gain on the futures. It is close to the receipt of £3,886,389
that the company was originally expecting given the spot rate on 10 July when the hedge was set
up. ($6.65m/1.71110). This shows how the hedge has protected the company against an adverse
exchange rate move.
这个总成本是实际即期市场换汇成本减去期货收益。 鉴于7月10日建立对冲时的即期汇率，
该公司最初预计的3,886,389 英镑已接近。 （$6.65m/1.71110） 。 这一过程表明了对冲是如何
保护公司免受不利汇率变动的影响。
Summary
All of the above is essential basic knowledge. As the exam is set at a particular point in time you
are unlikely to be given the futures price and spot rate on the future transaction date. Hence, an
effective rate would need to be calculated using basis. A lternatively, the future spot rate can be
assumed to equal the forward rate and then an estimate of the futures price on the transaction
date can be calculated using basis. The calculations can then be completed as above.
总结
以上都是必不可少的基本知识。 由于考试设置在特定时间点， 因此不太可能在未来交易日获
得期货价格和即期汇率。因此，需要使用基数计算有效汇率。或者，可以假设未来的即期汇
率等于远期汇率，然后可以使用基差计算实际交易日的期货价格估计值。然后可以按上述方
式完成计算。
The ability to do this would normally earn four marks in an exam. Equally, another one or two
marks could be earned for reasonable advice such as the fact that a futures hedge effectively fixes
the amount to be paid and that margins will be payable during the lifetime of the hedge. It is some
of these areas that we will now explore further.
这样做的方法通常会在考试中获得4分。同样，对于合理的建议，例如期货套期保值有效地
确定了要支付的金额， 并且在套期保值的生命周期内将支付保证金， 则可以再获得1到2分。
下面将进行进一步探讨。
Foreign exchange futures – other issues
交易所内交易的期货-其他事项
Initial margin
When a futures hedge is set up the market is concerned that the party opening a position by buying
or selling futures will not be able to cover any losses that may arise. Hence, the market demands
that a deposit is placed into a margin account with the bro ker being used – this deposit is called
the ‘initial margin’.
These funds still belong to the party setting up the hedge but are controlled by the broker and can
be used if a loss arises. Indeed, the party setting up the hedge will earn interest on the amount
held in their account with their broker. The broker in tur n keeps a margin account with the
exchange so that the exchange is holding sufficient deposits for all the positions held by brokers’
clients.
初始保证金
当建立期货对冲时， 市场担心通过买入或卖出期货开仓的一方将无法弥补可能出现的任何损
失。因此，交易所要求将存款存入正在使用的经纪人(可以理解为一个中介平台)的保证金账
户——这笔存款被称为“初始保证金”。
这些资金仍然属于设置对冲的一方，但由经纪人控制，可以在出现损失时使用。事实上，设
置对冲的一方将从其经纪人账户中持有的金额中获得利息。 经纪人反过来在交易所保留一个
保证金账户，以便交易所为经纪人客户持有的所有合约头寸持有足够的存款保证。
In the scenario above the CME contract specification for the £/$ futures states that an initial margin
of $1,375 per contract is required.
Hence, when setting up the hedge on 10 July the company would have to pay an initial margin of
$1,375 x 39 contracts = $53,625 into their margin account. At the current spot rate the £ cost of
this would be $53,625/1.71110 = £31,339.
在上述情况下，CME合约规格的£/$期货规定，每份合约的初始保证金为1,375美元。
39份合约的保证金合计为$1,375 x 39 contracts = $53,625，按照初始时点即期汇率换算
成英镑为$53,625/1.71110 = £31,339.
Marking to market
In the scenario given above, the gain was worked out in total on the transaction date. In reality,
the gain or loss is calculated on a daily basis and credited or debited to the margin account as
appropriate. This process is called ‘marking to market’.
日盯市制度
Hence, having set up the hedge on 10 July a gain or loss will be calculated based on the futures
settlement price of $1.70925/£1 on 11 July. This can be calculated in the same way as the total gain
was calculated:
操作 $/1英镑
7月 10号先卖出 1.71035
7月 11号的当日结算价格 (1.70925)
收益 0.00110
Gain in ticks – 0.00110/0.00001 = 110
1 ticks 的收益（如前述） ：0.00110/0.00001 = 110
Total gain – 110 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $4,290
当天39手合约的总收益：
This gain would be credited to the margin account taking the balance on this account to $53,625 +
$4,290 = $57,915.
当天结束后保证金账户的余额为原金额+当天收益：$53,625 + $4,290 = $57,915
At the end of the next trading day (Monday 14 July), a similar calculation would be performed:
在下一个交易日（7月14日星期一）结束时，将执行类似的计算：
操作 $/1英镑
7月 11号的结算价格 1.70925
7月 14号的结算价格 (1.70805)
收益 0.00120
Gain in ticks – 0.00120/0.00001 = 120
1 ticks 的收益（如前述） ：0.00120/0.00001 = 120
Total gain – 120 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $4,680.
当天39手合约的总收益：
120 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $4,680.
This gain would also be credited to the margin account taking the balance on this account to
$57,915 + $4,680 = $62,595.
当天结束后保证金账户的余额为原金额（11 号结束时的余额）+当天收益（14 号的损益）
$57,915 + $4,680 = $62,595
Similarly, at the end of the next trading day (15 July), the calculation would be performed again:
$/1 英镑
1.70805 1.70805
7 月 15 号的结算价格 (1.71350)
损失 0.00545
Loss in ticks – 0.00545/0.00001 = 545
1 ticks的损失（如前述） ：0.00545/0.00001 = 545
Total loss – 545 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $21,255.
当天39手合约的总损失：545 ticks x $1 x 39 contracts = $21,255.
This loss would be debited to the margin account, reducing the balance on this account to $62,595
– $21,255 = $41,340.
当天结束后保证金账户的余额为原金额（14号结束时的余额）-当天损失（15号的损益）=
$62,595 – $21,255 = $41,340.
This process would continue at the end of each trading day until the company chose to close out
their position by buying back 39 September futures.
这个过程将在每个交易日结束时继续进行，直到公司选择通过买入9 月期货来平仓。
Maintenance margin, variation margin and margin calls
Having set up the hedge and paid the initial margin into their margin account with their broker, the
company may be required to pay in extra amounts to maintain a suitably large deposit to protect
the market from losses the company may incur. The balance on the margin account must not fall
below what is called the ‘maintenance margin’. In our scenario, the CME contract specification for
the £/$ futures states that a maintenance margin of $1,250 per contract is required. Given that the
company is using 39 co ntracts, this means that the balance on the margin account must not fall
below 39 x $1,250 = $48,750.
维持保证金、变动保证金和追加保证金
在设置对冲并将初始保证金支付到其经纪人的保证金账户后， 在之后的交易过程中公司可能
需要支付额外的金额以维持适当足够保证金水平， 以避免可能遭受的超额损失。保证金账户
的余额不得低于所谓的“维持保证金”。在我们的场景中，CME 期货的£/$合约规范规定，
每份合约需要1,250美元的维持保证金。 鉴于公司使用的是 39 份合约，这意味着保证金账
户的余额不得低于 39 x 1,250 美元 = 48,750 美元。
As you can see, this does not present a problem on 11 July or 14 July as gains have been made and
the balance on the margin account has risen. However, on 15 July a significant loss is made and the
balance on the margin account has been reduced to $41,340,  which is below the required
minimum level of $48,750.
如上文所描述的，这在 7 月 11 日或 7 月 14 日不会出现问题，因为已经取得了收益并
且保证金账户的余额已经上升。 然而，7 月15日出现重大损失， 保证金账户余额减少到41，
340美元，已经低于规定的最低水平48,750美元。
Hence, the company must pay an extra $7,410 ($48,750 – $41,340) into their margin account in
order to maintain the hedge. This would have to be paid for at the spot rate prevailing at the time
of payment unless the company has sufficient $ available to fund it. When these extra funds are
demanded it is called a ‘margin call’. The necessary payment is called a ‘variation margin’.
因此，公司必须向其保证金账户额外支付7,410美元（48,750美元 – 41,340美元）以维
持对冲。除非公司有足够的资金为其提供资金，否则必须按付款时的即期汇率支付。当需要
这些额外资金时，这一追加被称为“保证金催缴通知”。必要的付款称为“变动保证金”。
If the company fails to make this payment, then the company no longer has sufficient deposit to
maintain the hedge and action will be taken to start closing down the hedge. In this scenario, if the
company failed to pay the variation margin the balance on the margin account would remain at
$41,340, and given the maintenance margin of $1,250 this is only sufficient to support a hedge of
$41,340/$1,250 ≈ 33 contracts. As 39 futures contracts were initially sold, six contracts would be
automatically bought back so that the markets exposure to the losses the company could make is
reduced to just 33 contracts. Equally, the company will now only have a hedge based on 33
contracts and, given the underlying transaction’s need for 39 contracts, will now be underhedged.
如果公司未能支付这笔款项，那么公司将不再有足够的保证金存款来维持对冲，交易所将采
取行动开始平仓并关闭对冲。在这种情况下，如果公司未能支付变动保证金，保证金账户的
余额将保持在 41,340 美元，而考虑到 1,250 美元的维持保证金，这仅足以支持 33 份合约
≈41,340美元/ 1,250 美元的对冲。由于最初卖出了39份期货合约，因此6份合约将平仓
买入，从而使公司可能遭受的损失的市场敞口减少到只有33 份合约。同样，该公司现在将
只有基于33份合约的对冲，鉴于基础交易需要39 份合约，现在最终对冲的效果将被削弱。
Conversely, a company can draw funds from their margin account so long as the balance on the
account remains at, or above, the maintenance margin level, which, in this case, is the $48,750
calculated.
相反， 公司可以从其保证金账户中提取资金， 只要账户余额保持等于或高于维持保证金水平，
也就是刚刚计算的 48,750 美元。
Foreign exchange options – the basics
交易所内的期权交易
Scenario
Imagine that today is the 30 July. A UK company has a €4.4m receipt expected on 26 August. The
current spot rate is £0.7915/€1. They are concerned that adverse exchange rate fluctuations could
reduce the £ receipt but are keen to benefit if favourable exch ange rate fluctuations were to
increase the £ receipt. Hence, they have decided to use €/£ exchange traded options to hedge their
position.
假设今天是 7 月 30 日。一家英国公司预计将在8 月 26 日收到 440 万欧元并在之后立即换
汇成英镑收入。目前的即期汇率为0.7915英镑/1欧元。他们担心不利的汇率波动可能会减
少英镑收入，但如果有利的汇率波动增加英镑收入，他们希望因此受益。因此，他们决定使
用欧元/英镑的交易所交易期权来管理他们的外汇头寸。
Research shows that €/£ options are available on the CME Europe exchange.
The contract size is €125,000 and the futures are quoted in £ per €1. The options are American
options and, hence, can be exercised at any time up to their maturity date.
研究表明，CME欧洲交易所提供欧元/英镑期权。
合约规模为125,000欧元，期货报价为每1 欧元。期权为美式期权，因此可在到期日前的任
何时间行使。
Setting up the hedge
Date? – September:
The available options mature at the end of March, June, September and December. The choice is
made in the same way as relevant futures contracts are chosen.
建立对冲交易
日期？–9月到期的期权：
可用的选项在 3 月底、6 月底、9 月底和 12 月底到期。选择的方式与选择相关期货合约
的方式相同（最近且晚于）。
Calls/Puts? – Puts:
As the contract size is denominated in € and the UK company will be selling € to buy £, they should
take the options to sell € for £ – put options.
期权类型：看跌期权
由于合约规模以欧元计价， 而英国公司将出售欧元以购买英镑， 因此他们应该选择购入能够
出售的欧元权利 - 看跌期权。
Which exercise price? – £ 0.79250/€1
An extract from the available exercise prices showed the following:

£/€1
Put premiums 期权费率
£/€1
0.79000 0.00465
0.79250 0.00585
As the company is selling €, it wants the maximum net £ receipt for each € sold. The maximum net
receipt is the exercise price minus the premium cost.
由于公司正在出售欧元， 因此它希望每售出一欧元的获得最大英镑净收入。 最高净收入为行
权价减去期权费。
Exercise price
£/€1
Put premiums
£/€1

£/€1
0.79000 0.00465 0.7900 – 0.00465 = 0.78535
0.79250 0.00585 0.79250 – 0.00585 = 0.78665
Hence, the company will choose the 0.79250 exercise price as it gives the maximum net receipt.
Alternatively, the outcome for all available exercise prices could be calculated.
In the exam, either both rates could be fully evaluated to show which is the better outcome for the
organisation or one exercise price could be evaluated, but with a justification for choosing that
exercise price over the other .
因此，公司将选择0.79250的行使价，因为它给出了最大的英镑净收入。或者，可以计算所
有可用的行权价格的结果。在考试中，可以全面评估两种费率，以显示哪个对公司更有利，
或者可以评估一种行使价格，但需要说明理由选择该行权价格而不是另一个的原因。
4. How many? – 35
This is calculated in a similar way to the calculation of the number of futures.  Hence, the number
of options required is:
€4.4m/€0.125m ≈ 35
合约数量：其计算方式与计算期货数量类似。 因此，所需的选项数量为：
€4.4m/€0.125m ≈ 35
Summary
The company will buy 35 September put options with an exercise price of £0.79250 /€1
Premium to pay – £/€0.00585 x 35 contracts x €125,000 = £25,594
总结
公司将购买9月到期的看跌期权，行使价为0.79250英镑/1欧元
支付期权费率为 – £/€0.00585 x 35 份合约 x €125,000 = £25,594
Outcome on 26 August:
On 26 August the following was true:
Spot rate – £ 0.79650/€1
As there has been a favourable exchange rate move, the option will be allowed to lapse, the funds
will be converted at the spot rate and the company will benefit from the favourable exchange rate
move.
Hence, €4.4m x 0.79650 = £3,504,600 will be received. The net receipt after deducting the
premium paid of £25,594 will be £3,479,006.
在 8 月 26 日的结果
即期汇率：£ 0.79650/€1
由于汇率变动有利对英国公司有利（单位欧元能够换到更多的英镑），期权将被放弃行权，
欧元将按即期汇率转换成英镑，公司将从有利的汇率变动中受益。
因此，最终公司将收到€4.4m x 0.79650 = £3,504,600，在扣除期权费的净额收入为£3,479,006
Note:
Strictly a finance charge should be added to the premium cost as it is paid when the hedge is set
up. However, the amount is rarely significant and, hence, it will be ignored in this article.
If we assume an adverse exchange rate move had occurred and the spot rate had moved to
£ 0.78000/€1, then the options could be exercised and the receipt arising would have been:

严格来说， 在设置对冲时支付的期权费成本中应加上财务费用。 但是， 该金额很小几乎可以
省略不计，因此在本文中将忽略它。
如果假设发生了不利的汇率变动，并且即期汇率在 8 月 26 日已经变化为 0.78000 英镑/1
欧元，那么公司可以行使期权，产生的收入结果将是：

Notes:注释
1. This net receipt is effectively the minimum receipt as if the spot rate on 26 August is anything
less than the exercise price of £ 0.79250/€1, the options can be exercised and approximately
£3,461,094 will be received. Small changes to this net receipt may occur as the €25,000
underhedged will be converted at the spot rate prevailing on the 26 August transaction da te.
Alternatively, the underhedged amount could be hedged on the forward market. This has not been
considered here as the underhedged amount is relatively small.
1. 此净收入实际上是最低收入，因为如果 8 月 26 日的即期汇率低于 0.79250 英镑/1
欧元的行权价，则可以行使期权权利，将收到大约 3,461,094 英镑。由于未覆盖的对冲不
足的部分 25,000 欧元将按 8 月 26 日交易日的即期汇率兑换，因此净收入可能会发生小幅
变化。或者，对冲不足的金额可以在远期市场上对冲。这里没有考虑这一点，因为对冲不足
的金额相对较小。
2. For simplicity it has been assumed that the options have been exercised. However, as the
transaction date is prior to the maturity date of the options the company would in reality sell the
options back to the market and thereby benefit from both the intrinsic and time value of the option.
By exercising they only benefit from the intrinsic value. Hence, the fact that American options can
be exercised at any time up to their maturity date gives them no real benefit over European options,
which can only be exercised on the maturity date, so long as the options are tradable in active
markets. The exception perhaps is traded equity options where exercising prior to maturity may
give the rights to upcoming dividends.
2. 简单起见， 假定已经在交易日行使了这些选择权。 然而， 由于交易日早于期权的到期日，
公司实际上会将期权卖回市场，从而从期权的内在价值和时间价值中受益。通过行权，他们
只能从内在价值中受益。因此，美式期权可以在到期日之前的任何时间行使，这一事实使它
们与欧式期权相比没有真正的好处， 欧式期权只能在到期日行使， 只要期权可以在活跃的市
场上交易。例外情况可能是交易股票期权，在到期前行使可能会获得即将派发的股息。
Summary
Much of the above is also essential basic knowledge. You are unlikely to be given the spot rate on
the transaction date. However, the future spot rate can be assumed to equal the forward rate
which is likely to be given in the exam. The ability to do this may earn up to six marks in the exam.
Equally, another one or two marks could be earned for reasonable advice.
Foreign exchange options – other terminology
总结：
以上大部分也是必不可少的基本知识。在实务中不太可能在获得未来实际交易日即期汇率。
但是， 可以假设未来的即期汇率等于考试中可能给出的远期汇率。 这样做的能力可以在考试
中获得最多6分。同样，合理的建议也可以再获得1 到 2分。
外汇期权 – 其他术语
This article will now focus on other terminology associated with foreign exchange options and
options and risk management generally. All too often students neglect these as they focus their
efforts on learning the basic computations required. However, know ledge of them would help
students understand the computations better and is essential knowledge if entering into a
discussion regarding options.
本文现在将重点介绍与外汇期权和期权以及风险管理相关的其他术语。学生经常忽略这些，
因为他们专注于学习所需的基本计算。但是，了解它们将有助于学生更好地理解计算，并且
在文字题题目中讨论选项时是必不可少的知识。
Long and short positions
A ‘long position’ is one held if you believe the value of the underlying asset will rise. For instance,
if you own shares in a company you have a long position as you presumably believe the shares will
rise in value in the future. You are said to be long in that company.
A ‘short position’ is one held if you believe the value of the underlying asset will fall. For instance,
if you buy options to sell a company’s shares, you have a short position as you would gain if the
value of the shares fell. You are said to be short in that company.
多头和空头头寸
“多头头寸”是指如果您认为标的资产的价值会上涨而持有的头寸。 例如， 如果你购买一家公
司的股票， 那么您拥有多头头寸， 因为您可能认为股票在未来会升值， 所以你选择了先低价
买入，未来某时点再高价卖出。
“空头头寸”是指如果您认为标的资产的价值会下跌而持有的头寸。 例如， 如果你购买期权以
出售公司股票， 则您有一个空头头寸， 因为如果股票价值下跌， 您将获得收益。 所以你选择
了做空该公司。
Underlying position
In our example above where a UK company was expecting a receipt in €, the company will gain if
the € gains in value – hence the company is long in €. Equally the company would gain if the £ falls
in value – hence, the company is short in £. This is their ‘underlying position’.
To create an effective hedge, the company must create the opposite position. This has been
achieved as, within the hedge, put options were purchased. Each of these options gives the
company the right to sell €125,000 at the exercise price and buying these options means that the
company will gain if the € falls in value. Hence, they are short in €.
Therefore, the position taken in the hedge is opposite to the underlying position and, in this way,
the risk associated with the underlying position is largely eliminated. However, the premium
payable can make this strategy expensive.
It is easy to become confused with option terminology. For instance, you may have learnt that the
buyer of an option is in a long position and the seller of an option is in a short position. This seems
at variance with what has been stated above, where buy ing the put options makes the company
short in €. However, an option buyer is said to be long because they believe that the value of the
option itself will rise. The value of put options for € will rise if the € falls in value. Hence, by buying
the €/£ put options the company is taking a short position in €, but is long the option.
标的头寸
在我们上面的例子中，一家英国公司预计未来会收到欧元并兑换成英镑，如果欧元升值，公
司将受益——因此该公司做多欧元。同样，如果英镑价值下降，公司也会受益——因此，公
司将做空英镑。这是他们的“标的头寸”。
为了创建有效的对冲，公司必须创建相反的头寸。这已经实现，因为在对冲中购买了看跌期
权。这些期权中将赋予公司以行权价出售 125,000 欧元的权利，购买这些期权意味着如果
欧元贬值，公司将获利。因此，它们选择做空欧元
因此，对冲中的头寸与标的头寸相反，通过这种方式，与标的头寸相关的风险在很大程度上
被消除。但是，需要额外支付的期权费可能会使这种策略变得昂贵。
实际上， 这些一些期权术语很容易混淆。 例如， 您可能已经了解到期权的买方处于多头头寸，
而期权的卖方处于空头头寸。这似乎与上述内容不一致，在上述情况下，购买看跌期权会使
公司做空欧元。 然而， 期权买家之所以被说成多头， 是因为他们相信期权本身的价值会上升。
如果欧元贬值，欧元看跌期权的价值将上升。因此，通过购买欧元/英镑看跌期权，该公司
做空欧元货币，但做多期权。
Hedge ratio
The hedge ratio is the ratio between the change in an option’s theoretical value and the change in
the price of the underlying asset. The hedge ratio equals N(d1), which is known as delta. Students
should be familiar with N(d1) from their studies of the Bl ack-Scholes option pricing model. What
students may not be aware of is that a variant of the Black -Scholes model (the Grabbe variant –
which is no longer examinable) can be used to value currency options and, hence, N(d1) or the
hedge ratio can also be calculated for currency options.
对冲比率
对冲比率是期权理论价值的变化与标的资产价格变化之间的比率。对冲比率等于 N（d1），
称为 delta。人们应该从他们对布莱克-斯科尔斯期权定价模型的研究中熟悉N（d1）。热门
可能不知道的是，布莱克-斯科尔斯模型的一个变体（Grabbe 变体）可用于评估货币期权，
因此，N（d1）或对冲比率也可以计算外汇期权。
Hence, if we were to assume that the hedge ratio or N(d1) for the €/£ exchange traded options
used in the example was 0.95 this would mean that any change in the relative values of the
underlying currencies would only cause a change in the option value equ ivalent to 95% of the
change in the value of the underlying currencies. Hence, a €0.01 per £ change in the spot market
would only cause a €0.0095 per £1 change in the option value.
因此，如果我们假设示例中使用的€/£交易所交易期权的对冲比率或N（d1）为0.95，这意
味着标的货币相对价值的任何变化只会导致期权价值的变化相当于标的货币价值变化的
95%。因此，现货市场每 1 英镑变化 0.01 欧元只会导致期权价值每 1 英镑变化 0.0095
欧元。
This information can be used to provide a better estimate of the number of options the company
should use to hedge their position, such that any loss in the spot market is more exactly matched
by the gain on the options:
公司应该用来对冲他们的头寸，这样现货市场的任何损失都与期权的收益更精确地匹配：
Number of options required = amount to hedge/(contract size x hedge ratio)
In our example above, the result would be:
€4.4m/(€0.125m x 0.95) ≈ 37 options
所需期权数量=对冲金额/（合约规模×对冲比率）
在上面的示例中，结果将是：
€4.4m/（€0.125m x 0.95） ≈ 37 手合约
Conclusion
This article has revisited some of the basic calculations required for foreign exchange futures and
options questions using real market data, and has additionally considered some other key issues
and terminology in order to further build knowledge and confidence in this area.、
结论
本文重新审视了使用真实市场数据进行外汇期货和期权问题所需的一些基本计算， 并还考虑
了其他一些关键问题和术语，以进一步建立该领域的知识和信心。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Exchange traded foreign exchange derivatives.pdf

stem_zh:
FOREIGN CURRENCY FUTURES - STEP BY STEP
外币期货 - 分步解析
This article highlights the different approaches which can be taken for foreign currency futures
hedging transactions in the AFM exam. There is the lock-in rate method which is most commonly
seen in published AFM answers, but there is also a future spot -rate method as well. You can use
either method in your AFM exam and receive full credit. To look at each method, we will consider
an example.
这篇文章强调了在 AFM 考试中可采取的不同外币期货对冲方法。最常见的是锁定汇率法，
这在已发布的 AFM 答案中经常看到，但也有未来即期汇率法。在你的AFM 考试中，你可以
使用任易一种方法获得满分。我们接下来对每种方法举例说明。
EXAMPLE 1
例 1
Norris Co is a company based in the eurozone which carries out trade in the country of Mengia,
whose currency is the dollar.
Norris 公司是一家位于欧元区的公司，在货币为美元的 Mengia 进行贸易。
Today's date is 1 May. On 30 November Norris Co is due to pay $20 million to a Mengian supplier.
Its treasury function is reviewing how using futures to manage the foreign exchange risk on these
transactions would work compared to using the forward market.
今天的日期是 5 月 1 日。 在11 月 30 日，Norris 公司需要向 Mengia 的供应商支付$20 million。
其财务部门正在审查相较于使用远期，如果使用期货来管理这些交易的话外汇风险会如何。
The following information is available:
以下信息可用：
Exchange rates (quoted as $/€1)
汇率（报价为 $/€1）
Spot 1.2100-1.2116
7 months' forward 1.2274-1.2308
Currency futures (Contract size €125,000, futures price quoted as $/€1)
外汇期货（合约大小为€125,000，期货报价单位是$/€1）
June 1.2180
September 1.2225
December 1.2300
Futures contracts mature at the month’s end. Basis can be assumed to diminish to zero at
contract maturity at a constant rate, based on monthly time intervals.
期货合约在月末到期。可以假设基差按月匀速减小并在合约到期时减少至零。
The number of contracts to be used should be rounded to the nearest whole number. If the
amount cannot be hedged using an exact number of futures contracts the balance should be
considered immaterial.
应使用的合约数量应四舍五入至最接近的整数。 如果无法使用恰好的整数合约进行对冲， 那
么余额（即未被对冲的金额）应被视为不重要。
SOLUTION
解决方案
Forward market hedging
远期市场对冲
$20m/1.2274 = €16,294,606
直接用题干信息中给的 7 months’ forward exchange rate， 可算出使用远期对冲时11 月 30 日
需支付的$20 million 相当于€16,294,606。
This method has the advantage of being tailored to the exact timing and amount of Norris Co's
exposure. However there is counterparty risk and, once entered into, the contract is not flexible
or liquid.
这种方法的优点是能够精确地根据 Norris 公司的外汇风险敞口的时间和金额进行定制。 然而，
存在对手方违约的风险，并且一旦签订合同，合同就不具有灵活性或流动性。
Futures
期货
As the transaction occurs in November, sell December euro futures now to hedge against a
weakening of the € relative to the dollar. Note – always follow the flow of the currency to set up
the hedge. We need to buy $ and sell € to pay the invoice. The futures contract is on the €, so the
hedge needs us to sell contracts. December contracts are used as the June and September
contracts will have expired before the actual transaction date.
交易发生在 11 月，因此现在需要出售 12 月到期的欧元期货以对冲欧元相对于美元的贬值。
注意——始终根据合约币种在交易中的交易方向来建立对冲。 我们需要购买美元并出售欧元
来进行支付。期货合约的合约币种是欧元，因此我们需要出售期货构建对冲。使用 12 月的
期货合约是因为 6 月和 9 月合约将在实际交易日期之前到期。
To work out the number of contracts for this transaction, the transaction value has to be divided
by the size of the contract. Since the transaction is in $ it needs to be translated into the contract
currency using the current futures price of 1.2300.
Number of contracts = ($20m/1.2300)/€125,000 = 130.1
Therefore round to 130 contracts
为了计算这笔交易的合约数量， 需要用交易金额除合约大小。 由于交易是以美元进行的， 因
此需要用当前期货价格 1.2300 将其转换为合约币种。
可算出合约数量为 130 手。
Lock-in rate method
锁定汇率法
基差 = 现货价格 – 期货价格
因此，在 5 月 1 日：基差 = 1.2100 – 1.2300 = -0.0200
The basis on the expiry date of the futures contract is always zero. Since Norris Co is selling
December contracts and futures contracts expire at the month’s end, the expiry date in this
example is 31 December.
期货合约到期日的基差为 0。 由于Norris 公司卖 12 月的期货合约， 且期货合约在月底到期，
因此在这个例子中到期日是 12 月 31 日。
Assuming that basis reduces to zero at contract maturity in a linear fashion, unexpired basis on
the transaction date (ie 30 November) can be calculated as follows:
假设在合约到期时基差以线性方式降至零， 那么在交易日期 （即11 月 30 日） 未到期的基差
可以按照以下方式计算：
未到期基差 = ([1.2100 – 1.2300] × 1/8) = – 0.0025
（计算未到期基差时所用的 1/8：总基差是现在 5 月 1 日到 12 月 31 日这 8 个月，11 月 30
日到 12 月 31 日还有 1 个月的时间，所以未到期的基差是总基差的 1/8）
“锁定汇率” = opening futures price + unexpired basis = 1.2300 + -0.0025 = 1.2275
因此，预期净成本（欧元） = $20m/1.2275 = €16,293,279
This calculation is an approximation to the full hedging transaction as shown below. It ignores the
rounding of contracts and basis risk, both of which will lead to the actual outcome being slightly
different to the calculated outcome. However, the benefit is that it gives an idea of the likely
outcome and does not need actual or assumed spot rates in November to complete the
calculation.
此计算是对下面展示的完整对冲交易的一个近似。 它忽略了合约的四舍五入和基差风险， 这
两者都会导致实际结果与计算结果略有不同。然而，其好处在于它给出了一个可能的结果，
并且不需要实际或假定的 11 月即期汇率来完成计算。
Future spot rate method
未来即期汇率法
It is possible to calculate the futures outcome by making an assumption about the spot price in 7
months’ time ie on 30 November, and then working through the implications for the spot market
and futures market transactions. For example, if the spot price on 30 November is assumed to be
1.2265, the following calculations would apply:
通过假设 7 个月后的即期汇率价格， 即11 月 30 日的价格， 然后分析现货市场和期货市场交
易的影响来计算。例如，如果假设 11 月 30 日的即期汇率价格为 1.2265，那么将适用以下
计算：
预期的期货价格（通过购买期货来平仓）=1.2265 – (-0.0025) = 1.2290$/€1
Note - since the spot price on 1 May (ie 1.2100) is lower than the opening futures price (ie
1.2300), the unexpired basis is  subtracted from the assumed spot price on 30 November when
calculating the expected futures price.
注意 - 由于 5 月 1 日的现货价格（即 1.2100）低于期货开仓价格（即 1.2300），因此在计
算预期期货价格时，未到期的基差将从假设的 11 月 30 日的现货价格中减去。
Because the opening trade was to sell futures, we have a gain because the futures price has
dropped.
由于开仓交易是卖出期货，因此期货价格下跌使得我们获得收益（以 1.2290 低价买入，以
1.2300 高价卖出）。
期货市场的收益= (1.2300 – 1.2290)$/€1 x 130 x €125,000 = $16,250
假设即期汇率为 1.2265，将收益转换为欧元，期货市场的收益= $16,250 / 1.2265 = €13,249
假设即期汇率为 1.2265，将现货市场上应付的$20m 转换为欧元应付款= $20m / 1.2265 =
€16,306,563
因此，futures outcome = €16,306,563 – €13,249 = €16,293,314
This is effectively the same outcome as from using the lock -in rate method previously. Any
assumed spot rate could be used to get to a similar futures outcome.
这实际上与之前使用锁定汇率法得到的结果几乎相同。基于任何假定的即期汇率都会算出类
似的期货对冲结果。
In either method, the futures outcome is marginally cheaper than the forward market outcome,
so futures would be the recommended method of hedging for Norris Co on a financial basis.
Additionally, the futures contracts have the benefit of flexibility (can close out hedge any time up
to end of the contract’s life) and there is no counterparty risk through using the exchange. The
transaction has not been hedged exactly, because the number of contracts was rounded down to
130, meaning that the hedge efficiency would be less than 100%.
无论采用哪种方法，基于期货的对冲结果都比远期市场的对冲结果更便宜， 因此从财务角度
来看，应推荐 Norris 公司用期货对冲。 此外， 期货合约具有灵活性的优势 （可以在合同结束
前的任何时间停止对冲） ， 并且通过在交易所交易而消除对手方的违约风险。但交易并没有
完全对冲，因为合约数量被向下四舍五入到 130 份，这意味着对冲效率将低于 100%。
EXAMPLE 2
例 2
This example will use the original exchange rate data as the previous example, but looks at how a
Mengian customer would hedge a payment of €15m to Norris Co on the same date.
本例题将使用与前一例题相同的原始汇率数据，但关注的是一位 Mengian 国的客户如何对
冲在同一天（即 11 月 30 日）向 Norris 公司支付€15m 的风险。
SOLUTION
解决方案
Forward market hedging
€15m x 1.2308 = $18,462,000
直接用题干信息中给的 7 months’ forward exchange rate， 可算出使用远期对冲时11 月 30 日
需支付的€15m 相当于$18,462,000。
Futures
期货
Again, December contracts will be used but the Mengian company needs to buy € and sell $. The
futures contract is on the €, so the hedge needs the company to buy contracts. The number of
contracts will be
Number of contracts =€15,000,000/€125,000 = 120
Therefore buy 120 contracts
再次，将使用 12 月的合约，但 Mengian 公司需要买入欧元并卖出美元。合约币种是欧元，
因此对冲需要公司买入期货合约。买入的期货合约的数量将会是 120 份。
Lock-in rate method
锁定汇率法
基差 = 现货价格 – 期货价格
因此，在 5 月 1 日：基差 = 1.2116 – 1.2300 = –0.0184
The basis on the expiry date of the futures contract is always zero. Since the Mengian company is
buying December contracts and futures contracts expire at the month’s end, the expiry date in
this example is 31 December .
期货合约到期日的基差为 0。由于 Mengian 公司购买的是 12 月合约，并且期货合约在月底
到期，因此在这个例子中到期日是 12 月 31 日。
Assuming that basis reduces to zero at contract maturity in a linear fashion, unexpired basis on
the tranasaction date (ie 30 November) can be calculated as follows:
假设基差在合约到期时以线性方式减少到零， 那么在交易日期 （即11 月 30 日） 未到期的基
差可以按照以下方式计算：
未到期基差 = ([1.2116– 1.2300] × 1/8) = –0.0023
（计算未到期基差时所用的 1/8：总基差是现在 5 月 1 日到 12 月 31 日这 8 个月，11 月 30
日到 12 月 31 日还有 1 个月的时间，所以未到期的基差是总基差的 1/8）
“锁定汇率” = opening futures price + unexpired basis = 1.2300 - 0.0023 = 1.2277
因此，预期净成本（美元）= €15m x 1.2277$/€1 = $18,415,500
Future spot rate method
未来即期汇率法
If the spot price on 30 November is assumed to be 1.2310, the following calculations would
apply:
假设 11 月 30 日的即期汇率为 1.2310，那么以下计算将适用：
预期的期货价格（通过出售期货来平仓）= 1.2310 – (-0.0023) = 1.2333
Because the opening trade was to buy futures, we have a gain because the futures price has
risen.
由于开仓交易是买入期货， 所以当期货价格上涨时， 我们获得了盈利（以 1.2300 低价买入，
以 1.2333 高价卖出）。
期货市场收益= (1.2333 – 1.2300) x 120 x €125,000 = $49,500
假设即期汇率 为 1.2310，将现货市场上应付的 €15m 转换为美元， = €15m x 1.2310 =
$18,465,000
因此，futures outcome = $18,465,000 - $49,500 = $18,415,500
Once again, the futures outcome is cheaper than the forward market outcome so, on a financial
basis, futures would be the recommended method of hedging for the Mengian customer with
the same non -financial benefits as before. In this example, the outcome from using the lock -in
rate method and the future spot rate method are the same, because this transaction uses an
exact number of contracts to match the transaction amount, meaning the hedge efficiency is 100%
in this example.
再一次，基于期货的对冲结果比远期市场的对冲结果更便宜， 因此， 从财务角度来看应推荐
Mengian 客户用期货，这与之前一样具有相同的非财务收益。在这个例子中，使用锁定汇率
法和未来即期汇率法的结果是相同的， 因为这次交易使用了精确数量的合约来匹配交易金额，
这意味着在这个例子中对冲效率是 100%。
If a different assumption is made about the spot price on 30 November, the outcome will be
exactly the same. For example, assume the spot price on 30 November is 1.2200 instead of
1.2310 as follows:
如果对 11 月 30 日的即期价格做出不同的假设，结果将完全相同。例如，像下面这样假设
11 月 30 日的即期价格是 1.2200 而不是 1.2310：
Because the opening trade was to buy futures, we have a loss because the futures price has
fallen.
因为开仓交易是买入期货，所以期货价格下跌我们会遭受损失。
假设即期汇率为 1.2200 ， 预期的期货价格 （通过出售期货来平仓）= 1.2200 – -0.0023 = 1.2223
期货市场的亏损 = (1.2223 – 1.2300) x 120 x €125,000 = $115,500
假设即期汇率 为 1.2200 ，将现货市场上应付的 €15m 转换为美元， = €15m x 1.2200 =
$18,300,000
因此，futures outcome = $18,300,000 + $115,500 = $18,415,500
This is exactly the same outcome as before. It does not matter if the assumed spot price on 30
November is not provided in an exam question because any assumption about the spot price on
30 November will result in the same outcome. In this case, although the spot price is more
favourable to the Mengian customer, resulting in a lower payment in dollars, any advantage is
cancelled out by the loss on the futures market.
这与之前的结果完全相同。 即使考试题目中没有提供11 月 30 日假设的现货价格也不会影响
计算，因为任何关于 11 月 30 日现货价格的假设都会导向相同的计算结果。在这种情况下，
尽管现货价格对 Mengian 客户更有利（导致美元支付减少），但任何优势都会被期货市场
的损失所抵消。
Conclusion
结论
This article has looked at different approaches which can be taken in the AFM exam for foreign
currency futures hedging transactions. As long as there is no basis risk, either method is eligible
for full credit, so the examining team recommends that you use the method which will best suit
you under exam conditions. This will enable you to maximise your marks on a question which
looks at foreign currency futures hedging transactions. If you are given an expected spot rate or
closing futures rate in the exam it may be easier to use the information provided to close out the
hedge than to use the lock-in rate.
这篇文章探讨了在 AFM 考试中可以采用的外币期货对冲方法。只要没有基差风险，任何一
种方法都可以获得满分， 因此考试团队建议你选择最适合你的方法。 这将使你在考察外币期
货对冲交易的题上尽可能多的得分。 如果考题中给了 expected spot rate 或 closing futures rate，
使用提供的信息来平仓对冲可能比使用锁定汇率法更容易。

stem_zh:
GREEN FINANCE
绿色金融
This short article looks at green financing, which is part of the AFM syllabus area B3.
What is green finance?
什么是绿色金融
Green finance does not have a globally agreed definition, but for the purposes of AFM it can be
considered to be specific financing for projects or initiatives which will have a positive
environmental impact.
绿色金融没有全球公认的定义，在 AFM 中，它可以被认为是为那些将产生积极环境影响的
项目或倡议提供的特定融资。
This means there is a commitment to investing the funds raised into ‘green’ projects such as:
renewable energy
low carbon solutions
sustainability initiatives
climate change resilience
这意味着承诺将筹集的资金投资于“绿色”项目，例如：
可再生能源
低碳解决方案
可持续性倡议
气候变化韧性
As a result, the use of green finance means that sustainability and environmental factors become
part of mainstream financial decision making.
因此，绿色金融的使用意味着可持续性和环境因素成为主流金融决策的一部分。
Advantages of green finance
绿色金融的优点
Green finance, which is typically debt finance, is often cheaper than conventional debt finance,
thereby creating a saving on interest expense for an organisation.
绿色金融通常是债务融资的一种形式， 通常比传统债务融资更便宜， 从而为一个组织节省了
利息支出。
Another aspect is that the use of green finance can demonstrate that a project is sustainable and
environmentally friendly. This can help to improve a corporate image and reputation.
另一个方面是， 使用绿色金融可以证明一个项目是可持续的和环保的。 这可以帮助改善公司
的形象和声誉。
Disadvantages of green finance
绿色金融的缺点
Green financing involves additional costs as a result of increased reporting requirements. It will
need to be shown that the project or initiative being financed will actually have a positive
environmental impact. This may require an independent audit and even if not, there is likely to
be an increased administrative burden because of this.
绿色融资增加了报告要求， 会带来额外的成本。 需要证明被融资的项目或倡议实际上会产生
积极的环境影响。这可能需要独立审计，即使不需要也可能会增加行政负担。
Conclusion
结论
In many cases, the benefits related to savings and the impact of additional costs should be fairly
straightforward for an organisation to assess. Then a cost -benefit analysis can be undertaken to
see whether the green finance is worthwhile.
在很多情况下，应该相对直接的去评估某一个组织与节约相关的好处与额外成本（即绿色金
融的优点与缺点）的影响，然后进行成本效益分析，以判断绿色金融是否是值得的。
Written by a member of the AFM examining team
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Green finance.pdf

2 / 3， 所以使用 3 月期货价格 1.0336 和 6 月期货价
格 1.0369 差值的 2 / 3。
This method is a shortcut, used to work out a ‘lock-in’ rate
that will be the net result of the underlying transaction
and the hedge gains or losses.
此方法是一种捷径， 用于计算 “锁定” 汇率， 该汇率是
基础交易和套期保值损益的净结果。
Or
Estimate from spot rate and June futures rate
Predicted futures rate at the end of May = 1.0292 + ([1.0369 – 1.0292] × 6/7) = 1.0358
Expected receipt = CHF12,250,000 × 1.0358 = $12,688,550
或者
根据即期汇率和 6 月期货汇率估算
5 月底预测期货汇率 = 1.0292 + ([1.0369 - 1.0292] × 6 / 7) = 1.0358
预期收款额 = 12,250,000 瑞士法郎 × 1.0358 = 12,688,550 美元
Futures and options contracts mature at the month’s end.
Basis can be assumed to diminish to zero at contract
maturity at a constant rate, based on monthly time
intervals.
期货和期权合约在月末到期。可假设基差在合约到期
时以恒定速率按月递减至零。
31 May is 6/7 of time between 30 November and 30
June, so use 6/7 of the difference  between spot rate of
1.0292 and June futures rate of 1.0369.
5 月 31 日是 11 月 30 日到 6 月 30 日时间间隔
的 6 / 7， 所以使用即期汇率 1.0292 和 6 月期货价格
1.0369 差值的 6 / 7。
This method is a shortcut, used to work out a ‘lock-in’ rate
that will be the net result of the underlying transaction
and the hedge gains or losses.
此方法是一种捷径， 用于计算 “锁定” 汇率， 该汇率是
基础交易和套期保值损益的净结果。
Outcome
结果
$
Futures
期货
12,688,550
Remainder on forward market
远期市场剩余金额
51,780
总计 12,740,330
4.
Options
期权
Buy CHF put options
买入瑞士法郎看跌期权
Buy CHF put options to hedge against sale of CHF when money received from Swiss customer.
买入瑞士法郎看跌期权，以对冲收到瑞士客户款项时的瑞士法郎销售。
Buy June options
买入 6 月期权
Put:
December
March
June
Must be June as only date after May.
必须是 6 月，因为只有该日期在 5 月之后。
Number of contracts and receipt
合约数量和收款额
Number of contracts = CHF12,300,000/125,000 = 98.4, say 98, hedging 98 × CHF 125,000 = CHF12,250,000 (as for
futures)
合约数量  = 12,300,000 瑞士法郎  / 125,000 瑞士法郎  = 98.4 ，取 98，对冲  98 × 125,000 瑞士法郎  =
12,250,000 瑞士法郎（与期货相同）
Receipt = CHF125,000 × 98 × 1.0375 = $12,709,375
收款额 = 125,000 瑞士法郎 × 98 × 1.0375 = 12,709,375 美元
Contract size is $125,000
合约规模为 125,000 瑞士法郎
Number of contracts =
Receipt/$125,000, rounded to the nearest contract
合约数量 =
收款额 / 125,000 瑞士法郎， 四舍五入到最接近的合约
数量
Remainder to be hedged on forward market
剩余金额在远期市场套期保值
Remainder to be hedged on the forward market = CHF12,300,000 – CHF12,250,000 = CHF 50,000
在远期市场套期保值的剩余金额 = 12,300,000 瑞士法郎 - 12,250,000 瑞士法郎 = 50,000 瑞士法郎
Receipt = CHF50,000 × 1.0356 = $51,780 (as for futures)
收款额 = 50,000 瑞士法郎 × 1.0356 = 51,780 美元（与期货相同）
Number of contracts does not cover full amount of transaction, so need to hedge residual receipt of CHF.
Underhedge = Amount of transaction – Amount hedged
Use same rate as for forward contract above of 1.0356.
合约数量未涵盖交易全额，所以需对瑞士法郎的剩余收款额进行套期保值。
套期不足 = 交易金额 - 已套期金额
使用与上述远期合约相同的汇率 1.0356。
Premium
期权费
1.0375 options = 98 × 125,000 × 0.0086 = $105,350
1.0375 期权的期权费 = 98 × 125,000 × 0.0086 = 105,350 美元
Put June 0.86 US cents per CHF
6 月看跌期权 0.86 美分 / 瑞士法郎
Multiply number of contracts × size of one contract ×
0.0086, as premium is quoted in US cents (not US dollars)
per CHF.
用合约数量 × 一份合约规模 × 0.0086 计算， 因为期权
费以每瑞士法郎对应的美分数报价（不是美元） 。
$
Receipt 收款额 12,709,375
$
Forward contract 远期合约   51,780
Premium 期权费 (105,350)
总计 12,655,805
Comments
评论
If the options are exercised, the futures would give the higher receipt. The options give a lower receipt because of
the premium that Nutourne Co has to pay. The futures will be subject to the risk that basis (the difference between
the futures price and the spot price) may not decrease linearly as the futures approach maturity, as assumed in the
above calculations. This will mean that the hedge of the CHF12,250,000 is imperfect, and the receipt may be
unpredictable despite a futures hedge being taken out.
如果期权被行权， 期货会带来更高的收款额。 期权的收款额较低是因为 Nutourne 公司必须支付期权费。 期
货会面临这样的风险： 正如上述计算中所假设的那样， 随着期货临近到期， 基差 （期货价格与即期价格之间
的差额）可能不会呈线性下降。这意味着对 12,250,000 瑞士法郎的套期保值并不完美，尽管进行了期货套
期保值，但收款额仍可能无法预测。
Conclusion
结论
This question has demonstrated how to use the data given in the question in foreign exchange hedging calculations.
Hopefully, it will help you tackle this type of question systematically.
这个问题展示了如何在外汇套期保值计算中使用题目中给出的数据。希望它能帮助你系统地解决这类问题。

stem_zh:
How to answer an interest rate risk management question
利率风险管理类题目的解答思路
Questions on risk management feature regularly in the Advanced Financial Management exam. Performance
information from recent exams suggests students tend to do less well on interest rate risk management questions than
questions about foreign exchange risk management. This article will therefore explain the significance of the
information you’ll be given in interest rate risk management questions and show you what you’ll be asked to do.
The scenario is adapted from Wardegul Co, Question 4 in the September/December 2017 sample questions which ACCA
has published.
风险管理相关问题在《高级财务管理》考试中经常出现。近期考试的成绩信息表明，相较于外汇风险管理问题，
学生在利率风险管理问题上的表现往往欠佳。 因此， 本文将阐释在利率风险管理题目中所给信息的重要性， 并说
明考试会要求考生做什么。
以下情景改编自 ACCA 于 2017 年 9 月 / 12 月发布的样题中第 4 题 Wardegul Co 的案例
Scenario
Assume Wardegul Co has a newly-acquired subsidiary in Euria, where the local currency is the dinar (D). The subsidiary
expects to receive D27,000,000 and wants to invest this D27,000,000. Assume it is now 1 October 2017 and the
subsidiary expects to receive the money on 31 January 2018. It wishes the money to be invested for five months until
30 June 2018.
假设 Wardegul Co在Euria 新收购了一家子公司， 当地货币为Dinar（D）。 该子公司预计将收到 D27,000,000，
并打算将这笔资金用于投资。 假设现在是 2017 年 10 月 1 日， 子公司预计在 2018 年 1 月 31 日收到这笔款
项。它希望将这笔资金投资五个月，直至 2018 年 6 月 30 日。
Currently the central bank base rate in Euria is 4·2%, but Wardegul Co’s treasury team has seen predictions that the
central bank base rate could increase by up to 1·1% or fall by up to 0·6% between now and 31 January 2018. The
treasury team believes that Wardegul Co can invest funds at the central bank base rate less 30 basis points.
The treasury team normally hedge interest rate exposure by using whichever of the following products is most
appropriate:
目前，Euria 的央行基准利率为 4.2%，但Wardegul Co的财务团队看到预测称， 从现在到 2018 年 1 月 31 日，
央行基准利率可能最多上调 1.1%， 或最多下调 0.6%。 财务团队认为，Wardegul Co能够以央行基准利率减去 30
个基点的利率进行资金投资。
财务团队通常会使用以下最合适的金融产品来对冲利率风险：
⚫ Forward rate agreements (FRAs)
⚫ Interest rate futures
⚫ Options on interest rate futures
⚫ 远期合约（FRAs）
⚫ 利率期货
⚫ 利率期权
Treasury function guidelines emphasise the importance of mitigating the impact of adverse movements in interest rates.
However, they also allow staff to take into consideration upside risks associated with interest rate exposure when
deciding which instrument to use.
司库职能指导方针强调了降低利率不利变动影响的重要性。 不过， 这些方针也允许工作人员在决定使用何种工具
时，考虑与利率风险敞口相关的上行风险。
A local bank in Euria, with which Wardegul Co has not dealt before, has offered the following FRA rates:
Euria 当地的一家银行（Wardegul Co 之前未曾与之有业务往来）提供了以下远期利率协议（FRA）利率：
⚫ 4–9: 5·02%
⚫ 5–10: 5·10%
The treasury team has also obtained the following information about exchange traded Dinar futures and options:
Three-month D futures, D500,000 contract size
Prices are quoted in basis points at 100 – annual % yield
财务团队还获得了以下有关 Dinar 外汇期货和期权的信息：三个月期 D 期货，合约规模为 500,000 Dinar，价格
以基点报价，为 100 - 年化收益率 %
December 2017 94.84
March 2018 94.78
June 2018 94.66
Options on three-month D futures, D500,000 contract size, option premiums are in annual %。
选择 3 个月的 Dinar 外汇期权，合同规模为每份合同 D500,000，合同的期权费按照年化利率呈现。
Tips:对于利率期权， 期权费是按照年化利率来呈现的。 我们在后面进行决策的时候， 可以用期权费的利息算出利
息要支出的金额作为成本，也可以直接用期权费的利率作为成本利率，直接进行最终有效利率的计算。
Call  Put
December March June  December March June
0.417 0.545 0.678 94.25 0.071 0.094 0.155
0.078 0.098 0.160 95.25 0.393 0.529 0.664
It can be assumed that futures and options contracts are settled at the end of each month. Basis can be assumed to
diminish to zero at contract maturity at a constant rate, based on monthly time intervals. It can also be assumed that
there is no basis risk and there are no margin requirements.
可以假定， 期货和期权合约在每个月末进行结算。 基于每月的时间间隔， 基差可假定在合约到期时以恒定速率减
小至零。还可以假定不存在基差风险，且没有保证金要求。
Requirements
Recommend a hedging strategy for the D27,000,000 investment, based on the hedging choices which treasury staff are
considering, if interest rates increase by 1·1% or decrease by 0·6%. Support your answer with appropriate calculations
and discussion. (18 marks)
基于财务人员正在考虑的对冲选择，针对 D27,000,000 的投资，在利率上升 1.1% 或下降 0.6% 的情况下，推
荐一种对冲策略。用适当的计算和讨论来支持你的答案。
Approaching the question
Read the requirements carefully
You should read the requirements first before reading the scenario in detail. Knowing what your answer has to cover,
and therefore what the key data will be, will help you analyse the scenario.
你应先阅读题目要求， 再详细研读案例场景。 清楚答案需涵盖哪些内容， 进而明确关键数据有哪些， 这将有助于
你分析案例场景。
Breaking down the requirements for Wardegul Co:
Recommend a
hedging strategy
建议对冲策略
You’ll have to make a clear recommendation
based on your calculations. Anyone reading the
recommendation should be able to see:
你必须依据计算结果给出明确的建议。任何
阅读该建议的人都应该能够明白
• How much would be received under each
instrument
每种金融工具（在相应情况下）会收到多少
钱？
• The effective annual  interest rate for each
instrument
每种工具（金融工具）的有效年利率
so that they can compare the results of the
hedging choices with the interest rate currently
available.
这样他们（相关人员）就能将对冲选择的结
果与当前可用的利率进行比较。
Based on the hedging choices which treasury
staff are considering
基于财务人员正在考虑的对冲选择
You need to consider all the hedging
instruments for which data is given, including
both the options.
你需要考虑所有给出数据的对冲工具，包括
两种期权
If interest rates increase by 1·1% or decrease
by 0·6%
如果利率上升 1.1% 或者下降 0.6%。
You should assess, for all the hedging
instruments, what will happen if interest rates
rise or fall.
对于所有的对冲工具，你都应该评估一下利
率上升或下降时会发生什么情况
Support your answer with appropriate
calculations and discussion
请通过恰当的计算和论述来支撑你的答案
You should make some comment on any
calculation you carry out in the  Advanced
Financial Management  exam. However,
mentioning discussion in the question
requirements here indicates that a number of
marks will be available for comments (four
marks maximum per the marking scheme).
Therefore, a single sentence comment won’t
be enough.
在《高级财务管理》考试中，对于你所进行
的任何计算都应加以说明 。然而，本题要求
中提及 “论述”，意味着会有一定分值（根据
评分标准，最多 4 分）用于考查你的论述。
因此，仅用一句话进行说明是不够的。
Identify the important data in the scenario
For interest rate hedging questions, you need to identify the information that will affect the calculations for each
instrument. Let’s have another look at the scenario, with the important data highlighted and referenced to explanations
below.
识别案例场景中的重要数据
对于利率套期保值问题， 你需要找出会影响每种工具计算的信息。 让我们再次查看案例场景， 突出重要数据并附
上以下解释说明。
Assume Wardegul Co has a newly-acquired subsidiary in Euria, where the local currency is the dinar (D). The subsidiary

As we are buying futures now, then selling futures later:
• we make a PROFIT if the expected futures price is GREATER than the current futures price

如果预期的期货价格高于现在的期货价格（低买高卖了）则我们会盈利；
反之，若未来的期货价格低于现在的期货价格，则会在期货市场上出现亏损。
Options
Buy call options as need to hedge against a fall in interest rates.

Comment
If one of the options is exercised for both interest rates, as the 94.25 is here, the calculations should give the same
result.
As these are CALL options, options to buy, choose the LOWER price and so:
• If the exercise price is LOWER than the expected futures price, EXERCISE
• If the exercise price is HIGHER than the expected futures price, DO NOT EXERCISE
如果针对两种利率都行使其中一项期权，就像此处 94.25 的情况，计算结果应该是相同的。
由于这些是看涨期权，即买入期权，应选择较低的价格，因此：
・如果行权价格低于预期期货价格，行使期权
・如果行权价格高于预期期货价格，不行使期权
结论重述，点明决策方案
Discussion
The forward rate agreement gives the highest guaranteed return. If Wardegul Co wishes to have a certain cash flow and
is primarily concerned with protecting itself against a fall in interest rates it will most likely choose the forward rate
agreement. The 95.25 option gives a better rate if interest rates rise, but a significantly lower rate if interest rates fall, so
if Wardegul Co is at all risk averse it will choose the forward rate agreement.
远期利率协议能提供最高的保底收益。在结论中需要说明对比的结果
如果瓦尔代古尔公司（Wardegul Co）希望拥有确定的现金流，并且主要关注防范利率下降的风险，那么它很可
能会选择远期利率协议。如果利率上升，执行价格为 95.25 的期权能带来更好的利率，但如果利率下降，其利
率会显著降低。所以，只要瓦尔代古尔公司有点厌恶风险，就会选择远期利率协议。
This assumes that the bank with Wardegul Co deals with is reliable and there is no risk of default. If Wardegul Co
believes that the current economic uncertainty may result in a risk that the bank will default, the choice will be
between the futures and the options, as these are guaranteed by the exchange. Again the 95.25 option may be ruled
out because it gives a much worse result if interest rates fall to 3.6%. The futures give a marginally better result than the
94.25 option in both scenarios but the difference is small. If Wardegul Co feels there is a possibility that interest rates
will be higher than 5.41%, the point at which the 94.25 option would not be exercised, it may choose this option rather
than the future.
这是假定与瓦尔代古尔公司交易的银行信誉可靠， 不存在违约风险。 如果瓦尔代古尔公司认为当前经济形势的不
确定性可能导致银行违约，那么其选择将在期货和期权之间，因为这两者由交易所提供担保。同样，行权价为
95.25 的期权可能会被排除，因为如果利率降至 3.6%，其收益会糟糕得多。在两种情形下，期货的收益都略好
于行权价为 94.25 的期权， 但差异不大。 如果瓦尔代古尔公司认为利率有可能高于 5.41%（即执行价格为 94.25
的期权不会被行权的利率点），那么它可能会选择该期权而非期货
Tips：5.41%=5.3%+0.11%，也就是 11 个 basis point,如果利率上升，行权价格 94.25 不变，而对应的平仓价格会逐
渐降低到临界的 94.25，因此公司无法从期权上获利，因此不会再选择行权，从而避免期货市场的亏损。而这个
亏损在期货市场上是无法避免的。
Comment
Identifying which of the possible strategies gives the highest value is only the start of the discussion and you need to
consider other factors that may influence the decision to obtain four marks:
确定哪种可行策略能带来最高价值仅仅是讨论的开始，要拿到 4 分，你还需考虑其他可能影响决策的因素
• The level of risk aversion that Wardegul Co has. The  treasury team appears to be weighing limiting downside against
the possibility of taking advantage of upside.
瓦尔代古尔公司（Wardegul Co）的风险厌恶程度。财务团队似乎需要权衡限制下行风险与把握上行获利可能性
之间的关系。
• Other risk considerations are also important. There may be counterparty risk, as FRAs are over -the-counter
instruments.
其他风险因素也很重要。可能存在交易对手风险，因为远期利率协议属于场外交易工具
• The decision may depend upon what is believed about future interest rates. Here, as rates are volatile, you should
consider whether the decision would change depending on what interest rates are expected.
该决策可能取决于对未来利率的预期。 鉴于当前利率波动， 你应当思考， 根据不同的利率预期， 决策是否会发生
变化
The discussion should be in full sentences and use information relevant to the scenario. A bullet point list or generic
statements relating to hedging are unlikely to be awarded many marks.
讨论应以完整的句子进行， 并运用与场景相关的信息。 列要点清单或给出与套期保值相关的笼统表述不太可能获
得高分。
Conclusion
This article has demonstrated how to use the data given in the question to calculate the impact of interest rate hedging.
Hopefully it will help you tackle interest rate risk management questions in a structured way, which should mean that
you score well.
本文展示了如何利用题目中给出的数据， 计算利率套期保值的影响。 希望本文能帮助你有条不紊地解答利率风险
管理相关问题，从而取得好成绩
Written by a member of the examining team for Advanced Financial Management

要求:
(a) 准备一份涵盖最初两年期的项目财务评估，假设 Penn 公司以适当的资本成本对美
元标价的名义现金流进行贴现来评估项目。清楚地说明您所做的任何假设。（11 分）
(b) 解释 Penn 公司选择承担该项目时面临的主要风险和问题。 （14 分）
（总计 25 分）
The key to a good Advanced Financial Management answer is to have a clear plan when
answering the question. Knowing where to start and how to progress through in a smooth
and efficient way is vital.
一个好的 AFM 答案的关键，是在回答问题时有一个明确的计划。知道从哪里开始，如
何以平稳有效的方式完成，都是至关重要的。
Time allocation
The 25-mark question needs to be completed in 45 minutes, allowing 1.8 minutes per
mark. A split of 20 minutes for part (a) and 25 minutes for part (b) is a good starting point
in terms of allocation, but with students often finding the numerical elements challenging,
allowing a minimum of 20 minutes for part (b) will give flexibility.
时间分配：25 分的问题需要在 45 分钟内完成，1.8 分钟/分。就分配而言，(a)部分和
(b)部分 20 分钟和(b)部分 25 分钟的分配是一个很好的起点，但由于学生经常发现数字
元素具有挑战性，因此为(b)部分留出至少 20 分钟将提供灵活性。
Understand the requirements
What are you been asked to do? Read the requirements and understand the key words.
Match them to your Advanced Financial Management knowledge.
了解要求：你被要求做什么？阅读要求并理解关键词。将它们与你的 AFM 知识相匹配。
In this case: 在这种情况下：
(a) Compute a net present value in $m. ‘Nominal’ cash flows means adjusted for relevant
inflation. ‘Appropriate’ cost of capital – my initial thoughts are either the company’s
weighted average cost of capital (WACC) or a risk adjusted WACC. I need to list out any
assumptions I make.
(b) ‘Risks and issues’ – I assume I will be able to derive most of these from the main body
of the question coupled with the relevant Advanced Financial Management knowledge
areas.
(a) 以 $m 计算净现值。“名义”现金流量是指根据相关通货膨胀率进行调整。“适当”的资
本成本 – 我最初的想法是公司的加权平均资本成本 WACC 或风险调整后的 WACC。我
需要列出我所做的任何假设。
(b) “风险和问题” – 我认为我能够从问题的主体以及相关的高级财务管理知识领域中得
出其中的大部分内容。
Which part to attempt first?
From experience, most candidates attempt the question in the order it is set. There are
no instructions that says you have to do this. Decide which order you feel most
comfortable with, but ensure you make a valid attempt at both parts of the question.
As for me, I would attempt Part (b) first. I feel I am more likely to be in control of my time
this way. However, I will show my answer in the order the question was set.
先尝试哪个部分？ 根据经验，大多数考生会按照设定的顺序尝试问题。没有 要求说必
须这样做。确定你觉得最舒服的顺序，但请确保您对问题的两 个部分都进行了有效的
尝试。至于我，我会先尝试 (b)部分。我觉得这样更有可能控制自己的时间。但是，我
将按照问题设置的顺序显示我的答案。
The read through
I understand the requirements. Now, I need to digest the details of the question. My
approach is a simple one.
通读：我了解这些要求。现在，我需要消化这个问题的细节。我的方法很简单。
Part (a) – Answer
To address this requirement I need to show a disciplined approach. Let me break this
down into stages and explain my thought process for each step.
(a)部分-答案：为了按照要求回答，我需要按照一种有规律的方法进行展示。我将其分
解为几个阶段，并解释我对每个步骤的思考过程。
(1) Dinar cash flows

The points to notes here: 此处的注释指出：
• Columnar layout corresponding with the timing of the cash flows specified in the
question.
按照问题中现金流发生的指定时间进行相对应的列式。
• Cash flows are in nominal dinars; they have already been adjusted for Zanadian
inflation.
名义现金流以 dinar 为单位;它们已经根据 Zanadian 的通胀进行了调整。
• Revenue is dinar 5000m allocated per the percentages specified in the question.
I see no reason to show a working for this.
收入直接按照题干指定的百分比分配 5000m dinar，不需要专门列示 working。
• Variable cost is just 30% of the revenue figures as indicated on the schedule.
浮动成本按照收入的 30%计提。
• Sub-totalled to show the taxable ‘profits’. As there is no TAD in this question,
these are equal to the operating cash flows.
小计加总得出应税“利润”。由于此问题中没有 TAD，因此这就是经营现金流。
• Taxation is a relevant cash flow. On the read through stage, I noted that there was
no time delay in the payment of the tax to the Zanadian government.
税收属于相关现金流。向 Zanadian 政府缴纳税款是在纳税义务发生的当年进行。
• Initial investment is a simply copy and paste.
初始投资就是简单的复制粘贴
• The total project cash flows show that Penn Co will need to initially buy dinars
(sell $s) to make the investment. Subsequently, Penn Co will be receiving dinars
that it will convert into $s. Care needs to be taken when choosing/calculating the
spot rates.
项目总现金流显示，Penn 公司最初需要购买 dinar(卖出美元)才能进行投资。
随后，Penn 公司将收到 dinar，并将其兑换成美元。在选择/计算即期汇率时需
要小心。
(2) Spot rates and conversion to $m 用即期汇率将外币 dinar 转成本币美元
Project cash flows (1,000) 210 420 840 630
Spot rate (W1) Dinar/$ 150 196 217 243 269
Free cash flows ($m) (6.67) 1.07 1.94 3.46 2.34
The question stated that the current spot rate was dinar 150 – 175/$. As Penn Co needs
to sell $s initially, the bid rate of dinar 150/$ will apply. As all other cash flows are receipts
in dinars, a working is needed to compute the projected offer spot rat es via the
purchasing power parity theory (PPPT) formula.
问题指出，目前的即期汇率为 150–175 Dinar/$。由于 Penn 公司最初需要卖美元买
dinar，因此选择150 Dinar/$的汇率。由于所有其他现金流都是以dinar为单位的收入，
【所以需要卖 dinar 买美元，因此需要以买美元所用的汇率 175 Dinar/$。】通过购买力
平价理论 PPPT 公式预测未来的即期汇率。
Dinar/$
Now  175
6 months (175 + 217) / 2  196
12 months 175 x 1.30 / 1.05 217
18 months (217 + 268) / 2  243
24 months 217 x 1.30 / 1.05 269
The PPPT formula is used to calculate the annual spot rates. The intervening half -year
rates are average values.
PPPT 公式用于计算年度即期汇率。中间的半年利率是使用平均值。
Finally, many students fail to convert the foreign cash flows into the home currency
correctly. Based on my experience, I have seen many answers where confusion has
reigned supreme, as students are not sure whether to divide or multiply. In this case the
project cash flows are in dinars and our spot rates are dinar/$. We divide the dinar c ash
flows to convert to $m.
最后，许多学生未能正确地将外国现金流转换为本国货币。根据我的经验，我看到了
很多困惑的答案，因为学生不确定是该除法还是乘法。在这种情况下，项目现金流以
dinar 为单位，我们的即期汇率为 dinar/$。我们将 dinar 现金流除以汇率转换为 $。
(3) Final answer
Description Now

(m)
months

(m)

Dinar (m)
