Revenue  500 1,000 2,000 1,500
Variable cost (30%)  (150) (300) (600) (450)
Taxable cash flows  350 700 1,400 1,050
Taxation@ 40%  (140) (280) (560) (420)
Initial investment (1,000)
Project cash flows (1,000) 210 420 840 630
Spot rate (W1) Dinar/$ 150 196 217 243 269
Free cash flows ($m) (6.67) 1.07 1.94 3.46 2.34
Cost of capital (12%)  1.000 0.945 0.893 0.844 0.797
Present values ($m) (6.67) 1.01 1.73 2.92 1.87
Net present value ($m) +$0.86m
Discounting at the risk adjusted WACC of 12% (see assumptions below) should be the
easy part. The discount factors for 12 and 24 months can be taken from the tables
provided at the back of the exam. Those same tables provide the formula to use to
calculate the six-month and 18-month discount factors remembering that r = 0.12 and
n = 0.5 and 1.5 respectively.
以 12% 风险调整后 WACC 贴现(见下面的假设)应该是最简单的部分。12 个月和 24 个月
的折扣系数可以从考试提供的公式表中获取。表格也提供了用于计算 6 个月和 18 个月
贴现系数的公式，需要注意所对应的 r = 0.12 和 n = 0.5 和 1.5。
(4) Assumptions 假设
The requirement invites the students to state any assumptions. Here is a sample of some
that could be made:
该要求学生陈述潜在的假设。以下是一些示例：
The nominal risk adjusted cost of capital of 12% is the appropriate discount rate given
that the project is based in a developing country.
鉴于项目位于发展中国家，12% 名义风险调整后资本成本是适当的贴现率。
There is no additional taxation to pay in Penn Co’s home country on the remitted dinar
cash flows.
在 Penn 公司的本国，汇入的现金流无需额外缴纳税款。
Inflation rates in both countries will remain constant at their respective rates for the next
two years.
未来两年，两国的通货膨胀率将保持在各自的水平不变。
The PPPT formula provides a materially accurate assessment of the projected spot rates.
PPPT 公式对未来预期的即期汇率提供了非常准确的评估。
There is no residual value for the machinery after two years, as it will continue in use after
this initial period.
两年后，机器没有剩余价值，因为在那之后它将继续使用。
Part (b) – Answer
As stated above, students could choose to ‘front load’ the answer to this requirement.
My thought process here is to derive as many relevant points from the information in the
scenario that relate to ‘risks and issues’ and expand on these. The examiner is looking for
students to apply their knowledge to the details given in the question. It is these points
that will ensure you achieve a sound pass mark for this requirement.
如上所述，学生可以选择“提前加载”该问题的答案。我的思考过程是从场景中与 “风险
和问题” 相关的信息中得出尽可能多的相关点，并对其进行扩展。考官希望学生将知识
应用于问题中给出的细节。这些要点将确保您获得较好分数。
In addition, I will include the relevant factual points that can be found in any of the
Advanced Financial Management approved textbooks. They will certainly earn some
marks.
此外，我也可以罗列在 AFM 的教科书中找到的相关事实要点。他们肯定也会获得一些
分数。
Risks and issues facing Penn Co   Penn 公司面临的风险和问题
Sensitivity analysis – irrespective of the final NPV, the values used to arrive at this number
are subject to estimation errors. The ones of particular concern are the cost of capital and
predicted spot rates. Penn Co should carry out sensitivity analysis to identify how any
changes to these variables affect the NPV.
敏感性分析 – 无论最终的 NPV 如何，用于得出此数字的值都会受到估计误差的影响。
特别值得关注的是资本成本和预测的即期汇率。Penn 公司应进行敏感性分析，以确定
这些变量如何影响 NPV。
Recent history – although many MNCs have invested in Zanadia, this has not always led
to success. Penn Co needs to investigate and ascertain a little more detail as to why this
has been the case. Blame could lie with both parties to the contract.
近期表现 – 尽管许多跨国公司都投资了 Zanadia，但这并不总是成功的。Penn 公司需
要调查并确定更多细节来说明为什么会这样。责任可能在于合同的双方。
Ad hoc Payments – Penn Co needs to obtain some clarity on this matter. These charges
would reduce the NPV of the project and may even change the decision that the company
takes. The terms of the contract need to be carefully reviewed to ensure that Penn Co is
aware of what the Zanadian government expects.
临时付款 – Penn 公司需要就此事进行一些澄清。这些费用会降低项目的 NPV，甚至可
能改变公司做出的决定。需要仔细审查合同条款，确保 Penn 公司了解 Zanadian 政府
的期望。
Supplier – Penn Co has a reputation for manufacturing as well as laying tramlines. In this
case, they will be installing lines purchased from another supplier. There may well be
quality and specification issues.
供应商 – Penn 公司以制造和铺设有轨电车而闻名。在案例中，他们将安装从其他供应
商处购买的线路。这很可能存在质量和规格方面的问题。
Prime minister – the prime minister has a lot of influence over this contract. He will
‘personally’ review the work carried out by Penn Co before deciding to extend the
contract. The criteria on which his decision will be made are not specified and may well
be highly judgmental.
首相 – 首相对这份合同有很大的影响力。在决定延长合同之前，他将“亲自”审查 Penn
公司所做的工作。他做出决定的标准没有具体说明，很可能是高度主观的。
20-year contract – this is a long project and the risks associated with time are very high.
The returns are said to be substantial but again not quantified.
20 年合同 – 这是一个长期项目，与时间相关的风险非常高。虽然回报是可观的，但是
回报没有量化。
Other issues – there are a number of other issues Penn Co should accrue for:
– The company would need to be aware of local customs and work practices.
– The legal and regulatory issues would need to be quantified.
– If managers would be recruited in Zanadia or sent from Penn Co’s home country.
– The project will not damage Penn Co’s business and ethical reputation.
其他问题 – Penn 公司还应该注意许多其他问题：
- 公司需要了解当地的习俗和工作惯例。
- 法律和监管问题需要量化。
- 管理层是在 Zanadia 招聘还是从 Penn 公司派遣过去。
- 该项目不会损害 Penn 公司的业务和道德声誉。
International project appraisal questions are challenging, but far from impossible.
Students need to follow a disciplined approach. The format is very similar to when
preparing a standard ‘home country based’ NPV which candidates have practised many
times as it is part of both Financial Management and Advanced Financial Management.
国际项目评估问题具有挑战性，但绝非不可能。学生需要遵循严格的方法。该格式与
标准的 “基于本国 ”的 NPV 时非常相似，考生已经练习了很多次，因为它是财务管理和
高级财务管理的一部分。
For projects based abroad it is about starting with the relevant nominal foreign currency
cash flows. The key differences are the computation of the spot rates and conversion of
foreign currency cash flows to domestic currency values. Discounting at risk -adjusted
rates should not be unexpected given the change in risk levels when investing abroad.
对于位于国外的项目，从相关的名义外币现金流开始 计算。主要区别在于即期汇率的
计算以及外币现金流量与本币价值的转换。鉴于海外投资时风险水平的变化， 应以风
险调整后的折现率进行折现。
There are certain skills that we have not seen tested above such as royalties, transfer
pricing and double taxation. These will be part of my next article when we return to Penn
Co and look at another international project it is considering as an investment opportunity.
有一些技能我们在上 述内容中发现没有被 测试到，例如特许权使用费、 转移定价和双
边税收。这些将是我下一篇文章的一部分内容，我们会回到 Penn 公司并看看它正在考
虑投资的另一个国际项目。
Sunil Bhandari, freelance tutor
www.SunilBhandari.com

stem_zh:
要求：准备向 Penn 公司的董事会提交一份报告，说明：
a) 提供截至 2014 年 1 月 1 日的 Nuruk 火车线项目最后阶段的财务评估。所有现金流
均以名义价值列报，项目的美元自由现金流应按适当的名义资本成本进行贴现。忽略
Elders 股份有限公司的报价和替代融资选择。 （22 分）
b) 讨论在进行财务评估时所做的假设。 （5 分）
c) 对 Elders 股份有限公司提出的在两年内从 Penn 公司购买合约的报价进行评估，要包
括对实务期权财务价值的估计。 （9 分）
d) 讨论两种替代融资方案，具体涉及：
(i) 如果 Penn 公司从位于 Nuruk 的银行筹集资金，这将如何影响项目的财务评估。无
需进一步计算。 （4 分）
(ii) Penn 公司应该注意通过伊斯兰金融选项筹集资金与传统形式的债务资本之间的主
要区别。 （6 分）
根据报告的格式、结构和呈现方式获得专业分数。（4 分）
总共 50 分
Students will not be surprised to see a scenario -based question 1 containing a vast
amount of information. Several areas of the syllabus will be tested, including international
project appraisal. Before focusing on the primary topic, I wish to demonstrate my step-
by-step approach to answering question 1.
学生看到包含大量基于场景信息的问题 1 不会感到惊讶。教学大纲的几个领域将接受
测试，包括国际项目评估。在关注主要主题之前，我想演示一下我回答问题  1 的分步
方法。
Understand The Requirements and Allocate Your Time
了解要求并分配您的时间
This question represents 50% of the exam – therefore, the answer should be completed
in 90 minutes. However, the requirements of the question should be understood. ‘Topic
recognition’ as I call it entails identifying which part of the syllabus is being tar geted by
each requirement. Simultaneously, I will allocate my time based upon the standard
approach of 1.8 minutes per mark.
此问题占考试的 50% - 因此，答案应在 90 分钟内完成。不过，应该了解问题的要求。
我称之为“主题识别”，它需要确定每个要求针对教学大纲的哪一部分。同时，我将根据
每个分数 1.8 分钟的标准方法安排我的时间。
a) Keywords ‘financial assessment’, ‘project’s dollar nominal cash flows’ and ‘discounted’
would trigger my thoughts. I have to prepare a schedule of free cash flows and compute
the net present value (NPV). 22 marks would indicate a time allotment of 40 minutes.
However, there are four professional marks for structure and presentation, which I can
spread across the requirements. Revised time allocation – 45 minutes.
关键词“财务评估”、“项目美元标价的名义现金流”和“折现”会触发我的想法。我必须准
备一份自由现金流表并计算净现值。22 分表示时间安排为 40 分钟。但是，结构和呈现
有 4 分专业分，我可以将它们安排在各项要求中。修改后的时间安排为 45 分钟。
b) ‘Assumptions’ relating to the financial assessment – lots of scope to score marks here
within nine minutes.
与财务评估相关的“假设” 需要在 9 分钟内完成，这里得分的空间很大。
c) ‘Real option’ takes my thought process directly to the Black -Scholes Option Pricing
model (BSOP). I have to compute the value of this PUT option and add the relevant
discussion points. Allocate 17 minutes.
“实物期权” 将我的思考过程直接带到了 BSOP 模型。我必须计算这个 PUT 期权的值并
添加相关的讨论。安排 17 分钟。
d) The topic under scrutiny here appears to be two different forms of debt finance.
However, the requirements need to be interpreted very carefully.
这里的问题似乎是两种不同形式的债务融资。不过需要非常仔细地解读这些要求。
(i) How raising loan finance will affect the project appraisal. My initial thoughts are to
explain the Adjusted Present Value (APV) appraisal method. (8 minutes)
筹集贷款融资将如何影响项目评估，我最初的想法是解释 APV 这个评估方法。 （8 分钟）
(ii) Islamic finance – I need to apply my knowledge of Islamic finance (sukuk bonds) to
answer this final requirement. (11 minutes)
伊斯兰金融 – 我需要运用我的伊斯兰金融债券的知识来回答这个最后的要求。 （11 分钟）
Answer Format
答案格式
The question has clearly stated that the answer should be presented in a report format.
The best way to do this is have appendices showing the computational elements, followed
by the discussion parts in the main body of the headed report. In this case, I would layout
my answer:
该问题明确指出，答案应以报告格式呈现。最好的方法是在标题报告的主体中加入计
算部分的附录，然后是讨论部分。在这种情况下，我会安排我的答案：
Appendix 1 – NPV and relevant workings
附录 1-净现值和相关的计算过程
Appendix 2 – Real option valuation using BSOP model
附录 2-使用 BSOP 进行实务期权估值
Headed report – With four subheadings matching the requirements.
报告标题-具有四个复核要求的副标题
From reading the examiner’s report published after each exam, there appear to be a
worrying number of candidates who don’t format their answer as requested, and then
missing out on the ‘easy-to-earn’ marks.
通过阅读每次考试后发布的考官报告，似乎有相当数量的考生没有按照 指定的格式提
交他们的答案，然后错过了“容易获得”的分数。
The Read Through 通读
I understand the requirements. Now, I need to digest the details of the question. My
approach is a simple one.
我已经了解题目要求。现在，我需要消化这个问题的细节。我的方法很简单。
Let me now return and concentrate on preparing an answer on the international project
appraisal aspects of this question.
现在让我回来集中精力准备这个问题的国际项目评估的答案。
Appendix 1 – NPV and Workings

My explanations and workings are as follows:
• Columnar layout corresponding with the timing of the cash flows specified in the
question. Even though the project will finish in Year 5, there will be some taxation
to pay one year later.
按照要求中指定现金流的时间进行列示。尽管该项目将在 第 5 年完成，但一年
后仍需缴纳一些税款。
• Revenue needs to be supported with a working:
销售收入的计算过程如下：
(W1) Revenue Time 1 Time 2 Time 3 Time 4 Time 5
Metres 5,700 6,500 10,900 8,100 6,300
Price (€) 55,000 56,650 58,350 60,100 61,903
€m 313.50 368.23 636.01 486.81 389.99
• I have noted that €55,000 is the nominal price at the end of Year 1 and then it
increases by 3% pa. So many past questions have asked students to adopt this
approach for converting real cash flows into nominal values.
我注意到 55,000 欧元是第一年年底的名义价格，然后每年上涨  3%。以前很多
题目都要求学生采用这种方法将实际现金流转换为名义价值。
• When computing the variable cost, my working incorporates the 5% pa increase
in the unit cost from Year 2 onwards.
在计算变动成本时，计算需要变动成本单价从第 2 年开始每年增涨 5% 。
(W2) Variable cost Time 1 Time 2 Time 3 Time 4 Time 5
Unit cost 23,000 24,150 25,358 26,625 27,957
Metres x unit cost (€m) 131.10 156.98 276.40 215.67 176.13
• Incremental fixed costs can be directly entered on to the schedule of cash flows
accruing for the 6% pa increase from Year 2.
额外发生的固定成本也是从第 2 年开始每年增涨 6%。
• Royalty – this needs some care. I have incorporated this twice on the schedule of
cash flows. The royalty will be income earned in dollars for Penn Co in Ayjai.
However, it will be an operational cost for the Nurukian subsidiary and the dollar
values need to be converted, at the predicted spot rate, into euros.
特许权费——这里需要小心一些。我在海外 NPV 计算中两次体现了这个相关现
金流。特许权费对于在 Ayjai 的 Penn 公司来说是一笔应税收入，但是对于在
Nurukian 的子公司来说是一笔运营成本，并且需要以预测的即期汇率将美元价
值转化为欧元。
(W3) Royalty Time 1 Time 2 Time 3 Time 4 Time 5
Metres x $15,000 ($m) $85.50 $97.50 $163.50 $121.50 $94.50
Spot rate (€/$) – (W4) €0.7420 €0.7049 €0.6696 €0.6361 €0.6043
€m 63.44 68.72 109.48 77.29 57.11
• The predicted spot rate is a mid -point number (an average of the bid and offer
rates) and is the value of one dollar in euros. The dollar is predicted to devalue
by 5% pa.
预测的即期汇率是一个中间值（买价和卖价两者的平均值） ，反映单位一美元
可以兑换的欧元数量。其中，预计美元每年将贬值 5%。
(W4)
Spot rates
Time 0 Time 1 Time 2 Time 3 Time 4 Time 5 Time 6
Spot rate
(€/$)
€0.7810 €0.7420 €0.7049 €0.6696 €0.6361 €0.6043 €0.5741
• TAD is not a cash flow. It is an allowable expense so I can compute the taxable
profit and the relevant taxation cash flow. The TAD is then added back.
折旧不是现金流，但它是一项可以抵税的费用，因此我可以计算应税利润和相
关的应税现金流，然后算完税将折旧再加回来。
• Taxation is computed at the rate of 20% of the taxable profit. However, it will be
paid one year after the profit was earned.
税款按照 20%的税率进行计算，不过，税需要递延一年支付。
• Initial investment and scrap proceeds are just a copy and paste.
初始投资和项目残值只需要复制粘贴题干信息即可。
• Working capital – my ‘thought process’ is to assume the project needs to have a
unique bank account. It needs a cash investment on 1 January 2014 of €31.35m
(10% x €313.50m). At end of the first year an incremental adjustment is needed
that can be computed as 10% x (€368.23m – €313.50m). Similar adjustments are
made at the end of T2, T3 and T4. At the end of the project, I assume this bank
account is closed and whatever is left is withdrawn.
营运资本——我的思考过程是假设项目需要有一个唯一的银行账户，它需要在
2014 年 1 月 1 日进行€31.35m 的现金投资，在第一年结束的时候，需要进行增
量调整，其计算方式为 10% x (€368.23m – €313.50m)，在第 2 年，第 3 年和第 4
年年末都进行了类似的调整。在项目结束时，我假设这个银行账户被关闭，账
户里剩下的所有东西都被提取出来。
• The euro cash flows are converted into dollars at the predicted spot rates. As I
stated in my last article, some students make errors at this point. In this case, I
have to divide the euro cash flows by the spot rate (euro per dollar) to find the
dollar amounts.
欧元现金流按照预测的即期汇率转换为美元，正如我在上一篇文章中所说的，
一些学生会在这一点上犯错。在这种情况下，我必须将欧元现金流除以即期汇
率（用欧元兑换美元）才能得到美元金额。
• As mentioned above, the dollar value of the royalty appears twice on the schedule.
Penn Co will receive the royalties and these will be subject to taxation in Ayjai.
如上所述，特许权费的美元价值在 NPV 计算中出现了两次，Penn 公司将获得
版权费收入，这笔收入需要在 Ayjai 纳税。
• The bi-lateral tax agreement mentioned in the question leads to an additional
cash flow. The working clarifies the position.
题目中提及的双边税收协定带来了额外的现金流，详见下面的计算过程：
(W5) Additional taxation Time 1 Time 2 Time 3 Time 4 Time 5
€m €m €m €m €m
Taxable profit 28.96 50.13 155.19 96.21 56.25
(25–20)% x Taxable Profit (1.45) (2.51) (7.76) (4.81) (2.81)
Spot Rate (€/$) – W4 €0.7420 €0.7049 €0.6696 €0.6361 €0.6043
€m (1.95) (3.56) (11.59) (7.56) (4.65)
• As the project will not alter Penn Co’s business and financial risk, the appropriate
cost of capital is 10%, the company’s WACC.
由于这个项目不会改变Penn 公司的现有经营风险和财务风险，所以公司现有的
加权平均资本成本 10%可以作为该项目的折现率。
Extract from the Report
报告摘录
Let me turn my attention to the report. I will show you an extract from this so as you can
get a feel as to what you need to produce in the exam.
让我把注意力转向这份报告。我将向您展示其中的摘录，以便您了解您需要再考试中
产生什么。
To: BoD of Penn Co
From: xxxxxx
Subject: Nurukian Train Line Project
Date: xx-xx-xx
收件人：Penn 公司董事会
发件人：xxxxxx
主题：Nurukian 火车线项目
日期：xx-xx-xx
----------------------------------------------
Financial assessment
财务评估
I have prepared a forecast of the nominal free cash flows for the Nurukian train line
project in Appendix (1). After discounting these at the Penn Co’s current cost of capital
(10%), the project increases shareholder wealth by just under $150m. Based on this value,
the company should accept this project.
我在附录 1 中准备了 Nurukian 火车线项目的名义自由现金流预测。按照 Penn 公司目
前的资本成本 10%折现后，该项目可以使股东财富增加近 $150m。基于这个值，公司
应该接受这个项目。
All forecasts are subject to estimation errors. This should be taken into account when the
BoD arrives at its final decision.
不过所有预测都会受到估计误差的影响。董事会做出最终决定时，应考虑到这一点。
Assumptions
假设
There are a number of assumptions that have been made when computing the NPV.
Some of these are considered below:
计算 NPV 时是基于许多假设。其中一些考虑如下：
Inflation – specific inflation rates have been incorporated into the appraisal and are
expected to remain constant for the five-year period.
通货膨胀率——特定的通胀率已经被纳入评估，预计在五年内将保持不变。
Taxation – the current tax rates and allowances used to arrive at the taxation cash flows
may vary over the life of the project.
税收——用于计算税收现金流的当前税率和免税额在项目周期内可能会有所不同。
Scrap proceeds – the Nuruk government have guaranteed to purchase the machinery for
a value of €500m. This may be subject to the condition of the machine as there will be
wear and tear.
项目残值——Nuruk 政府保证以价值€500m 的价格购买这些即期，这可能取决于机器
的状况，因为会有磨损。
Exchange rates – future spot rates are affected by many factors and, hence, the values
used in the assessment may be incorrect.
汇率——未来的即期汇率收到许多因素的影响，因此，评估中使用的值可能不正确。
Finance – the project requires €1,000m ($1280m) initial finance. It has been assumed that
this will be raised in the Ayjain financial markets. This is a large value relative to the
company’s current entity value. The project may be too big for Penn Co to undertake.
财务——项目需要€1,000m ($1280m)的初始投资，据推测，这将在 Ayjain 的金融市场
上募集。这个价值相较于公司目前的实体价值来说是一个比较大的值，该项目对于
Penn 公司的承受能力来说可能太大了。
Sensitivity analysis should be carried out to identify how changes in key variables affect
the NPV.
应该进行敏感性风险以确定关键变量的变化如何影响 NPV。
Appendix (2) and Remaining Part of the Report
附录 2 和报告的剩余部分
The primary objective of this second article was to show how to deal with international
project appraisal within Section A of Advanced Financial Management. This has now been
achieved. Appendix (2) and the remaining elements of this report are on other key areas
of the Advanced Financial Management syllabus. Although, these are just as important,
they are not the focus of these series of articles. My intention was to provide you with a
logical way of attacking questions on international project appraisal only.
第二篇文章的主要目的是展示如何处理 AFM 的 A 部分的国际项目评估。附录 2 和本报
告的剩余内容是关于 AFM 教学大纲的其他关键领域。尽管这些同样重要，但它们并不
是这个系列文章的重点。我的目的是为您提供一种合乎逻辑的方式，让您仅回答有关
国际项目评估的问题。
International project appraisal is an important element within the Advanced Financial
Management syllabus. Students preparing to attempt this exam should ensure they study
this topic very carefully. Know your subject well.
国际项目评估是 AFM 教学大纲中的一个重要元素。准备参加此考试的学生应确保他们
非常仔细地学习此主题。充分了解这个主题。
As I have demonstrated in this series of articles, a question on this area can appear in
either section of the exam. As long as you take a disciplined approach and apply the
knowledge you gave gained from your studies, you can earn a mark worthy of a pass.
正如我在本系列文章中所演示的那样，有关此 内容的问题可以出现在考试的任一部分。
只要你采取严格的方法并应用你从学习中获得的知识，你就可以获得 能够通过的分数。
Sunil Bhandari, freelance tutor
www.SunilBhandari.com

stem_zh:
Islamic finance – theory and practical use of sukuk bonds
伊斯兰金融-伊斯兰债券的理论和实际应用
This article looks at Islamic finance as a growing and important source of finance, including the
success and failure of the use.
The growth and popularity of the use of Islamic finance has been exceptional since the Central
Bank of Bahrain issued the first sovereign sukuk bonds in 2001. It is estimated that by the end of
2012 Islamic financial assets will have exceeded $1,600bn, which is around 1 –2% of global
financial assets worldwide.
A successful Advanced Financial Management  student must: ‘Demonstrate an understanding of
the role of, and developments in, Islamic financing as a growing source of finance for
organisations; explaining the rationale for its use, and identifying its benefits and deficiencies.’
This article is focused on Islamic finance as a growing and important source of finance. In
particular, it looks at the success and failure of the use of sukuk bonds to finance the purchase of
assets. However, for the purpose of reference, the attached appendix explains the basic
principles of Islamic finance.
这篇文章探讨了伊斯兰金融作为一个不断增长且重要的融资来源， 包括其使用中的成功与失
败。
自 2001 年巴林中央银行发行第一笔主权 Sukuk 债券以来，伊斯兰金融的使用增长和流行程
度异常显著。据估计，到 2012 年底，伊斯兰金融资产将超过 1.6 万亿美元，约占全球金融
资产的 1-2%。
一个成功的高级财务管理学生必须学会：“展示对伊斯兰融资作为组织不断增长的融资来源
的角色和发展趋势的理解；解释其使用的理由，并识别其优势和不足。”
本文专注于伊斯兰金融作为一个不断增长且重要的融资来源。 特别是， 它探讨了Sukuk 债券
用于资产购买融资的成功与失败。 然而， 为了参考的目的， 附录解释了伊斯兰金融的基本原
则。
Sukuk finance
What is sukuk finance? The official definition provided by the Accounting and Auditing
Organization for Islamic Financial Institutions (AAOIFI), the Bahrain -based Islamic financial
standard setter, is ‘certificates of equal value representing undivided shares in the ownership of
tangible assets, usufructs and services or (in the ownership of) the assets of particular projects or
special investment activity.’
什么是 Sukuk 融资？由巴林的伊斯兰金融标准制定者——伊斯兰金融机构会计与审计组织
（AAOIFI）提供的官方定义是：“代表对实物资产、用益权和服务或特定项目或特殊投资活
动的资产所有权的不可分割份额的等值证书。
Sukuk is about the finance provider having ownership of real assets and earning a return sourced
from those assets. This contrasts with conventional bonds where the investor has a debt
instrument earning the return predominately via the payment of interest (riba). Riba or excess is
not allowed under Sharia law.
Sukuk 是关于融资提供者拥有实物资产并从这些资产中获得回报。这与传统债券不同，在传
统债券中， 投资者拥有一种债务工具，主要通过支付利息 （利巴） 获得回报。根据伊斯兰教
法，利息或超额孳息是不允许的
There has been considerable debate as to whether sukuk instruments are akin to conventional
debt or equity finance. This is because there are two types of sukuk:
Asset based – raising finance where the principal is covered by the capital value of the asset but
the returns and repayments to sukuk holders are not directly financed by these assets.
Asset backed – raising finance where the principal is covered by the capital value of the asset but
the returns and repayments to sukuk holders are directly financed by these assets.
There are fundamental differences between these. The diagrams set out below explain the
mechanics of how each sukuk operates.
关于 Sukuk 工具是否类似于传统债务或股权融资一直存在相当的争论。 这是因为有两种类型
的 Sukuk：
基于资产的： 通过资产的资本价值筹集资金， 但Sukuk 持有人的回报和还款不是直接由这些
资产融资的。
支持资产的： 通过资产的资本价值筹集资金， 但Sukuk 持有人的回报和还款是直接由这些资
产融资的。
这两者之间存在根本区别。下面的图表解释了每种 Sukuk 的运作机制
Asset-based sukuk
Sukuk Al-ijarah: financing acquisition of asserts or raising capital through sale and lease back.
通过出售和回租融资获取资产或筹集资本
1. Sukuk holders subscribe by paying an issue price to a special purpose vehicle (SPV) company.
2. In return, the SPV issues certificates indicating the percentage they own in the SPV.
3. The SPV uses the funds raised and purchases the asset from the obligor (seller).
4. In return, legal ownership is passed to the SPV.
5. The SPV then, acting as a lessor, leases the asset back to the obligor under an Ijarah
agreement.
6. The obligor or lessee pays rentals to the SPV, as the SPV is the owner and lessor of the asset.
7. The SPV then make periodic distributions (rental and capital) to the sukuk holders.
(1) Sukuk 持有人通过向特殊目的实体（SPV，通常指仅为特定、专向目的而设立的法律实
体,主要用于隔离风险，简单理解为“壳公司”，一般无实际经营业务）公司支付发行
价格进行认购。
(2) 作为回报，SPV 发行证书，表明他们在 SPV 中所占的百分比。
(3) SPV 使用筹集的资金从债务人（卖方）处购买资产。
(4) 作为回报，法律所有权转移给 SPV。
(5) 然后，SPV 作为出租人，根据 Ijarah 协议将资产回租给债务人。
(6) 债务人或承租人向 SPV 支付租金，因为 SPV 是资产的所有者和出租人。
(7) SPV 然后向 Sukuk 持有人进行定期分配（租金和资本）。
Asset-backed sukuk
Sukuk: Securitisation of Leasing Portfolio 租赁组合的证券化
1. Sukuk holders subscribe by paying an issue price to a SPV company.
2. In return, the SPV issues certificates indicating the percentage they own in the SPV.
3. The SPV will then purchase a portfolio of assets, which are already generating an income
stream.
4. In return, the SPV obtains the title deeds to the leasing portfolio.
5. The leased assets will be earning positive returns, which are now paid to the SPV company.
6. The SPV then makes periodic distributions (rental and capital) to the sukuk holders.
7. With an asset-based sukuk, ownership of the asset lies with the sukuk holders via the SPV.
1) Sukuk 持有人通过向 SPV 公司支付发行价格进行认购。
2) 作为回报，SPV 发行证书，表明他们在 SPV 中所占的百分比。
3) SPV 然后购买一组已经产生收入流的资产。
4) 作为回报，SPV 获得租赁组合的所有权证书。
5) 租赁资产将获得正回报，这些回报现在支付给 SPV 公司。
6) SPV 然后向 Sukuk 持有人进行定期分配（租金和资本
7) 对于基于资产的 Sukuk，资产的所有权属于通过 SPV 的 Sukuk 持有人
Hence, they would have to maintain and insure the asset. The payment of rentals provides the
return and the final redemption of the sukuk is at a pre-agreed value. As the obligor is the lessee,
the sukuk holders have recourse to him if default occurs. This makes this type of sukuk more akin
to debt or bonds.
因此，他们必须维护和保险资产。租金的支付提供了回报，Sukuk 的最终赎回是预先约定的
价值。由于债务人是承租人，如果发生违约，Sukuk 持有人可以向他追索。这使得这种类型
的 Sukuk 更类似于债务或债券。
Asset-backed sukuk certainly have the attributes of equity finance – the asset is owned by the
SPV. All of the risks and rewards of ownership passes to the SPV. Hence, should the returns fail to
arise the sukuk holders suffer the losses. In addition, redemption for the sukuk holders is at open
market value, which could be nil.
支持资产的 Sukuk 肯定具有股权融资的属性——资产由 SPV 拥有。 所有权的所有风险和回报
都转移给 SPV。因此，如果回报未能产生，Sukuk 持有人将遭受损失。此外，Sukuk 持有人
的赎回是开放市场价格，这可能是零。
Sukuk finance case studies -Sukuk 融资案例研究
For Emirates airline, the use of sukuk finance has been a huge success. The company issued its
first sukuk (Islamic Bond), with a seven -year term, in 2005, which was listed on the Luxembourg
Stock Exchange. The $550m was repaid in full in June 2012.
‘The repayment of our first ever sukuk bond is part of Emirates’ varied financing strategy and
reflects our robust financial position,’ said Sheikh Ahmed bin Saeed Al Maktoum, chairman of the
Emirates Group and chief executive of Emirates airline.
Emirates’ initial injection of equity finance at the time of its creation 24 years ago has been
supplemented by a variety of financing options, including leasing, EU/US export credits,
commercial asset-backed debt, Islamic financing, conventional bonds, as well as sukuk.
Tim Clark, Emirates’ president, recently stated that the airline had traditionally used European
debt to finance purchase of its fast -growing Boeing and Airbus fleet. The French banks were
particularly forthcoming with finance solutions.
However, since the global debt crisis in 2008, the traditional debt markets have taken a risk
averse position – even with a business like Emirates, which has an unbroken profit -making
record.
Clark outlined that obtaining funding for new planes using sukuk could be tricky because Islamic
finance, in addition to forbidding payment of interest, prohibits pure monetary speculation and
requires deals to involve concrete assets. It would be harder to win a seal of approval from
Islamic finance scholars for a sukuk that was based on assets, which the airline did not yet own.
For new aircraft, it is not impossible but it is much more complicated as the cash would have to
go from investors through a special purpose vehicle to the manufacturer before a lease -back
arrangement is put in place. Hence, using existing assets to obtain sukuk finance is far easier.
Emirates currently has two aircraft-based sukuk instruments that have been issued globally, and
is backed by existing aircraft: a $500m issue from GE Capital in November 2009, and a $100m
deal for Nomura in July 2010.
对于阿联酋航空公司来说，使用 Sukuk 融资取得了巨大成功。该公司在 2005 年发行了其第
一笔 Sukuk（伊斯兰债券），期限为七年，于 2012 年 6 月在卢森堡证券交易所全额偿还了
5.5 亿美元。
阿联酋集团董事长兼阿联酋航空公司首席执行官谢赫 ·艾哈迈德·本·赛义德·阿勒马克图姆表
示：“我们第一笔 Sukuk 债券的偿还是我们多样化融资战略的一部分，反映了我们稳健的财
务状况。”
阿联酋在成立时最初的股权融资注入在过去 24 年中通过多种融资选择进行了补充，包括租
赁、欧盟/美国出口信贷、商业资产支持债务、伊斯兰融资、传统债券以及Sukuk。
阿联酋航空公司总裁蒂姆·克拉克最近表示，该航空公司传统上使用欧洲债务为其快速增长
的波音和空客机队融资。法国银行特别愿意提供融资解决方案。
然而，自 2008 年全球债务危机以来，传统债务市场采取了风险规避的立场——即使对于像
阿联酋这样有着不间断盈利记录的业务也是如此。
克拉克指出， 使用Sukuk 为新飞机融资可能会很棘手， 因为伊斯兰金融除了禁止支付利息外，
还禁止纯粹的货币投机， 并要求交易涉及实物资产。 对于尚未拥有的资产的Sukuk 获得伊斯
兰金融学者的批准会更困难。
对于新飞机来说， 这并非不可能， 但要复杂得多， 因为现金必须从投资者通过特殊目的实体
流向制造商，然后才能建立回租安排。因此，使用现有资产获得 Sukuk 融资要容易得多。
阿联酋目前在全球发行了两种基于飞机的 Sukuk 工具，并由现有飞机支持：2009 年 11 月通
用电气资本发行的 5 亿美元，以及 2010 年 7 月 Nomura 发行的 1 亿美元。
Emirates is not the only success story when it comes to the use of sukuk finance. Dubai shopping
mall developer Majid Al Futtaim decided against issuing a conventional bond because of pricing
concerns. It mandated its banks to set up a separate sukuk programme. Turkish Airlines has
followed suit and will finance the purchase of its expanding fleet with Islamic bonds.
阿联酋并不是使用 Sukuk 融资成功的唯一案例。迪拜购物中心开发商马吉德·阿尔·福塔姆因
定价问题决定不发行传统债券。 它授权其银行设立一个单独的Sukuk 计划。 土耳其航空也效
仿，并将通过伊斯兰债券为其不断扩大的机队融资。
The sukuk market has been relatively resilient during the instability in global financial markets,
which has made it more difficult for even highly rated companies around the world to issue
conventional bonds. That is partly because Islamic investors in the Gulf remain cash -rich, partly
due to the limited supply of sukuk, and partly since sukuk investors tend to hold the bonds until
maturity. If these bonds are not being sold on to other investors, there is little or no chance of
the bond value fluctuating.
Recent events have shown the same is not true for conventional bonds. The influence of the
credit rating agencies with their regular reassessment of government and corporate credit ratings
has caused downward movement in prices. As one commentator recently stated: ‘Equities are
the only game in town – bonds carry more risk.’
Sukuk 市场在全球金融市场不稳定期间相对具有弹性， 这使得即使是全球范围内高评级的公
司也更难发行传统债券。部分原因是海湾地区的伊斯兰投资者仍然资金充裕，部分原因是
Sukuk 供应有限，部分原因是 Sukuk 投资者倾向于持有债券直至到期。如果这些债券不被出
售给其他投资者，债券价值几乎没有波动的机会。
最近的事件表明， 传统债券并非如此。 信用评级机构定期重新评估政府和公司信用评级的影
响导致价格下跌。正如一位评论员最近所说：“股票是唯一的选择——债券风险更大”
However, the story of Dubai World, the sovereign investment fund of the Dubai royal family, gives
the other side of the story when it comes to the use of Islamic finance. On 25 November 2009,
the financial world was shocked when Dubai World requested a restructuring of $26bn in debts.
The main concern was the delay in the repayment of the $4bn sukuk, or Islamic bond, of Dubai
World’s developer Nakheel, which was especially known for construction of the Dubai Palm
Islands.
The Nakheel sukuk was quite a complicated instrument. It was broadly based on the
aforementioned Ijarah structure. In theory, the SPV has legal ownership over the asset in this sale
and leaseback arrangement. However, in this case the SPV only had a long leasehold interest for a
period of 50 years. The issue is that leasehold right is not seen as a real right or property right
under UAE law as applicable in Dubai. What may have seemed secure was not.
Nevertheless, the Nakheel sukuk was backed by a few additional guarantees that should have
provided sukuk investors with some recourse. As such, these guarantees gave investors the
confidence to invest in the sukuk. A guarantee from the state -owned parent company, which
implicitly provides a government guarantee for the sukuk (despite the fact that the prospectus
clearly stated otherwise), had reassured investors. This misplaced assumption misled investors in
their risk–return decision on the investment.
The issue, however, did not end there; the complications worsened when the parent company
that acted as guarantor found itself in a situation that made it no better placed than Nakheel to
repay the sukuk. Dubai World is also just a holding company for a number of other companies
beside Nakheel. However, all of Dubai World’s subsidiaries have their own creditors and their
own debts to service, and the important thing for Nakheel sukuk -holders is that the creditors of
Dubai World, through the guarantee, are subordinated to the creditors of the subsidiaries of
Dubai World.
A public statement on 30 November 2009 by the Dubai Finance Department director -general,
that the Dubai World debts are ‘not guaranteed by the government’, appears to correctly reflect
the legal position, as the Dubai government was not required by the lenders, and nor did it
provide, any contractual guarantees in respect of the Dubai World debt.
As history tells us, Nakheel did not default on its Islamic bond. The well reported $10bn bailout,
including providing $4.1bn to assist Nakheel directly from Dubai’s rich neighbours Abu Dhabi,
calmed the markets. But this was only part of the solution. Nakheel also issued new sukuk bonds
to some of its creditors in lieu of amounts due to them. This was a key part of the company's
restructuring.
然而，迪拜世界的故事为伊斯兰金融的使用提供了另一面。2009 年 11 月 25 日，迪拜世界
请求重组 260 亿美元债务的消息震惊了金融界。主要关注的是迪拜世界开发商纳赫尔的 40
亿美元 Sukuk（伊斯兰债券）的还款延迟，纳赫尔以建设迪拜棕榈岛而闻名。
纳赫尔 Sukuk 是一种相当复杂的工具。它主要基于上述Ijarah 结构。理论上，在这种出售和
回租安排中，SPV 对资产拥有法律所有权。然而，在这种情况下，SPV 仅拥有 50 年的长期
租赁权。问题在于，根据适用于迪拜的阿联酋法律，租赁权不被视为真正的权利或财产权。
看似安全的东西实际上并非如此。
尽管如此， 纳赫尔Sukuk 得到了一些额外的担保支持， 这些担保本应为Sukuk 投资者提供一
些追索权。 因此， 这些担保使投资者对投资Sukuk 充满信心。 国有母公司提供的担保， 隐含
地为 Sukuk 提供了政府担保（尽管招股说明书明确声明并非如此）， 使投资者放心。这种错
误的假设误导了投资者在投资的风险-回报决策中。
然而， 问题并没有到此结束； 当作为担保人的母公司发现自己无法比纳赫尔更好地偿还Sukuk
时， 情况变得更加复杂。 迪拜世界也只是一个控股公司， 除了纳赫尔之外， 还拥有其他几家
子公司。 然而， 迪拜世界的所有子公司都有自己的债权人和自己的债务需要偿还， 对纳赫尔
Sukuk 持有人来说重要的是，通过担保，迪拜世界的债权人在法律上次于迪拜世界子公司的
债权人。
2009 年 11 月 30 日， 迪拜财政部局长发表的公开声明称， 迪拜世界债务“未得到政府担保”，
似乎正确反映了法律立场， 因为迪拜政府既未被贷方要求， 也未提供任何关于迪拜世界债务
的合同担保。
正如历史告诉我们， 纳赫尔并未违约其伊斯兰债券。 广为报道的100 亿美元救助， 包括从迪
拜的富裕邻居阿布扎比直接向纳赫尔提供 41 亿美元，平息了市场。但这只是解决方案的一
部分。 纳赫尔还向一些债权人发行了新的Sukuk 债券， 以替代他们应得的金额。 这是公司重
组的关键部分。
In a prospectus attached to the new sukuk, Nakheel revealed that it wrote down the value of its
property and project portfolio by almost Dh74bn (US$20.14bn) in 2009 as its fortunes flagged.
The company also said it changed tactics in response to the financial crisis, forging ahead with a
selection of its projects and putting others on hold.
在新 Sukuk 的招股说明书中，纳赫尔透露，由于其财务状况不佳，2009 年其房地产和项目
组合的价值减记了近 740 亿迪拉姆（201.4 亿美元）。公司还表示，它改变了应对金融危机
的策略， 推进了部分全球债务危机给金融市场带来了冲击， 在撰写本文时， 西方银行仍然不
愿向商业界贷款。伊斯兰金融，特别是Sukuk，在一定程度上填补了传统债务市场留下的空
白。
Conclusion
The global debt crisis sent shockwaves through the financial markets and, at the time of writing
this article, the western banks remain reluctant to loan cash to the business community. Islamic
finance, and in particular sukuk, has to some extent filled the gap left by the traditional debt
markets.
The Sharia principle on which it is based is fundamentally important and should ensure it is a safe
and sensible finance option for both the company needing the finance as well as the sukuk holder.
Clearly, companies like Emirates have shown the way on how to make sukuk one part of their
finance portfolio.
However, the Nakheel story paints a different picture. A complicated Ijarah structure and a lack of
legal clarity as to ownership of the underlying asset have clouded the water. If the Abu Dhabi
bailout failed to materialize, then the story may have been significantly different.
结论：
全球债务危机给金融市场带来了冲击， 在撰写本文时， 西方银行仍然不愿向商业界贷款。 伊
斯兰金融，特别是 Sukuk，在一定程度上填补了传统债务市场留下的空白。
其基于的伊斯兰教法原则至关重要， 应确保其成为需要融资的公司以及Sukuk 持有人的安全
且合理的融资选择。 显然， 像阿联酋这样的公司展示了如何将Sukuk 作为其融资组合的一部
分。
然而， 纳赫尔的故事描绘了不同的画面。 复杂的Ijarah 结构和对基础资产所有权的法律不明
确使得情况变得复杂。如果阿布扎比的救助未能实现，那么故事可能会有很大的不同。
Appendix – The basic principles of Islamic finance
The Islamic economic model has developed over time based on the rulings of Sharia on
commercial and financial transactions. The Islamic finance framework is based on:
⚫ equity, such that all parties involved in a transaction can make informed decisions without
being misled or cheated
⚫ pursuing personal economic gain but without entering into those transactions that are
forbidden (for example, transactions involving alcohol, pork -related products, armaments,
gambling and other socially detrimental activities). Also, speculation is also prohibited (so
options and futures are ruled out)
⚫ the strict prohibition of interest (riba = excess).
伊斯兰经济模型随着时间的推移发展， 基于伊斯兰教法对商业和金融交易的规定。 伊斯兰金
融框架基于：
⚫ 公平，使交易各方能够做出知情决策而不被误导或欺骗
⚫ 追求个人经济利益，但不从事被禁止的交易 （例如，涉及酒精、猪肉产品、武器、赌
博和其他对社会有害的活动） 。 此外， 投机也被禁止 （因此， 期权和期货被排除在外）
⚫ 严格禁止利息（利巴=超额资金孳息）
As stated above, earning interest (riba) is not allowed.
In an Islamic bank, the money provided in the form of deposits is not loaned, but is instead
channelled into an underlying investment activity, which will earn profit. The depositor is
rewarded by a share in that profit, after a management fee is deducted by the bank.
如上所述，赚取利息（利巴）是不允许的。
在伊斯兰银行中， 以存款形式提供的资金不是被贷出， 而是被用于基础投资活动， 以赚取利
润。存款者在银行扣除管理费后，通过分享利润获得回报。
A typical illustration would be how an Islamic bank may purchase a property from a seller and
resell it to a buyer at a profit. The buyer will be allowed to pay in instalments. Compare this to a
typical mortgage where the bank lends money to the buyer and charges interest.
Hence, returns are made from cash returns from a productive source – for example, profits from
selling assets or allowing the use of an asset (rent).
In Islamic banking there are broadly two categories of financing techniques:
⚫ ‘fixed Income’ modes of finance – murabaha, ijara, sukuk
⚫ equity modes of finance – mudaraba, musharaka.
一个典型的例子是伊斯兰银行如何从卖方购买房产并以利润转售给买方。 买方将被允许分期
付款。将其与典型的抵押贷款进行比较，银行向买方贷款并收取利息。
因此，回报来自生产性来源的现金回报——例如，出售资产的利润或允许使用资产（租金）
的利润。
在伊斯兰银行中，融资方式大致分为两类：
⚫ 固定收益模式：Murabaha、Ijara、Sukuk
⚫ 股权模式：Mudaraba、Musharaka
Fixed income modes
(a) Murabaha
Murabaha is a form of trade credit or loan. The key distinction between a murabaha and a loan is
that, with a murabaha, the bank will take actual constructive or physical ownership of the asset.
The asset is then sold to the ‘borrower’ or ‘buyer’ for a profit but they are allowed to pay the
bank over a set number of instalments.
The period of the repayments could be extended, but no penalties or additional mark-up may be
added by the bank. Early payment discounts are not within the contract.
(a) Murabaha
Murabaha 是一种贸易信贷或贷款的形式。Murabaha 与贷款的关键区别在于，Murabaha 中，
银行将实际拥有资产的建设性或物理所有权。然后，资产以利润出售给“借款人”或“买方”，
但他们被允许在一定数量的分期付款中向银行支付。
还款期限可以延长，但银行不得增加任何罚款或额外的加价。合同中不包括提前付款折扣
(b) Ijara
Ijara is the equivalent of lease finance. It is defined as when the use of the underlying asset or
service is transferred for consideration. Under this concept, the bank makes available to the
customer the use of assets or equipment such as plant or motor vehicles for a fixed period and
price. Some of the specifications of an Ijara contact include:
⚫ the use of the leased asset must be specified in the contract
⚫ the lessor (the bank) is responsible for the major maintenance of the underlying assets
(ownership costs)
⚫ the lessee is held for maintaining the asset in proper order.
Ijara 相当于租赁融资。 它被定义为将基础资产或服务的使用权转让以换取对价。 根据这一概
念，银行向客户提供资产或设备（如工厂或机动车）的使用权，为期固定时间和价格。Ijara
合同的一些规定包括：
⚫ 租赁资产的使用必须在合同中指定
⚫ 出租人（银行）负责基础资产的主要维护（所有权成本）
⚫ 承租人负责保持资产的适当状态。
(c) Sukuk
Companies often issue bonds to enable them to raise debt finance. The bond holder receives
interest and this is paid before dividends.
This is prohibited under Islamic law. Instead, Islamic bonds (or sukuk) are linked to an underlying
asset, such that a sukuk holder is a partial owner in the underlying assets and profit is linked to
the performance of the underlying asset. So, for example, a sukuk holder will participate in the
ownership of the company issuing the sukuk and has a right to profits (but will equally bear their
share of any losses).
公司经常发行债券以筹集债务融资。债券持有人获得利息，这在支付股息之前支付。
这在伊斯兰法下是被禁止的。 相反， 伊斯兰债券 （或Sukuk） 与基础资产相关联， 使得Sukuk
持有人成为基础资产的部分所有者，利润与基础资产的表现相关。因此，例如，Sukuk 持有
人将参与发行 Sukuk 的公司的所有权，并有权获得利润（但也同样承担任何损失的份额）。
Equity modes
(a) Mudaraba
Mudaraba is a special kind of partnership where one partner gives money to another for
investing it in a commercial enterprise. The investment comes from the first partner (who is
called ‘rab ul mal’), while the management and work is an exclusive responsibility of the other
(who is called ‘mudarib’).
The Mudaraba (profit sharing) is a contract, with one party providing 100% of the capital and the
other party providing its specialist knowledge to invest the capital and manage the investment
project. Profits generated are shared between the parties according to a pre -agreed ratio. In a
Mudaraba only the lender of the money has to take losses.
This arrangement is therefore most closely aligned with equity finance.
(a) Mudaraba
Mudaraba 是一种特殊的合伙关系，其中一方将资金交给另一方用于投资商业企业。投资来
自第一方投资者（称为“Rab ul Mal”），而管理和工作是另一方投资经理（称为“Mudarib”）
的专有责任。
Mudaraba（利润分享）是一种合同，一方提供 100%的资本，另一方提供其专业知识以投资
资本并管理投资项目。产生的利润根据预先约定的比例在各方之间分享。在 Mudaraba 中，
只有资金的提供方需要承担损失。
因此，这种安排与股权融资最为接近。
(b) Musharaka
Musharaka is a relationship between two or more parties that contribute capital to a business,
and divide the net profit and loss pro rata. It is most closely aligned with the concept of venture
capital. All providers of capital are entitled to participate in management, but are not required to
do so. The profit is distributed among the partners in pre-agreed ratios, while the loss is borne by
each partner strictly in proportion to their respective capital.
(b) Musharaka
Musharaka 是两个或更多方之间的一种关系， 各方贡献资本到企业中， 并按比例分配净利润
和损失。它与风险投资的概念最为接近。 所有资本提供者都有权参与管理，但不要求这样
做。 利润根据预先约定的比例在合作伙伴之间分配， 而损失则由每个合作伙伴严格按照其各
自的资本比例承担。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 Islamic finance – theory and practical use of sukuk bonds.pdf

stem_zh:
Net present value (NPV) and real options
净现值（NPV）和实物期权
The conventional NPV method assumes that a project commences immediately and proceeds until it finishes, as originally
predicted. Therefore it assumes that a decision has to be made on a now or never basis, and once made, it cannot be
changed. It does not recognise that most investment appraisal decisions are flexible and give managers a choice of what
actions to undertake.
传统的净现值方法假设一个项目立即开始， 并按照最初的预测进行直到完成。 因此， 它假定必须在现在立即开始
或永远放弃这个项目的基础上做出决定， 一旦做出决定， 就不能改变。 这种方法没有认识到， 大多数投资评估决
策都是灵活的，管理者可以选择采取什么行动。
The real options method estimates a value for this flexibility and choice, which is present when managers are making a
decision on whether or not to undertake a project. Real options build on net present value in situations where uncertainty
exists and, for example: (i) when the decision does not have to be made on a now or never basis, but can be delayed, (ii)
when a decision can be changed once it has been made, or (iii) when there are opportunities to exploit in the future
contingent on an initial project being undertaken. Therefore, where an organisation has some flexibility in the decision
that has been, or is going to be made, an option exists for the organisation to alter its decision at a future date and this
choice has a value.
当管理者决定是否实施一个项目时， 灵活性和选择就会存在， 实物期权方法估计了这种灵活性和选择的价值。 实
物期权是用于计算净现值存在不确定性的情况，例如： （i）决策不一定要立即做或永远放弃的基础上做出，可以
推迟， （ii）做出决策后可以改变，或（iii）正在进行的项目在未来有机会进行再投资。因此，如果一个组织在已
经或将要做出的决定中具有一定的灵活性， 或者存在一个在未来日期改变其决定的选择权， 那么这些选择具有价
值。
With conventional NPV, risks and uncertainties related to the project are accounted for in the cost of capital, through
attaching probabilities to discrete outcomes and/or conducting sensitivity analysis or stress tests. Options, on the other
hand, view risks and uncertainties as opportunities, where upside outcomes can be exploited, but the organisation has
the option to disregard any downside impact.
在传统的净现值中，与项目相关的风险和不确定性通过将结果的离散性和/或进行敏感性分析或压力测试换算为
概率来计入资本成本。 而实物期权将风险和不确定性视为机会， 可以利用有利结果同时可以选择性忽略任何不利
影响。
Real options methodology takes into account the time available before a decision has to be made and the risks and
uncertainties attached to a project. It uses these factors to estimate an additional value that can be attributable to the
project.
实物期权方法考虑了作出决定之前的可用时间以及项目所附带的风险和不确定性。它评估了这些因素给项目带
来的附加值。
Estimating the value of real options
实物期权的估值
Although there are numerous types of real options, in Advanced Financial Management, candidates are only expected to
explain and compute an estimate of the value attributable to three types of real options:
尽管实物期权有多种类型，但在高级财务管理课程中，考生只需要解释和计算三种实物期权的估值：
(i) The option to delay a decision to a future date (which is a type of call option)
(ii) The option to abandon a project once it has commenced if circumstances no longer justify the continuation of the
project (which is a type of put option), and
(iii) The option to exploit follow-on opportunities which may arise from taking on an initial project (which is a type of call
option).
（i）将推迟决策（投资）到未来日期的期权（这是一种看涨期权）
（ii）在项目开始后当条件不合适再续投资时就放弃该项目的期权（这是一种看跌期权） ，以及
（iii）在项目开始后可能产生的后续投资机会的期权（这是一种看涨期权） 。
In addition to this, candidates are expected to be able to explain (but not compute the value of) redeployment or
switching options, where assets used in projects can be switched to other projects and activities.
除此之外， 考生还应能够解释 （但不计算其价值） 重新部署或转换的期权， 即项目中使用的资产可以转换到其他
项目。
For the Advanced Financial Management exam purposes, it can be assumed that real options are European-style options,
which can be exercised at a particular time in the future and their value will be estimated using the Black-Scholes Option
Pricing (BSOP) model and the put -call parity to estimate the option values. However, assuming that the option is a
European-style option and using the BSOP model may not provide the best estimate of the option’s value (see the section
on limitations and assumptions below).
在高级财务管理考试中，均假设实物期权是欧式期权即可以在未来的特定时间行权，其价值将使用Black-Scholes
期权定价（BSOP）模型来估算。但是，该假设及使用BSOP 模型可能无法提供期权的最佳估值（参见下面的限制
和假设部分） 。
Five variables are used in calculating the value of real options using the BSOP model as follows:
使用 BSOP 模型计算实物期权价值时会使用以下五个变量：
The underlying asset value (Pa), which is the present value of future cash flows arising from the project.
The exercise price (Pe), which is the amount paid when the call option is exercised or amount received if the put option
is exercised.
The risk-free (r), which is normally given or taken from the return offered by a short-dated government bill. Although this
is normally the discrete annualised rate and the BSOP model uses the continuously compounded rate, for Advanced
Financial Management purposes the continuous and discrete rates can be assumed to be the same when estimating the
value of real options.
The volatility (s), which is the risk attached to the project or underlying asset, measured by the standard deviation.
The time (t), which is the time, in years, that is left before the opportunity to exercise ends.
基础资产价值（Pa） ，即项目产生的未来现金流的现值。
行权价（Pe） ，即在行使看涨期权时支付的金额或在行使看跌期权时收到的金额。
无风险利率（r） ，通常即短期政府债券的回报率。尽管这通常是离散年化利率，BSOP 模型需要使用连续复合利
率，但对于高级财务管理科目，在估算实物期权价值时，可以假定连续和离散利率相同。
波动性（s）用标准差衡量的项目或标的资产的风险波动性。
时间（t） ，即在行权之前剩余的时间，以年为单位。
The following three examples demonstrate how the BSOP model can be used to estimate the value of each of the three
types of options.
以下三个示例演示了如何使用 BSOP 模型来估算三种期权的价值。
Example 1: Delaying the decision to undertake a project
A company is considering bidding for the exclusive rights to undertake a project, which will initially cost $35m.
一家公司正在考虑竞标一个项目的独家经营权，初始费用为 35m 美元。
The company has forecast the following end of year cash flows for the four-year project.
公司预测了项目四年的现金流。
Year 1 2 3 4
Cash flows($m) 20 15 10 5
The relevant cost of capital for this project is 11% and the risk free rate is 4.5%. The likely volatility (standard deviation)
of the cash flows is estimated to be 50%.
本项目相关资本成本为 11%，无风险率为 4.5%。现金流标准差为 50%。
Solution:
NPV without any option to delay the decision
Year 0 1 2 3 4
Cash flows($m) -35 20 15 10 5
PV(11%)($m) -35 18 12.2 7.3 3.3
NPV = $5.8m
Supposing the company does not have to make the decision right now but can wait for two years before it needs to make
the decision.
假设公司现在不必做出决定，但可以等待两年后才需要做出决定。
NPV with the option to delay the decision for two years
Year 3 4 5 6
Cash flows($m) 20 15 10 5
PV(11%)($m) 14.6 9.9 5.9 2.7
Variables to be used in the BSOP model
BSOP 的各项变量
Asset value (Pa) = $14.6m + $9.9m + $5.9m + $2.7m = $33.1m
Exercise price (Pe) = $35m
Exercise date (t) = 2
Risk free rate (r) = 4.5%
Volatility (s) = 50%
Using the BSOP model
Putting the above values into the BSOP calculator given in the exam will give the following:
Based on the facts that the company can delay its decision by two years (a call option) and a high volatility, it can bid as
much as $9.56m instead of $5.8m for the exclusive rights to undertake the project. The increase in value reflects the time
before the decision has to be made and the volatility of the cash flows.
基于该公司可以将决定推迟两年（看涨期权）和高波动性的事实，它可以出价高达9.56m 美元，而不是 5.8m 美
元，以获得该项目的独家权利。价值的增加是由作出决定之前的时间以及现金流的波动性产生的。
Example 2: Exploiting a follow-on project
A company is considering a project with a small positive NPV of $3m but there is a possibility of further expansion using
the technologies developed for the initial project. The expansion would involve undertaking a second project in four years’
time. Currently, the present values of the cash flows of the second project are estimated to be $90m and its estimated
cost in four years is expected to be $140m. The standard deviation of the project’s cash flows is likely to be 40% and the
risk free rate of return is currently 5%.
一家公司正在考虑一个 NPV 为 300 万美元的小项目，但随着技术进步有机会追加投资。追加投资将涉及在四年
内进行第二个项目。 目前， 第二个项目现金流的现值估计为9000 万美元， 四年后的估计成本预计为1.4 亿美元。
项目现金流的标准差可能为 40%，无风险回报率目前为 5%。
Solution:
The variables to be used in the BSOP model for the second (follow-on) project are as follows:
Asset Value (Pa) = $90m
Exercise price (Pe) = $140m
Exercise date (t) = 4
Risk free rate (r) = 5%
Volatility (s) = 40%
Using the BSOP model
Putting the above values into the BSOP calculator given in the exam will give the following:
As stated earlier, a follow-on project is also a call option. Therefore, the overall value to the company is $23.85m, when
both the projects are considered together. At present the cost of $140m seems substantial compared to the present value
of the cash flows arising from the second project. Conventional NPV would probably return a negative NPV for the second
project and therefore the company would most likely not undertake the first project either. However, there are four years
to go before a decision on whether or not to undertake the second project needs to be made. A lot could happen to the
cash flows given the high volatility rate, in that time. The company can use the value of $23.85m to decide whether or
not to invest in the first project or whether it should invest its funds in other activities. It could even consider the
possibility that it may be able to sell the combined rights to both projects for $23.85m.
如前所述， 后续项目也是一种看涨期权。 因此， 当两个项目一起考虑时， 公司的整体价值为23.85m 美元。 目前，
与第二个项目产生的现金流的现值相比，140m 美元的成本似乎相当可观。传统的 NPV 可能得出第二个项目的
NPV 为负，因此公司很可能也不会投资第一个项目。然而，在决定是否进行第二个项目之前，还有四年的时间。
在这段时间，由于高波动率，现金流可能会发生很多变化。该公司可以使用23.85m 美元的估值来决定是否投资
第一个项目， 或者是否应该将其资金投入其他活动。 它甚至可以考虑以23.85m 美元的价格合并出售这两个项目。
Example 3: The option to abandon a project
Duck Co is considering a five-year project with an initial cost of $37,500,000 and has estimated the present values of the
project’s cash flows as follows:
Duck Co 正在考虑一个为期五年的项目，初始成本为 37,500,000 美元，并估计了项目现金流的现值如下：
Year 1 2 3 4 5
Present
Value($ 000s)
1,496.9 4,938.8 9,946.5 7,064.2 1,3602.9
Swan Co has approached Duck Co and offered to buy the entire project for $28m at the start of year three. The risk free
rate of return is 4%. Duck Co’s finance director is of the opinion that there are many uncertainties surrounding the project
and has assessed that the cash flows can vary by a standard deviation of as much as 35% because of these uncertainties.
Swan Co 已经与 Duck Co 接触， 并提出在第三年年初以28m 美元的价格收购整个项目。 无风险回报率为4%。Duck
Co 的财务总监认为，该项目存在许多不确定性，并评估称，由于这些不确定性，现金流量可能会因高达 35%的
标准差。
Solution:
Swan Co’s offer can be considered to be a real option for Duck Co. Since it is an offer to sell the project as an abandonment
option, a put option value is calculated based on the finance director’s assessment of the standard deviation and using
the Black-Scholes option pricing (BSOP) model, together with the put-call parity formula.
Although Duck Co will not actually obtain any immediate cash flow from Swan Co’s offer, the real option computation
below, indicates that the project is worth pursuing because the volatility may result in increases in future cash flows.
Swan Co 的报价可被视为 Duck Co 的实物期权。由于它是将项目作为放弃期权出售的报价，因此根据财务总监对
标准差的评估并使用 Black-Scholes 期权定价来计算看跌期权价值（BSOP）模型，以及看跌期权奇偶公式。
虽然 Duck Co 实际上不会从 Swan Co 的报价中获得任何即时现金流，但下面的实物期权计算表明，该项目值得追
求，因为波动可能导致未来现金流增加。
Without the real option
Year 1 2 3 4 5
Present
Value($ 000s)
1,496.9 4,938.8 9,946.5 7,064.2 13,602.9
Present value of cash flows approx. = $37,049,300
Cost of initial investment = $37,500,000
NPV of project = $37,049,300 – $37,500,000 = $(450,700)
With the real option
The asset value of the real option is the sum of the PV of cash flows foregone in years three, four and five, if the option
is exercised ($9.9m + $7.1m + $13.6m = $30.6m)
实物期权的资产价值是在行使期权的情况下，放弃的第三、第四和第五年发生的现金流量现值的总和
Asset Value (Pa) = $30.6m
Exercise price (Pe) = $28m
Exercise date (t) = 2
Risk free rate (r) = 4%
Volatility (s) = 35%
Net present value of the project with the put option is approximately $3.00m ($3.45m – $0.45m).
If Swan Co’s offer is not considered, then the project gives a marginal negative net present value, although the results of
any sensitivity analysis need to be considered as well. It could be recommended that, if only these results are taken into
consideration, the company should not proceed with the project. However, after taking account of Swan Co’s offer and
the finance director’s assessment, the net present value of the project is positive. This would suggest that Duck Co should
undertake the project.
如果不考虑 Swan Co 的报价， 则该项目将给出边际负净现值， 尽管任何敏感性分析的结果也需要考虑。 可以建议，
如果只考虑到这些结果，该公司不应继续进行该项目。然而，在考虑到Swan Co 的报价和财务总监的评估后，项
目的净现值为正。这将建议 Duck 公司承担该项目。
Limitations and assumptions
Many of the limitations and assumptions discussed below stem from the fact that a model developed for financial
products is used to assess flexibility and choice embedded within physical, long-term investments.
下文讨论了 BSOP 估值的限制和假设源于这样一个事实：该模型初始是为金融产品开发，在这里用来评估长期实
物投资的灵活性和选择。
European-style options or American-style options
The BSOP model is a simplification of the binomial model and it assumes that the real option is a European-style option,
which can only be exercised on the date that the option expires. An American-style option can be exercised at any time
up to the expiry date. Most options, real or financial, would, in reality, be American-style options.
BSOP 模型是二项式模型的简化，它假设实物期权是欧式期权，只能在期权到期之日行使。美式期权可以在到期
日之前的任何时间行使。大多数期权，无论是实物期权还是金融期权，实际上都是美式期权。
In many cases the value of a European-style option and an equivalent American-style option would be largely the same,
because unless the underlying asset on which the option is based is due to receive some income before the option expires,
there is no benefit in exercising the option early. An option prior to expiry will have a time-value attached to it and this
means that the value of an option prior to expiry will be greater than any intrinsic value the option may have, if it were
exercised.
在许多情况下， 欧式期权和等效的美式期权的价值在很大程度上是相同的， 因为除非期权所基于的标的资产在到
期前会获得一些收入， 否则提前行权没有好处。 到期前的期权具有时间价值， 这意味着期权在到期前的价值将大
于期权可能拥有的任何实值（如果被行权）。
However, if the underlying asset on which the option is based is due to receive some income before the option’s expiry;
say for example, a dividend payment for an equity share, then an early exercise for an option on that share may be
beneficial. With real options, a similar situation may occur when the possible actions of competitors may make an exercise
of an option before expiry the better decision. In these situations the American-style option will have a value greater than
the equivalent European-style option.
然而， 如果期权所基于的标的资产在期权到期之前将收到一些收入； 例如股息， 那么对该股的期权提前行权可能
是有益的。 对于实物期权， 当竞争对手的行动可能使期权在到期之前行权是更好的决定时， 期权提前行权可能是
有益的。在这种情况下，美式期权的价值将大于等效的欧式期权。
Because of these reasons, the BSOP model will either underestimate the value of an option or give a value close to its
true value. Nevertheless, estimating and adding the value of real options embedded within a project, to a net present
value computation will give a more accurate assessment of the true value of the project and reduce the propensity of
organisations to under-invest.
由于这些原因，BSOP 模型要么低估期权的价值，要么给出接近其真实价值的价值。然而，把实物期权的价值添
加到净现值计算中，将更准确地评估项目的真实价值，并减少投资不足的倾向。
Estimating volatility
The BSOP model assumes that the volatility or risk of the underlying asset can be determined accurately and readily.
Whereas for traded financial assets this would most probably be the case, as there is likely to be sufficient historical data
available to assess the underlying asset’s volatility, this is probably not going to be the case for real options. Real options
would probably be available on large, one-off projects, for which there would be little or no historical data available.
BSOP 模型假设基础资产的波动性或风险可以准确和容易地确定。而对于交易的金融资产来说，这种情况很可能
是准确的， 因为可能有足够的历史数据来评估标的资产的波动性， 而对于实物期权来说， 情况可能并非如此。 大
型一次性项目可能会适用，而这些项目的历史数据很少或根本没有。
Volatility in such situations would need to be estimated using simulations, such as the Monte -Carlo simulation model,
with the need to ensure that the model is developed accurately and the data input used to generate the simulations
reasonably reflects what is likely to happen in practice.
这种情况下的波动性需要使用蒙特卡洛模拟模型等模拟来估计， 需要确保模型的准确性， 用于生成模拟的数据输
入能合理地反映实际可能发生的情况。
Other limitations of real options
The BSOP model requires further assumptions to be made involving the variables used in the model, the primary ones
being:
BSOP 模型需要进一步假设，涉及模型中使用的变量，主要的假设是：
(a) The BSOP model assumes that the underlying project or asset is traded within a situation of perfect markets where
information on the asset is available freely and is reflected in the asset value correctly. Further it assumes that a market
exists to trade the underlying project or asset without restrictions (that is, that the market is frictionless)
(a)BSOP 模型假设标的项目或资产在完美市场的情况下进行交易，在这种情况下，资产信息可以自由获取，并正
确反映在资产价值中。此外，它假设存在一个市场，可以不受限制地交易标的项目或资产（即市场是无摩擦的）
(b) The BSOP model assumes that interest rates and the underlying asset volatility remain constant until the expiry time
ends. Further, it assumes that the time to expiry can be estimated accurately
(b)BSOP 模型假设利率和标的资产波动性保持不变，直到到期日。此外，它假定到期时间可以准确估计
(c) The BSOP model assumes that the project and the asset’s cash flows follow a lognormal distribution, similar to equity
markets on which the model is based
(c)BSOP 模型假设项目和资产的现金流量遵循正态分布，类似于模型所基于的股票市场
(d) The BSOP model does not take account of behavioural anomalies which may be displayed by managers when making
decisions, such as over- or under-optimism
(d)BSOP 模式没有考虑到管理人员在作出决定时可能表现出的行为异常，例如过于乐观或不乐观
(e) The BSOP model assumes that any contractual obligations involving future commitments made between parties,
which are then used in constructing the option, will be binding and will be fulfilled. For example, in example three above,
it is assumed that Swan Co will fulfil its commitment to purchase the project from Duck Co in two years’ time for $28m
and there is therefore no risk of non-fulfilment of that commitment.
(e)BSOP 模式假定，涉及各方之间做出的未来承诺的任何合同义务将具有约束力并将得到履行，这些承诺随后用
于构建期权。例如，在上述例三中，假定 Swan Co 将履行其承诺，在两年内以 28m 美元从 Duck Co 购买该项目，
因此不存在不履行承诺的风险。
In any given situation, one or more of these assumptions may not apply. The BSOP model therefore does not provide a
‘correct’ value, but instead it provides an indicative value which can be attached to the flexibility of a choice of possible
future actions that may be embedded within a project.
在任何特定情况下， 这些假设中的一个或多个假设可能不适用。 因此，BSOP 模型不提供“正确”的值， 而是提供了
一个指示性价值，可以将灵活性附加到项目未来可能的行动的上。
Conclusion
This article discussed how real options thinking can add to investment appraisal decisions and in particular NPV
estimations by considering the value which can be attached to flexibility which may be embedded within a project
because of the choice managers may have when making investment decisions. It then worked through computations of
three real options situations, using the BSOP model. The article then considered the limitations of, and assumptions made
when, applying the BSOP model to real options computations. The value computed can therefore be considered indicative
rather than conclusive or correct.
本文讨论了实物期权如何通过考虑可附加到项目中的灵活性的价值来帮助投资评估决策， 特别是在 NPV 估值时，
这可能是由于经理在做出投资决策时将选择性嵌入到项目中。本文使用 BSOP 模型计算了三种实物期权情况。然
后，本文考虑了将 BSOP 模型应用于实物期权计算的局限性和假设。因此，计算出的值可以被视为指示性的，而
不是结论性的或准确的。
The second article will consider how managers can use real options to make strategic investment appraisal decisions.
第二篇文章将考虑管理者如何使用实物期权进行战略投资评估决策。
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:更新-AFM 考官文章解析 NPV and real options.pdf

stem_zh:
Using real options when making financial strategy decisions
在做财务战略决策时使用实物期权
A traditional project and option valuation
传统项目与期权估值
Let us imagine that a company is proposing a major expansion project. The operational
management team have forecast the cash flows relevant to the project in line with the standard
assumptions and policies set down by the financial management. The financial management have
then discounted these cash flows at a cost of capital of 11% and this has resulted in an NPV of just
$5m. For the sake of simplicity tax, inflation and other real world complications have been
ignored.
让我们想象一下， 一家公司正在提出一个重大的扩张项目。 运营管理团队根据财务管理部门
制定的假设和政策预测项目相关的现金流。财务管理部门按 11%的资本成本对这些现金流
进行折现，算出净现值仅为$5m。为了简单起见，不考虑税收、通货膨胀和其他现实世界的
复杂问题。
NPV calculation – overall:
净现值计算—总体：
Those executives who are keen proponents of this expansion are disappointed by the low NPV and
fear that the project is unlikely to win approval when competing for funds against other projects.
They consider that the project is being undervalued and, hence, a meeting with the financial
management team is arranged. At this meeting the financial management team query the large net
cash outflow that is forecast to occur at the end of year two. As a result of this, it becomes
apparent that the project comprises an initial investment of $600m which will produce net cash
inflows of $110m for the following 10 years, followed by a further investment of $300m after two
years which will increase the net cash inflows by $48m to $158m per year for the remaining eight
years. Further discussion reveals that the additional investment after two years is discretionary and
does not necessarily need to be made.
那些热衷于此次扩张的高管对低净现值感到失望， 并担心该项目在与其他项目竞争资金时不
太可能获得批准。 他们认为该项目被低估了， 因此， 安排了一次与财务管理团队的会议。 在
这次会议上， 财务管理团队询问了预计在第二年年底产生的大额净现金流出。因此得知，该
项目初始投资的初始投资$600m 使得未来 10 年中每年获得$110m 的净现金流入，第二年年
底投资的$300m 将使得接下来的 8 年中每年获得的净现金流入增加$48m（从$110m 增加至
$158m）。进一步的讨论表明，两年后是否追加投资是可选的，不一定需要进行。
Hence, the project could be viewed as an initial expansion costing $600m – phase 1 – followed by
an option to expand further after two years – phase 2.
因此， 该项目可以被视为一个耗资6 亿美元的初始扩建项目——第一阶段，和初始扩建两年
后的一个是否要进一步扩建的选择——第二阶段。
If separate NPV calculations are carried out for each of these phases. The results below are
obtained.
如果对每个阶段进行单独的净现值计算。所得结果如下。
NPV calculation – phase 1:
净现值计算-第一阶段：
NPV calculation – phase 2:
净现值计算——第二阶段：
The total NPV is fundamentally unchanged as $47.8m – $43m = $4.8m, which is about the same
as the $5m calculated initially. To the extent there is a difference, this can be attributed to
rounding. This analysis alone provides some insight as, given that there is no obligation for the
company to carry out phase two, the overall NPV must be at least $47.8m which far exceeds the
initial NPV calculated of $5m.
总净现值基本不变，为 $47.8m – $43m = $4.8m。这与最初计算的$5m 是差不多的，误差可
以归因于四舍五入。但考虑到公司没有一定要执行第二阶段的义务， 总体净现值必须至少为
$47.8m，远远超过最初计算的 $5m 的净现值。
It is worth noting that the discretionary spend at the end of year two has also been discounted at
the 11% cost of capital. Although this is the approach commonly taken it could be more accurate
to discount such discretionary expenditure at the risk free rate. This is because discretionary
expenditure has much less operational risk than the net cash inflows that it is hoped will arise from
such expenditure. If a risk free rate of 5% is used the present value of the $300m expenditure at
the end of year two would be $272.1m. This is $28.5m (272.1 – 243.6) more than the present
value if the cost of capital is used. Hence, the original overall NPV and the NPV of phase two
should perhaps be reduced by this $28.5m. This approach would be consistent with the treatment
of the exercise price in the Black Scholes Option Pricing model.
值得注意的是，第二年年底的可自由支配支出也按 11%的资本成本贴现。虽然这是通常采
取的方法， 但以无风险率贴现这种可自由支配的支出会更准确。 这是因为可自由支配支出的
经营风险远低于希望由这种支出产生的净现金流入。如果使用5%的无风险率，那么在第二
年年底的$300m 支出的现值将为$272.1m，这比用资本成本折现高了$28.5m (272.1 – 243.6)。
因此， 总净现值和第二阶段净现值可能应该减少$28.5m。 这种方法与布莱克斯科尔斯期权定
价模型中对行使价格的处理方法一致。
Although phase two does not currently seem worthwhile the option to carry out this phase can
only add value as an option can never have a negative value. If the cash flows expected from
phase two were to become favourable, the company will have the ability to carry out phase two
and reap the benefit. Hence, the overall NPV will be $47.8m plus the value of the option to carry
out phase two.
虽然第二阶段目前似乎不值得， 但这一阶段相关的期权只会增加价值， 因为期权永远不会有
负值。 如果第二阶段的现金流变得有利， 该公司将有权力执行第二阶段并从中获得受益。 因
此，总的净现值将为$47.8m 加上执行第二阶段的期权的价值。
In order to value the option to carry out phase two we must first attribute figures to the inputs
required for the Black Scholes option pricing (BSOP) formula:
• Pe = the investment required after two years to carry out phase two = $300m
• Pa = the PV of the net cash inflows currently forecast to arise from phase two = $200.6m
(this must exclude the Pe)
• t = the time until phase two will begin = 2 years
• s = the volatility – assumed to be 0.4 (standard deviation)
• r = the risk free rate – assumed to be 5%
The Pe and Pa figures can be seen in the calculation of the NPV for phase two and, as we know,
the company has the option to expand into phase two after two years. The s and r will both be
given within any exam question and, hence, suitable figures have been assumed.
为了计算执行第二阶段的期权 的价值 ，我们必须首先 找到布莱克斯科尔斯期权定价 模型
（BSOP）所需输入的数字：
• Pe =两年后实施第二阶段所需投资的金额=$300m
• Pa =第二阶段产生的现金净流入的现值=$200.6m （不含 Pe）
• t=多久后开始第二阶段=2 年
• s=波动率-假设为 0.4（标准差）
• r=无风险率-假设为 5%
Pe 和 Pa 的数值可以在第二阶段的净现值的计算中找到， 正如我们所知， 该公司可以选择在
两年后开始第二阶段。s 和 r 在考试时会直接给出，因此，已经假定了合适的数字。
Using these inputs into the BSOP calculator given in the exam will give the following:
将这些数字输入到考试中给出的 BSOP 计算器中，将得到以下内容：
Option value:
d1 = 0.2519
d2 = – 0.8175
N(d1) = 0.4006
N(d2) = 0.2068
c = $24.22m
p = $95.07m
An option to expand (or follow -on) is a type of call option. Hence, the total NPV for the project
with the option to expand = $47.8m + $24.22m =  $72.02m. As a result of the financial
management taking the time to better understand the project and the real options within it a project
which seemed fairly marginal, has been shown to be attractive and is far more likely to win
approval.
扩建（或继续进行）的期权是一种看涨期权（即期权价值为$24.22m）。因此，有扩建权的
该项目的总净现值为 = $47.8m + $24.22m = $72.02m。由于财务管理人员花时间更深入地分
析了解了项目和其中的实物期权， 一个原本边缘化项目被证明是有吸引力的， 而且使得该项
目更有可能获得批准。
The attractiveness of the project arises because phase one of the project is itself attractive and the
company can potentially benefit if the phase two expansion finally becomes worthwhile.
该项目的吸引力是因为项目的第一阶段本身就有吸引力，而且如果第二阶段的扩建最终变得
值得公司就有可能受益。
As discussed in the previous real options article, there are significant problems associated with
using BSOP to value real options and, hence, the option value of $24.22m calculated should be
treated as indicative only and should be used with care.
正如之前的实物期权的文章中所讨论的那样， 使用BSOP 评估实物期权存在重大问题， 因此，
计算出的$24.22m 的期权价值应被仅视为是仅供参考的，应谨慎使用它。
A portfolio of projects and option valuation
项目组合和期权估值
As soon as executives launch a strategy conditions change in the environment within which they
are operating and indeed their knowledge of that environment is updated. Hence, managers must
actively manage and respond accordingly. Traditional NPV analysis is probably too static a tool to
reflect this active management. This is because it tends to assume a company will follow a
previously agreed plan and does not account so well for how events may unfold. Instead managers
should perhaps view the projects they could undertake to achieve their goals as a portfolio of real
options which they could potentially exercise over time.
一旦高管在他们所处的运营环境中发布了一个战略条件变化， 他们对这个环境的了解就更新
了。因此，经理们必须积极地据此进行管理。 传统的净现值分析可能是一个过于静态的工具，
无法反映这种积极管理的程度。 这是因为它倾向于认为一家公司会遵循先前商定的计划， 而
没有很好地解释事件可能如何开展。 管理者或许应该把他们为实现目标而可以进行的项目视
为一个他们未来可能行权的一个实物期权组合。
Imagine a company is faced with six independent projects as follows:
想象一下，一个公司面临着以下六个独立的项目：
Given this basic NPV analysis projects U and V would be accepted and a total NPV of $8m would
be generated for the company.
鉴于基本的净现值分析项目 U 和 V 将被接受，并将给公司带来 8m 的净现值。
Let us now imagine that projects U and X have to be carried out immediately or not at all. In other
words there is no option to delay these projects. Hence, project U would be accepted to generate
an NPV of $5m and project X would be rejected. A useful analogy here is that of fruit growing in
a garden. Projects U and X represent fruits which have to be picked now. Project U represents the
perfectly ripe fruit which can be sold or eaten, while project X represents the rotten fruit which
must be picked but then discarded.
现在让我们想象一下，U 和 X 项目必须立即执行，或者根本不执行。换句话说，没有办法
推迟这些项目。因此，项目 U 将被接受并产生$5m 的净现值，项目 X 将被拒绝。这里的一
个类比是在花园里种植的水果。项目 U 和 X 代表了现在必须被挑选出来的水果。U 项目代
表可以出售或食用的完全成熟的水果，而 X 项目代表必须采摘但随后丢弃的腐烂水果。
The remaining projects can be delayed and represent fruits which do not have to be picked
immediately and which have the potential to develop into perfectly ripe fruit. Given the volatility
of the cash flows from these projects and the period by which they could be delayed option values
can be calculated for these projects. This data and the resulting option values are presented below:
其余的项目可能会被推迟， 它们代表的水果不必立即采摘， 而且有潜力发展成完全成熟的果
实。基于这些项目的现金流的波动性以及它们可能被延迟的时期， 可以计算出这些项目的期
权价值。这些数据和相应的期权价值如下：
These option values have been calculated using an assumed risk free rate of 5%.
这些期权值是假设无风险利率为 5%算出来的。
These option values total to a value of $30.5m. If the NPV of project U, which has already been
accepted, is added to this a total value of $35.5m is created. This value is significantly higher than
the original NPV of $8m which could have been generated by accepting projects U and V.
However, careful management is necessary if as much value as possible is to be generated.
这些期权的价值共为$30.5m。 如果将已被接受的项目U 的净现值相加， 则其总价值为$35.5m。
这一价值明显高于接受 U 和 V 项目可能产生的$8m 的净现值。然而，如果要产生尽可能多
的价值，就需要仔细的进行管理。
We will now consider each of projects V, W, Y and Z separately:
现在，我们将分别考虑 V 项目、W 项目、Y 项目和 Z 项目：
Project V:
This project looks most promising. It has a positive NPV if exercised now but its value as an
option is significantly higher. Hence, exercising now would appear sub -optimal as with further
nurturing a higher value could be generated. To continue the fruit analogy this project represents a
fruit which could be picked now and eaten or sold but which, with careful cultivation, could
become a larger and better fruit. However, just as ripening fruit can be eaten by pests, there is
potential for project value to be lost – through the actions of competitors for instance. Hence, the
company may decide to exercise the option early to realise the existing positive NPV.
这个项目看起来最有希望。 如果现在进行该项目， 它的净现值为正， 但它作为期权的价值要
高得多。因此，现在进行似乎是次优的，因为随着进一步的培养，可能会产生更高的价值。
继续以水果类比， 这个项目代表了一种现在可以采摘、 食用或出售的水果， 但经过仔细种植，
可以成为更大更好的水果。 然而， 就像成熟的水果可以被害虫吃掉一样， 项目的价值也有可
能失去——例如，因为竞争对手的行动而失去价值。 因此， 该公司可能会决定提前行使期权，
以实现现有的正净现值。
Project W:
This project is not at all promising. It has a negative NPV if exercised now and, as it has a low
volatility and there is a relatively short time until a decision has to be made regarding this project,
it has a low option value. Hence, this project will probably never be worth exercising. This project
could be thought of as the small, late developing fruit which is unlikely to ripen before the season
ends.
项目 W：
这个项目一点也没有前途。 如果现在开展， 它的净现值为负， 而且由于它的波动性较低， 而
且距离必须对该项目做出决定的时间相对较短， 因此它的期权价值较低。 因此， 这个项目可
能永远都不值得开展。 这个项目可以被认为是一种小的， 发育较晚的果实， 不太可能在季节
结束前成熟。
Project Y:
Despite currently having a negative NPV this project has a high value as an option. This is due to
the fact that it will not expire for three years and has a relatively high volatility when compared to
the other projects. Hence, this project will probably be worth exercising at some later date. This
project represents the unripe fruit which cannot be picked now, but which is expected to mature
into a perfectly ripe fruit in the future.
项目 Y：
尽管目前的净现值为负， 但该项目作为一种期权具有很高的价值。 这是因为它在三年内不会
到期， 而且与其他项目相比具有相对较高的波动性。 因此， 这个项目可能值得在以后的某些
时候实施。 这个项目代表了未成熟的果实， 现在不能采摘， 但有望在未来成熟成完全成熟的
果实。
Project Z:
This project is similar to project Y but is much less promising. It currently has the same negative
NPV as project Y but as it has a lower volatility and a shorter time until it expires it has a lower
value as an option when compared to project Y. This project seems unlikely to be worth
exercising but there is still a reasonable chance that it could move ‘into the money’ and, hence,
may become worth exercising in the future. As with project Y this project is also an unripe fruit
which cannot be picked now. However, its chances of maturing into a perfectly ripe fruit seem
much less.
项目 Z：
这个项目与 Y 项目相似，但前景要小得多。目前与 Y 项目有相同的负的净现值，但它波动
性较低，时间距到期日的时间较短，与 Y 项目比起来 Z 项目的期权价值更低。这个项目目
前似乎不太可能值得实施，但它仍有合理的可能在未来“赚钱”并在未来可能变得值得开展。
与 Y 项目一样，这个项目也是一个未成熟的水果，现在不能采摘。然而，它成熟成一个完
全成熟的果实的机会似乎要小得多。
How does the use of option valuation analysis help when compared to a traditional NPV
analysis?
与传统的净现值分析相比，使用期权估值分析有何帮助？
As previously stated traditional NPV analysis would lead to the acceptance of projects U and V,
resulting in an NPV of $8m, and the rejection of the other four projects. However, option
valuation analysis results in the acceptance of project U generating an NPV of $5m, the rejection
of project X and options to carry out the four other projects in the future the total value of which
has been estimated as $30.5m.
如前所述，传统的净现值分析将导致接受 U 和 V 项目，导致总净现值为$8m，并拒绝接受
其他四个项目。然而，期权估值分析的结果是接受项目 U 产生$5m 的净现值，拒绝项目 X
和获取未来执行其他四个项目的期权（期权总价值预期为$30.5m）。
Further the analysis encourages active management:
进一步的分析鼓励了积极的管理：
Project V has been identified as worthy of careful management to ensure it is carried out at the
optimal time to maximise the value created but at the same time ensuring that the existing positive
NPV is not destroyed.
项目 V 已被确定为值得仔细管理，以确保在现有的正净现值不被破坏的情况下在最佳时间
进行行权以使所创造的价值最大化。
Of the remaining projects Project W has been identified as the project deserving least attention
while with careful nurturing project Y and even project Z could finally create value for the
company.
在剩余的项目中，W 项目被确定为最不值得关注的项目，然而 Y 项目和 Z 项目都应认真培
养以最终为公司创造价值。
It is crucial to recognise that projects, just like fruit, do require nurturing. This is because as the
time approaches when the project must be carried out or abandoned, the option value will always
tend to decline assuming all other variables remain constant. This is because the time to maturity
falls and the present value of the exercise price, the investment to be made to carry out the project,
is rising.
至关重要的是要认识到， 这些项目， 就像水果一样，确实需要培育。这是因为，随着项目必
须执行或放弃的时间的临近， 假设所有其他变量保持不变，期权价值总是趋于下降。 这是因
为到期时间（t）变小，而行使价格的现值即实施项目的投资（Pe）正在变大。
Hence, without nurturing all the option values will move to zero over time. For this not to happen
there must be good luck or active management. Good luck could add value to a project because,
for instance, sudden growth in the economy could mean the returns from a project become higher
than originally forecast. Active management could involve taking action to reduce the costs or
increase the revenues associated with a project. Equally action could be taken to reduce the initial
investment required in the project.
因此， 如果不培养， 所有的期权价值将随着时间的推移而变为零。 要不让这种情况发生， 就
必须有好运或积极的管理。 好运可能会增加一个项目的价值， 例如， 经济的突然增长可能意
味着一个项目的回报会高于预期。 积极的管理可能包括采取行动来降低成本或增加与项目相
关的收入。同样可以采取行动，减少该项目所需的初始投资。
Using the option valuation approach has helped identify those projects most likely to benefit from
such nurturing and, hence, is useful as a company follows and develops its financial strategy.
使用期权估值方法有助于确定那些最有可能从这种培育中受益的项目， 因此， 对于公司遵循
和发展其财务战略是有用的。
Inter-dependent projects
相互依赖的项目
So far this article has looked at how our understanding of a single project and a portfolio of
independent projects could be enhanced by the use of option valuation. Let us now consider
briefly interdependent projects.
到目前为止， 本文研究了如何通过使用期权估值来增强我们对单个项目和一个独立项目组合
的理解。现在让我们简要地考虑一下相互依赖的项目。
Just as you and I are faced with a myriad of options when buying a new car the car manufacturer
also has many options.
就像你和我在购买一辆新车时面临着无数的选择一样，汽车制造商也有很多选择。
For instance a car manufacturer may have a project to launch a new saloon model. From this
saloon model, an estate model could then be launched, and from this a 4x4 version of the estate
model could be launched. This is a strategy followed by Skoda with their Octavia and Superb
ranges. In effect the company has the initial project (the launch of the saloon), followed by a call
option on the launch of the estate, which itself has a call option on the launch of the 4x4 estate.
Hence, the company has a call on a call! This is known as a nest of options or nested options.
例如， 汽车制造商可能会有一个推出新款轿车的项目。基于这个新款轿车车型， 可以选择是
否推出客货两用款车型，然后据客货两用款车型考虑是否要推出四轮驱动款车型。斯柯达
Octavia 和 Superb 系列就是采用此策略的。 实际上， 该公司的初始项目 （推出新款轿车车型）
是有一个推出客货两用款车型的看涨期权的，而该看涨期权本身也有一个推出四轮驱动车型
的看涨期权。 因此， 该公司的看涨期权中还有一个看涨期权，这被称为期权嵌套或嵌套期权。
Another car manufacturer may also have a project to launch a new saloon model. From this saloon,
an estate model could be launched and a sports utility vehicle (SUV) model could also be
launched. This is a strategy followed by BMW with their 3 series and 5 series ranges. In effect the
company has the initial project (the launch of the saloon), followed by a call option on the launch
of the estate and a call option on the launch of the SUV model (the BMW X3 and X5). While
these options are not nested they are obviously still very much related.
另一家汽车制造商可能也有一个项目， 以推出一个新的轿车车型。基于这个轿车车型， 可以
推出客货两用款车型， 也可以推出运动型多用途车（SUV）车型。 这是宝马3 系列和 5 系列
所采取得策略。 实际上， 该公司的初始项目 （推出新款轿车车型）是有一个推出客货两用款
车型的看涨期权和一个推出 SUV 车型（宝马 X3 和 X5）的看涨期权得。虽然这些期权不是
嵌套的，但它们显然是非常相关的。
Understanding how these options inter -relate is obviously useful to a company as they develop
their strategy. Once again good luck and active management play their part. For instance a few
bad winters is likely to enhance future sales of SUV’s and 4x4 estates. This in turn enhances the
value of the call options to manufacture these models and, hence, the value of the original product
launch.
了解这些期权之间是如何相互关联的， 对公司在制定战略时显然是有用的。 好运和积极的管
理再次发挥了他们的作用。例如，有时寒冷的冬天可能会提高 SUV 和四轮驱动车的销量，
这会提高推出这些车型的看涨期权的价值，从而提高初始项目的价值。
Active management could involve the development of a new more fuel efficient 4x4 system. Any
such increase in fuel efficiency is also likely to enhance the sales of 4x4 equipped SUV’s and
estates, and enhance the value of the call options to manufacture these models and, hence, the
value of the original product launch. Conversely, extra sales of 4x4 equipped models may reduce
sales of other models.
积极的管理可能包括开发一种新的更省油的四轮驱动系统。 燃油效率的提高也有可能提高配
备四轮驱动的 SUV 车和客货两用车的销量，并提高推出这些车型的看涨期权的价值，从而
提高初始项目价值。相对的，四轮驱动车型销售额增加可能意味着其他车型的销量会降低。
Obviously the initial product launch should be marketed in such a way as to maximise its success.
Active management should also ensure that this initial product launch should ensure consumers’
perception of the new model is developed in such a way that the chances of success of the follow
on models is optimised.
显然，初始项目发布时应以最大化其成功之处的方式进行营销。此外，积极的管理还应确保
最初的产品发布时拓展了消费者对新车型的认知，从而提高后续车型的成功机会。
Our knowledge of the determinants of option values can also be useful. For instance if sales of
SUV’s are high in a more volatile market such as China this adds value to the option to develop
the SUV variant of the model in the future, and, hence, enhances the value of the initial project to
launch the saloon.
我们对期权的决定因素的了解也可能很有用。例如，如果 SUV 的销量在中国等波动较大的
市场很高，这就增加了未来开发 SUV 车型的期权价值，因此，提高了初始的推出新车型项
目的价值。
Conclusion
结论
This article has demonstrated how option valuation techniques can help understand the potential
value of projects and how financial strategy decisions can be made using this knowledge in order
to maximise the results arising from projects and, hence, maximise company value.
本文展示了期权估值技术如何帮助我们理解项目的潜在价值， 以及如何利用这些知识做出财
务战略决策，以最大化项目产出从而实现公司价值最大化。

stem_zh:
F阶段
高顿千人模考 & ACCA 官方 Pre-Mock 试卷  - 解析看课图解
千人模考解析直播/回放路径：
课件在直播入口下方（用电脑打开才能下载）
电脑登录网址：
一、按照历年真题这个顺序刷：
2024 年 - 2023 SD - 2023 MJ - 2022 SD - 2022 MJ
二、考前 3 小时控时模考做以下两套模考卷：
1）先设置自己当个考季的考试时间，系统自动匹配到当个考季课包
2）进入当个考季的 冲刺备考包 ，找到 模考 试卷
3）同时千人模考有 解析直播，回放隔天生成
www.gaodun.com
2、 当 个 考 季 官方 Pre-Mock 试卷
1）官方 Pre-Mock 试卷进入网址（ 输入注册 ACCA ID的邮箱号进入） ：
https://cbeptcn.accaglobal.com/
登录不进去官网可以尝试以下步骤：
2、【谷歌浏览器 +热点】不停的刷新和重进尝试登录
3、如果还无法登录，是因为防火墙的原因，建议【挂  VPN 】再去登录
4、还是登录失败，换台电脑设备再重复以上操作
2）官方 12 月季考 Pre-Mock 试卷，路径图解文件（ ACCA官网上 怎么找试卷和答案）：
链接:
https://pan.baidu.com/s/1Z6cPDVia9FLzuOrYYOXLiQ?pwd=gpcd
提取码: gpcd
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:P阶段图解-高顿千人模考 & ACCA官方Pre-Mock试卷.pdf

stem_zh:
24 年 12 月季考 Pre-Mock 使用路径图解
一、登录官方 Pre-Mock 的网址链接（使用注册 ACCA ID 的邮箱号进入）：
https://cbeptcn.accaglobal.com/
二、找试卷图解
找到科目，注意科目版本的选择（TX/UK，SBR-INT，AAA-INT）
特殊状况：翻页不成功
滑动条同时拉到底等待几秒，才能翻页
结束并退出考试才能在 Mark 菜单中才能看见答案
三、找答案图解
答案是灰色字体“Click here”不太好找，跟着图解走
source_doc: ACCA:afm:case:当个考季官方Pre-Mock 试卷路径图解.pdf
